metadata.purpose: Per-sub-group view for AI evaluation in SG phases. Each sub-group carries its label + description and the lean per-verse rows (vr_id, reference, verse_text, strongs, translit, gloss, meaning). No VCG context, no review status — the input is the verses + the SG frame, and the question is whether the verses fit the SG.
metadata.effective_subgroup_rule: new_subgroup.code if present else current.subgroup_code; unrouted verses surface under '(unrouted)'.

subgroups.label: Wisdom as holistic inner character and orientation
subgroups.description: Terms that name wisdom as a constituted holistic quality of the inner person — a settled orientation of the whole person that shapes perception, decision, and character. Includes the structural opposite (unwise). Spirit-given, covenantally oriented, morally constitutive.

verses.verse_text: Eph 5:15 Look carefully then how you walk , not as unwise but as wise ,
verses.meaning: Names the manner of life to be avoided — the term characterises the inner orientation of those who walk without wisdom, contrasted with those who walk wisely.

verses.verse_text: Mat 11:19 The Son of Man came eating and drinking , and they say , ‘ Look at him ! A glutton and a drunkard , a friend of tax collectors and sinners !’ Yet wisdom is justified by her deeds .”
verses.meaning: Names the quality whose vindication is demonstrated through deeds — the term identifies wisdom as the inner capacity that justifies itself by its visible outcomes.

verses.verse_text: Mat 12:42 The queen of the South will rise up at the judgment with this generation and condemn it , for she came from the ends of the earth to hear the wisdom of Solomon , and behold , something greater than Solomon is here .
verses.meaning: Names the celebrated quality that drew a queen from distant lands — the term identifies Solomon's wisdom as the inner endowment that commanded international recognition.

verses.verse_text: Mat 13:54 and coming to his hometown he taught them in their synagogue , so that they were astonished , and said , “ Where did this man get this wisdom and these mighty works ?
verses.meaning: Names the quality that astonishes those who hear Jesus — the term identifies his wisdom as the inner source of his teaching ability, raising the question of its origin.

verses.verse_text: Mar 6:2 And on the Sabbath he began to teach in the synagogue , and many who heard him were astonished , saying , “ Where did this man get these things ? What is the wisdom given to him ? How are such mighty works done by his hands ?
verses.meaning: Names the wisdom given to Jesus — the term identifies it as a received inner endowment underlying his teaching, which astonishes the synagogue audience.

verses.verse_text: Luk 2:40 And the child grew and became strong , filled with wisdom . And the favor of God was upon him .
verses.meaning: Names the quality with which the child Jesus was filled — the term identifies wisdom as an inner attribute that grew alongside him and was accompanied by divine favour.

verses.verse_text: Luk 2:52 And Jesus increased in wisdom and in stature and in favor with God and man .
verses.meaning: Names the quality in which Jesus increased — the term identifies wisdom as an inner capacity that developed progressively alongside his physical and relational growth.

verses.verse_text: Luk 7:35 Yet wisdom is justified by all her children .”
verses.meaning: Names the quality vindicated by its fruits — the term identifies wisdom as an inner reality demonstrated and justified by its outcomes in persons.

verses.verse_text: Luk 11:31 The queen of the South will rise up at the judgment with the men of this generation and condemn them , for she came from the ends of the earth to hear the wisdom of Solomon , and behold , something greater than Solomon is here .
verses.meaning: Names the quality that motivated a queen's journey — the term identifies Solomon's wisdom as the inner endowment whose fame reached to the ends of the earth.

verses.verse_text: Luk 11:49 Therefore also the Wisdom of God said , ‘I will send them prophets and apostles , some of whom they will kill and persecute ,’
verses.meaning: Names wisdom as a divine agent — the term identifies it as the inner deliberative capacity of God through which the sending of prophets and apostles is announced.

verses.verse_text: Luk 21:15 for I will give you a mouth and wisdom , which none of your adversaries will be able to withstand or contradict .
verses.meaning: Names wisdom as a gift to be given — the term identifies it as an inner enabling that Jesus promises to bestow, making his followers' speech irresistible to adversaries.

verses.verse_text: Act 6:3 Therefore , brothers , pick out from among you seven men of good repute , full of the Spirit and of wisdom , whom we will appoint to this duty .
verses.meaning: Names the inner quality required of those appointed to serve — the term identifies wisdom as a necessary personal endowment for the task of community leadership.

verses.verse_text: Act 6:10 But they could not withstand the wisdom and the Spirit with which he was speaking .
verses.meaning: Names the inner quality that Stephen's opponents cannot withstand — the term identifies wisdom as the active inner capacity driving his speech, paired with the Spirit.

verses.verse_text: Act 7:10 and rescued him out of all his afflictions and gave him favor and wisdom before Pharaoh , king of Egypt , who made him ruler over Egypt and over all his household .
verses.meaning: Names the quality God granted Joseph — the term identifies wisdom as an inner endowment that gave Joseph favour and effective capacity before Pharaoh.

verses.verse_text: Act 7:22 And Moses was instructed in all the wisdom of the Egyptians , and he was mighty in his words and deeds .
verses.meaning: Names the content of Moses' education — the term identifies wisdom as the accumulated inner understanding imparted through Egyptian instruction.

verses.verse_text: Rom 11:33 Oh , the depth of the riches and wisdom and knowledge of God ! How unsearchable are his judgments and how inscrutable his ways !
verses.meaning: Names one of the depths belonging to God — the term identifies wisdom as a divine inner attribute whose inscrutability prompts doxology.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 1:17 For Christ did not send me to baptize but to preach the gospel , and not with words of eloquent wisdom , lest the cross of Christ be emptied of its power.
verses.meaning: Names the human eloquence Paul deliberately avoided — the term identifies wisdom of speech as an inner rhetorical resource that, if deployed, would diminish the cross.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 1:19 For it is written , “I will destroy the wisdom of the wise , and the discernment of the discerning I will thwart .”
verses.meaning: sofia names wisdom as the human category that God will destroy — already noted above under G4680 sofos for the same verse.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 1:20 Where is the one who is wise ? Where is the scribe ? Where is the debater of this age ? Has not God made foolish the wisdom of the world ?
verses.meaning: Names the capacity God has rendered foolish — the term identifies worldly wisdom as an inner intellectual orientation that God overturns through the proclamation of the cross.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 1:21 For since , in the wisdom of God , the world did not know God through wisdom , it pleased God through the folly of what we preach to save those who believe .
verses.meaning: Names the framework within which the world failed to know God — the term identifies wisdom as the inner cognitive mode through which the world attempted to reach God and failed.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 1:22 For Jews demand signs and Greeks seek wisdom ,
verses.meaning: Names what Greeks seek — the term identifies wisdom as the inner quality Greeks prize, contrasted with the signs Jews demand.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 1:24 but to those who are called , both Jews and Greeks , Christ the power of God and the wisdom of God .
verses.meaning: Names what Christ is for those called — the term identifies wisdom as a quality Christ himself embodies and becomes for believers.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 1:30 And because of him you are in Christ Jesus , who became to us wisdom from God , righteousness and sanctification and redemption ,
verses.meaning: Names what Christ has become for believers — the term identifies wisdom as a quality Christ personally instantiates, alongside righteousness, sanctification, and redemption.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 2:1 And I , when I came to you , brothers , did not come proclaiming to you the testimony of God with lofty speech or wisdom .
verses.meaning: sofia names human wisdom as a mode of presentation Paul deliberately avoided — the cross does not commend itself through the category of wisdom.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 2:4 and my speech and my message were not in plausible words of wisdom , but in demonstration of the Spirit and of power ,
verses.meaning: sofia names human wisdom as the rhetorical category Paul's preaching did not employ — Spirit and power replaced it.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 2:5 so that your faith might not rest in the wisdom of men but in the power of God .
verses.meaning: sofia names human wisdom as the wrong foundation for faith — power of God is the alternative.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 2:6 Yet among the mature we do impart wisdom , although it is not a wisdom of this age or of the rulers of this age , who are doomed to pass away .
verses.meaning: sofia names the wisdom Paul does impart — but it is not the human category. The term is used for both kinds, distinguished by source and age.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 2:7 But we impart a secret and hidden wisdom of God , which God decreed before the ages for our glory .
verses.meaning: sofia names the divine wisdom — hidden, decreed before the ages, for our glory. This is not human wisdom but God's own.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 2:13 And we impart this in words not taught by human wisdom but taught by the Spirit , interpreting spiritual truths to those who are spiritual .
verses.meaning: sofia names human wisdom as the source of teaching that Paul explicitly was not using — Spirit teaching is the alternative.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 3:19 For the wisdom of this world is folly with God . For it is written , “He catches the wise in their craftiness ,”
verses.meaning: sofia names worldly wisdom as what God judges to be folly — the human category is inverted by the divine assessment.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 12:8 For to one is given through the Spirit the utterance of wisdom , and to another the utterance of knowledge according to the same Spirit ,
verses.meaning: sofia names wisdom as a Spirit-given inner endowment — distributed as a gift for the community, not a natural human capacity.

verses.verse_text: 2Cor 1:12 For our boast is this , the testimony of our conscience , that we behaved in the world with simplicity and godly sincerity , not by earthly wisdom but by the grace of God , and supremely so toward you .
verses.meaning: sofia names earthly wisdom as the mode of conduct Paul and his companions did not employ — grace is the alternative.

verses.verse_text: Eph 1:8 which he lavished upon us , in all wisdom and insight
verses.meaning: sofia names wisdom as what God lavishes on believers — a divine gift of inner capacity and orientation.

verses.verse_text: Eph 1:17 that the God of our Lord Jesus Christ , the Father of glory , may give you the Spirit of wisdom and of revelation in the knowledge of him ,
verses.meaning: sofia names wisdom as Spirit-given — a gift enabling knowledge of God.

verses.verse_text: Eph 3:10 so that through the church the manifold wisdom of God might now be made known to the rulers and authorities in the heavenly places .
verses.meaning: sofia names God's manifold wisdom as what the church displays to spiritual powers — wisdom as a divine attribute made visible in the community.

verses.verse_text: Col 1:9 And so , from the day we heard , we have not ceased to pray for you , asking that you may be filled with the knowledge of his will in all spiritual wisdom and understanding ,
verses.meaning: sofia names spiritual wisdom as the content of Paul's prayer — God-given, Spirit-oriented, enabling knowledge of God's will.

verses.verse_text: Col 1:28 Him we proclaim , warning everyone and teaching everyone with all wisdom , that we may present everyone mature in Christ .
verses.meaning: sofia names wisdom as the mode of proclamation — the inner quality that shapes how Paul presents Christ.

verses.verse_text: Col 2:3 in whom are hidden all the treasures of wisdom and knowledge .
verses.meaning: sofia names wisdom as hidden in Christ — all wisdom belongs to him as its source and treasury.

verses.verse_text: Col 2:23 These have indeed an appearance of wisdom in promoting self-made religion and asceticism and severity to the body , but they are of no value in stopping the indulgence of the flesh .
verses.meaning: sofia names the appearance of wisdom without its substance — the ascetic practices look like wisdom but produce no inner transformation.

verses.verse_text: Col 3:16 Let the word of Christ dwell in you richly , teaching and admonishing one another in all wisdom , singing psalms and hymns and spiritual songs , with thankfulness in your hearts to God .
verses.meaning: sofia names wisdom as the quality that shapes community teaching — the inner orientation that enables wise instruction.

verses.verse_text: Col 4:5 Walk in wisdom toward outsiders , making the best use of the time .
verses.meaning: sofia names wisdom as the inner quality that should shape conduct in relation to those outside the community — relational and situational discernment.

verses.verse_text: Jam 1:5 If any of you lacks wisdom , let him ask God , who gives generously to all without reproach , and it will be given him .
verses.meaning: sofia names wisdom as something God gives in response to request — an inner capacity not possessed naturally but received from God.

verses.verse_text: Jam 3:13 Who is wise and understanding among you ? By his good conduct let him show his works in the meekness of wisdom .
verses.meaning: sofia names wisdom as an inner quality visible in meek, good conduct — wisdom shows in the manner, not in the claim.

verses.verse_text: Jam 3:15 This is not the wisdom that comes down from above , but is earthly , unspiritual , demonic .
verses.meaning: sofia names false wisdom by its source and character — earthly, unspiritual, demonic wisdom is named and judged.

verses.verse_text: Jam 3:17 But the wisdom from above is first pure , then peaceable , gentle , open to reason , full of mercy and good fruits , impartial and sincere .
verses.meaning: sofia names the wisdom from above by its qualities — purity, peace, gentleness, openness, mercy, sincerity. The inner quality and its outward expression are both described.

verses.verse_text: 2Pe 3:15 And count the patience of our Lord as salvation , just as our beloved brother Paul also wrote to you according to the wisdom given him ,
verses.meaning: sofia names the wisdom given to Paul — a divine endowment that shaped his writing.

verses.verse_text: Rev 5:12 saying with a loud voice , “ Worthy is the Lamb who was slain , to receive power and wealth and wisdom and might and honor and glory and blessing !”
verses.meaning: sofia names wisdom as an attribute belonging to the Lamb — included in the doxological acclamation of what Christ is worthy to receive.

verses.verse_text: Rev 7:12 saying , “ Amen ! Blessing and glory and wisdom and thanksgiving and honor and power and might be to our God forever and ever ! Amen .”
verses.meaning: sofia names wisdom as a divine attribute in the heavenly doxology — belonging to God as his own.

verses.verse_text: Rev 13:18 This calls for wisdom : let the one who has understanding calculate the number of the beast , for it is the number of a man , and his number is 666 .
verses.meaning: sofia names wisdom as the inner capacity needed to engage with the mystery of the beast's number — a call for discernment in reading apocalyptic revelation.

verses.verse_text: Rev 17:9 This calls for a mind with wisdom : the seven heads are seven mountains on which the woman is seated ;
verses.meaning: sofia names wisdom as the inner quality of the mind needed to interpret the vision — the wise mind can receive and decode what is shown.

verses.verse_text: 2Ti 3:15 and how from childhood you have been acquainted with the sacred writings , which are able to make you wise for salvation through faith in Christ Jesus .
verses.meaning: sofizo names the act of making wise — Scripture's transformative work on the inner person. The writings impart wisdom that orients the person toward salvation.

verses.verse_text: 2Pe 1:16 For we did not follow cleverly devised myths when we made known to you the power and coming of our Lord Jesus Christ , but we were eyewitnesses of his majesty .
verses.meaning: sofizo names the devising of myths — the inner capacity for clever construction turned to fabrication. The term here names what the apostles explicitly did not do.

verses.verse_text: Mat 11:25 At that time Jesus declared , “I thank you , Father , Lord of heaven and earth , that you have hidden these things from the wise and understanding and revealed them to little children ;
verses.meaning: sofos names the wise as those from whom divine revelation is hidden — wisdom as a human category that is excluded from what God discloses. The wise are defined by what they do not receive.

verses.verse_text: Mat 23:34 Therefore I send you prophets and wise men and scribes , some of whom you will kill and crucify , and some you will flog in your synagogues and persecute from town to town ,
verses.meaning: sofos names wise men as a class sent by Christ — their wisdom qualifies them for the mission, alongside prophets and scribes.

verses.verse_text: Rom 1:14 I am under obligation both to Greeks and to barbarians , both to the wise and to the foolish .
verses.meaning: Names a class of persons — the term identifies the wise as one of two human categories Paul acknowledges an obligation to, alongside the foolish.

verses.verse_text: Rom 1:22 Claiming to be wise , they became fools ,
verses.meaning: Names the self-designation that precedes a fall — the term identifies claiming wisdom as an inner posture that is immediately confounded by the outcome of becoming fools.

verses.verse_text: Rom 16:19 For your obedience is known to all , so that I rejoice over you , but I want you to be wise as to what is good and innocent as to what is evil .
verses.meaning: Names the inner quality Paul desires for the community — the term identifies wisdom toward what is good as the positive disposition he wants them to cultivate.

verses.verse_text: Rom 16:27 to the only wise God be glory forevermore through Jesus Christ ! Amen .
verses.meaning: sofos names wisdom as a divine attribute — God alone is wise in this absolute sense. The doxology attributes wisdom exclusively to God.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 1:19 For it is written , “I will destroy the wisdom of the wise , and the discernment of the discerning I will thwart .”
verses.meaning: sofos names the wise as those whose wisdom God will destroy — human wisdom as a category that stands under divine judgment.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 1:20 Where is the one who is wise ? Where is the scribe ? Where is the debater of this age ? Has not God made foolish the wisdom of the world ?
verses.meaning: sofos names the wise person whose position is challenged — the cross has rendered the category of human wisdom inadequate.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 1:25 For the foolishness of God is wiser than men , and the weakness of God is stronger than men .
verses.meaning: sofos names the comparative — what appears as God's foolishness surpasses human wisdom. The term anchors the contrast.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 1:26 For consider your calling , brothers : not many of you were wise according to worldly standards , not many were powerful , not many were of noble birth .
verses.meaning: Names the social standing that few in the community hold — the term identifies wisdom according to worldly standards as a quality largely absent among those God called.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 1:27 But God chose what is foolish in the world to shame the wise ; God chose what is weak in the world to shame the strong ;
verses.meaning: sofos names the wise as those whom God's choice of the foolish puts to shame — the human wisdom category is overturned by divine election.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 3:10 According to the grace of God given to me , like a skilled master builder I laid a foundation , and someone else is building upon it. Let each one take care how he builds upon it.
verses.meaning: sofos names Paul's inner quality as a skilled builder — wisdom as craft competence applied to apostolic work. Note: this use is not the cross-overturning pattern but commendation of genuine skill.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 3:18 Let no one deceive himself . If anyone among you thinks that he is wise in this age , let him become a fool that he may become wise .
verses.meaning: sofos names the self-assessed wisdom of the age — the person who claims it must relinquish it to receive genuine wisdom.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 3:19 For the wisdom of this world is folly with God . For it is written , “He catches the wise in their craftiness ,”
verses.meaning: sofos names the wise whose own craftiness becomes the trap — human wisdom turned against itself by divine action.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 3:20 and again , “The Lord knows the thoughts of the wise , that they are futile .”
verses.meaning: sofos names the wise whose inner thoughts are known to God and judged futile — the inner life of the wisdom class is transparent to God and found wanting.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 6:5 I say this to your shame . Can it be that there is no one among you wise enough to settle a dispute between the brothers ,
verses.meaning: sofos names the inner capacity for judgment that should be present in the community — wisdom as the qualification for arbitration.

verses.verse_text: Eph 5:15 Look carefully then how you walk , not as unwise but as wise ,
verses.meaning: sofos names wisdom as the inner quality of the person whose conduct is deliberate and careful — the wise walk is contrasted with the unwise walk.

verses.verse_text: Jam 3:13 Who is wise and understanding among you ? By his good conduct let him show his works in the meekness of wisdom .
verses.meaning: sofos names wisdom as an inner quality that must be visible in conduct — the wise person demonstrates their wisdom through meekness and good works, not through claim.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 1:19 For it is written , “I will destroy the wisdom of the wise , and the discernment of the discerning I will thwart .”
verses.meaning: sunesis names discernment as what God will thwart — the inner comprehension of the discerning is the category God acts against in the cross-wisdom reversal.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 1:19 For it is written , “I will destroy the wisdom of the wise , and the discernment of the discerning I will thwart .”
verses.meaning: sunetos names the discerning person whose inner capacity God thwarts — intelligence as the human category overturned by God's action.

verses.verse_text: Mat 16:23 But he turned and said to Peter , “ Get behind me , Satan ! You are a hindrance to me . For you are not setting your mind on the things of God , but on the things of man .”
verses.meaning: froneō names the inner orientation — what the mind is set on. Peter's mind is oriented toward human concerns, not divine ones. The rebuke names the direction of the inner person's habitual attention.

verses.verse_text: Mar 8:33 But turning and seeing his disciples , he rebuked Peter and said , “ Get behind me , Satan ! For you are not setting your mind on the things of God , but on the things of man .”
verses.meaning: froneō names the same inner orientation as Mat 16:23 — the parallel passage. Same meaning.

verses.verse_text: Act 28:22 But we desire to hear from you what your views are, for with regard to this sect we know that everywhere it is spoken against .”
verses.meaning: Names the inner orientation or views held — the term identifies the content of what the group thinks about Paul's message, which they wish to disclose.

verses.verse_text: Rom 8:5 For those who live according to the flesh set their minds on the things of the flesh , but those who live according to the Spirit set their minds on the things of the Spirit .
verses.meaning: Names the governing inner orientation of those living according to flesh or Spirit — the term identifies the mind's settled direction as the defining mark of each way of life.

verses.verse_text: Rom 12:3 For by the grace given to me I say to everyone among you not to think of himself more highly than he ought to think , but to think with sober judgment , each according to the measure of faith that God has assigned .
verses.meaning: Names the act of self-assessment — the term identifies the inner judgement a person makes about their own standing, which Paul warns should be sober and measured.

verses.verse_text: Rom 12:16 Live in harmony with one another . Do not be haughty , but associate with the lowly . Never be wise in your own sight.
verses.meaning: froneō names self-attributed inner wisdom — the same pattern as Rom 11:25. The mind set on one's own superiority is warned against.

verses.verse_text: Rom 14:6 The one who observes the day , observes it in honor of the Lord . The one who eats , eats in honor of the Lord , since he gives thanks to God , while the one who abstains , abstains in honor of the Lord and gives thanks to God .
verses.meaning: Names the inner disposition of honour — the term identifies the orientation of mind that governs observance of days and eating practices, in each case directed toward the Lord.

verses.verse_text: Rom 15:5 May the God of endurance and encouragement grant you to live in such harmony with one another , in accord with Christ Jesus ,
verses.meaning: Names the inner disposition of harmony — the term identifies the state of like-mindedness Paul prays God will grant so the community may live in accord.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 13:11 When I was a child , I spoke like a child , I thought like a child , I reasoned like a child . When I became a man , I gave up childish ways .
verses.meaning: froneō names the inner mode of thinking characteristic of a stage of life — childish thinking gives way to adult thinking. Wisdom here is associated with maturity of inner orientation.

verses.verse_text: 2Cor 13:11 Finally , brothers , rejoice . Aim for restoration , comfort one another, agree with one another , live in peace ; and the God of love and peace will be with you .
verses.meaning: froneō names the inner orientation of mutual alignment — being of the same mind with one another as an expression of wisdom in community.

verses.verse_text: Gal 5:10 I have confidence in the Lord that you will take no other view , and the one who is troubling you will bear the penalty , whoever he is .
verses.meaning: froneō names the inner orientation — the view held. Paul appeals to the Galatians' alignment with truth as what they will maintain.

verses.verse_text: Phili 1:7 It is right for me to feel this way about you all , because I hold you in my heart , for you are all partakers with me of grace , both in my imprisonment and in the defense and confirmation of the gospel .
verses.meaning: froneō names the inner orientation of affection and regard — Paul's whole-person disposition toward the Philippians is shaped by their shared participation in the gospel.

verses.verse_text: Phili 2:2 complete my joy by being of the same mind , having the same love , being in full accord and of one mind .
verses.meaning: froneō names the inner orientation of unity — being of the same mind is the expression of love and accord in the community.

verses.verse_text: Phili 2:5 Have this mind among yourselves , which is yours in Christ Jesus ,
verses.meaning: froneō names the inner orientation that is to be shared — the mind of Christ as the pattern for the community's whole-person disposition.

verses.verse_text: Phili 3:15 Let those of us who are mature think this way, and if in anything you think otherwise , God will reveal that also to you .
verses.meaning: froneō names the inner orientation of the mature — the settled disposition that characterises those who have grown in Christ.

verses.verse_text: Phili 3:19 Their end is destruction , their god is their belly , and they glory in their shame , with minds set on earthly things .
verses.meaning: froneō names the habitual inner orientation of those whose end is destruction — the whole person is directed toward earthly rather than divine things.

verses.verse_text: Phili 4:2 I entreat Euodia and I entreat Syntyche to agree in the Lord .
verses.meaning: froneō names the inner orientation of mutual alignment — being of the same mind as the resolution of conflict in the community.

verses.verse_text: Phili 4:10 I rejoiced in the Lord greatly that now at length you have revived your concern for me . You were indeed concerned for me, but you had no opportunity .
verses.meaning: froneō names the inner orientation of care and attention directed toward Paul — the Philippians' mind turning back to him in practical concern.

verses.verse_text: Col 3:2 Set your minds on things that are above , not on things that are on earth .
verses.meaning: froneō names the deliberate inner orientation — what the whole person is directed toward. The command names the object of the mind's setting.

verses.verse_text: Mat 7:24 “ Everyone then who hears these words of mine and does them will be like a wise man who built his house on the rock .
verses.meaning: fronimos names the wise person as one whose inner quality shows in hearing and doing — wisdom is the orientation of the person who acts on what Christ says.

verses.verse_text: Mat 10:16 “ Behold , I am sending you out as sheep in the midst of wolves , so be wise as serpents and innocent as doves .
verses.meaning: fronimos names practical shrewd wariness as the inner quality needed for navigating a hostile world — wisdom as perceptive caution.

verses.verse_text: Mat 24:45 “ Who then is the faithful and wise servant , whom his master has set over his household , to give them their food at the proper time ?
verses.meaning: fronimos names wisdom as the inner quality of the trustworthy servant — paired with faithfulness as the characteristic of the one who acts reliably in the master's absence.

verses.verse_text: Mat 25:2 Five of them were foolish , and five were wise .
verses.meaning: fronimos names the five virgins as wise — their inner quality is their defining characteristic, set against the foolish five.

verses.verse_text: Mat 25:4 but the wise took flasks of oil with their lamps .
verses.meaning: fronimos names the inner quality that produces foresighted action — the wise prepared in advance for what would be needed.

verses.verse_text: Mat 25:8 And the foolish said to the wise , ‘ Give us some of your oil , for our lamps are going out .’
verses.meaning: fronimos names the wise as those who have prepared — their inner quality is visible in what they hold and the foolish lack.

verses.verse_text: Mat 25:9 But the wise answered , saying , ‘ Since there will not be enough for us and for you , go rather to the dealers and buy for yourselves .’
verses.meaning: fronimos names the wise whose inner quality produces clear-eyed assessment of what is possible — they do not give what they cannot spare.

verses.verse_text: Luk 12:42 And the Lord said , “ Who then is the faithful and wise manager , whom his master will set over his household , to give them their portion of food at the proper time ?
verses.meaning: fronimos names the wise manager — same pattern as Mat 24:45. Wisdom and faithfulness as the paired inner qualities of trustworthy stewardship.

verses.verse_text: Luk 16:8 The master commended the dishonest manager for his shrewdness . For the sons of this world are more shrewd in dealing with their own generation than the sons of light .
verses.meaning: fronimos names worldly shrewdness as an inner quality that the sons of this world exercise more effectively than the sons of light — a challenge to exercise equivalent wisdom in spiritual matters.

verses.verse_text: Rom 11:25 Lest you be wise in your own sight, I do not want you to be unaware of this mystery , brothers : a partial hardening has come upon Israel , until the fullness of the Gentiles has come in .
verses.meaning: Names the self-referential form of wisdom Paul warns against — the term identifies the disposition of being wise in one's own estimation, which obscures the mystery of Israel.

verses.verse_text: Rom 12:16 Live in harmony with one another . Do not be haughty , but associate with the lowly . Never be wise in your own sight.
verses.meaning: Names the self-regarding form of inner estimation Paul prohibits — the term characterises the disposition of considering oneself wise, set against humility and harmony.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 4:10 We are fools for Christ’s sake , but you are wise in Christ . We are weak , but you are strong . You are held in honor , but we in disrepute .
verses.meaning: fronimos names the Corinthians' self-assessed wisdom — Paul uses the term ironically, contrasting their self-regard with the apostles' apparent foolishness.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 10:15 I speak as to sensible people ; judge for yourselves what I say .
verses.meaning: fronimos names the capacity for sound rational judgment that Paul appeals to — the inner faculty of discernment that enables the Corinthians to evaluate his argument.

verses.verse_text: 2Cor 11:19 For you gladly bear with fools , being wise yourselves!
verses.meaning: fronimos names the Corinthians' self-assessed wisdom ironically — their supposed wisdom makes them tolerant of foolishness, which Paul exposes as its own form of foolishness.

verses.verse_text: Neh 8:8 They read from the book , from the Law of God , clearly , and they gave the sense , so that the people understood the reading .
verses.meaning: bin names the inner act of grasping — the people receiving and comprehending what was read and explained.

verses.verse_text: Jer 9:17 Thus says the Lord of hosts : “ Consider , and call for the mourning women to come ; send for the skillful women to come ;
verses.meaning: bin names the reflective perceptive act being commanded — attend to the situation and act accordingly.

verses.verse_text: Hos 14:9 Whoever is wise , let him understand these things; whoever is discerning , let him know them; for the ways of the Lord are right , and the upright walk in them, but transgressors stumble in them .
verses.meaning: bin names discernment as the inner capacity that perceives the rightness of God's ways — called for in the closing appeal of the book.

verses.verse_text: Dan 2:21 He changes times and seasons ; he removes kings and sets up kings ; he gives wisdom to the wise and knowledge to those who have understanding ;
verses.meaning: bi.nah names understanding as the inner capacity that makes the person a recipient of God's knowledge — the faculty that qualifies reception.

verses.verse_text: Dan 2:12 Because of this the king was angry and very furious , and commanded that all the wise men of Babylon be destroyed .
verses.meaning: chak.kim names the professional class of court wise men — their failure to interpret the dream has made them a target. Their title and their incapacity stand together.

verses.verse_text: Dan 2:13 So the decree went out , and the wise men were about to be killed ; and they sought Daniel and his companions , to kill them.
verses.meaning: chak.kim names the class facing destruction — their wisdom could not meet the king's demand.

verses.verse_text: Dan 2:14 Then Daniel replied with prudence and discretion to Arioch , the captain of the king’s guard , who had gone out to kill the wise men of Babylon .
verses.meaning: chak.kim names the class Daniel is being grouped with — he intercedes for them though they could not do what he can.

verses.verse_text: Dan 2:18 and told them to seek mercy from the God of heaven concerning this mystery , so that Daniel and his companions might not be destroyed with the rest of the wise men of Babylon .
verses.meaning: chak.kim names the class with whom Daniel is at risk — solidarity in danger despite difference in capacity.

verses.verse_text: Dan 2:21 He changes times and seasons ; he removes kings and sets up kings ; he gives wisdom to the wise and knowledge to those who have understanding ;
verses.meaning: chak.kim names the wise as recipients of what God gives — even the capacity they have is given by him. The wise men of Babylon could not access this.

verses.verse_text: Dan 2:24 Therefore Daniel went in to Arioch , whom the king had appointed to destroy the wise men of Babylon . He went and said thus to him: “Do not destroy the wise men of Babylon ; bring me in before the king , and I will show the king the interpretation .”
verses.meaning: chak.kim names the class Daniel is interceding for — he will do what they could not, but he does not abandon them.

verses.verse_text: Dan 2:27 Daniel answered the king and said , “ No wise men , enchanters , magicians , or astrologers can show to the king the mystery that the king has asked ,
verses.meaning: chak.kim names the limitation of the professional wisdom class — the mystery is beyond their reach by definition.

verses.verse_text: Dan 2:48 Then the king gave Daniel high honors and many great gifts , and made him ruler over the whole province of Babylon and chief prefect over all the wise men of Babylon .
verses.meaning: chak.kim names the professional class over which Daniel is appointed — he is set above the institutional wisdom of Babylon by the king's recognition of what God has given him.

verses.verse_text: Dan 4:6 So I made a decree that all the wise men of Babylon should be brought before me, that they might make known to me the interpretation of the dream .
verses.meaning: chak.kim names the professional class summoned to interpret — again they are the foil for what Spirit-given wisdom can do.

verses.verse_text: Dan 4:18 This dream I , King Nebuchadnezzar , saw . And you , O Belteshazzar , tell me the interpretation , because all the wise men of my kingdom are not able to make known to me the interpretation , but you are able , for the spirit of the holy gods is in you .”
verses.meaning: chak.kim names the class whose inability frames Daniel's ability — the distinction is the Spirit, not professional qualification.

verses.verse_text: Dan 5:7 The king called loudly to bring in the enchanters , the Chaldeans , and the astrologers . The king declared to the wise men of Babylon , “ Whoever reads this writing , and shows me its interpretation , shall be clothed with purple and have a chain of gold around his neck and shall be the third ruler in the kingdom .”
verses.meaning: chak.kim names the professional class summoned to read the writing — again the foil for Spirit-given capacity.

verses.verse_text: Dan 5:8 Then all the king’s wise men came in , but they could not read the writing or make known to the king the interpretation .
verses.meaning: chak.kim names the class whose incapacity is complete — the writing is beyond them.

verses.verse_text: Dan 5:15 Now the wise men, the enchanters , have been brought in before me to read this writing and make known to me its interpretation , but they could not show the interpretation of the matter .
verses.meaning: chak.kim names the professional class whose failure is complete and repeated — they cannot do what Spirit-given wisdom can.

verses.verse_text: Gen 41:8 So in the morning his spirit was troubled , and he sent and called for all the magicians of Egypt and all its wise men . Pharaoh told them his dreams , but there was none who could interpret them to Pharaoh .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the professional class of court experts summoned for their supposed capacity to interpret. The span identifies them by their role. Their failure is the point — the title is present, the capacity is absent.

verses.verse_text: Gen 41:39 Then Pharaoh said to Joseph , “ Since God has shown you all this , there is none so discerning and wise as you are.
verses.meaning: cha.kham names wisdom as a quality recognised in Joseph by Pharaoh — it has been demonstrated through the revelation and interpretation. God's disclosure is the ground of the recognition.

verses.verse_text: Exo 7:11 Then Pharaoh summoned the wise men and the sorcerers , and they , the magicians of Egypt , also did the same by their secret arts .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the court class of experts with esoteric knowledge and technique. The wise men function here as the institutional counterpart to Moses — matching the sign but not surpassing it.

verses.verse_text: Exo 28:3 You shall speak to all the skillful , whom I have filled with a spirit of skill , that they make Aaron’s garments to consecrate him for my priesthood .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names persons whose inner capacity for skilled work is explicitly God-given — filled with a spirit of skill. The wisdom is a divine endowment for a specific sacred task.

verses.verse_text: Exo 31:6 And behold , I have appointed with him Oholiab , the son of Ahisamach , of the tribe of Dan . And I have given to all able men ability , that they may make all that I have commanded you :
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the capacity for skilled work as a God-given endowment. The able men are able because God gave them ability for the sacred construction.

verses.verse_text: Exo 35:10 “Let every skillful craftsman among you come and make all that the Lord has commanded :
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the craftsman whose inner skill qualifies them for the work of constructing the sanctuary.

verses.verse_text: Exo 35:25 And every skillful woman spun with her hands , and they all brought what they had spun in blue and purple and scarlet yarns and fine twined linen .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the inner skill of the woman expressed in her craft — contributing her capacity to the sanctuary work.

verses.verse_text: Exo 36:1 “ Bezalel and Oholiab and every craftsman in whom the Lord has put skill and intelligence to know how to do any work in the construction of the sanctuary shall work in accordance with all that the Lord has commanded .”
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the craftsman defined by what God has placed within them — skill and intelligence are inner endowments for sacred work.

verses.verse_text: Exo 36:2 And Moses called Bezalel and Oholiab and every craftsman in whose mind the Lord had put skill , everyone whose heart stirred him up to come to do the work .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the craftsman whose wisdom is seated in the mind/heart — God-placed skill paired with inner motivation.

verses.verse_text: Exo 36:4 so that all the craftsmen who were doing every sort of task on the sanctuary came , each from the task that he was doing ,
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the craftsmen as a class defined by their skill — those whose inner capacity qualifies them for the sanctuary work.

verses.verse_text: Exo 36:8 And all the craftsmen among the workmen made the tabernacle with ten curtains . They were made of fine twined linen and blue and purple and scarlet yarns, with cherubim skillfully worked .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the craftsmen as those executing the work. In this verse both cha.kham and cha.shav appear — the craftsmen and the skillful working are both in the span.

verses.verse_text: Deu 1:13 Choose for your tribes wise , understanding , and experienced men , and I will appoint them as your heads .’
verses.meaning: cha.kham names wisdom as one of three inner qualities required for leadership appointment — paired with understanding and experience. Wisdom is the qualification.

verses.verse_text: Deu 1:15 So I took the heads of your tribes , wise and experienced men , and set them as heads over you, commanders of thousands , commanders of hundreds , commanders of fifties , commanders of tens , and officers , throughout your tribes .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names wisdom as the constituted inner quality of those selected for governance — the men are defined by it.

verses.verse_text: Deu 4:6 Keep them and do them, for that will be your wisdom and your understanding in the sight of the peoples , who , when they hear all these statutes , will say , ‘ Surely this great nation is a wise and understanding people .’
verses.meaning: cha.kham names wisdom as what covenant obedience will be seen to be — obedience is the outward form that wisdom takes. Israel's inner quality becomes visible through its conduct.

verses.verse_text: Deu 16:19 You shall not pervert justice . You shall not show partiality , and you shall not accept a bribe , for a bribe blinds the eyes of the wise and subverts the cause of the righteous .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the wise person as one whose inner perception can be corrupted by external pressure. The wise are not immune — their wisdom is vulnerable to what blinds.

verses.verse_text: Deu 32:6 Do you thus repay the Lord , you foolish and senseless people ? Is not he your father , who created you, who made you and established you ?
verses.meaning: cha.kham is used negatively — the people are called senseless. The span names what they are not: wisdom is absent. The repaying of God's care with rebellion is the mark of its absence.

verses.verse_text: Judg 5:29 Her wisest princesses answer , indeed , she answers herself ,
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the wisest of the princesses — those with the greatest inner capacity for counsel and discernment. The verse is Sisera's mother's household rationalising his delay. The wisest counsel here is in service of self-deception.

verses.verse_text: 2Sa 13:3 But Amnon had a friend , whose name was Jonadab , the son of Shimeah , David’s brother . And Jonadab was a very crafty man .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names an inner quality of Jonadab — but the quality serves manipulation. Craftiness is wisdom turned to self-serving cunning. The word names the capacity; the moral direction is the person's.

verses.verse_text: 2Sa 14:2 And Joab sent to Tekoa and brought from there a wise woman and said to her, “Pretend to be a mourner and put on mourning garments . Do not anoint yourself with oil , but behave like a woman who has been mourning many days for the dead .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the woman as someone whose inner quality of wisdom makes her suitable for the role Joab needs — she can hold a plan, read a situation, and speak persuasively.

verses.verse_text: 2Sa 14:20 In order to change the course of things your servant Joab did this . But my lord has wisdom like the wisdom of the angel of God to know all things that are on the earth .”
verses.meaning: cha.kham names David's wisdom in superlative terms — an inner all-comprehending perception compared to angelic knowledge. Wisdom here is cognitive reach and insight.

verses.verse_text: 2Sa 20:16 Then a wise woman called from the city , “ Listen ! Listen ! Tell Joab , ‘ Come here , that I may speak to you .’”
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the woman as one whose inner quality of wisdom prompts her to intervene and negotiate — she acts from wisdom to prevent destruction.

verses.verse_text: 1Ki 2:9 Now therefore do not hold him guiltless , for you are a wise man . You will know what you ought to do to him, and you shall bring his gray head down with blood to Sheol .”
verses.meaning: cha.kham names Solomon's wisdom as the capacity that will guide the right action in a morally complex situation. Wisdom is what enables right judgment. Note: assigned to 4458-001 — this appears to be a positive use of wisdom, not the cross-overturning category.

verses.verse_text: 1Ki 3:12 behold , I now do according to your word . Behold , I give you a wise and discerning mind , so that none like you has been before you and none like you shall arise after you .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the wisdom God gives Solomon as an inner quality of the mind — unparalleled, divinely bestowed, seated in the person.

verses.verse_text: 1Ki 5:7 As soon as Hiram heard the words of Solomon , he rejoiced greatly and said , “ Blessed be the Lord this day , who has given to David a wise son to be over this great people .”
verses.meaning: cha.kham names Solomon's wisdom as a God-given inner quality that qualifies him for kingship over Israel.

verses.verse_text: 1Ch 22:15 You have an abundance of workmen : stonecutters , masons , carpenters , and all kinds of craftsmen without number, skilled in working
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the craftsmen by their inner skill — defined by what they are able to do.

verses.verse_text: 2Ch 2:7 So now send me a man skilled to work in gold , silver , bronze , and iron , and in purple , crimson , and blue fabrics, trained also in engraving , to be with the skilled workers who are with me in Judah and Jerusalem , whom David my father provided .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names skilled craftsmanship as the inner capacity being requested — a person defined by their craft ability.

verses.verse_text: 2Ch 2:12 Hiram also said , “ Blessed be the Lord God of Israel , who made heaven and earth , who has given King David a wise son , who has discretion and understanding , who will build a temple for the Lord and a royal palace for himself.
verses.meaning: cha.kham names Solomon's wisdom as a divine gift — an inner quality that equips him for building the temple. Paired with discretion (se.khel) and understanding.

verses.verse_text: 2Ch 2:13 “ Now I have sent a skilled man , who has understanding , Huram-abi ,
verses.meaning: cha.kham names Huram-abi's skill as his defining inner quality — paired with understanding, given as his qualification.

verses.verse_text: 2Ch 2:14 the son of a woman of the daughters of Dan , and his father was a man of Tyre . He is trained to work in gold , silver , bronze , iron , stone , and wood , and in purple , blue , and crimson fabrics and fine linen , and to do all sorts of engraving and execute any design that may be assigned him, with your craftsmen , the craftsmen of my lord , David your father .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the craftsmen as those whose inner skill enables execution of designs — in collaboration with Solomon's workers.

verses.verse_text: Est 1:13 Then the king said to the wise men who knew the times (for this was the king’s procedure toward all who were versed in law and judgment ,
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the court experts as those whose inner knowledge of law and custom qualifies them to advise the king.

verses.verse_text: Est 6:13 And Haman told his wife Zeresh and all his friends everything that had happened to him. Then his wise men and his wife Zeresh said to him, “ If Mordecai , before whom you have begun to fall , is of the Jewish people , you will not overcome him but will surely fall before him.”
verses.meaning: cha.kham names Haman's wise men as his advisors — but their wisdom here delivers a judgment of doom against him. Their counsel is accurate but unwelcome.

verses.verse_text: Job 9:4 He is wise in heart and mighty in strength — who has hardened himself against him, and succeeded ?—
verses.meaning: cha.kham names God's wisdom as an inner quality of the heart — paired with might. No one who has hardened themselves against this wisdom has prevailed.

verses.verse_text: Job 15:2 “Should a wise man answer with windy knowledge , and fill his belly with the east wind?
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the wise person whose speech should reflect their inner quality — but Eliphaz challenges whether Job's words meet that standard. The span points to the inner quality as the measure of speech.

verses.verse_text: Job 15:18 (what wise men have told , without hiding it from their fathers ,
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the wise as those whose inner understanding has been passed down — a tradition of wisdom held and transmitted.

verses.verse_text: Job 17:10 But you, come on again, all of you, and I shall not find a wise man among you.
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the quality Job finds absent in his friends — their counsel does not rise to the level of wisdom.

verses.verse_text: Job 34:2 “ Hear my words , you wise men , and give ear to me , you who know ;
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the wise as those who have the inner quality to receive and weigh what Elihu is about to say — addressed as competent hearers.

verses.verse_text: Job 34:34 Men of understanding will say to me, and the wise man who hears me will say:
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the wise person as one whose inner quality enables right hearing and agreement — their response confirms the truth of the argument.

verses.verse_text: Job 37:24 Therefore men fear him; he does not regard any who are wise in their own conceit .”
verses.meaning: cha.kham names wisdom claimed as self-attribution — conceit is the opposite of the fear that produces genuine wisdom. God does not regard those who hold wisdom as their own.

verses.verse_text: Psa 49:10 For he sees that even the wise die ; the fool and the stupid alike must perish and leave their wealth to others .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the wise person whose inner quality does not exempt them from death — wisdom and folly are equalised by mortality. The observation is about the limits of what wisdom can secure.

verses.verse_text: Psa 107:43 Whoever is wise , let him attend to these things; let them consider the steadfast love of the Lord .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names wisdom as the inner quality that enables attention and consideration of God's acts — the wise person is the one who sees and reflects on what God has done.

verses.verse_text: Pro 1:5 Let the wise hear and increase in learning , and the one who understands obtain guidance ,
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the wise person as one whose inner quality makes them open to further instruction — wisdom is not static but grows by receiving.

verses.verse_text: Pro 1:6 to understand a proverb and a saying , the words of the wise and their riddles .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the wise as those whose words and riddles carry inner depth — understanding them requires wisdom in the hearer.

verses.verse_text: Pro 3:7 Be not wise in your own eyes ; fear the Lord , and turn away from evil .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names self-attributed wisdom — being wise in one's own eyes is the opposite of fearing the Lord. The admonition contrasts self-sourced wisdom with the wisdom rooted in reverence.

verses.verse_text: Pro 3:35 The wise will inherit honor , but fools get disgrace .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the wise person as the one whose inner quality leads to an outcome — honour. The verse states the trajectory of wisdom, not its content.

verses.verse_text: Pro 9:8 Do not reprove a scoffer , or he will hate you; reprove a wise man , and he will love you .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the wise person as one whose inner quality makes them receptive to correction — they receive rebuke as a gift, not an insult.

verses.verse_text: Pro 9:9 Give instruction to a wise man , and he will be still wiser ; teach a righteous man, and he will increase in learning .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the wise as those whose inner quality grows with instruction — wisdom is receptive and cumulative.

verses.verse_text: Pro 10:1 The proverbs of Solomon . A wise son makes a glad father , but a foolish son is a sorrow to his mother .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names wisdom as an inner quality of the son that produces relational joy — the wise person's character has an effect on those around them.

verses.verse_text: Pro 10:8 The wise of heart will receive commandments , but a babbling fool will come to ruin .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names wisdom as seated in the heart — the wise of heart are those whose inner disposition is receptive to instruction and command.

verses.verse_text: Pro 10:14 The wise lay up knowledge , but the mouth of a fool brings ruin near .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the wise as those who accumulate and store knowledge — an active inner orientation toward receiving and retaining what is true.

verses.verse_text: Pro 11:29 Whoever troubles his own household will inherit the wind , and the fool will be servant to the wise of heart .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names wisdom as a quality of the heart — the wise of heart occupy the position of leadership or advantage over those without it.

verses.verse_text: Pro 11:30 The fruit of the righteous is a tree of life , and whoever captures souls is wise .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the wise person as one whose inner quality enables them to draw others toward life — a relational effectiveness rooted in wisdom.

verses.verse_text: Pro 12:15 The way of a fool is right in his own eyes , but a wise man listens to advice .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the wise person as one whose inner quality makes them open to counsel — in contrast to the fool who trusts only their own judgment.

verses.verse_text: Pro 12:18 There is one whose rash words are like sword thrusts , but the tongue of the wise brings healing .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names wisdom as an inner quality whose expression — speech — has a healing effect on others.

verses.verse_text: Pro 13:1 A wise son hears his father’s instruction , but a scoffer does not listen to rebuke .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the wise son as one whose inner quality makes him receptive to parental teaching — wisdom and teachability are linked.

verses.verse_text: Pro 13:14 The teaching of the wise is a fountain of life , that one may turn away from the snares of death .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the wise person whose inner quality produces teaching that gives life — wisdom is generative and protective.

verses.verse_text: Pro 13:20 Whoever walks with the wise becomes wise , but the companion of fools will suffer harm .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names wisdom as a quality that is communicated through association — the inner quality of the wise shapes those around them.

verses.verse_text: Pro 14:1 The wisest of women builds her house , but folly with her own hands tears it down .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the wisest woman as one whose inner quality expresses itself in constructive, building action — wisdom builds.

verses.verse_text: Pro 14:3 By the mouth of a fool comes a rod for his back , but the lips of the wise will preserve them .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names wisdom as an inner quality whose expression in speech preserves the person — wise speech protects.

verses.verse_text: Pro 14:16 One who is wise is cautious and turns away from evil , but a fool is reckless and careless .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names wisdom as producing caution — the wise person's inner quality makes them alert to danger and disposed to avoid it.

verses.verse_text: Pro 14:24 The crown of the wise is their wealth , but the folly of fools brings folly .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the wise person whose inner quality produces a crown — the outcome of wisdom is something that adorns and elevates.

verses.verse_text: Pro 15:12 A scoffer does not like to be reproved ; he will not go to the wise .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the wise as those whose inner quality makes them the right source of correction — the scoffer avoids them precisely because of it.

verses.verse_text: Pro 15:20 A wise son makes a glad father , but a foolish man despises his mother .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the wise son whose inner quality produces relational joy in his father — same pattern as Pro 10:1.

verses.verse_text: Pro 15:31 The ear that listens to life-giving reproof will dwell among the wise .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the community of the wise — those whose inner quality includes receptivity to rebuke. Dwelling among them is the outcome of the same quality.

verses.verse_text: Pro 16:14 A king’s wrath is a messenger of death , and a wise man will appease it .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the wise person as one whose inner quality enables them to navigate dangerous relational situations — wisdom as social and situational intelligence.

verses.verse_text: Pro 16:21 The wise of heart is called discerning , and sweetness of speech increases persuasiveness .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names wisdom as seated in the heart — the wise of heart are publicly recognised as discerning. Inner quality and its social recognition are connected.

verses.verse_text: Pro 16:23 The heart of the wise makes his speech judicious and adds persuasiveness to his lips .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names wisdom as a quality seated in the heart that shapes speech — the inner quality produces wise and persuasive expression.

verses.verse_text: Pro 18:15 An intelligent heart acquires knowledge , and the ear of the wise seeks knowledge .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names wisdom as an inner orientation that actively seeks knowledge — the wise person is characterised by their appetite for understanding.

verses.verse_text: Pro 20:26 A wise king winnows the wicked and drives the wheel over them.
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the inner quality of the wise king as what enables just discrimination — wisdom applied to governance and justice.

verses.verse_text: Pro 21:11 When a scoffer is punished , the simple becomes wise ; when a wise man is instructed , he gains knowledge .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names wisdom as a quality that makes instruction productive — the wise person receives and is enriched by it.

verses.verse_text: Pro 21:20 Precious treasure and oil are in a wise man’s dwelling , but a foolish man devours it .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the wise man as one whose inner quality produces tangible provision — wisdom is associated with material stewardship and abundance.

verses.verse_text: Pro 21:22 A wise man scales the city of the mighty and brings down the stronghold in which they trust .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names wisdom as an inner capacity that overcomes what strength cannot — wisdom achieves what force cannot.

verses.verse_text: Pro 23:24 The father of the righteous will greatly rejoice ; he who fathers a wise son will be glad in him.
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the wise son whose inner quality produces joy in his father — the relational impact of wisdom on the family.

verses.verse_text: Pro 24:5 A wise man is full of strength , and a man of knowledge enhances his might ,
verses.meaning: cha.kham names wisdom as an inner quality that produces strength — wisdom and might are paired as complementary inner resources.

verses.verse_text: Pro 24:23 These also are sayings of the wise . Partiality in judging is not good .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the wise as those whose inner quality produces authoritative sayings — wisdom expressed in transmissible form.

verses.verse_text: Pro 25:12 Like a gold ring or an ornament of gold is a wise reprover to a listening ear .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the wise person as one whose correction has value — wisdom makes the reproof worth hearing.

verses.verse_text: Pro 26:5 Answer a fool according to his folly , lest he be wise in his own eyes .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names self-attributed wisdom — answering the fool risks giving him grounds for self-congratulation, claiming wisdom he does not have.

verses.verse_text: Pro 26:12 Do you see a man who is wise in his own eyes ? There is more hope for a fool than for him .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names self-assessed wisdom as a worse condition than open foolishness — the person who claims wisdom has cut themselves off from the correction that might help them.

verses.verse_text: Pro 26:16 The sluggard is wiser in his own eyes than seven men who can answer sensibly .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the sluggard's self-assessment as wiser — a claim to inner superiority that is visibly false. The irony exposes the self-deception of claimed wisdom.

verses.verse_text: Pro 28:11 A rich man is wise in his own eyes , but a poor man who has understanding will find him out .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the rich man's self-attributed wisdom — wealth creates the illusion of inner capacity. The poor person with real understanding sees through it.

verses.verse_text: Pro 29:8 Scoffers set a city aflame , but the wise turn away wrath .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names wisdom as the inner quality that produces de-escalation — the wise person absorbs and redirects what would otherwise ignite.

verses.verse_text: Pro 29:9 If a wise man has an argument with a fool , the fool only rages and laughs , and there is no quiet .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the wise person in encounter with folly — wisdom cannot produce wisdom in the fool. The contrast exposes the incommunicability of wisdom to those who will not receive it.

verses.verse_text: Pro 29:11 A fool gives full vent to his spirit , but a wise man quietly holds it back .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names wisdom as the inner capacity for self-restraint — holding back the spirit where the fool vents it. Wisdom governs inner impulse.

verses.verse_text: Pro 30:24 Four things on earth are small , but they are exceedingly wise :
verses.meaning: cha.kham names wisdom as an inner quality found in small creatures — wisdom is not proportional to size or power but is a distinct inner capacity.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 2:14 The wise person has his eyes in his head , but the fool walks in darkness . And yet I perceived that the same event happens to all of them.
verses.meaning: cha.kham names wisdom as an inner quality that produces perceptual clarity — the wise see where they are going.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 2:16 For of the wise as of the fool there is no enduring remembrance , seeing that in the days to come all will have been long forgotten . How the wise dies just like the fool !
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the wise person alongside the fool under the equalising effect of death and forgetfulness — wisdom does not guarantee lasting legacy.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 2:19 and who knows whether he will be wise or a fool ? Yet he will be master of all for which I toiled and used my wisdom under the sun . This also is vanity .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names wisdom as an uncertain quality in a future heir — the fruit of one's labour may pass to someone without the inner quality to steward it.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 4:13 Better was a poor and wise youth than an old and foolish king who no longer knew how to take advice .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names wisdom as an inner quality that surpasses age and status — the young wise person outranks the old fool because of what they hold inwardly.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 6:8 For what advantage has the wise man over the fool ? And what does the poor man have who knows how to conduct himself before the living ?
verses.meaning: cha.kham names wisdom as an inner quality whose advantage is called into question by Qoheleth — under the sun, wisdom's edge is limited.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 7:4 The heart of the wise is in the house of mourning , but the heart of fools is in the house of mirth .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names wisdom as an inner orientation of the heart — the wise are oriented toward what is real and mortal, not toward mere pleasure.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 7:5 It is better for a man to hear the rebuke of the wise than to hear the song of fools .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the wise person whose rebuke has value — their inner quality makes their correction worth more than the fool's flattery.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 7:7 Surely oppression drives the wise into madness , and a bribe corrupts the heart .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names wisdom as an inner quality that is not immune to corruption by external pressure — even the wise can be driven from their grounding.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 7:19 Wisdom gives strength to the wise man more than ten rulers who are in a city .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the wise person as one whose inner quality produces a strength that surpasses political power.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 8:1 Who is like the wise ? And who knows the interpretation of a thing ? A man’s wisdom makes his face shine , and the hardness of his face is changed .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names wisdom as an inner quality that produces outward change — wisdom shows on the face, transforming the person's bearing.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 8:5 Whoever keeps a command will know no evil thing , and the wise heart will know the proper time and the just way .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names wisdom as seated in the heart — the wise heart perceives timing and justice. Wisdom is a perceptive inner faculty.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 8:17 then I saw all the work of God , that man cannot find out the work that is done under the sun . However much man may toil in seeking , he will not find it out. Even though a wise man claims to know , he cannot find it out.
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the wise person confronted with the limits of their inner capacity — wisdom cannot penetrate God's work under the sun.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 9:1 But all this I laid to heart , examining it all , how the righteous and the wise and their deeds are in the hand of God . Whether it is love or hate , man does not know ; both are before him.
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the wise as those in God's hand — their inner quality does not exempt them from divine sovereignty over their lives and deeds.

verses.verse_text: Isa 5:21 Woe to those who are wise in their own eyes , and shrewd in their own sight !
verses.meaning: Names the self-referential form of wisdom condemned — the term identifies being wise in one's own eyes as an inner disposition that draws prophetic woe.

verses.verse_text: Isa 19:11 The princes of Zoan are utterly foolish ; the wisest counselors of Pharaoh give stupid counsel . How can you say to Pharaoh , “I am a son of the wise , a son of ancient kings ”?
verses.meaning: Names the lineage the princes of Zoan claim — the term identifies being descended from the wise as the basis of their boast, which the oracle exposes as empty.

verses.verse_text: Isa 31:2 And yet he is wise and brings disaster ; he does not call back his words , but will arise against the house of the evildoers and against the helpers of those who work iniquity .
verses.meaning: Names a quality attributed to God — the term identifies wisdom as the inner attribute that makes God's judgment effective even when it brings disaster.

verses.verse_text: Isa 40:20 He who is too impoverished for an offering chooses wood that will not rot ; he seeks out a skillful craftsman to set up an idol that will not move .
verses.meaning: Names the type of craftsman sought — the term identifies the skilled or wise artisan as one whose inner competence is required to produce an idol that will not move.

verses.verse_text: Isa 44:25 who frustrates the signs of liars and makes fools of diviners , who turns wise men back and makes their knowledge foolish ,
verses.meaning: Names those whose counsel God overturns — the term identifies wise men as those whose inner knowledge God renders foolish, frustrating the confidence of their expertise.

verses.verse_text: Jer 4:22 “ For my people are foolish ; they know me not ; they are stupid children ; they have no understanding . They are ‘ wise ’—in doing evil ! But how to do good they know not .”
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the people's wisdom as misdirected — their inner capacity functions in the domain of evil, not good. Wisdom without moral alignment is corrupted.

verses.verse_text: Jer 8:8 “ How can you say , ‘We are wise , and the law of the Lord is with us’? But behold , the lying pen of the scribes has made it into a lie .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names a self-claim to wisdom that is exposed as false — the lying pen of the scribes has emptied the law of its truth, making the claim hollow.

verses.verse_text: Jer 8:9 The wise men shall be put to shame ; they shall be dismayed and taken ; behold , they have rejected the word of the Lord , so what wisdom is in them ?
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the wise whose rejection of God's word has emptied their wisdom — what wisdom is in them without the word?

verses.verse_text: Jer 9:12 Who is the man so wise that he can understand this ? To whom has the mouth of the Lord spoken , that he may declare it? Why is the land ruined and laid waste like a wilderness , so that no one passes through ?
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the inner capacity being called for — understanding God's judgment on the land requires a wisdom that no one apparently possesses. The question implies wisdom of this order is absent.

verses.verse_text: Jer 9:17 Thus says the Lord of hosts : “ Consider , and call for the mourning women to come ; send for the skillful women to come ;
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the skilled mourning women — their skill is a craft of lament, a professional inner and performative capacity. This verse is P-status (pending).

verses.verse_text: Jer 9:23 Thus says the Lord : “Let not the wise man boast in his wisdom , let not the mighty man boast in his might , let not the rich man boast in his riches ,
verses.meaning: cha.kham names wisdom as something the wise person is tempted to claim as their own glory — the admonition cuts against the pride of inner capacity.

verses.verse_text: Jer 10:7 Who would not fear you, O King of the nations ? For this is your due ; for among all the wise ones of the nations and in all their kingdoms there is none like you .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the wise of the nations as those with the greatest inner capacity — yet none compares to the Lord. The wise ones are the measure, and God surpasses them all.

verses.verse_text: Jer 10:9 Beaten silver is brought from Tarshish , and gold from Uphaz . They are the work of the craftsman and of the hands of the goldsmith ; their clothing is violet and purple ; they are all the work of skilled men .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the skilled craftsmen whose work produces the idols — craft skill in service of false worship.

verses.verse_text: Jer 18:18 Then they said , “ Come , let us make plots against Jeremiah , for the law shall not perish from the priest , nor counsel from the wise , nor the word from the prophet . Come , let us strike him with the tongue , and let us not pay attention to any of his words .”
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the wise as the institutional source of counsel — their inner quality produces guidance for the community. The conspirators assume this will continue even as they plot against the prophet.

verses.verse_text: Jer 50:35 “A sword against the Chaldeans , declares the Lord , and against the inhabitants of Babylon , and against her officials and her wise men !
verses.meaning: cha.kham names Babylon's wise men as a class to be destroyed — their wisdom is named as part of what gives Babylon its power and identity.

verses.verse_text: Jer 51:57 I will make drunk her officials and her wise men , her governors , her commanders , and her warriors ; they shall sleep a perpetual sleep and not wake , declares the King , whose name is the Lord of hosts .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names Babylon's wise men among those whom God will render helpless — wisdom offers no protection against divine judgment.

verses.verse_text: Eze 27:8 The inhabitants of Sidon and Arvad were your rowers ; your skilled men, O Tyre , were in you; they were your pilots .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names Tyre's skilled men whose inner capacity for navigation makes them the pilots of the city's commerce. Wisdom as craft excellence in service of a great commercial enterprise.

verses.verse_text: Eze 27:9 The elders of Gebal and her skilled men were in you, caulking your seams ; all the ships of the sea with their mariners were in you to barter for your wares .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the skilled craftsmen of Gebal whose inner capacity for craft work maintains Tyre's ships.

verses.verse_text: Eze 28:3 you are indeed wiser than Daniel ; no secret is hidden from you ;
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the prince of Tyre's self-assessed wisdom as surpassing even Daniel — the claim is ironic, setting up the judgment against pride.

verses.verse_text: Hos 13:13 The pangs of childbirth come for him, but he is an unwise son , for at the right time he does not present himself at the opening of the womb .
verses.meaning: cha.kham used negatively — the son is unwise because he fails to act at the moment of opportunity. Wisdom here is the capacity to recognise and respond to the right moment.

verses.verse_text: Hos 14:9 Whoever is wise , let him understand these things; whoever is discerning , let him know them; for the ways of the Lord are right , and the upright walk in them, but transgressors stumble in them .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names wisdom as the inner quality that enables understanding of God's ways — wisdom and discernment are the capacities that perceive the rightness of God's paths.

verses.verse_text: Obd 8 Will I not on that day , declares the Lord , destroy the wise men out of Edom , and understanding out of Mount Esau ?
verses.meaning: cha.kham names Edom's wise men as what God will remove — their inner wisdom is a source of national strength and identity. Its removal is judgment. P-status verse.

verses.verse_text: Ezr 7:25 “And you , Ezra , according to the wisdom of your God that is in your hand , appoint magistrates and judges who may judge all the people in the province Beyond the River , all such as know the laws of your God . And those who do not know them, you shall teach .
verses.meaning: chokh.mah names the wisdom of God as something given to Ezra — held in his hand, operative in him. It qualifies him to appoint those who will judge by God's laws.

verses.verse_text: Dan 2:20 Daniel answered and said : “ Blessed be the name of God forever and ever , to whom belong wisdom and might .
verses.meaning: chokh.mah names wisdom as a divine attribute — belonging to God. Daniel's prayer attributes wisdom to God as his possession, not a human achievement.

verses.verse_text: Dan 2:21 He changes times and seasons ; he removes kings and sets up kings ; he gives wisdom to the wise and knowledge to those who have understanding ;
verses.meaning: chokh.mah names wisdom as God's gift — the same verse as above, different span. God is the source; wisdom flows from him to those who receive it.

verses.verse_text: Dan 2:23 To you , O God of my fathers , I give thanks and praise , for you have given me wisdom and might , and have now made known to me what we asked of you , for you have made known to us the king’s matter .”
verses.meaning: chokh.mah names wisdom as what God has given Daniel — the source of his capacity to interpret is entirely God's gift, not personal ability.

verses.verse_text: Dan 2:30 But as for me , this mystery has been revealed to me, not because of any wisdom that I have more than all the living , but in order that the interpretation may be made known to the king , and that you may know the thoughts of your mind .
verses.meaning: chokh.mah names wisdom as what Daniel explicitly disclaims as the source of the revelation — it is not his inner capacity but God's gift that enables the interpretation.

verses.verse_text: Dan 5:11 There is a man in your kingdom in whom is the spirit of the holy gods .
