In the days of your father , light and understanding and wisdom like the wisdom of the gods were found in him, and King Nebuchadnezzar , your father —your father the king — made him chief of the magicians , enchanters , Chaldeans , and astrologers ,
verses.meaning: chokh.mah names the wisdom found in Daniel as divine in quality — located in a person as an inner endowment, not professional training.

verses.verse_text: Dan 5:14 I have heard of you that the spirit of the gods is in you, and that light and understanding and excellent wisdom are found in you .
verses.meaning: chokh.mah names wisdom as found in Daniel — recognised by the queen, attributed to the Spirit within him.

verses.verse_text: Exo 26:1 “Moreover, you shall make the tabernacle with ten curtains of fine twined linen and blue and purple and scarlet yarns; you shall make them with cherubim skillfully worked into them .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the act of skilled artistic design worked into the fabric. The span points to the crafted execution — the mind's devising expressed in the material.

verses.verse_text: Exo 26:31 “And you shall make a veil of blue and purple and scarlet yarns and fine twined linen . It shall be made with cherubim skillfully worked into it .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the same skilled design work — the deliberate, crafted working of pattern into sacred textile.

verses.verse_text: Exo 28:6 “And they shall make the ephod of gold , of blue and purple and scarlet yarns, and of fine twined linen , skillfully worked .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the skilled design execution applied to a sacred garment.

verses.verse_text: Exo 28:15 “You shall make a breastpiece of judgment , in skilled work . In the style of the ephod you shall make it—of gold , blue and purple and scarlet yarns, and fine twined linen shall you make it .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the skilled inner capacity expressed in the work — the judgment breastpiece made by deliberate design.

verses.verse_text: Exo 31:4 to devise artistic designs , to work in gold , silver , and bronze ,
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the inner act of devising — the mental forming of artistic design that precedes and directs the physical work.

verses.verse_text: Exo 35:32 to devise artistic designs , to work in gold and silver and bronze ,
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the inner devising of design — the same capacity as Exo 31:4, here in the account of Bezalel's endowment.

verses.verse_text: Exo 35:35 He has filled them with skill to do every sort of work done by an engraver or by a designer or by an embroiderer in blue and purple and scarlet yarns and fine twined linen , or by a weaver —by any sort of workman or skilled designer .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the designer as the person whose inner capacity is to form and devise patterns — the mind that originates the design.

verses.verse_text: Exo 36:8 And all the craftsmen among the workmen made the tabernacle with ten curtains . They were made of fine twined linen and blue and purple and scarlet yarns, with cherubim skillfully worked .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the skilled working into the fabric — the design capacity expressed in execution. Same verse as above, different span. Note: this row is assigned to 4458-001, not 528-004.

verses.verse_text: Exo 36:35 He made the veil of blue and purple and scarlet yarns and fine twined linen ; with cherubim skillfully worked into it he made it.
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the crafted design execution in the sacred veil.

verses.verse_text: Exo 38:23 and with him was Oholiab the son of Ahisamach , of the tribe of Dan , an engraver and designer and embroiderer in blue and purple and scarlet yarns and fine twined linen .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the designer as a person defined by the inner capacity to form and execute patterns — a title for the person whose craft is devising.

verses.verse_text: Exo 39:3 And they hammered out gold leaf , and he cut it into threads to work into the blue and purple and the scarlet yarns, and into the fine twined linen , in skilled design .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the skilled design capacity applied to the material — deliberate, crafted working.

verses.verse_text: Exo 39:8 He made the breastpiece , in skilled work , in the style of the ephod , of gold , blue and purple and scarlet yarns, and fine twined linen .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the skilled execution of the sacred breastpiece.

verses.verse_text: 2Sa 14:14 We must all die ; we are like water spilled on the ground , which cannot be gathered up again. But God will not take away life , and he devises means so that the banished one will not remain an outcast .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names God's purposive devising — forming a way where none existed. The span points to intentional inner purposing directed toward a merciful outcome. This is divine planning, not craftsmanship.

verses.verse_text: 2Ch 2:14 the son of a woman of the daughters of Dan , and his father was a man of Tyre . He is trained to work in gold , silver , bronze , iron , stone , and wood , and in purple , blue , and crimson fabrics and fine linen , and to do all sorts of engraving and execute any design that may be assigned him, with your craftsmen , the craftsmen of my lord , David your father .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the inner capacity for executing devised designs — the skilled working-out of plans. Note: assigned to 4458-001 in the database.

verses.verse_text: 2Ch 26:15 In Jerusalem he made machines , invented by skillful men , to be on the towers and the corners , to shoot arrows and great stones . And his fame spread far , for he was marvelously helped , till he was strong .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the skillful men as those whose inner capacity is invention and design — the devising of machines for a military purpose.

verses.verse_text: Est 8:3 Then Esther spoke again to the king . She fell at his feet and wept and pleaded with him to avert the evil plan of Haman the Agagite and the plot that he had devised against the Jews .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names Haman's inner devising of an evil plan — purposeful, deliberate scheming directed at destruction.

verses.verse_text: Est 9:25 But when it came before the king , he gave orders in writing that his evil plan that he had devised against the Jews should return on his own head , and that he and his sons should be hanged on the gallows .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the devised plan that rebounds on its author — deliberate inner scheming that ends in self-destruction.

verses.verse_text: Psa 35:20 For they do not speak peace , but against those who are quiet in the land they devise words of deceit .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names deliberate inner devising of deceptive words — purposeful scheming directed against the peaceful.

verses.verse_text: Amo 6:5 who sing idle songs to the sound of the harp and like David invent for themselves instruments of music ,
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the inner act of inventing — devising new instruments. The capacity is the same as in the sanctuary craftsmanship contexts, but here it is directed toward self-indulgence rather than sacred purpose.

verses.verse_text: Deu 1:13 Choose for your tribes wise , understanding , and experienced men , and I will appoint them as your heads .’
verses.meaning: Names the quality required for tribal leadership — the term identifies being experienced or known-to-be-wise as a criterion for appointment as head.

verses.verse_text: Deu 1:15 So I took the heads of your tribes , wise and experienced men , and set them as heads over you, commanders of thousands , commanders of hundreds , commanders of fifties , commanders of tens , and officers , throughout your tribes .
verses.meaning: Names the quality of the men appointed — the term identifies them as experienced or known-to-be-wise men set as commanders and leaders.

verses.verse_text: Est 1:13 Then the king said to the wise men who knew the times (for this was the king’s procedure toward all who were versed in law and judgment ,
verses.meaning: Names the inner capacity of interpretive expertise — the term identifies the wise men as those who knew the times, possessing the inner understanding required for royal counsel.

verses.verse_text: Job 11:6 and that he would tell you the secrets of wisdom ! For he is manifold in understanding. Know then that God exacts of you less than your guilt deserves.
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal knowledge Zophar urges on Job — an inner recognition of divine mercy.

verses.verse_text: Job 26:3 How you have counseled him who has no wisdom , and plentifully declared sound knowledge !
verses.meaning: Names the capacity of sound counsel — the term identifies Job's counsellor as someone who has declared sound knowledge, which Bildad now questions.

verses.verse_text: Job 34:2 “ Hear my words , you wise men , and give ear to me , you who know ;
verses.meaning: ya.da names the knowledge of the wise — moral and experiential knowing that qualifies for hearing.

verses.verse_text: Pro 9:9 Give instruction to a wise man , and he will be still wiser ; teach a righteous man, and he will increase in learning .
verses.meaning: Names the inner benefit of instruction — the term identifies what a wise man gains when taught: he will become still wiser, increasing his inner knowledge.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 2:19 and who knows whether he will be wise or a fool ? Yet he will be master of all for which I toiled and used my wisdom under the sun . This also is vanity .
verses.meaning: Names the unresolvable inner uncertainty — the term identifies not knowing whether an heir will be wise or foolish as the vanity that overshadows accumulated toil.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 6:8 For what advantage has the wise man over the fool ? And what does the poor man have who knows how to conduct himself before the living ?
verses.meaning: Names the inner practical wisdom of self-conduct — the term identifies knowing how to conduct oneself before the living as the one advantage a poor man might have.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 8:1 Who is like the wise ? And who knows the interpretation of a thing ? A man’s wisdom makes his face shine , and the hardness of his face is changed .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the moral knowledge of meaning — the wise person can know the interpretation; this is a capacity of the inner person.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 8:5 Whoever keeps a command will know no evil thing , and the wise heart will know the proper time and the just way .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the moral-practical knowing of the wise heart — timing and justice known from within.

verses.verse_text: Hos 14:9 Whoever is wise , let him understand these things; whoever is discerning , let him know them; for the ways of the Lord are right , and the upright walk in them, but transgressors stumble in them .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the moral knowing of God's ways as what discernment produces — right perception leads to knowing.

verses.verse_text: Job 5:12 He frustrates the devices of the crafty , so that their hands achieve no success .
verses.meaning: a.rum names craftiness as an inner disposition of cunning scheming that God frustrates — the prudent inner quality turned toward deception. Note: assigned to 5099-002 (abstaining from taste). This appears to be a misassignment; the verse is about craftiness, not tasting.

verses.verse_text: 1Sa 25:3 Now the name of the man was Nabal , and the name of his wife Abigail . The woman was discerning and beautiful , but the man was harsh and badly behaved ; he was a Calebite .
verses.meaning: se.khel names the inner quality of discernment that characterises Abigail — set in direct contrast to Nabal's harshness. The quality is a personal characteristic, observable and defining.

verses.verse_text: 1Ch 22:12 Only , may the Lord grant you discretion and understanding , that when he gives you charge over Israel you may keep the law of the Lord your God .
verses.meaning: se.khel names discretion as something God grants — an inner quality enabling the keeping of the law. Wisdom here is law-enabling and God-given.

verses.verse_text: 1Ch 26:14 The lot for the east fell to Shelemiah . They cast lots also for his son Zechariah , a shrewd counselor , and his lot came out for the north .
verses.meaning: se.khel names shrewdness as a personal inner quality that defines Zechariah — it qualifies him as a counselor.

verses.verse_text: 2Ch 2:12 Hiram also said , “ Blessed be the Lord God of Israel , who made heaven and earth , who has given King David a wise son , who has discretion and understanding , who will build a temple for the Lord and a royal palace for himself.
verses.meaning: se.khel names discretion as one of Solomon's inner qualities — paired with wisdom and understanding as the endowments for his task. Same verse, different span.

verses.verse_text: 2Ch 30:22 And Hezekiah spoke encouragingly to all the Levites who showed good skill in the service of the Lord . So they ate the food of the festival for seven days , sacrificing peace offerings and giving thanks to the Lord , the God of their fathers .
verses.meaning: se.khel names skill in service as an inner quality recognised and commended — good se.khel expressed in faithful liturgical performance.

verses.verse_text: Ezr 8:18 And by the good hand of our God on us, they brought us a man of discretion , of the sons of Mahli the son of Levi , son of Israel , namely Sherebiah with his sons and kinsmen , 18 ;
verses.meaning: se.khel names discretion as the defining inner quality of Sherebiah — God's hand is credited with bringing him, connecting the quality to divine provision.

verses.verse_text: Neh 8:8 They read from the book , from the Law of God , clearly , and they gave the sense , so that the people understood the reading .
verses.meaning: se.khel names the sense or meaning conveyed in reading — the inner intelligibility of the text made clear to the hearers.

verses.verse_text: Job 17:4 Since you have closed their hearts to understanding , therefore you will not let them triumph .
verses.meaning: se.khel names understanding as something located in the heart — God has closed it, making triumph impossible. The inner quality and its absence are both God's doing.

verses.verse_text: Psa 111:10 The fear of the Lord is the beginning of wisdom ; all those who practice it have a good understanding . His praise endures forever !
verses.meaning: se.khel names good understanding as the outcome of practising the fear of the Lord — the inner quality produced by covenant living.

verses.verse_text: Pro 3:4 So you will find favor and good success in the sight of God and man .
verses.meaning: se.khel names the good outcome — success — that follows from the inner quality of wisdom lived out. The span points to the fruit of the inner disposition.

verses.verse_text: Pro 12:8 A man is commended according to his good sense , but one of twisted mind is despised .
verses.meaning: se.khel names good sense as what earns public recognition — an inner quality that shows in conduct and is judged accordingly.

verses.verse_text: Pro 13:15 Good sense wins favor , but the way of the treacherous is their ruin .
verses.meaning: se.khel names good sense as the inner quality that produces favour — a practical, relational wisdom that brings positive outcomes.

verses.verse_text: Pro 16:22 Good sense is a fountain of life to him who has it, but the instruction of fools is folly .
verses.meaning: se.khel names good sense as an inner quality that is life-giving to its possessor — a self-sustaining inner resource.

verses.verse_text: Pro 19:11 Good sense makes one slow to anger , and it is his glory to overlook an offense .
verses.meaning: se.khel names good sense as an inner quality that produces emotional restraint — wisdom governs the response to offence.

verses.verse_text: Pro 23:9 Do not speak in the hearing of a fool , for he will despise the good sense of your words .
verses.meaning: se.khel names good sense as what the fool is incapable of receiving — the inner quality of the speaker is wasted on those who lack the capacity to receive it.

verses.verse_text: Dan 8:25 By his cunning he shall make deceit prosper under his hand , and in his own mind he shall become great . Without warning he shall destroy many . And he shall even rise up against the Prince of princes , and he shall be broken —but by no human hand .
verses.meaning: se.khel names the inner quality of the ruler as cunning — the same capacity for practical wisdom used in the service of deceit and self-aggrandisement.

verses.verse_text: Job 5:12 He frustrates the devices of the crafty , so that their hands achieve no success .
verses.meaning: tu.shiy.yah names the effective outcome — success — that God withholds from the crafty. The span points to what their scheming cannot produce: sound effective result.

verses.verse_text: Job 6:13 Have I any help in me, when resource is driven from me ?
verses.meaning: tu.shiy.yah names an inner resource — the effective capacity that sustains. Job asks whether any such resource remains when it has been stripped away.

verses.verse_text: Job 11:6 and that he would tell you the secrets of wisdom ! For he is manifold in understanding. Know then that God exacts of you less than your guilt deserves.
verses.meaning: tu.shiy.yah names wisdom as something God holds in its fullness — secrets of wisdom, manifold in depth. The span points to the inexhaustible inner resource of divine wisdom.

verses.verse_text: Job 12:16 With him are strength and sound wisdom ; the deceived and the deceiver are his.
verses.meaning: tu.shiy.yah names sound wisdom as a divine attribute — belonging to God, paired with strength. Both the deceived and deceiver are under his sovereign comprehension.

verses.verse_text: Job 26:3 How you have counseled him who has no wisdom , and plentifully declared sound knowledge !
verses.meaning: tu.shiy.yah names sound effective knowledge — the real thing, declared in abundance. The verse is ironic: Bildad has done this poorly, despite claiming to do it.

verses.verse_text: Job 30:22 You lift me up on the wind ; you make me ride on it, and you toss me about in the roar of the storm.
verses.meaning: tu.shiy.yah here is translated as toss — an unusual and disputed rendering. The span appears to carry the meaning of being driven or dissolved rather than sound wisdom. This is a difficult text.

verses.verse_text: Pro 2:7 he stores up sound wisdom for the upright ; he is a shield to those who walk in integrity ,
verses.meaning: tu.shiy.yah names sound wisdom as something God stores and gives — an inner resource available to those who walk uprightly, held in reserve by God for them.

verses.verse_text: Pro 3:21 My son , do not lose sight of these— keep sound wisdom and discretion ,
verses.meaning: tu.shiy.yah names sound wisdom as something to be held and kept — an inner resource that can be retained or lost, valued for its protective function.

verses.verse_text: Pro 8:14 I have counsel and sound wisdom ; I have insight ; I have strength .
verses.meaning: tu.shiy.yah names sound wisdom as one of wisdom's own possessions — Wisdom personified claims it as her inner resource alongside counsel, insight, and strength.

verses.verse_text: Pro 18:1 Whoever isolates himself seeks his own desire ; he breaks out against all sound judgment .
verses.meaning: tu.shiy.yah names sound judgment as the inner standard that isolation violates — the self-seeking person acts against what is effectively wise.

verses.verse_text: Isa 28:29 This also comes from the Lord of hosts ; he is wonderful in counsel and excellent in wisdom .
verses.meaning: Names the excellence attributed to God's counsel — the term identifies wisdom as the inner quality of divine planning that makes God's counsel wonderful.

verses.verse_text: Mic 6:9 The voice of the Lord cries to the city — and it is sound wisdom to fear your name : “ Hear of the rod and of him who appointed it !
verses.meaning: tu.shiy.yah names sound wisdom as what the fear of God is — fearing God's name is not mere piety but the effective inner orientation that constitutes genuine wisdom.

subgroups.label: Understanding as inner perceptive faculty
subgroups.description: Terms that name the inner faculty of understanding — the capacity to receive, perceive, and grasp what is being revealed or communicated. Morally conditioned: the faculty is enabled by humility before God and blocked by heart-dullness. Includes the structural opposite (senseless).

verses.verse_text: Mat 15:16 And he said , “ Are you also still without understanding ?
verses.meaning: asunetos names the absence of the inner perceptive faculty in the disciples — they have not grasped the point of what Jesus has just said.

verses.verse_text: Mar 7:18 And he said to them , “ Then are you also without understanding ? Do you not see that whatever goes into a person from outside cannot defile him ,
verses.meaning: asunetos names the absence of the inner perceptive faculty in the disciples — they have not grasped the meaning of the teaching on defilement.

verses.verse_text: Rom 1:21 For although they knew God , they did not honor him as God or give thanks to him, but they became futile in their thinking , and their foolish hearts were darkened .
verses.meaning: asunetos names the foolish inner condition of those who refused to honour God — the heart without understanding is darkened.

verses.verse_text: Rom 1:31 foolish , faithless , heartless , ruthless .
verses.meaning: asunetos names foolishness as one item in a list of moral failures — the absence of the inner perceptive faculty alongside the absence of faithfulness and compassion.

verses.verse_text: Rom 10:19 But I ask , did Israel not understand ? First Moses says , “ I will make you jealous of those who are not a nation ; with a foolish nation I will make you angry .”
verses.meaning: asunetos names the Gentile nation as foolish — lacking the inner perceptive faculty, yet used by God to provoke Israel.

verses.verse_text: 1Ti 4:7 Have nothing to do with irreverent , silly myths . Rather train yourself for godliness ;
verses.meaning: gumnazō names the habituating inner training toward godliness — the deliberate, repeated self-discipline that forms the inner person. The training is a sustained programme of inner formation.

verses.verse_text: Heb 5:14 But solid food is for the mature , for those who have their powers of discernment trained by constant practice to distinguish good from evil .
verses.meaning: gumnazō names the training of the inner discernment faculty — the formation of discernment through habitual exercise is the means by which the mature inner life distinguishes good from evil.

verses.verse_text: Heb 12:11 For the moment all discipline seems painful rather than pleasant , but later it yields the peaceful fruit of righteousness to those who have been trained by it .
verses.meaning: gumnazō names the training effect of discipline — those whose inner person has been trained through suffering yield righteousness.

verses.verse_text: 2Pe 2:14 They have eyes full of adultery , insatiable for sin . They entice unsteady souls . They have hearts trained in greed . Accursed children !
verses.meaning: gumnazō names the inner habituation toward vice — the heart trained in greed has been formed through repeated practice, just as the heart trained in godliness (1Ti 4:7) has been formed. Greed as an inner disposition formed by habitual practice.

verses.verse_text: 1Ti 1:7 desiring to be teachers of the law , without understanding either what they are saying or the things about which they make confident assertions .
verses.meaning: diabebaioō names the outward insistence arising from inner conviction — they assert with confidence without the inner understanding that should ground such conviction. The form is present without the inner substance.

verses.verse_text: Tit 3:8 The saying is trustworthy , and I want you to insist on these things , so that those who have believed in God may be careful to devote themselves to good works . These things are excellent and profitable for people .
verses.meaning: diabebaioō names the outward insisting that flows from inner conviction — Titus is to assert these things with the force of settled inner certainty.

verses.verse_text: Mar 9:34 But they kept silent , for on the way they had argued with one another about who was the greatest .
verses.meaning: dialegō names the argument/disputation as an engagement of inner faculties — the disciples had been in a reasoning dispute that their silence now conceals.

verses.verse_text: Act 17:2 And Paul went in , as was his custom , and on three Sabbath days he reasoned with them from the Scriptures ,
verses.meaning: dialegō names reasoning/discourse aimed at persuading the inner person — Paul's engagement with the synagogue through reasoned argument from Scripture. The goal is to reach the inner life through the engagement of the mind.

verses.verse_text: Act 17:17 So he reasoned in the synagogue with the Jews and the devout persons , and in the marketplace every day with those who happened to be there .
verses.meaning: dialegō names sustained reasoned discourse as Paul's evangelistic method in Athens — engaging the inner faculties of all he encounters through deliberate argumentation.

verses.verse_text: Act 18:4 And he reasoned in the synagogue every Sabbath , and tried to persuade Jews and Greeks .
verses.meaning: dialegō names Paul's synagogue reasoning paired with persuasion — the inner-faculty engagement of those he encounters through argument that aims to convince.

verses.verse_text: Act 18:19 And they came to Ephesus , and he left them there , but he himself went into the synagogue and reasoned with the Jews .
verses.meaning: dialegō names Paul's reasoning with the Jews in Ephesus — consistent pattern of engaging inner faculties through Scripture-based argument.

verses.verse_text: Act 19:8 And he entered the synagogue and for three months spoke boldly , reasoning and persuading them about the kingdom of God .
verses.meaning: dialegō names reasoning about the kingdom as Paul's sustained method — three months of inner-faculty engagement through argument.

verses.verse_text: Act 19:9 But when some became stubborn and continued in unbelief , speaking evil of the Way before the congregation , he withdrew from them and took the disciples with him, reasoning daily in the hall of Tyrannus .
verses.meaning: dialegō names the daily reasoning in a public venue — Paul's systematic engagement of the inner life of all who came through reasoned discourse.

verses.verse_text: Act 20:7 On the first day of the week , when we were gathered together to break bread , Paul talked with them , intending to depart on the next day , and he prolonged his speech until midnight .
verses.meaning: dialegō names the extended discourse that stretched until midnight — the urgency of inner-life engagement through teaching that delays departure.

verses.verse_text: Act 24:12 and they did not find me disputing with anyone or stirring up a crowd , either in the temple or in the synagogues or in the city .
verses.meaning: dialegō names Paul's denial of disruptive disputing — the term here covers the kind of public reasoned debate that he did not engage in inflammatory form.

verses.verse_text: Act 24:25 And as he reasoned about righteousness and self-control and the coming judgment , Felix was alarmed and said , “ Go away for the present . When I get an opportunity I will summon you .”
verses.meaning: dialegō names Paul's reasoning directed at Felix's inner life — the topics (righteousness, self-control, judgment) address the conscience. The result is alarm — the inner person is reached.

verses.verse_text: Heb 12:5 And have you forgotten the exhortation that addresses you as sons ? “ My son , do not regard lightly the discipline of the Lord , nor be weary when reproved by him .
verses.meaning: dialegō names the word of Scripture that directly engages the inner person — the exhortation speaks to the hearer as sons, addressing the conscience and will.

verses.verse_text: Mar 8:17 And Jesus , aware of this, said to them , “ Why are you discussing the fact that you have no bread ? Do you not yet perceive or understand ? Are your hearts hardened ?
verses.meaning: dialogizomai names the shared reasoning Jesus identifies as incomprehension — the disciples' deliberation about bread is a symptom of inner hardness and failure of understanding.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 14:28 But if there is no one to interpret , let each of them keep silent in church and speak to himself and to God .
verses.meaning: diermēneutēs names the interpreter whose absence requires the speaker to redirect tongues toward private inner communion with God — without interpretation, the only inner-life engagement possible is personal, not communal.

verses.verse_text: Jam 1:6 But let him ask in faith , with no doubting , for the one who doubts is like a wave of the sea that is driven and tossed by the wind .
verses.meaning: eikō names the simile that characterises inner instability — the doubter's inner condition is characterised through comparison with a wave. The simile reveals the nature of the inner state: directionless, tossed, unstable.

verses.verse_text: Jam 1:23 For if anyone is a hearer of the word and not a doer , he is like a man who looks intently at his natural face in a mirror .
verses.meaning: eikō names the simile that characterises the hearer-not-doer — the comparison with the mirror-looker who immediately forgets reveals the nature of the inner response: seeing without retaining or acting.

verses.verse_text: Jam 3:13 Who is wise and understanding among you ? By his good conduct let him show his works in the meekness of wisdom .
verses.meaning: epistēmōn names genuine knowing as an inner quality verified by conduct — understanding is not claimed but demonstrated.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 12:10 to another the working of miracles , to another prophecy , to another the ability to distinguish between spirits , to another various kinds of tongues , to another the interpretation of tongues .
verses.meaning: hermēneia names the Spirit-given gift of interpreting tongues — an inner spiritual capacity that enables the community to be built up through understanding.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 14:26 What then , brothers ? When you come together , each one has a hymn , a lesson , a revelation , a tongue , or an interpretation . Let all things be done for building up .
verses.meaning: hermēneia names interpretation as one of the Spirit-given community contributions — the capacity that enables tongues to become intelligible inner content for the hearer.

verses.verse_text: Mat 1:23 “ Behold , the virgin shall conceive and bear a son , and they shall call his name Immanuel ” ( which means , God with us ).
verses.meaning: methermēneuō names the translation that reveals the inner theological meaning of the name — the act of translating Immanuel discloses that the name carries "God with us" as its inner content. Translation as theological revelation.

verses.verse_text: Luk 10:21 In that same hour he rejoiced in the Holy Spirit and said , “I thank you , Father , Lord of heaven and earth , that you have hidden these things from the wise and understanding and revealed them to little children ; yes , Father , for such was your gracious will .
verses.meaning: sofos names the wise as a category excluded from divine disclosure — parallel to Mat 11:25. The wise and the understanding are the human wisdom class; the little children are the recipients.

verses.verse_text: Act 5:2 and with his wife’s knowledge he kept back for himself some of the proceeds and brought only a part of it and laid it at the apostles ’ feet .
verses.meaning: suneidō names the wife's inner conscious awareness — she was a co-knower of the deception. Moral complicity as shared inner awareness.

verses.verse_text: Act 12:12 When he realized this, he went to the house of Mary , the mother of John whose other name was Mark , where many were gathered together and were praying .
verses.meaning: suneidō names Peter's inner conscious coming-to-awareness — the recognition that produces a purposive response. Cognitive recognition as the broader semantic range of the same conscience faculty.

verses.verse_text: Act 14:6 they learned of it and fled to Lystra and Derbe , cities of Lycaonia , and to the surrounding country ,
verses.meaning: suneidō names Paul and Barnabas becoming aware of the plot — the conscious inner recognition that produces urgent action.

verses.verse_text: Mar 12:33 And to love him with all the heart and with all the understanding and with all the strength , and to love one’s neighbor as oneself , is much more than all whole burnt offerings and sacrifices .”
verses.meaning: sunesis names understanding as one of the faculties with which God is to be loved — the inner comprehension directed wholly toward God as an act of love.

verses.verse_text: Luk 2:47 And all who heard him were amazed at his understanding and his answers .
verses.meaning: sunesis names the understanding that amazes — the inner comprehension visible in the twelve-year-old Jesus's responses to the teachers.

verses.verse_text: Eph 3:4 When you read this , you can perceive my insight into the mystery of Christ ,
verses.meaning: sunesis names Paul's inner comprehension of the mystery — visible to those who read, it is a God-given understanding of what was hidden.

verses.verse_text: Col 1:9 And so , from the day we heard , we have not ceased to pray for you , asking that you may be filled with the knowledge of his will in all spiritual wisdom and understanding ,
verses.meaning: sunesis names understanding as one of the Spirit-given capacities Paul prays for — the inner comprehension of God's will directed toward him.

verses.verse_text: Col 2:2 that their hearts may be encouraged , being knit together in love , to reach all the riches of full assurance of understanding and the knowledge of God’s mystery , which is Christ ,
verses.meaning: sunesis names the inner comprehension of the mystery as the goal — full assurance of understanding as the destination of encouraged, love-knit hearts.

verses.verse_text: 2Ti 2:7 Think over what I say , for the Lord will give you understanding in everything .
verses.meaning: sunesis names understanding as a divine gift — the Lord gives the inner comprehension Timothy needs for everything.

verses.verse_text: Mat 11:25 At that time Jesus declared , “I thank you , Father , Lord of heaven and earth , that you have hidden these things from the wise and understanding and revealed them to little children ;
verses.meaning: sunetos names the understanding person as one from whom divine revelation is hidden — intelligence/discernment as a human category excluded from divine disclosure.

verses.verse_text: Luk 10:21 In that same hour he rejoiced in the Holy Spirit and said , “I thank you , Father , Lord of heaven and earth , that you have hidden these things from the wise and understanding and revealed them to little children ; yes , Father , for such was your gracious will .
verses.meaning: sunetos names the intelligent/discerning person from whom divine things are hidden — the human capacity for comprehension as the category excluded from divine disclosure.

verses.verse_text: Act 13:7 He was with the proconsul , Sergius Paulus , a man of intelligence , who summoned Barnabas and Saul and sought to hear the word of God .
verses.meaning: sunetos names intelligence as the defining inner quality of the proconsul — his capacity for comprehension draws him toward the word.

verses.verse_text: Mat 13:13 This is why I speak to them in parables , because seeing they do not see , and hearing they do not hear , nor do they understand .
verses.meaning: suniēmi names the failed inner perceptive act — the faculty of understanding is present but not activated; perception without reception.

verses.verse_text: Mat 13:14 Indeed , in their case the prophecy of Isaiah is fulfilled that says : “‘“You will indeed hear but never understand , and you will indeed see but never perceive .”
verses.meaning: suniēmi names the judicial withholding of understanding — they hear but the inner reception is blocked.

verses.verse_text: Mat 13:15 For this people’s heart has grown dull , and with their ears they can barely hear , and their eyes they have closed , lest they should see with their eyes and hear with their ears and understand with their heart and turn , and I would heal them .’
verses.meaning: suniēmi names the inner comprehension of the heart that would lead to turning — the faculty that, if activated, would produce repentance.

verses.verse_text: Mat 13:19 When anyone hears the word of the kingdom and does not understand it, the evil one comes and snatches away what has been sown in his heart . This is what was sown along the path .
verses.meaning: suniēmi names the inner reception that is absent — without understanding, the word has no root and can be snatched away.

verses.verse_text: Mat 13:23 As for what was sown on good soil , this is the one who hears the word and understands it . He indeed bears fruit and yields , in one case a hundredfold , in another sixty , and in another thirty .”
verses.meaning: suniēmi names the inner perceptive reception that produces fruit — understanding is what makes the word fruitful in the person.

verses.verse_text: Mat 13:51 “Have you understood all these things?” They said to him , “ Yes .”
verses.meaning: suniēmi names the inner comprehension of the disciples — have they grasped what the parables mean?

verses.verse_text: Mat 15:10 And he called the people to him and said to them , “ Hear and understand :
verses.meaning: suniēmi names the inner perceptive reception being commanded — the crowd is called to hear in such a way that they grasp the meaning.

verses.verse_text: Mat 16:12 Then they understood that he did not tell them to beware of the leaven of bread , but of the teaching of the Pharisees and Sadducees .
verses.meaning: suniēmi names the inner perceptive act of grasping meaning — the disciples understand what Jesus was referring to.

verses.verse_text: Mat 17:13 Then the disciples understood that he was speaking to them of John the Baptist .
verses.meaning: suniēmi names the inner comprehension — they grasp the reference.

verses.verse_text: Mar 4:12 so that “'they may indeed see but not perceive , and may indeed hear but not understand , lest they should turn and be forgiven .'”
verses.meaning: suniēmi names the withheld inner perceptive reception — parable-speaking prevents understanding that would lead to turning.

verses.verse_text: Mar 6:52 for they did not understand about the loaves , but their hearts were hardened .
verses.meaning: suniēmi names the failed inner comprehension linked to heart-hardness — the two are directly connected. Hardened hearts cannot understand.

verses.verse_text: Mar 7:14 And he called the people to him again and said to them , “ Hear me , all of you, and understand :
verses.meaning: suniēmi names the inner perceptive reception being commanded — not just hearing but grasping the meaning.

verses.verse_text: Mar 8:17 And Jesus , aware of this, said to them , “ Why are you discussing the fact that you have no bread ? Do you not yet perceive or understand ? Are your hearts hardened ?
verses.meaning: suniēmi names the absent inner comprehension paired with heart-hardness — the question links the two directly.

verses.verse_text: Mar 8:21 And he said to them , “Do you not yet understand ?”
verses.meaning: suniēmi names the inner comprehension the disciples have not yet reached — the question implies the repeated failure to grasp.

verses.verse_text: Luk 2:50 And they did not understand the saying that he spoke to them .
verses.meaning: suniēmi names the failed inner comprehension of Mary and Joseph — they cannot grasp the significance of what Jesus said about his Father's house.

verses.verse_text: Luk 8:10 he said , “To you it has been given to know the secrets of the kingdom of God , but for others they are in parables , so that ‘ seeing they may not see , and hearing they may not understand .’
verses.meaning: suniēmi names the withheld understanding — the parable form protects the mystery from those who are not given to receive it.

verses.verse_text: Luk 18:34 But they understood none of these things . This saying was hidden from them , and they did not grasp what was said .
verses.meaning: suniēmi names the complete failure of inner comprehension — the disciples grasped nothing of the passion prediction.

verses.verse_text: Luk 24:45 Then he opened their minds to understand the Scriptures ,
verses.meaning: suniēmi names the inner comprehension Christ opens — minds previously closed to the meaning of Scripture are opened by his action.

verses.verse_text: Act 7:25 He supposed that his brothers would understand that God was giving them salvation by his hand , but they did not understand .
verses.meaning: suniēmi names the failed inner comprehension of Israel — they did not perceive Moses's deliverance as God's saving act.

verses.verse_text: Act 28:26 “‘ Go to this people , and say , “You will indeed hear but never understand , and you will indeed see but never perceive .”
verses.meaning: suniēmi names the judicial withholding of inner comprehension — the Isaiah judgment applied to Israel's response to Paul's preaching.

verses.verse_text: Act 28:27 For this people’s heart has grown dull , and with their ears they can barely hear , and their eyes they have closed ; lest they should see with their eyes and hear with their ears and understand with their heart and turn , and I would heal them .’
verses.meaning: suniēmi names the inner comprehension of the heart that would lead to turning — the faculty blocked by the people's own hardening.

verses.verse_text: Rom 3:11 no one understands ; no one seeks for God .
verses.meaning: suniēmi names the universal failed inner comprehension of fallen humanity — paired with the failure to seek God.

verses.verse_text: Rom 15:21 but as it is written , “Those who have never been told of him will see , and those who have never heard will understand .”
verses.meaning: suniēmi names the inner perceptive comprehension that will come to the Gentiles who have not yet been told — the spread of understanding as the spread of the gospel.

verses.verse_text: 2Cor 10:12 Not that we dare to classify or compare ourselves with some of those who are commending themselves . But when they measure themselves by one another and compare themselves with one another , they are without understanding .
verses.meaning: suniēmi names the failed inner comprehension of those who measure themselves by themselves — self-comparison is a form of non-understanding.

verses.verse_text: Eph 5:17 Therefore do not be foolish , but understand what the will of the Lord is.
verses.meaning: suniēmi names the practical inner comprehension of God's will — a deliberate act of perception directed toward knowing what the Lord requires.

verses.verse_text: Luk 1:17 and he will go before him in the spirit and power of Elijah , to turn the hearts of the fathers to the children , and the disobedient to the wisdom of the just , to make ready for the Lord a people prepared .”
verses.meaning: fronesis names the inner wisdom/understanding that characterises the just — the quality the disobedient are to be turned toward. Understanding as moral orientation.

verses.verse_text: Eph 1:8 which he lavished upon us , in all wisdom and insight
verses.meaning: fronesis names insight as a divine gift lavished on believers — an inner capacity for understanding God's will given alongside wisdom.

verses.verse_text: Gen 41:33 Now therefore let Pharaoh select a discerning and wise man , and set him over the land of Egypt .
verses.meaning: bin names the inner perceptive faculty as a qualification for appointment — the discerning person perceives situations rightly and can govern.

verses.verse_text: Gen 41:39 Then Pharaoh said to Joseph , “ Since God has shown you all this , there is none so discerning and wise as you are.
verses.meaning: bin names the perceptive faculty recognised in Joseph — God's disclosure is what has demonstrated the quality.

verses.verse_text: Deu 1:13 Choose for your tribes wise , understanding , and experienced men , and I will appoint them as your heads .’
verses.meaning: bin names the inner perceptive faculty as one of three qualities required for leadership — the capacity to grasp situations and discern rightly.

verses.verse_text: Deu 4:6 Keep them and do them, for that will be your wisdom and your understanding in the sight of the peoples , who , when they hear all these statutes , will say , ‘ Surely this great nation is a wise and understanding people .’
verses.meaning: bin names understanding as what covenant obedience will be seen to be — understanding demonstrated through faithfulness. Assigned to 816-002.

verses.verse_text: Deu 32:7 Remember the days of old ; consider the years of many generations ; ask your father , and he will show you, your elders , and they will tell you .
verses.meaning: bin names the reflective perceptive act — considering the past in order to understand the present. The faculty is directed toward historical memory.

verses.verse_text: Deu 32:10 “He found him in a desert land , and in the howling waste of the wilderness ; he encircled him, he cared for him, he kept him as the apple of his eye .
verses.meaning: bin names God's attentive care — his perceptive attending to Israel in the wilderness. The faculty here is divine attention directed toward the person.

verses.verse_text: 1Sa 3:8 And the Lord called Samuel again the third time . And he arose and went to Eli and said , “ Here I am, for you called me.” Then Eli perceived that the Lord was calling the boy .
verses.meaning: bin names the inner perceptive act — Eli reads the situation correctly on the third occurrence and grasps what is happening.

verses.verse_text: 1Sa 16:18 One of the young men answered , “ Behold , I have seen a son of Jesse the Bethlehemite , who is skillful in playing , a man of valor , a man of war , prudent in speech , and a man of good presence , and the Lord is with him .”
verses.meaning: bin names prudence in speech as an expression of the inner perceptive faculty — David is described as one whose understanding governs his words.

verses.verse_text: 2Sa 12:19 But when David saw that his servants were whispering together, David understood that the child was dead . And David said to his servants , “Is the child dead ?” They said , “He is dead .”
verses.meaning: bin names the inner perceptive act — reading the servants' behaviour and grasping what it means without being told.

verses.verse_text: 1Ki 3:9 Give your servant therefore an understanding mind to govern your people , that I may discern between good and evil , for who is able to govern this your great people ?”
verses.meaning: bin names the discerning faculty as what Solomon requests — an inner capacity to perceive and distinguish, seated in the heart/mind, necessary for governance.

verses.verse_text: 1Ki 3:11 And God said to him, “ Because you have asked this , and have not asked for yourself long life or riches or the life of your enemies , but have asked for yourself understanding to discern what is right ,
verses.meaning: bin names the understanding Solomon asked for — the inner faculty that enables right discernment, valued by God as the right thing to ask for.

verses.verse_text: 1Ki 3:12 behold , I now do according to your word . Behold , I give you a wise and discerning mind , so that none like you has been before you and none like you shall arise after you .
verses.meaning: bin names the discerning faculty God gives Solomon — seated in the mind, unparalleled, enabling right perception and judgment.

verses.verse_text: 1Ki 3:21 When I rose in the morning to nurse my child , behold , he was dead . But when I looked at him closely in the morning , behold , he was not the child that I had borne .”
verses.meaning: bin names the perceptive looking — the mother's careful examination that reveals the truth. The faculty is directed toward physical observation with understanding.

verses.verse_text: 1Ch 15:22 Chenaniah , leader of the Levites in music , should direct the music , for he understood it.
verses.meaning: bin names the perceptive mastery of a craft — understanding music as an inner competence that qualifies for leadership of it.

verses.verse_text: 1Ch 25:7 The number of them along with their brothers , who were trained in singing to the Lord , all who were skillful , was 288 .
verses.meaning: bin names skillfulness in music as an inner competence — understanding of the craft qualifying the person for temple service.

verses.verse_text: 1Ch 25:8 And they cast lots for their duties , small and great , teacher and pupil alike .
verses.meaning: bin names the teacher as one whose inner perceptive faculty qualifies them to instruct — the one who understands enough to teach.

verses.verse_text: 1Ch 27:32 Jonathan , David’s uncle , was a counselor , being a man of understanding and a scribe . He and Jehiel the son of Hachmoni attended the king’s sons .
verses.meaning: bin names understanding as a defining inner quality of Jonathan — it qualifies him as counselor. Understanding and scribal knowledge are paired.

verses.verse_text: 1Ch 28:9 “And you , Solomon my son , know the God of your father and serve him with a whole heart and with a willing mind , for the Lord searches all hearts and understands every plan and thought . If you seek him, he will be found by you, but if you forsake him, he will cast you off forever .
verses.meaning: bin names God's comprehensive inner perception of the human person — he understands every plan and thought. The faculty here is divine.

verses.verse_text: 2Ch 11:23 And he dealt wisely and distributed some of his sons through all the districts of Judah and Benjamin , in all the fortified cities , and he gave them abundant provisions and procured wives for them.
verses.meaning: bin names the wise perceptive action — the inner faculty expressed in prudent strategic distribution of his sons.

verses.verse_text: 2Ch 26:5 He set himself to seek God in the days of Zechariah , who instructed him in the fear of God , and as long as he sought the Lord , God made him prosper .
verses.meaning: bin names the instruction in understanding — Zechariah's inner comprehension of the fear of God that he imparts.

verses.verse_text: 2Ch 34:12 And the men did the work faithfully . Over them were set Jahath and Obadiah the Levites , of the sons of Merari , and Zechariah and Meshullam , of the sons of the Kohathites , to have oversight . The Levites , all who were skillful with instruments of music ,
verses.meaning: bin names skillfulness in music as an inner competence qualifying for temple service.

verses.verse_text: 2Ch 35:3 And he said to the Levites who taught all Israel and who were holy to the Lord , “ Put the holy ark in the house that Solomon the son of David , king of Israel , built . You need not carry it on your shoulders . Now serve the Lord your God and his people Israel .
verses.meaning: bin names the teaching role — those whose inner understanding of the law enables them to teach it.

verses.verse_text: Ezr 8:15 I gathered them to the river that runs to Ahava , and there we camped three days . As I reviewed the people and the priests , I found there none of the sons of Levi .
verses.meaning: bin names the act of careful examination — reviewing with discerning attention. Set aside as no_inner_being — the span here is administrative review rather than inner perceptive faculty.

verses.verse_text: Ezr 8:16 Then I sent for Eliezer , Ariel , Shemaiah , Elnathan , Jarib , Elnathan , Nathan , Zechariah , and Meshullam , leading men , and for Joiarib and Elnathan , who were men of insight ,
verses.meaning: bin names insight as the defining inner quality of those Ezra sends — they are chosen for their perceptive understanding.

verses.verse_text: Neh 8:2 So Ezra the priest brought the Law before the assembly , both men and women and all who could understand what they heard , on the first day of the seventh month .
verses.meaning: bin names the inner capacity to understand the Law as heard — those who have the faculty to receive and grasp what is read.

verses.verse_text: Neh 8:3 And he read from it facing the square before the Water Gate from early morning until midday , in the presence of the men and the women and those who could understand . And the ears of all the people were attentive to the Book of the Law .
verses.meaning: bin names the same inner capacity — those able to receive and grasp the reading of the Law.

verses.verse_text: Neh 8:7 Also Jeshua , Bani , Sherebiah , Jamin , Akkub , Shabbethai , Hodiah , Maaseiah , Kelita , Azariah , Jozabad , Hanan , Pelaiah , the Levites , helped the people to understand the Law , while the people remained in their places .
verses.meaning: bin names the enabling of understanding — the Levites' inner comprehension being used to open the Law to others.

verses.verse_text: Neh 8:9 And Nehemiah , who was the governor , and Ezra the priest and scribe , and the Levites who taught the people said to all the people , “This day is holy to the Lord your God ; do not mourn or weep .” For all the people wept as they heard the words of the Law .
verses.meaning: bin names teaching as the expression of inner understanding — those who comprehend the Law enabling others to receive it.

verses.verse_text: Neh 8:12 And all the people went their way to eat and drink and to send portions and to make great rejoicing , because they had understood the words that were declared to them .
verses.meaning: bin names the inner act of comprehending — the people's understanding of the words producing joy.

verses.verse_text: Neh 10:28 “The rest of the people , the priests , the Levites , the gatekeepers , the singers , the temple servants , and all who have separated themselves from the peoples of the lands to the Law of God , their wives , their sons , their daughters , all who have knowledge and understanding ,
verses.meaning: bin names understanding as one of the defining inner capacities of those who separate themselves to covenant faithfulness.

verses.verse_text: Neh 13:7 and came to Jerusalem , and I then discovered the evil that Eliashib had done for Tobiah , preparing for him a chamber in the courts of the house of God .
verses.meaning: bin names the perceptive discovery — Nehemiah perceiving and grasping the wrongdoing on his arrival.

verses.verse_text: Job 6:24 “ Teach me , and I will be silent ; make me understand how I have gone astray .
verses.meaning: bin names the inner faculty Job asks to have activated — he wants the perceptive understanding of where he has erred. The posture is humble petition.

verses.verse_text: Job 6:30 Is there any injustice on my tongue ? Cannot my palate discern the cause of calamity ?
verses.meaning: bin names the inner perceptive faculty — Job claims his inner understanding can distinguish real injustice from supposed fault.

verses.verse_text: Job 9:11 Behold, he passes by me, and I see him not; he moves on , but I do not perceive him .
verses.meaning: bin names the failed perceptive act — Job cannot discern God's passing. The faculty is present but the object is beyond it.

verses.verse_text: Job 11:11 For he knows worthless men ; when he sees iniquity , will he not consider it?
verses.meaning: bin names God's perceptive attending — he sees and considers iniquity. Divine understanding is thorough and attentive.

verses.verse_text: Job 13:1 “Behold, my eye has seen all this, my ear has heard and understood it .
verses.meaning: bin names the inner act of grasping what is heard — understanding as the reception and comprehension of what the ear receives.

verses.verse_text: Job 14:21 His sons come to honor , and he does not know it; they are brought low , and he perceives it not .
verses.meaning: bin names the failed perceptive faculty of the dead — the person beyond death cannot perceive what happens to those they loved.

verses.verse_text: Job 15:9 What do you know that we do not know ? What do you understand that is not clear to us ?
verses.meaning: bin names the inner perceptive capacity that Eliphaz claims equals or surpasses Job's — a challenge about the reach of Job's understanding.

verses.verse_text: Job 18:2 “How long will you hunt for words ? Consider , and then we will speak .
verses.meaning: bin names the reflective perceptive act — Bildad calls Job to think carefully before speaking.

verses.verse_text: Job 23:5 I would know what he would answer me and understand what he would say to me .
verses.meaning: bin names the inner faculty Job wants to exercise in response to God — the desire to comprehend what God would answer.

verses.verse_text: Job 23:8 “ Behold , I go forward , but he is not there, and backward , but I do not perceive him ;
verses.meaning: bin names the failed perceptive act — Job cannot locate God by going forward or backward. The faculty cannot reach its object.

verses.verse_text: Job 23:15 Therefore I am terrified at his presence ; when I consider , I am in dread of him .
verses.meaning: bin names the reflective perceptive act — contemplating God produces dread. Understanding and terror are linked.

verses.verse_text: Job 26:14 Behold , these are but the outskirts of his ways , and how small a whisper do we hear of him! But the thunder of his power who can understand ?”
verses.meaning: bin names the inner faculty confronted with its limits — divine power exceeds human understanding.

verses.verse_text: Job 28:23 “ God understands the way to it, and he knows its place .
verses.meaning: bin names God's inner comprehension of the location of wisdom and understanding — he alone knows where it is found.

verses.verse_text: Job 30:20 I cry to you for help and you do not answer me; I stand , and you only look at me .
verses.meaning: bin names God's attending — he looks at Job without answering. The faculty is present but not responsive. A difficult use — closer to "look at" than to comprehend.

verses.verse_text: Job 31:1 “I have made a covenant with my eyes ; how then could I gaze at a virgin ?
verses.meaning: bin names the directed perceptive act — the gaze that would violate the covenant of the eyes. The inner faculty of attention directed toward a forbidden object.

verses.verse_text: Job 32:8 But it is the spirit in man , the breath of the Almighty , that makes him understand.
verses.meaning: bin names understanding as what the breath of God produces in the person — the Spirit as the source of inner comprehension.

verses.verse_text: Job 32:9 It is not the old who are wise , nor the aged who understand what is right .
verses.meaning: bin names the inner perceptive faculty for moral discernment — understanding what is right is not a function of age but of something else (implied: the Spirit).

verses.verse_text: Job 32:12 I gave you my attention , and, behold , there was none among you who refuted Job or who answered his words .
verses.meaning: bin names the act of attentive perceptive listening — the inner faculty directed toward what others say.

verses.verse_text: Job 36:29 Can anyone understand the spreading of the clouds , the thunderings of his pavilion ?
verses.meaning: bin names the inner faculty confronted with natural phenomena beyond human comprehension — the limits of understanding before divine works.

verses.verse_text: Job 37:14 “ Hear this, O Job ; stop and consider the wondrous works of God .
verses.meaning: bin names the reflective perceptive act directed toward divine activity — the call to use the inner faculty of understanding to attend to God's works.

verses.verse_text: Job 38:18 Have you comprehended the expanse of the earth ? Declare , if you know all this.
verses.meaning: bin names the inner perceptive faculty confronted with cosmic scale — comprehending the earth's expanse is beyond human reach.

verses.verse_text: Job 38:20 that you may take it to its territory and that you may discern the paths to its home ?
verses.meaning: bin names the inner perceptive faculty God challenges Job to exercise — discerning the paths of light and darkness is beyond human capacity.

verses.verse_text: Job 42:3 ‘ Who is this that hides counsel without knowledge ?’ Therefore I have uttered what I did not understand , things too wonderful for me, which I did not know .
verses.meaning: bin names Job's recognition that his inner faculty of understanding was insufficient for what he was speaking about — self-acknowledged failure of comprehension.

verses.verse_text: To the choirmaster : for the flutes . A Psalm of David . Psa 5:1 Give ear to my words , O Lord ; consider my groaning .
verses.meaning: bin names God's attentive perceptive attending — the psalmist asks God to consider, to perceive and attend to, his inner groaning.

verses.verse_text: Psa 19:12 Who can discern his errors ? Declare me innocent from hidden faults.
verses.meaning: bin names the inner perceptive faculty directed toward self — the question expresses the limits of self-understanding regarding hidden faults.

verses.verse_text: Psa 28:5 Because they do not regard the works of the Lord or the work of his hands , he will tear them down and build them up no more.
verses.meaning: bin names the failure to perceive and attend to — not regarding is the absence of the inner faculty applied to God's works.

verses.verse_text: Psa 32:9 Be not like a horse or a mule , without understanding , which must be curbed with bit and bridle , or it will not stay near you .
verses.meaning: bin names understanding as the faculty that distinguishes the human person from an animal — it enables voluntary, responsive following rather than coercion.

verses.verse_text: Psa 33:15 he who fashions the hearts of them all and observes all their deeds .
verses.meaning: bin names God's perceptive observing — his inner comprehension of human deeds as well as hearts.

verses.verse_text: Psa 37:10 In just a little while , the wicked will be no more ; though you look carefully at his place , he will not be there.
verses.meaning: bin names the careful perceptive looking — the faculty of attentive discernment applied to searching for the wicked.

verses.verse_text: Psa 49:20 Man in his pomp yet without understanding is like the beasts that perish .
verses.meaning: bin names the inner perceptive faculty whose absence equates the human person to animals — without understanding, status and pomp count for nothing.

verses.verse_text: Psa 50:22 “ Mark this , then , you who forget God , lest I tear you apart , and there be none to deliver !
verses.meaning: bin names the perceptive attending being commanded — to mark is to perceive and take heed.

verses.verse_text: Psa 58:9 Sooner than your pots can feel the heat of thorns , whether green or ablaze , may he sweep them away !
verses.meaning: Names the capacity to perceive or distinguish — the term appears in a simile describing the speed of divine judgment, likened to the moment before a pot can register the heat of thorns.

verses.verse_text: Psa 73:17 until I went into the sanctuary of God ; then I discerned their end .
verses.meaning: bin names the inner perceptive act enabled by the sanctuary encounter — the psalmist could not understand the prosperity of the wicked until God gave him perception of their end.

verses.verse_text: Psa 82:5 They have neither knowledge nor understanding , they walk about in darkness ; all the foundations of the earth are shaken .
verses.meaning: bin names the absence of the inner perceptive faculty — without understanding, the result is moral and directional darkness.

verses.verse_text: Psa 92:6 The stupid man cannot know ; the fool cannot understand this :
verses.meaning: bin names the perceptive faculty the fool lacks — the capacity to perceive the significance of God's acts.

verses.verse_text: Psa 94:7 and they say , “The Lord does not see ; the God of Jacob does not perceive .”
verses.meaning: bin names the perceptive faculty being denied to God by the wicked — their claim that God does not perceive their actions.

verses.verse_text: Psa 107:43 Whoever is wise , let him attend to these things; let them consider the steadfast love of the Lord .
verses.meaning: bin names the perceptive reflective act directed toward God's love — the wise person uses their inner faculty to perceive and attend to what God has done.

verses.verse_text: Psa 119:34 Give me understanding , that I may keep your law and observe it with my whole heart .
verses.meaning: bin names understanding as what enables covenant keeping — sought from God, enabling the whole-heart observance.

verses.verse_text: Yodh Psa 119:73 Your hands have made and fashioned me; give me understanding that I may learn your commandments .
verses.meaning: bin names understanding as what enables learning — petitioned from God, making instruction receivable.

verses.verse_text: Psa 119:95 The wicked lie in wait to destroy me, but I consider your testimonies .
verses.meaning: bin names the reflective perceptive act directed toward God's testimonies — while the wicked wait to destroy, the psalmist's inner faculty is on the testimonies.

verses.verse_text: Psa 119:100 I understand more than the aged , for I keep your precepts .
verses.meaning: bin names the inner understanding produced by keeping the precepts — covenant faithfulness produces perceptive understanding.

verses.verse_text: Psa 119:104 Through your precepts I get understanding ; therefore I hate every false way .
verses.meaning: bin names understanding as the fruit of the precepts — received through engagement with God's word.

verses.verse_text: Psa 119:125 I am your servant ; give me understanding , that I may know your testimonies !
verses.meaning: bin names the inner faculty sought from God — without it the testimonies cannot be truly known.

verses.verse_text: Psa 119:130 The unfolding of your words gives light ; it imparts understanding to the simple .
verses.meaning: bin names understanding as what God's word imparts — the inner perceptive faculty opened by the word, given even to the simple.

verses.verse_text: Psa 119:144 Your testimonies are righteous forever ; give me understanding that I may live .
verses.meaning: bin names understanding as life-giving — the inner faculty sought from God as the condition of living.

verses.verse_text: Taw Psa 119:169 Let my cry come before you, O Lord ; give me understanding according to your word !
verses.meaning: bin names the understanding that comes through the word — petitioned from God as the means of the word taking effect within.

verses.verse_text: Psa 139:2 You know when I sit down and when I rise up ; you discern my thoughts from afar .
verses.meaning: bin names God's perceptive comprehension of the inner person — he discerns thoughts, the inner content of the person, from any distance.

verses.verse_text: Pro 1:2 To know wisdom and instruction , to understand words of insight ,
verses.meaning: bin names the inner perceptive act that the book of Proverbs aims to produce — grasping insight as the goal of the collection.

verses.verse_text: Pro 1:5 Let the wise hear and increase in learning , and the one who understands obtain guidance ,
verses.meaning: bin names the inner perceptive faculty that makes the person receptive to guidance — understanding is the condition for receiving.

verses.verse_text: Pro 1:6 to understand a proverb and a saying , the words of the wise and their riddles .
verses.meaning: bin names the perceptive faculty required to grasp proverbs and riddles — understanding is the inner key to the wisdom literature.

verses.verse_text: Pro 2:5 then you will understand the fear of the Lord and find the knowledge of God .
verses.meaning: bin names the inner perceptive act that seeking wisdom produces — understanding the fear of the Lord is a result of the search.

verses.verse_text: Pro 2:9 Then you will understand righteousness and justice and equity , every good path ;
verses.meaning: bin names the inner perceptive faculty applied to moral categories — understanding righteousness, justice, and equity as the fruit of seeking wisdom.

verses.verse_text: Pro 7:7 and I have seen among the simple , I have perceived among the youths , a young man lacking sense ,
verses.meaning: bin names the inner perceptive act of the teacher — seeing and understanding what he observes among the young.

verses.verse_text: Pro 8:5 O simple ones , learn prudence ; O fools , learn sense .
verses.meaning: bin names the inner perceptive capacity being called for — learning sense is the acquisition of the understanding faculty.

verses.verse_text: Pro 8:9 They are all straight to him who understands , and right to those who find knowledge .
verses.meaning: bin names the inner faculty that makes wisdom's words accessible — the one who understands finds them straightforward.

verses.verse_text: Pro 10:13 On the lips of him who has understanding , wisdom is found , but a rod is for the back of him who lacks sense .
verses.meaning: bin names the inner perceptive faculty that produces wisdom in speech — understanding within finds expression outwardly.

verses.verse_text: Pro 14:6 A scoffer seeks wisdom in vain , but knowledge is easy for a man of understanding .
verses.meaning: bin names the inner perceptive faculty that makes knowledge accessible — the person of understanding receives knowledge readily.

verses.verse_text: Pro 14:33 Wisdom rests in the heart of a man of understanding , but it makes itself known even in the midst of fools .
verses.meaning: bin names the person of understanding as the dwelling place of wisdom — the inner faculty as the home of wisdom.

verses.verse_text: Pro 15:14 The heart of him who has understanding seeks knowledge , but the mouths of fools feed on folly .
verses.meaning: bin names understanding as what the heart holds — the inner faculty that actively seeks further knowledge.

verses.verse_text: Pro 16:21 The wise of heart is called discerning , and sweetness of speech increases persuasiveness .
verses.meaning: bin names discernment as what others recognise in the wise of heart — the inner perceptive faculty visible in the person's character and conduct.

verses.verse_text: Pro 17:10 A rebuke goes deeper into a man of understanding than a hundred blows into a fool .
verses.meaning: bin names the inner perceptive faculty as what makes the person responsive to correction — the person of understanding receives rebuke and it penetrates.

verses.verse_text: Pro 17:24 The discerning sets his face toward wisdom , but the eyes of a fool are on the ends of the earth .
verses.meaning: bin names the inner perceptive faculty oriented toward its proper object — the discerning person directs their understanding toward wisdom.

verses.verse_text: Pro 17:28 Even a fool who keeps silent is considered wise ; when he closes his lips , he is deemed intelligent .
verses.meaning: bin names intelligence as what silence simulates — the appearance of the inner perceptive faculty through restraint of speech.

verses.verse_text: Pro 18:15 An intelligent heart acquires knowledge , and the ear of the wise seeks knowledge .
verses.meaning: bin names intelligence as the inner quality of the heart that actively acquires knowledge — the perceptive faculty is acquisitive.

verses.verse_text: Pro 19:25 Strike a scoffer , and the simple will learn prudence ; reprove a man of understanding , and he will gain knowledge .
verses.meaning: bin names the inner perceptive faculty as what makes correction productive — the person of understanding gains from rebuke.

verses.verse_text: Pro 20:24 A man’s steps are from the Lord ; how then can man understand his way ?
verses.meaning: bin names the inner faculty confronted with its own limits — human steps are from the Lord; the person cannot fully understand their own path.

verses.verse_text: Pro 21:29 A wicked man puts on a bold face , but the upright gives thought to his ways .
verses.meaning: bin names the reflective perceptive attending to one's own conduct — the inner faculty used for self-examination and direction.

verses.verse_text: Pro 23:1 When you sit down to eat with a ruler , observe carefully what is before you,
verses.meaning: bin names the careful perceptive attending to one's social situation — wisdom in a powerful person's presence.

verses.verse_text: Pro 24:12 If you say , “ Behold , we did not know this ,” does not he who weighs the heart perceive it? Does not he who keeps watch over your soul know it, and will he not repay man according to his work ?
verses.meaning: bin names God's perceptive comprehension of the heart — he perceives what the person claims not to know.

verses.verse_text: Pro 28:2 When a land transgresses , it has many rulers , but with a man of understanding and knowledge , its stability will long continue .
verses.meaning: bin names understanding as the inner quality that produces stable governance — the person of understanding sustains what others cannot.

verses.verse_text: Pro 28:7 The one who keeps the law is a son with understanding , but a companion of gluttons shames his father .
verses.meaning: bin names understanding as what covenant-keeping constitutes — the law-keeper is a son of understanding.

verses.verse_text: Pro 28:11 A rich man is wise in his own eyes , but a poor man who has understanding will find him out .
verses.meaning: bin names the inner perceptive faculty of the poor person that enables them to see through the rich man's pretension.

verses.verse_text: Pro 29:7 A righteous man knows the rights of the poor ; a wicked man does not understand such knowledge .
verses.meaning: bin names the inner perceptive faculty blocked by wickedness — the wicked person cannot perceive the rights of the poor.

verses.verse_text: Pro 29:19 By mere words a servant is not disciplined , for though he understands , he will not respond .
verses.meaning: bin names the inner perceptive faculty present but unproductive — the servant comprehends but will not comply. Understanding without response.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 9:11 Again I saw that under the sun the race is not to the swift , nor the battle to the strong , nor bread to the wise , nor riches to the intelligent , nor favor to those with knowledge , but time and chance happen to them all .
verses.meaning: bin names intelligence as an inner quality that does not guarantee material outcome — time and chance equalise.

verses.verse_text: Isa 1:3 The ox knows its owner , and the donkey its master’s crib , but Israel does not know , my people do not understand .”
verses.meaning: bin names the absent inner perceptive faculty of Israel — the ox and donkey recognise their master, but Israel does not perceive God.

verses.verse_text: Isa 5:21 Woe to those who are wise in their own eyes , and shrewd in their own sight !
verses.meaning: bin names self-assessed shrewdness — the inner perceptive faculty claimed as self-sourced rather than received.

verses.verse_text: Isa 6:9 And he said , “ Go , and say to this people : “‘Keep on hearing , but do not understand ; keep on seeing , but do not perceive .’
verses.meaning: bin names the inner perceptive faculty that will not be activated — a judicial withholding of understanding from those who will not receive.

verses.verse_text: Isa 6:10 Make the heart of this people dull , and their ears heavy , and blind their eyes ; lest they see with their eyes , and hear with their ears , and understand with their hearts , and turn and be healed .”
verses.meaning: bin names the perceptive reception of divine truth in the heart — the faculty whose activation would lead to turning and healing. God's judgment holds it closed.

verses.verse_text: Isa 10:13 For he says : “ By the strength of my hand I have done it, and by my wisdom , for I have understanding ; I remove the boundaries of peoples , and plunder their treasures; like a bull I bring down those who sit on thrones.
verses.meaning: bin names the inner perceptive faculty that the Assyrian king claims as his own — self-attributed understanding as the ground of his arrogance.

verses.verse_text: Isa 14:16 Those who see you will stare at you and ponder over you: ‘Is this the man who made the earth tremble , who shook kingdoms ,
verses.meaning: bin names the reflective perceptive act of those who see the fallen tyrant — they stare and try to grasp how this could have happened.

verses.verse_text: Isa 28:19 As often as it passes through it will take you; for morning by morning it will pass through , by day and by night ; and it will be sheer terror to understand the message .
verses.meaning: bin names the inner perceptive act of grasping divine judgment — understanding the message is not comfort but terror. The faculty here receives something overwhelming.

verses.verse_text: Isa 29:14 therefore , behold , I will again do wonderful things with this people , with wonder upon wonder ; and the wisdom of their wise men shall perish , and the discernment of their discerning men shall be hidden .”
verses.meaning: bin names the inner perceptive faculty of the discerning — what God will hide is not the faculty but its operation.

verses.verse_text: Isa 32:4 The heart of the hasty will understand and know , and the tongue of the stammerers will hasten to speak distinctly .
verses.meaning: bin names understanding as what the heart of the hasty will attain in the coming renewal — the inner perceptive faculty opened in a future restored state.

verses.verse_text: Isa 40:14 Whom did he consult , and who made him understand ? Who taught him the path of justice , and taught him knowledge , and showed him the way of understanding ?
verses.meaning: bin names the inner perceptive faculty no one gave God — the rhetorical question asserts God's self-sufficient understanding.

verses.verse_text: Isa 43:18 “ Remember not the former things , nor consider the things of old .
verses.meaning: bin names the reflective perceptive attending that God here redirects — do not fix your inner attention on the former things.

verses.verse_text: Isa 52:15 so shall he sprinkle many nations ; kings shall shut their mouths because of him, for that which has not been told them they see , and that which they have not heard they understand .
verses.meaning: bin names the inner perceptive reception of new revelation — nations who have never heard will understand when the Servant is revealed.

verses.verse_text: Isa 56:11 The dogs have a mighty appetite ; they never have enough . But they are shepherds who have no understanding ; they have all turned to their own way , each to his own gain , one and all .
verses.meaning: bin names the absent inner perceptive faculty of the false shepherds — they cannot perceive beyond their own appetite.

verses.verse_text: Isa 57:1 The righteous man perishes , and no one lays it to heart ; devout men are taken away , while no one understands . For the righteous man is taken away from calamity ;
verses.meaning: bin names the failed perceptive faculty of the community — no one perceives that the righteous person's death is a mercy.

verses.verse_text: Jer 2:10 For cross to the coasts of Cyprus and see , or send to Kedar and examine with care ; see if there has been such a thing .
verses.meaning: bin names the careful perceptive examination being commanded — attentive discernment applied to historical comparison.
