verses.verse_text: Jer 4:22 “ For my people are foolish ; they know me not ; they are stupid children ; they have no understanding . They are ‘ wise ’—in doing evil ! But how to do good they know not .”
verses.meaning: bin names the absent inner perceptive faculty of Israel — they are skilled in evil but cannot perceive how to do good.

verses.verse_text: Jer 9:12 Who is the man so wise that he can understand this ? To whom has the mouth of the Lord spoken , that he may declare it? Why is the land ruined and laid waste like a wilderness , so that no one passes through ?
verses.meaning: bin names the inner perceptive faculty needed to grasp divine judgment — the question implies it is absent.

verses.verse_text: Jer 23:20 The anger of the Lord will not turn back until he has executed and accomplished the intents of his heart . In the latter days you will understand it clearly .
verses.meaning: bin names the future inner perceptive comprehension of God's purposes — what cannot be grasped now will be understood then.

verses.verse_text: Jer 30:24 The fierce anger of the Lord will not turn back until he has executed and accomplished the intentions of his mind . In the latter days you will understand this .
verses.meaning: bin names the future inner perceptive comprehension — eschatological understanding of God's fierce anger and its purpose.

verses.verse_text: Jer 49:7 Concerning Edom . Thus says the Lord of hosts : “Is wisdom no more in Teman ? Has counsel perished from the prudent ? Has their wisdom vanished ?
verses.meaning: bin names the inner perceptive quality of the prudent as the source of counsel — prudence and counsel are linked. Edom is famous for this wisdom; now it will be gone.

verses.verse_text: Dan 1:17 As for these four youths , God gave them learning and skill in all literature and wisdom , and Daniel had understanding in all visions and dreams .
verses.meaning: bin names the specific inner perceptive capacity given to Daniel — not general understanding but perceptive insight into revelatory visions. God-given.

verses.verse_text: Dan 8:5 As I was considering , behold , a male goat came from the west across the face of the whole earth , without touching the ground . And the goat had a conspicuous horn between his eyes .
verses.meaning: bin names the reflective perceptive attending — Daniel considering the vision when the next element of it appears.

verses.verse_text: Dan 8:16 And I heard a man’s voice between the banks of the Ulai , and it called , “ Gabriel , make this man understand the vision .”
verses.meaning: bin names the inner perceptive comprehension Gabriel is sent to produce in Daniel — the vision must be made intelligible to him.

verses.verse_text: Dan 8:17 So he came near where I stood . And when he came , I was frightened and fell on my face . But he said to me, “ Understand , O son of man , that the vision is for the time of the end .”
verses.meaning: bin names the inner comprehension Gabriel calls Daniel to — the command to receive and grasp the vision's significance.

verses.verse_text: Dan 8:23 And at the latter end of their kingdom , when the transgressors have reached their limit , a king of bold face , one who understands riddles , shall arise .
verses.meaning: bin names the inner perceptive capacity of the coming king — understanding riddles as an attribute of the dangerous figure who arises.

verses.verse_text: Dan 8:27 And I , Daniel , was overcome and lay sick for some days . Then I rose and went about the king’s business , but I was appalled by the vision and did not understand it.
verses.meaning: bin names the failed inner comprehension — Daniel could not grasp the meaning of what he had seen.

verses.verse_text: Dan 9:2 in the first year of his reign , I , Daniel , perceived in the books the number of years that , according to the word of the Lord to Jeremiah the prophet , must pass before the end of the desolations of Jerusalem , namely, seventy years .
verses.meaning: bin names the inner perceptive act of reading — Daniel grasps the significance of Jeremiah's prophecy from the text.

verses.verse_text: Dan 9:22 He made me understand , speaking with me and saying , “O Daniel , I have now come out to give you insight and understanding .
verses.meaning: bin names the understanding Gabriel brings to Daniel — a gift of inner comprehension of the vision delivered by the messenger.

verses.verse_text: Dan 9:23 At the beginning of your pleas for mercy a word went out , and I have come to tell it to you, for you are greatly loved . Therefore consider the word and understand the vision .
verses.meaning: bin names the reflective perceptive attending Daniel is commanded to give — the inner faculty directed toward the word and the vision.

verses.verse_text: Dan 10:1 In the third year of Cyrus king of Persia a word was revealed to Daniel , who was named Belteshazzar . And the word was true , and it was a great conflict . And he understood the word and had understanding of the vision .
verses.meaning: bin names the inner comprehension Daniel reaches — the perceptive grasp of both the word and its visionary content.

verses.verse_text: Dan 10:11 And he said to me, “O Daniel , man greatly loved , understand the words that I speak to you, and stand upright , for now I have been sent to you.” And when he had spoken this word to me, I stood up trembling .
verses.meaning: bin names the inner perceptive reception being commanded — the messenger calls Daniel to grasp what he is about to say.

verses.verse_text: Dan 10:12 Then he said to me, “ Fear not , Daniel , for from the first day that you set your heart to understand and humbled yourself before your God , your words have been heard , and I have come because of your words .
verses.meaning: bin names the deliberate orientation of the inner faculty — Daniel set his heart toward understanding as a posture of seeking.

verses.verse_text: Dan 10:14 and came to make you understand what is to happen to your people in the latter days . For the vision is for days yet to come.”
verses.meaning: bin names the inner comprehension Gabriel's mission is to produce — what happens to Daniel's people in the latter days must be understood.

verses.verse_text: Dan 11:30 For ships of Kittim shall come against him, and he shall be afraid and withdraw, and shall turn back and be enraged and take action against the holy covenant . He shall turn back and pay attention to those who forsake the holy covenant .
verses.meaning: bin names directional attending — the king turns his perceptive attention toward the apostates. This is perceptive attention rather than understanding as comprehension.

verses.verse_text: Dan 11:37 He shall pay no attention to the gods of his fathers , or to the one beloved by women . He shall not pay attention to any other god , for he shall magnify himself above all .
verses.meaning: bin names the deliberate withdrawal of perceptive attention — the king refuses to direct his understanding toward any god.

verses.verse_text: Dan 12:8 I heard , but I did not understand . Then I said , “O my lord , what shall be the outcome of these things?”
verses.meaning: bin names the failed inner comprehension — Daniel heard the words but could not grasp their meaning.

verses.verse_text: Dan 12:10 Many shall purify themselves and make themselves white and be refined , but the wicked shall act wickedly . And none of the wicked shall understand , but those who are wise shall understand .
verses.meaning: bin names the inner perceptive faculty as morally conditioned — the wicked cannot understand; the wise can.

verses.verse_text: Hos 4:14 I will not punish your daughters when they play the whore , nor your brides when they commit adultery ; for the men themselves go aside with prostitutes and sacrifice with cult prostitutes , and a people without understanding shall come to ruin .
verses.meaning: bin names the absence of the inner perceptive faculty as the cause of ruin — the people cannot perceive the moral reality of what they are doing.

verses.verse_text: Mic 4:12 But they do not know the thoughts of the Lord ; they do not understand his plan , that he has gathered them as sheaves to the threshing floor .
verses.meaning: bin names the inner perceptive faculty blocked from perceiving God's purpose — the nations cannot grasp what God is doing.

verses.verse_text: Deu 4:6 Keep them and do them, for that will be your wisdom and your understanding in the sight of the peoples , who , when they hear all these statutes , will say , ‘ Surely this great nation is a wise and understanding people .’
verses.meaning: bi.nah names understanding as what Israel's covenant faithfulness will display — same verse, different span. Assigned to 816-002.

verses.verse_text: 1Ch 12:32 Of Issachar , men who had understanding of the times , to know what Israel ought to do , 200 chiefs , and all their kinsmen under their command .
verses.meaning: bi.nah names understanding of times as a perceptive inner capacity — the ability to read the situation and know what action is called for.

verses.verse_text: 1Ch 22:12 Only , may the Lord grant you discretion and understanding , that when he gives you charge over Israel you may keep the law of the Lord your God .
verses.meaning: bi.nah names understanding as a God-given inner capacity enabling covenant faithfulness — paired with discretion, oriented toward the law.

verses.verse_text: 2Ch 2:12 Hiram also said , “ Blessed be the Lord God of Israel , who made heaven and earth , who has given King David a wise son , who has discretion and understanding , who will build a temple for the Lord and a royal palace for himself.
verses.meaning: bi.nah names understanding as a divine gift to Solomon — paired with discretion, enabling his task.

verses.verse_text: 2Ch 2:13 “ Now I have sent a skilled man , who has understanding , Huram-abi ,
verses.meaning: bi.nah names understanding as Huram-abi's inner qualification — paired with skill, enabling the work.

verses.verse_text: Job 20:3 I hear censure that insults me, and out of my understanding a spirit answers me .
verses.meaning: bi.nah names the inner perceptive faculty from which Zophar's response arises — understanding as the source of his answer.

verses.verse_text: Job 28:12 “But where shall wisdom be found ? And where is the place of understanding ?
verses.meaning: bi.nah names understanding as something sought but not locatable by human means — the rhetorical question frames understanding as beyond ordinary reach.

verses.verse_text: Job 28:20 “ From where , then, does wisdom come ? And where is the place of understanding ?
verses.meaning: bi.nah names understanding as the object of the same question — its source and location are beyond human searching.

verses.verse_text: Job 28:28 And he said to man , ‘ Behold , the fear of the Lord , that is wisdom , and to turn away from evil is understanding .’”
verses.meaning: bi.nah names understanding as what moral turning away constitutes — understanding is not abstract knowledge but enacted orientation away from evil.

verses.verse_text: Job 34:16 “ If you have understanding , hear this ; listen to what I say .
verses.meaning: bi.nah names understanding as the precondition for receiving what is said — the hearer must have the inner faculty to receive the argument.

verses.verse_text: Job 38:4 “ Where were you when I laid the foundation of the earth ? Tell me, if you have understanding .
verses.meaning: bi.nah names understanding as what the one who could answer God's challenge would need — a condition for grasping the foundations of creation.

verses.verse_text: Job 38:36 Who has put wisdom in the inward parts or given understanding to the mind ?
verses.meaning: bi.nah names understanding as a divine gift placed within the person — God is the one who gives the inner faculty of understanding.

verses.verse_text: Job 39:17 because God has made her forget wisdom and given her no share in understanding .
verses.meaning: bi.nah names understanding as something God withholds from the ostrich — the inner faculty is a divine endowment, not a given. Its absence explains the creature's behaviour.

verses.verse_text: Job 39:26 “Is it by your understanding that the hawk soars and spreads his wings toward the south ?
verses.meaning: bi.nah names understanding as the faculty God challenges Job about — the hawk's instinct is not the product of human understanding.

verses.verse_text: Pro 1:2 To know wisdom and instruction , to understand words of insight ,
verses.meaning: bi.nah names insight as the content that bin seeks to grasp — the inner quality that the words of wisdom carry.

verses.verse_text: Pro 2:3 yes, if you call out for insight and raise your voice for understanding ,
verses.meaning: bi.nah names insight as what is to be urgently sought — the inner faculty petitioned for.

verses.verse_text: Pro 3:5 Trust in the Lord with all your heart , and do not lean on your own understanding .
verses.meaning: bi.nah names the inner perceptive faculty as something the person can either trust in or surrender — leaning on one's own understanding is the alternative to trusting God.

verses.verse_text: Pro 4:1 Hear , O sons , a father’s instruction , and be attentive , that you may gain insight ,
verses.meaning: bi.nah names insight as the goal of attentive listening — the inner capacity that attention to wisdom instruction produces.

verses.verse_text: Pro 4:5 Get wisdom ; get insight ; do not forget , and do not turn away from the words of my mouth .
verses.meaning: bi.nah names insight as one of two things to be acquired above all else — the inner capacity paired with wisdom as the essential possession.

verses.verse_text: Pro 4:7 The beginning of wisdom is this: Get wisdom , and whatever you get , get insight .
verses.meaning: bi.nah names insight as the supreme acquisition — the inner capacity that should accompany wisdom as the primary pursuit.

verses.verse_text: Pro 7:4 Say to wisdom , “You are my sister ,” and call insight your intimate friend ,
verses.meaning: bi.nah names insight as a relational companion — the inner perceptive faculty personified as intimacy.

verses.verse_text: Pro 8:14 I have counsel and sound wisdom ; I have insight ; I have strength .
verses.meaning: bi.nah names insight as one of Wisdom's own possessions — an inner quality she holds and offers.

verses.verse_text: Pro 9:6 Leave your simple ways , and live , and walk in the way of insight .”
verses.meaning: bi.nah names the path of insight as the way of life — the inner capacity expressed as a direction of travel.

verses.verse_text: Pro 9:10 The fear of the Lord is the beginning of wisdom , and the knowledge of the Holy One is insight .
verses.meaning: bi.nah names insight as what knowing the Holy One is — the knowledge of God is not merely cognitive but the inner perceptive capacity itself.

verses.verse_text: Pro 16:16 How much better to get wisdom than gold ! To get understanding is to be chosen rather than silver .
verses.meaning: bi.nah names understanding as more valuable than silver — the inner capacity worth more than material wealth.

verses.verse_text: Pro 23:4 Do not toil to acquire wealth ; be discerning enough to desist .
verses.meaning: bi.nah names discernment as the inner perceptive quality that enables restraint — seeing clearly enough to know when to stop.

verses.verse_text: Pro 23:23 Buy truth , and do not sell it; buy wisdom , instruction , and understanding .
verses.meaning: bi.nah names understanding as one of three things to be acquired — an inner capacity worth purchasing and not selling.

verses.verse_text: Pro 30:2 Surely I am too stupid to be a man . I have not the understanding of a man .
verses.meaning: bi.nah names the inner perceptive faculty that Agur disclaims — a confession of his own inadequacy before the mysteries.

verses.verse_text: Isa 11:2 And the Spirit of the Lord shall rest upon him, the Spirit of wisdom and understanding , the Spirit of counsel and might , the Spirit of knowledge and the fear of the Lord .
verses.meaning: bi.nah names understanding as one of the Spirit's endowments on the Messiah — a Spirit-given inner capacity alongside wisdom, counsel, might, and knowledge.

verses.verse_text: Isa 27:11 When its boughs are dry , they are broken ; women come and make a fire of them. For this is a people without discernment ; therefore he who made them will not have compassion on them; he who formed them will show them no favor .
verses.meaning: bi.nah names discernment as the inner perceptive faculty whose absence results in divine withdrawal of compassion — the link between inner capacity and covenant relationship.

verses.verse_text: Isa 29:14 therefore , behold , I will again do wonderful things with this people , with wonder upon wonder ; and the wisdom of their wise men shall perish , and the discernment of their discerning men shall be hidden .”
verses.meaning: bi.nah names discernment as what God hides — the inner capacity is present but its access to truth is blocked by God's action.

verses.verse_text: Isa 29:24 And those who go astray in spirit will come to understanding , and those who murmur will accept instruction .”
verses.meaning: bi.nah names understanding as the destination of restoration — those who erred will arrive at the inner perceptive faculty. Understanding is the fruit of turning back.

verses.verse_text: Isa 33:19 You will see no more the insolent people , the people of an obscure speech that you cannot comprehend , stammering in a tongue that you cannot understand .
verses.meaning: bi.nah names the perceptive faculty confronted with unintelligible speech — the inner capacity to grasp meaning, here applied to language comprehension.

verses.verse_text: Jer 23:20 The anger of the Lord will not turn back until he has executed and accomplished the intents of his heart . In the latter days you will understand it clearly .
verses.meaning: bi.nah names the clarity of future understanding — the quality of the perceptive grasp that will come.

verses.verse_text: Dan 1:20 And in every matter of wisdom and understanding about which the king inquired of them, he found them ten times better than all the magicians and enchanters that were in all his kingdom .
verses.meaning: bi.nah names understanding as one of the two inner qualities in which the four youths surpassed the court experts — a tested and verified inner capacity.

verses.verse_text: Dan 8:15 When I , Daniel , had seen the vision , I sought to understand it . And behold , there stood before me one having the appearance of a man .
verses.meaning: bi.nah names the object of Daniel's seeking — he wants to grasp the meaning of what he has seen.

verses.verse_text: Dan 9:22 He made me understand , speaking with me and saying , “O Daniel , I have now come out to give you insight and understanding .
verses.meaning: bi.nah names the understanding conveyed — the inner capacity to grasp the revelation given by Gabriel.

verses.verse_text: Dan 10:1 In the third year of Cyrus king of Persia a word was revealed to Daniel , who was named Belteshazzar . And the word was true , and it was a great conflict . And he understood the word and had understanding of the vision .
verses.meaning: bi.nah names understanding of the vision as what Daniel possesses — same verse, bi.nah as the noun form of the inner capacity attained.

verses.verse_text: Exo 31:3 and I have filled him with the Spirit of God , with ability and intelligence , with knowledge and all craftsmanship ,
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as one of the Spirit-given inner endowments for sacred craft — an inner capacity given by God alongside ability, intelligence, and craftsmanship. Note: assigned to 1838-001 rather than 955-003 — this appears to be a misassignment; the verse is the same as Exo 35:31 which is correctly in 955-003.

verses.verse_text: Exo 35:31 and he has filled him with the Spirit of God , with skill , with intelligence , with knowledge , and with all craftsmanship ,
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as a Spirit-given inner endowment — God bestows it from above for the sacred task.

verses.verse_text: 1Ki 7:14 He was the son of a widow of the tribe of Naphtali , and his father was a man of Tyre , a worker in bronze . And he was full of wisdom , understanding , and skill for making any work in bronze . He came to King Solomon and did all his work .
verses.meaning: Names the capacity of deep understanding and skill — the term identifies the quality of inner competence and technical mastery that filled Hiram for his work.

verses.verse_text: Gen 41:33 Now therefore let Pharaoh select a discerning and wise man , and set him over the land of Egypt .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the inner quality being sought as qualification for appointment. Wisdom and discernment are paired as what the person must possess to govern.

verses.verse_text: Pro 17:28 Even a fool who keeps silent is considered wise ; when he closes his lips , he is deemed intelligent .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names wisdom as a quality that silence can simulate — the appearance of wisdom without its substance. The verse observes that wisdom is judged by restraint of speech.

verses.verse_text: Isa 29:14 therefore , behold , I will again do wonderful things with this people , with wonder upon wonder ; and the wisdom of their wise men shall perish , and the discernment of their discerning men shall be hidden .”
verses.meaning: Names the class whose wisdom will be destroyed — the term identifies the wise as those whose inner capacity for understanding is specifically targeted by God's wonderful work.

verses.verse_text: Job 11:11 For he knows worthless men ; when he sees iniquity , will he not consider it?
verses.meaning: ya.da names God's comprehensive moral knowledge of persons — he sees and knows iniquity.

verses.verse_text: Job 14:21 His sons come to honor , and he does not know it; they are brought low , and he perceives it not .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the knowledge the dead lack — the person in Sheol cannot know what happens to those they leave behind.

verses.verse_text: Job 28:23 “ God understands the way to it, and he knows its place .
verses.meaning: ya.da names God's comprehensive knowledge of wisdom's location — only God knows where it is found.

verses.verse_text: Job 38:4 “ Where were you when I laid the foundation of the earth ? Tell me, if you have understanding .
verses.meaning: Names the inner capacity required by the divine challenge — the term identifies understanding as the prerequisite for answering the question of where Job was at creation.

verses.verse_text: Job 38:18 Have you comprehended the expanse of the earth ? Declare , if you know all this.
verses.meaning: Names the inner comprehension the challenge exposes as absent — the term identifies comprehending the expanse of the earth as the knowledge required to answer.

verses.verse_text: Pro 1:2 To know wisdom and instruction , to understand words of insight ,
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal knowing that Proverbs aims to produce — knowing wisdom is not merely information but inner orientation.

verses.verse_text: Pro 4:1 Hear , O sons , a father’s instruction , and be attentive , that you may gain insight ,
verses.meaning: ya.da names the knowing that comes from attentiveness — receiving what the father offers through focused receptivity.

verses.verse_text: Pro 14:33 Wisdom rests in the heart of a man of understanding , but it makes itself known even in the midst of fools .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the visibility of wisdom — even among fools, wisdom makes itself known, though the fools cannot receive it.

verses.verse_text: Pro 24:12 If you say , “ Behold , we did not know this ,” does not he who weighs the heart perceive it? Does not he who keeps watch over your soul know it, and will he not repay man according to his work ?
verses.meaning: ya.da names God's comprehensive knowing of the inner person — the soul-keeper knows what the person claims not to know.

verses.verse_text: Pro 28:2 When a land transgresses , it has many rulers , but with a man of understanding and knowledge , its stability will long continue .
verses.meaning: ya.da names moral knowledge as qualification for good governance — the inner capacity that produces stable rule.

verses.verse_text: Isa 6:9 And he said , “ Go , and say to this people : “‘Keep on hearing , but do not understand ; keep on seeing , but do not perceive .’
verses.meaning: ya.da names the judicial withholding of moral knowing — the people will see but their inner perception of meaning will not be activated.

verses.verse_text: Isa 56:11 The dogs have a mighty appetite ; they never have enough . But they are shepherds who have no understanding ; they have all turned to their own way , each to his own gain , one and all .
verses.meaning: Names the absence of inner comprehension — the term identifies the shepherds as those who have no understanding, each turned to their own gain.

verses.verse_text: Mic 4:12 But they do not know the thoughts of the Lord ; they do not understand his plan , that he has gathered them as sheaves to the threshing floor .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the absent covenantal knowledge of God's purposes — the nations cannot perceive what God is doing.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 8:1 Who is like the wise ? And who knows the interpretation of a thing ? A man’s wisdom makes his face shine , and the hardness of his face is changed .
verses.meaning: pe.sher names the interpretive capacity as an expression of inner wisdom — knowing the interpretation of a thing is the mark of the wise person whose wisdom even transforms their outward bearing. The inner wisdom is so thoroughgoing that it reshapes the face.

verses.verse_text: Exo 31:3 and I have filled him with the Spirit of God , with ability and intelligence , with knowledge and all craftsmanship ,
verses.meaning: te.vu.nah names intelligence as a Spirit-given inner endowment alongside knowledge and craftsmanship — all God-placed for sacred work.

verses.verse_text: Exo 35:31 and he has filled him with the Spirit of God , with skill , with intelligence , with knowledge , and with all craftsmanship ,
verses.meaning: te.vu.nah names intelligence as part of the Spirit-given cluster of inner capacities for sacred construction.

verses.verse_text: Exo 36:1 “ Bezalel and Oholiab and every craftsman in whom the Lord has put skill and intelligence to know how to do any work in the construction of the sanctuary shall work in accordance with all that the Lord has commanded .”
verses.meaning: te.vu.nah names intelligence as a God-placed inner capacity enabling the craftsman to know how to execute the sacred work.

verses.verse_text: Deu 32:28 “ For they are a nation void of counsel , and there is no understanding in them.
verses.meaning: te.vu.nah names understanding as what is absent in the nation — the inner capacity for perceiving rightly, without which counsel fails.

verses.verse_text: 1Ki 4:29 And God gave Solomon wisdom and understanding beyond measure , and breadth of mind like the sand on the seashore ,
verses.meaning: te.vu.nah names understanding as a divine gift of immeasurable scope — an inner capacity of breadth and depth given by God.

verses.verse_text: 1Ki 7:14 He was the son of a widow of the tribe of Naphtali , and his father was a man of Tyre , a worker in bronze . And he was full of wisdom , understanding , and skill for making any work in bronze . He came to King Solomon and did all his work .
verses.meaning: te.vu.nah names understanding as one of three inner qualities of Hiram — alongside wisdom and skill, enabling his craft.

verses.verse_text: Job 12:12 Wisdom is with the aged , and understanding in length of days .
verses.meaning: te.vu.nah names understanding as something that grows with age and experience — an inner capacity deepened by time.

verses.verse_text: Job 12:13 “With God are wisdom and might ; he has counsel and understanding .
verses.meaning: te.vu.nah names understanding as a divine attribute — belonging to God alongside wisdom, might, and counsel.

verses.verse_text: Job 26:12 By his power he stilled the sea ; by his understanding he shattered Rahab .
verses.meaning: te.vu.nah names understanding as a divine creative-governing attribute — by which God orders and subdues chaos.

verses.verse_text: Job 32:11 “ Behold , I waited for your words , I listened for your wise sayings , while you searched out what to say.
verses.meaning: te.vu.nah names wise sayings as the expression of the inner understanding faculty — the words that understanding produces.

verses.verse_text: Psa 78:72 With upright heart he shepherded them and guided them with his skillful hand .
verses.meaning: te.vu.nah names skillful guidance as an expression of the inner understanding faculty — David's shepherding expressed in skilled, understanding-guided action.

verses.verse_text: Psa 136:5 to him who by understanding made the heavens , for his steadfast love endures forever ;
verses.meaning: te.vu.nah names understanding as the divine attribute by which creation is accomplished — an inner quality of God expressed in creative act.

verses.verse_text: Psa 147:5 Great is our Lord , and abundant in power ; his understanding is beyond measure .
verses.meaning: te.vu.nah names understanding as an infinite divine attribute — belonging to God without limit.

verses.verse_text: Pro 2:2 making your ear attentive to wisdom and inclining your heart to understanding ;
verses.meaning: te.vu.nah names understanding as what the heart is to be directed toward — the inner faculty as the object of deliberate orientation.

verses.verse_text: Pro 2:3 yes, if you call out for insight and raise your voice for understanding ,
verses.meaning: te.vu.nah names understanding as the object of urgent petition — the inner capacity worth crying out for.

verses.verse_text: Pro 2:6 For the Lord gives wisdom ; from his mouth come knowledge and understanding ;
verses.meaning: te.vu.nah names understanding as what comes from God's mouth — a divine gift transmitted through his word.

verses.verse_text: Pro 2:11 discretion will watch over you, understanding will guard you,
verses.meaning: te.vu.nah names understanding as a protective inner quality — it watches over the person who has it.

verses.verse_text: Pro 3:13 Blessed is the one who finds wisdom , and the one who gets understanding ,
verses.meaning: te.vu.nah names understanding as a blessing — an inner capacity worth acquiring above silver.

verses.verse_text: Pro 3:19 The Lord by wisdom founded the earth ; by understanding he established the heavens ;
verses.meaning: te.vu.nah names understanding as a divine creative attribute — by which God orders the cosmos.

verses.verse_text: Pro 5:1 My son , be attentive to my wisdom ; incline your ear to my understanding ,
verses.meaning: te.vu.nah names understanding as what the teacher offers and the learner receives — the inner capacity transmitted through instruction.

verses.verse_text: Pro 8:1 Does not wisdom call ? Does not understanding raise her voice ?
verses.meaning: te.vu.nah names understanding personified as Wisdom's companion — calling out publicly alongside wisdom.

verses.verse_text: Pro 10:23 Doing wrong is like a joke to a fool , but wisdom is pleasure to a man of understanding .
verses.meaning: te.vu.nah names understanding as the inner quality that delights in wisdom — the faculty and its object are matched.

verses.verse_text: Pro 11:12 Whoever belittles his neighbor lacks sense , but a man of understanding remains silent .
verses.meaning: te.vu.nah names understanding as the inner quality that produces wise restraint of speech — the person who understands knows when not to speak.

verses.verse_text: Pro 14:29 Whoever is slow to anger has great understanding , but he who has a hasty temper exalts folly .
verses.meaning: te.vu.nah names understanding as the inner quality that produces emotional regulation — great understanding expressed in patience.

verses.verse_text: Pro 15:21 Folly is a joy to him who lacks sense , but a man of understanding walks straight ahead .
verses.meaning: te.vu.nah names understanding as the inner quality that produces directional clarity in conduct — the person of understanding moves straight without distraction.

verses.verse_text: Pro 17:27 Whoever restrains his words has knowledge , and he who has a cool spirit is a man of understanding .
verses.meaning: te.vu.nah names understanding as the inner quality expressed in emotional composure — the cool spirit is the outward sign of inner understanding.

verses.verse_text: Pro 18:2 A fool takes no pleasure in understanding , but only in expressing his opinion .
verses.meaning: te.vu.nah names understanding as what the fool refuses — the inner capacity the fool will not pursue or receive.

verses.verse_text: Pro 19:8 Whoever gets sense loves his own soul ; he who keeps understanding will discover good .
verses.meaning: te.vu.nah names understanding as something to be kept — the inner capacity retained and guarded, producing good as its fruit.

verses.verse_text: Pro 20:5 The purpose in a man’s heart is like deep water , but a man of understanding will draw it out .
verses.meaning: te.vu.nah names understanding as the inner capacity that can draw out the deep purposes of another person's heart.

verses.verse_text: Pro 21:30 No wisdom , no understanding , no counsel can avail against the Lord .
verses.meaning: te.vu.nah names understanding as a human capacity that cannot stand against God — even inner understanding is subject to divine sovereignty.

verses.verse_text: Pro 24:3 By wisdom a house is built , and by understanding it is established ;
verses.meaning: te.vu.nah names understanding as what establishes what wisdom builds — the inner quality that gives structure and stability.

verses.verse_text: Pro 28:16 A ruler who lacks understanding is a cruel oppressor , but he who hates unjust gain will prolong his days .
verses.meaning: te.vu.nah names understanding as the inner quality whose absence in a ruler produces cruelty — the link between inner capacity and just governance.

verses.verse_text: Isa 40:14 Whom did he consult , and who made him understand ? Who taught him the path of justice , and taught him knowledge , and showed him the way of understanding ?
verses.meaning: te.vu.nah names understanding as what no one taught God — his is original and uncreated. Same verse, different span.

verses.verse_text: Isa 40:28 Have you not known ? Have you not heard ? The Lord is the everlasting God , the Creator of the ends of the earth . He does not faint or grow weary ; his understanding is unsearchable .
verses.meaning: te.vu.nah names understanding as a divine attribute of infinite depth — beyond the reach of any search.

verses.verse_text: Jer 10:12 It is he who made the earth by his power , who established the world by his wisdom , and by his understanding stretched out the heavens .
verses.meaning: te.vu.nah names understanding as the divine attribute of creative ordering — by which God establishes cosmic structure.

verses.verse_text: Jer 51:15 “It is he who made the earth by his power , who established the world by his wisdom , and by his understanding stretched out the heavens .
verses.meaning: te.vu.nah names the divine creative attribute — repeated from Jer 10:12. Same meaning.

verses.verse_text: Eze 28:4 by your wisdom and your understanding you have made wealth for yourself, and have gathered gold and silver into your treasuries ;
verses.meaning: te.vu.nah names understanding as an inner capacity the prince of Tyre claims and uses for material gain — the faculty turned toward self-enrichment.

verses.verse_text: Hos 13:2 And now they sin more and more , and make for themselves metal images , idols skillfully made of their silver , all of them the work of craftsmen . It is said of them, “Those who offer human sacrifice kiss calves !”
verses.meaning: te.vu.nah names the craft skill applied to idol-making — the inner understanding faculty used in service of idolatry.

verses.verse_text: Obd 7 All your allies have driven you to your border ; those at peace with you have deceived you; they have prevailed against you; those who eat your bread have set a trap beneath you— you have no understanding .
verses.meaning: te.vu.nah names understanding as what Edom lacks — the inner perceptive faculty whose absence left them unable to perceive their allies' deception.

verses.verse_text: Obd 8 Will I not on that day , declares the Lord , destroy the wise men out of Edom , and understanding out of Mount Esau ?
verses.meaning: te.vu.nah names understanding as what God will remove from Edom — the inner perceptive capacity that gave them national strength.

verses.verse_text: Hos 11:3 Yet it was I who taught Ephraim to walk; I took them up by their arms , but they did not know that I healed them .
verses.meaning: tir.gal names the intimate teaching-to-walk — God as the parent who holds the child's arms and guides the first steps. The inner relational dynamic is of tender, unrecognised nurture. The pathos is that the one taught did not know who taught them.

subgroups.label: Knowledge as inner content and covenantal knowing
subgroups.description: Terms that name knowledge as both inner content held in the knowing self and the constitutive relational orientation of knowing God. Includes the covenantal knowing of God and its structural opposite (spiritual ignorance/agnoeō).

verses.verse_text: Mar 9:32 But they did not understand the saying , and were afraid to ask him .
verses.meaning: agnoeō names the disciples' inner failure of comprehension — they do not understand the passion prediction. P-status: the span is not carrying inner-being content distinctively beyond the suniēmi parallel.

verses.verse_text: Luk 9:45 But they did not understand this saying , and it was concealed from them , so that they might not perceive it . And they were afraid to ask him about this saying .
verses.meaning: agnoeō names the judicial concealment of understanding — the disciples could not grasp what was being said. P-status.

verses.verse_text: Act 13:27 For those who live in Jerusalem and their rulers , because they did not recognize him nor understand the utterances of the prophets , which are read every Sabbath , fulfilled them by condemning him.
verses.meaning: agnoeō names the culpable inner failure to recognise Jesus — the Jerusalem rulers' ignorance of both Jesus and the prophets. This ignorance becomes the mechanism of fulfilment.

verses.verse_text: Act 17:23 For as I passed along and observed the objects of your worship , I found also an altar with this inscription , ‘To the unknown god .’ What therefore you worship as unknown , this I proclaim to you .
verses.meaning: agnoeō names the worshippers' inner ignorance of the one they worship — the Athenians have a devotion without knowing its object. Paul names this as the condition he addresses.

verses.verse_text: Rom 2:4 Or do you presume on the riches of his kindness and forbearance and patience , not knowing that God’s kindness is meant to lead you to repentance ?
verses.meaning: agnoeō names culpable ignorance of God's purpose — the presumptuous person does not know that divine patience is intended as a call to repentance.

verses.verse_text: Rom 6:3 Do you not know that all of us who have been baptized into Christ Jesus were baptized into his death ?
verses.meaning: Poses a rhetorical challenge to an assumed ignorance about baptism's union with Christ's death — the term identifies the inner failure of understanding that would make the doctrinal point necessary.

verses.verse_text: Rom 7:1 Or do you not know , brothers — for I am speaking to those who know the law — that the law is binding on a person only as long as he lives ?
verses.meaning: Challenges an assumed ignorance about the law's binding scope — the term frames the argument as correcting a deficit of understanding in the audience.

verses.verse_text: Rom 10:3 For , being ignorant of the righteousness of God , and seeking to establish their own , they did not submit to God’s righteousness .
verses.meaning: agnoeō names the inner ignorance of God's righteousness as the root of Israel's failure — not knowing God's righteousness, they pursue self-righteousness instead.

verses.verse_text: Rom 11:25 Lest you be wise in your own sight, I do not want you to be unaware of this mystery , brothers : a partial hardening has come upon Israel , until the fullness of the Gentiles has come in .
verses.meaning: Names the self-inflating inner disposition Paul warns against — the term identifies a conceit that makes the audience susceptible to missing the mystery now being disclosed.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 10:1 For I do not want you to be unaware , brothers , that our fathers were all under the cloud , and all passed through the sea ,
verses.meaning: Names the state of unawareness Paul is preventing — the term introduces a corrective disclosure about Israel's wilderness experience.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 12:1 Now concerning spiritual gifts , brothers , I do not want you to be uninformed .
verses.meaning: Names the uninformed inner state Paul addresses — the term opens a corrective teaching about spiritual gifts.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 14:38 If anyone does not recognize this, he is not recognized .
verses.meaning: Names the condition of non-recognition — the term marks out a person whose failure to acknowledge the prophetic word results in their own being unacknowledged.

verses.verse_text: 2Cor 1:8 For we do not want you to be unaware , brothers , of the affliction we experienced in Asia . For we were so utterly burdened beyond our strength that we despaired of life itself .
verses.meaning: Names the state of unawareness Paul wishes to prevent — the term frames the account of severe affliction as a necessary disclosure to the community.

verses.verse_text: 2Cor 2:11 so that we would not be outwitted by Satan ; for we are not ignorant of his designs .
verses.meaning: agnoeō names culpable ignorance of Satan's strategies — Paul and his companions are not in this condition; they know Satan's designs. The inner knowing protects against being outwitted.

verses.verse_text: 2Cor 6:9 as unknown , and yet well known ; as dying , and behold , we live ; as punished , and yet not killed ;
verses.meaning: Characterises the apostolic condition of being unrecognised — the term names the outward obscurity that conceals the inner reality of ongoing life.

verses.verse_text: Gal 1:22 And I was still unknown in person to the churches of Judea that are in Christ .
verses.meaning: Names Paul's state of personal anonymity to the Judean churches — the term describes an absence of inner recognition or familiarity on the part of those communities.

verses.verse_text: 1Th 4:13 But we do not want you to be uninformed , brothers , about those who are asleep , that you may not grieve as others do who have no hope .
verses.meaning: Names the uninformed inner state Paul is correcting — the term introduces eschatological instruction about the dead that is meant to reshape inner orientation away from grief.

verses.verse_text: 1Ti 1:13 though formerly I was a blasphemer , persecutor , and insolent opponent . But I received mercy because I had acted ignorantly in unbelief ,
verses.meaning: agnoeō names the culpable inner ignorance of Paul's pre-conversion persecution — not knowing what he was doing becomes the grounds of mercy.

verses.verse_text: Heb 5:2 He can deal gently with the ignorant and wayward , since he himself is beset with weakness .
verses.meaning: Names the condition of those who are ignorant and morally wayward — the term characterises the high priest's capacity for gentle dealing as appropriate to the inner frailty of this group.

verses.verse_text: 2Pe 2:12 But these , like irrational animals , creatures of instinct, born to be caught and destroyed , blaspheming about matters of which they are ignorant , will also be destroyed in their destruction ,
verses.meaning: agnoeō names the culpable inner ignorance of false teachers — they blaspheme what they do not know, and their ignorance does not excuse them.

verses.verse_text: Rom 2:2 We know that the judgment of God rightly falls on those who practice such things .
verses.meaning: oida names settled inner conviction about divine judgment — a conviction that orients moral life.

verses.verse_text: Rom 3:19 Now we know that whatever the law says it speaks to those who are under the law , so that every mouth may be stopped , and the whole world may be held accountable to God .
verses.meaning: oida names the settled inner knowing of the law's function — conviction that shapes understanding of the whole human condition.

verses.verse_text: Rom 5:3 Not only that , but we rejoice in our sufferings , knowing that suffering produces endurance ,
verses.meaning: oida names the settled conviction that sustains rejoicing in suffering — inner knowledge of the process.

verses.verse_text: Rom 6:9 We know that Christ , being raised from the dead , will never die again; death no longer has dominion over him .
verses.meaning: oida names the settled inner conviction of resurrection — the knowledge that Christ's death is final and unrepeated.

verses.verse_text: Rom 6:16 Do you not know that if you present yourselves to anyone as obedient slaves , you are slaves of the one whom you obey , either of sin , which leads to death , or of obedience , which leads to righteousness ?
verses.meaning: oida names the inner knowledge of the law of moral slavery — conviction about how allegiance works.

verses.verse_text: Rom 7:14 For we know that the law is spiritual , but I am of the flesh , sold under sin .
verses.meaning: oida names the settled conviction about the law's nature — inner knowing that frames the conflict Paul will describe.

verses.verse_text: Rom 7:18 For I know that nothing good dwells in me , that is , in my flesh . For I have the desire to do what is right , but not the ability to carry it out.
verses.meaning: oida names the self-knowledge of inner spiritual conflict — awareness of the flesh as the seat of inability.

verses.verse_text: Rom 8:22 For we know that the whole creation has been groaning together in the pains of childbirth until now .
verses.meaning: oida names the settled inner conviction about creation's condition — knowledge shared by all who have the Spirit.

verses.verse_text: Rom 8:26 Likewise the Spirit helps us in our weakness . For we do not know what to pray for as we ought , but the Spirit himself intercedes for us with groanings too deep for words .
verses.meaning: oida names the acknowledged limit of self-knowledge in prayer — the Spirit intercedes because the person's own knowing is insufficient.

verses.verse_text: Rom 8:27 And he who searches hearts knows what is the mind of the Spirit , because the Spirit intercedes for the saints according to the will of God .
verses.meaning: oida names God's comprehensive inner knowing of persons and the Spirit's mind — searching hearts is the divine activity of knowing.

verses.verse_text: Rom 8:28 And we know that for those who love God all things work together for good , for those who are called according to his purpose .
verses.meaning: oida names the settled convictional knowing that sustains through suffering — love of God grounds the knowing.

verses.verse_text: Rom 11:2 God has not rejected his people whom he foreknew . Do you not know what the Scripture says of Elijah , how he appeals to God against Israel ?
verses.meaning: oida names the knowledge from Scripture that should be known — a settled conviction about what the text says.

verses.verse_text: Rom 13:11 Besides this you know the time , that the hour has come for you to wake from sleep . For salvation is nearer to us now than when we first believed .
verses.meaning: oida names the settled inner conviction about the eschatological moment — knowledge that shapes conduct.

verses.verse_text: Rom 14:14 I know and am persuaded in the Lord Jesus that nothing is unclean in itself , but it is unclean for anyone who thinks it unclean .
verses.meaning: oida names the inner conviction about ritual purity — a settled knowing in the Lord that grounds the position.

verses.verse_text: Rom 15:29 I know that when I come to you I will come in the fullness of the blessing of Christ .
verses.meaning: oida names Paul's settled inner confidence about his coming visit — knowledge held as conviction.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 1:16 (I did baptize also the household of Stephanas . Beyond that, I do not know whether I baptized anyone else .)
verses.meaning: Names the limit of Paul's certain recollection — the term identifies the inner state of not knowing as the honest boundary of his memory about whom he baptised.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 2:2 For I decided to know nothing among you except Jesus Christ and him crucified .
verses.meaning: Names the deliberate inner resolve — the term identifies Paul's decision to know nothing among them except Christ crucified as a chosen orientation of mind.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 2:11 For who knows a person’s thoughts except the spirit of that person , which is in him ? So also no one comprehends the thoughts of God except the Spirit of God .
verses.meaning: oida names inner self-knowledge — only the person's own spirit knows their thoughts. God's Spirit alone knows God's thoughts. A key statement about the limits and depth of self-knowledge.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 3:16 Do you not know that you are God’s temple and that God’s Spirit dwells in you ?
verses.meaning: oida names the identity-shaping knowledge that the community possesses — knowing who they are in Christ transforms their self-understanding.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 5:6 Your boasting is not good . Do you not know that a little leaven leavens the whole lump ?
verses.meaning: oida names the settled conviction about moral contagion — an inner knowing that should shape communal discipline.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 6:2 Or do you not know that the saints will judge the world ? And if the world is to be judged by you , are you incompetent to try trivial cases ?
verses.meaning: oida names the identity-shaping knowledge of the saints' eschatological role — knowing this should change how they handle disputes.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 6:3 Do you not know that we are to judge angels ? How much more , then, matters pertaining to this life !
verses.meaning: oida names the identity-shaping knowledge of the community's eschatological standing — knowing this transforms self-assessment.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 6:9 Or do you not know that the unrighteous will not inherit the kingdom of God ? Do not be deceived : neither the sexually immoral , nor idolaters , nor adulterers , nor men who practice homosexuality ,
verses.meaning: oida names the identity-shaping knowledge of the kingdom's character — knowing who does and does not inherit shapes moral orientation.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 6:15 Do you not know that your bodies are members of Christ ? Shall I then take the members of Christ and make them members of a prostitute ? Never !
verses.meaning: oida names the identity-shaping knowledge of bodily union with Christ — knowing this makes certain acts impossible.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 6:16 Or do you not know that he who is joined to a prostitute becomes one body with her? For , as it is written , “The two will become one flesh .”
verses.meaning: oida names the identity-shaping knowledge of bodily union — knowing how union works shapes how it must be guarded.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 8:1 Now concerning food offered to idols : we know that “ all of us possess knowledge .” This “ knowledge ” puffs up , but love builds up .
verses.meaning: oida names knowledge divorced from love — a claim to knowing that inflates rather than builds. The tension between knowledge and love is explicit.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 8:4 Therefore , as to the eating of food offered to idols , we know that “an idol has no real existence ,” and that “there is no God but one .”
verses.meaning: oida names the settled theological conviction — inner knowledge about the nature of idols.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 9:13 Do you not know that those who are employed in the temple service get their food from the temple , and those who serve at the altar share in the sacrificial offerings ?
verses.meaning: oida names the settled inner knowing of a practice — used to ground an argument about apostolic rights.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 9:24 Do you not know that in a race all the runners run , but only one receives the prize ? So run that you may obtain it.
verses.meaning: oida names the settled inner knowing of how competition works — used to motivate purposeful spiritual effort.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 11:3 But I want you to understand that the head of every man is Christ , the head of a wife is her husband , and the head of Christ is God .
verses.meaning: oida names the inner conviction about order — knowledge that should shape community practice.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 12:2 You know that when you were pagans you were led astray to mute idols , however you were led .
verses.meaning: oida names the inner knowledge of their own past — conviction about what they were before Christ.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 13:2 And if I have prophetic powers , and understand all mysteries and all knowledge , and if I have all faith , so as to remove mountains , but have not love , I am nothing .
verses.meaning: oida names knowledge divorced from love — understanding all mysteries without love is inner emptiness. The most explicit statement of knowledge without love.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 14:11 but if I do not know the meaning of the language , I will be a foreigner to the speaker and the speaker a foreigner to me .
verses.meaning: Names the prerequisite for meaningful communication — the term identifies knowing the meaning of a language as the inner comprehension without which speech becomes mere noise.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 14:16 Otherwise , if you give thanks with your spirit , how can anyone in the position of an outsider say “ Amen ” to your thanksgiving when he does not know what you are saying ?
verses.meaning: Names the inner state of the uninitiated hearer — the term identifies the outsider's lack of comprehension of what is being said as the condition that makes uninterpreted tongues unhelpful.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 15:58 Therefore , my beloved brothers , be steadfast , immovable , always abounding in the work of the Lord , knowing that in the Lord your labor is not in vain .
verses.meaning: oida names the settled conviction that sustains labour — inner knowing of the resurrection's guarantee.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 16:15 Now I urge you , brothers —you know that the household of Stephanas were the first converts in Achaia , and that they have devoted themselves to the service of the saints —
verses.meaning: Names what the community already knows — the term identifies their inner awareness of Stephanas's household and its service as established knowledge Paul appeals to.

verses.verse_text: 2Cor 1:7 Our hope for you is unshaken , for we know that as you share in our sufferings , you will also share in our comfort .
verses.meaning: oida names the settled conviction about the logic of suffering and comfort — inner knowing that grounds hope.

verses.verse_text: 2Cor 4:14 knowing that he who raised the Lord Jesus will raise us also with Jesus and bring us with you into his presence .
verses.meaning: oida names the settled conviction of resurrection — inner knowledge that sustains ministry through suffering.

verses.verse_text: 2Cor 5:1 For we know that if the tent that is our earthly home is destroyed , we have a building from God , a house not made with hands , eternal in the heavens .
verses.meaning: oida names the settled conviction about the eternal body — inner knowing that relativises present suffering.

verses.verse_text: 2Cor 5:6 So we are always of good courage . We know that while we are at home in the body we are away from the Lord ,
verses.meaning: oida names the settled inner conviction about present existence — knowledge that shapes the orientation of hope.

verses.verse_text: 2Cor 5:11 Therefore , knowing the fear of the Lord , we persuade others . But what we are is known to God , and I hope it is known also to your conscience .
verses.meaning: oida names self-knowledge of inner motivations — what Paul is, is known to God, and hopefully to conscience.

verses.verse_text: 2Cor 5:16 From now on , therefore , we regard no one according to the flesh . Even though we once regarded Christ according to the flesh , we regard him thus no longer .
verses.meaning: oida names the new inner knowing — regard shaped by resurrection rather than by worldly categories.

verses.verse_text: 2Cor 9:2 for I know your readiness , of which I boast about you to the people of Macedonia , saying that Achaia has been ready since last year . And your zeal has stirred up most of them.
verses.meaning: oida names Paul's knowledge of the Corinthian community — inner knowing of their generosity that he makes public.

verses.verse_text: 2Cor 11:11 And why ? Because I do not love you ? God knows I do!
verses.meaning: oida names God's comprehensive inner knowing of Paul's love — appealed to as the ultimate witness.

verses.verse_text: 2Cor 11:31 The God and Father of the Lord Jesus , he who is blessed forever , knows that I am not lying .
verses.meaning: oida names God's knowing as the ground of Paul's oath — divine inner knowing is the ultimate guarantor.

verses.verse_text: Gal 2:16 yet we know that a person is not justified by works of the law but through faith in Jesus Christ , so we also have believed in Christ Jesus , in order to be justified by faith in Christ and not by works of the law , because by works of the law no one will be justified .
verses.meaning: oida names the settled theological conviction — inner knowing that grounds the argument for faith.

verses.verse_text: Gal 4:8 Formerly , when you did not know God , you were enslaved to those that by nature are not gods .
verses.meaning: oida names the identity-shaping knowledge of God — not knowing God defines the pre-conversion condition of enslavement. Knowing God defines freedom.

verses.verse_text: Gal 4:13 You know it was because of a bodily ailment that I preached the gospel to you at first ,
verses.meaning: Names what the Galatians know from experience — the term identifies their inner awareness of the bodily ailment that occasioned Paul's first preaching to them.

verses.verse_text: Eph 1:18 having the eyes of your hearts enlightened , that you may know what is the hope to which he has called you, what are the riches of his glorious inheritance in the saints ,
verses.meaning: oida names the identity-shaping knowledge of calling and inheritance — the eyes of the heart enlightened to know.

verses.verse_text: Eph 5:5 For you may be sure of this , that everyone who is sexually immoral or impure , or who is covetous ( that is , an idolater ), has no inheritance in the kingdom of Christ and God .
verses.meaning: oida names settled inner certainty about moral consequence — conviction that shapes conduct.

verses.verse_text: Eph 6:8 knowing that whatever good anyone does , this he will receive back from the Lord , whether he is a bondservant or is free .
verses.meaning: oida names the settled conviction about divine recompense — inner knowing that motivates faithful service.

verses.verse_text: Eph 6:9 Masters , do the same to them , and stop your threatening , knowing that he who is both their Master and yours is in heaven , and that there is no partiality with him .
verses.meaning: oida names the settled conviction about shared accountability — inner knowing that shapes how power is used.

verses.verse_text: Eph 6:21 So that you also may know how I am and what I am doing , Tychicus the beloved brother and faithful minister in the Lord will tell you everything .
verses.meaning: Names the purpose of Tychicus's mission — the term identifies making the recipients know Paul's situation as the inner goal of sending a trusted messenger.

verses.verse_text: Col 2:1 For I want you to know how great a struggle I have for you and for those at Laodicea and for all who have not seen me face to face ,
verses.meaning: oida names Paul's desire for the community to know his inner condition — transparency about the apostolic inner life.

verses.verse_text: Col 3:24 knowing that from the Lord you will receive the inheritance as your reward . You are serving the Lord Christ .
verses.meaning: oida names the settled conviction about eschatological reward — inner knowing that makes service worth giving.

verses.verse_text: Col 4:1 Masters , treat your bondservants justly and fairly , knowing that you also have a Master in heaven .
verses.meaning: oida names the settled conviction about common accountability — inner knowing that shapes how authority is exercised.

verses.verse_text: Pro 28:5 Evil men do not understand justice , but those who seek the Lord understand it completely .
verses.meaning: bin names the inner perceptive faculty opened by seeking God — those who seek the Lord understand justice in full.

verses.verse_text: Isa 28:9 “To whom will he teach knowledge , and to whom will he explain the message ? Those who are weaned from the milk , those taken from the breast ?
verses.meaning: bin names the inner perceptive faculty needed to receive explanation — the question implies the audience lacks the capacity to receive.

verses.verse_text: Isa 40:21 Do you not know ? Do you not hear ? Has it not been told you from the beginning ? Have you not understood from the foundations of the earth ?
verses.meaning: bin names the inner perceptive reception of what has been available from the beginning — the question challenges those who act as if they do not know.

verses.verse_text: Isa 43:10 “ You are my witnesses ,” declares the Lord , “and my servant whom I have chosen , that you may know and believe me and understand that I am he. Before me no god was formed , nor shall there be any after me.
verses.meaning: bin names the inner perceptive reception of God's identity — understanding that God is who he is, as the goal of Israel's witness role.

verses.verse_text: Dan 1:4 youths without blemish , of good appearance and skillful in all wisdom , endowed with knowledge , understanding learning , and competent to stand in the king’s palace , and to teach them the literature and language of the Chaldeans .
verses.meaning: bin names understanding as one of the inner qualities the youths possess — alongside knowledge and learning, qualifying them for royal service.

verses.verse_text: Job 32:6 And Elihu the son of Barachel the Buzite answered and said : “I am young in years , and you are aged ; therefore I was timid and afraid to declare my opinion to you .
verses.meaning: de.a names the inner formed judgment or opinion held inwardly — Elihu has withheld his inner view out of deference to age.

verses.verse_text: Job 32:10 Therefore I say , ‘ Listen to me; let me also declare my opinion .’
verses.meaning: de.a names the inner formed opinion now to be expressed — Elihu's inner position held and now released.

verses.verse_text: Job 32:17 I also will answer with my share ; I also will declare my opinion .
verses.meaning: de.a names the same — the inner formed view ready for expression.

verses.verse_text: Job 36:3 I will get my knowledge from afar and ascribe righteousness to my Maker .
verses.meaning: de.a names Elihu's claim to knowledge oriented toward God — inner formed understanding directed away from self and toward God.

verses.verse_text: Job 37:16 Do you know the balancings of the clouds , the wondrous works of him who is perfect in knowledge ,
verses.meaning: de.a names perfect knowledge as a divine attribute — God's inner knowing of his own works is complete.

verses.verse_text: 1Sa 2:3 Talk no more so very proudly , let not arrogance come from your mouth ; for the Lord is a God of knowledge , and by him actions are weighed .
verses.meaning: de.ah names knowledge as a divine attribute — God's perfect, comprehensive knowing that weighs all human action.

verses.verse_text: Job 36:4 For truly my words are not false ; one who is perfect in knowledge is with you .
verses.meaning: de.ah names perfect knowledge as a divine attribute — Elihu either applies it to himself (boldly) or to God. Either way, perfect de.ah is a standard of inner knowing.

verses.verse_text: Psa 73:11 And they say , “ How can God know ? Is there knowledge in the Most High ?”
verses.meaning: de.ah names the divine knowing that the wicked deny — they claim God does not know, thereby refusing moral scrutiny.

verses.verse_text: Isa 11:9 They shall not hurt or destroy in all my holy mountain ; for the earth shall be full of the knowledge of the Lord as the waters cover the sea .
verses.meaning: de.ah names the knowledge of the Lord as the content of the eschatological filling — universal inner knowing of God as the destiny of creation.

verses.verse_text: Isa 28:9 “To whom will he teach knowledge , and to whom will he explain the message ? Those who are weaned from the milk , those taken from the breast ?
verses.meaning: de.ah names knowledge as what the teacher would impart — the inner content of instruction. The scoffers mock the simplicity of the message.

verses.verse_text: Jer 3:15 “‘And I will give you shepherds after my own heart , who will feed you with knowledge and understanding .
verses.meaning: de.ah names knowledge as what the coming shepherds will give — inner content of formation, alongside understanding.

verses.verse_text: Gen 2:9 And out of the ground the Lord God made to spring up every tree that is pleasant to the sight and good for food . The tree of life was in the midst of the garden , and the tree of the knowledge of good and evil .
verses.meaning: da.at names the knowledge that the tree embodies — moral knowledge of good and evil as a defining inner capacity. The tree is named by what it contains; knowledge of good and evil is not mere information but a transformative inner orientation.

verses.verse_text: Gen 2:17 but of the tree of the knowledge of good and evil you shall not eat , for in the day that you eat of it you shall surely die .”
verses.meaning: da.at names the knowledge God withholds from the human — moral self-determination, the capacity to define good and evil for oneself. The prohibition frames this knowledge as dangerous.

verses.verse_text: Num 24:16 the oracle of him who hears the words of God , and knows the knowledge of the Most High , who sees the vision of the Almighty , falling down with his eyes uncovered :
verses.meaning: da.at names the knowledge of the Most High as an inner capacity of the seer — divine knowledge held within the person as a gift. Note: assigned to 1838-001; however this is a positive description of the seer's inner capacity, not culpable ignorance. The assignment appears to be a misrouting.

verses.verse_text: Deu 4:42 that the manslayer might flee there , anyone who kills his neighbor unintentionally , without being at enmity with him in time past ; he may flee to one of these cities and save his life :
verses.meaning: da.at names the inner state of intent — killing without da.at is killing without the moral knowledge/intent of enmity. The presence or absence of da.at determines culpability.

verses.verse_text: Deu 19:4 “ This is the provision for the manslayer , who by fleeing there may save his life . If anyone kills his neighbor unintentionally without having hated him in the past —
verses.meaning: da.at names the inner state of intent — absence of da.at (without having hated) is the moral condition that determines the act's character. Same pattern as Deu 4:42.

verses.verse_text: Jos 20:3 that the manslayer who strikes any person without intent or unknowingly may flee there . They shall be for you a refuge from the avenger of blood .
verses.meaning: da.at names the inner state of intent — killing without da.at is unintentional. Same pattern as Deu 4:42.

verses.verse_text: Jos 20:5 And if the avenger of blood pursues him, they shall not give up the manslayer into his hand , because he struck his neighbor unknowingly , and did not hate him in the past .
verses.meaning: da.at names the absence of inner enmity — the act was not driven by the moral knowledge of hatred.

verses.verse_text: Job 10:7 although you know that I am not guilty , and there is none to deliver out of your hand ?
verses.meaning: da.at names God's knowledge of Job's innocence — the inner moral condition that God knows but apparently acts against.

verses.verse_text: Job 13:2 What you know , I also know; I am not inferior to you .
verses.meaning: da.at names the knowledge held by Job and his friends — inner intellectual and experiential knowledge as the ground of dispute.

verses.verse_text: Job 15:2 “Should a wise man answer with windy knowledge , and fill his belly with the east wind?
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge emptied of substance — Eliphaz accuses Job of speaking words without real inner content.

verses.verse_text: Job 21:14 They say to God , ‘ Depart from us! We do not desire the knowledge of your ways .
verses.meaning: da.at names the knowledge of God's ways as what the wicked refuse — the inner orientation toward God that they actively reject.

verses.verse_text: Job 21:22 Will any teach God knowledge , seeing that he judges those who are on high ?
verses.meaning: da.at names the knowledge that belongs to God — no one can instruct God; his knowing is self-sufficient and above all judgment.

verses.verse_text: Job 33:3 My words declare the uprightness of my heart , and what my lips know they speak sincerely .
verses.meaning: da.at names the knowledge that the lips hold — inner knowledge expressed outwardly in sincere speech.

verses.verse_text: Job 34:35 ‘ Job speaks without knowledge ; his words are without insight .’
verses.meaning: da.at names the knowledge Elihu says Job's speech lacks — inner content of substance. Empty speech is speech without da.at.

verses.verse_text: Job 35:16 Job opens his mouth in empty talk ; he multiplies words without knowledge .”
verses.meaning: da.at names the inner substance absent in Job's words — words without real knowledge behind them.

verses.verse_text: Job 36:12 But if they do not listen , they perish by the sword and die without knowledge .
verses.meaning: da.at names the inner orientation toward God whose absence leads to ruin — dying without knowledge is dying without having known God's ways.

verses.verse_text: Job 38:2 “ Who is this that darkens counsel by words without knowledge ?
verses.meaning: da.at names the inner substance God charges Job with lacking — his words darkened rather than illuminated counsel.

verses.verse_text: Job 42:3 ‘ Who is this that hides counsel without knowledge ?’ Therefore I have uttered what I did not understand , things too wonderful for me, which I did not know .
verses.meaning: da.at names the knowledge Job lacked — he spoke beyond his inner knowledge. The repentance acknowledges the limit.

verses.verse_text: Psa 19:2 Day to day pours out speech , and night to night reveals knowledge .
verses.meaning: da.at names the knowledge that creation pours out — the heavens disclose inner knowing of God's glory. Note: assigned to 1838-001 (culpable ignorance), which does not fit. This is positive disclosure of knowledge, not its absence.

verses.verse_text: Psa 94:10 He who disciplines the nations , does he not rebuke ? He who teaches man knowledge —
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as a divine gift God teaches to humans — an inner endowment transmitted by God.

verses.verse_text: Psa 119:66 Teach me good judgment and knowledge , for I believe in your commandments .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as what the psalmist seeks from God — inner capacity oriented toward the commandments, sought in prayer.

verses.verse_text: Psa 139:6 Such knowledge is too wonderful for me; it is high ; I cannot attain it .
verses.meaning: da.at names God's comprehensive knowledge of the psalmist — too vast to be comprehended by the one who is known. The knowing is God's; its object is the psalmist.

verses.verse_text: Pro 1:4 to give prudence to the simple , knowledge and discretion to the youth —
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as what Proverbs aims to give the young — inner capacity paired with prudence and discretion as formative endowments.

verses.verse_text: Pro 1:7 The fear of the Lord is the beginning of knowledge ; fools despise wisdom and instruction .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as having its ground in the fear of the Lord — the inner orientation toward God is the foundation of all genuine knowing.

verses.verse_text: Pro 1:22 “How long , O simple ones , will you love being simple ? How long will scoffers delight in their scoffing and fools hate knowledge ?
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as what fools actively reject — the inner capacity and orientation is an object of hatred for those turned from wisdom.

verses.verse_text: Pro 1:29 Because they hated knowledge and did not choose the fear of the Lord ,
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as what the rejected call to wisdom offered — it was available and refused.

verses.verse_text: Pro 2:5 then you will understand the fear of the Lord and find the knowledge of God .
verses.meaning: da.at names the knowledge of God as the goal of seeking wisdom — not mere information but the defining inner orientation toward God.

verses.verse_text: Pro 2:6 For the Lord gives wisdom ; from his mouth come knowledge and understanding ;
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as a divine gift from God's mouth — an inner endowment transmitted through word.

verses.verse_text: Pro 2:10 for wisdom will come into your heart , and knowledge will be pleasant to your soul ;
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as something experienced inwardly as delight — it enters the soul as pleasure, not as burden. The inner-being resonance is direct.

verses.verse_text: Pro 3:20 by his knowledge the deeps broke open , and the clouds drop down the dew .
verses.meaning: Names the active understanding by which God ordered creation — the term identifies divine knowledge as the inner faculty through which the cosmic deeps were opened.

verses.verse_text: Pro 5:2 that you may keep discretion , and your lips may guard knowledge .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as something held and guarded — an inner possession that must be kept.

verses.verse_text: Pro 8:9 They are all straight to him who understands , and right to those who find knowledge .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as what makes wisdom's words accessible — those who have the inner capacity find them straight.

verses.verse_text: Pro 8:10 Take my instruction instead of silver , and knowledge rather than choice gold ,
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as more valuable than gold — its worth is inner, not material.

verses.verse_text: Pro 8:12 “ I , wisdom , dwell with prudence , and I find knowledge and discretion .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as what Wisdom holds within herself — an inner possession she finds and offers.

verses.verse_text: Pro 9:10 The fear of the Lord is the beginning of wisdom , and the knowledge of the Holy One is insight .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge of the Holy One as what constitutes insight — knowing God is not an add-on but the substance of understanding.

verses.verse_text: Pro 10:14 The wise lay up knowledge , but the mouth of a fool brings ruin near .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as something accumulated and stored inwardly by the wise — an inner treasury.

verses.verse_text: Pro 11:9 With his mouth the godless man would destroy his neighbor , but by knowledge the righteous are delivered .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as a protective inner capacity — what delivers the righteous from the godless person's destruction.

verses.verse_text: Pro 12:1 Whoever loves discipline loves knowledge , but he who hates reproof is stupid .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as what discipline produces — the person who receives correction is on the path of inner knowing.

verses.verse_text: Pro 12:23 A prudent man conceals knowledge , but the heart of fools proclaims folly .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as something the prudent hold inwardly rather than displaying — restraint is a sign of genuine inner knowing.

verses.verse_text: Pro 13:16 Every prudent man acts with knowledge , but a fool flaunts his folly .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as what underlies prudent action — the inner capacity expressed in conduct.

verses.verse_text: Pro 14:6 A scoffer seeks wisdom in vain , but knowledge is easy for a man of understanding .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as accessible to those who have understanding — the inner faculty opens the way to knowing.

verses.verse_text: Pro 14:7 Leave the presence of a fool , for there you do not meet words of knowledge .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as absent in the fool's speech — leaving the fool's presence means leaving where knowledge is not found.

verses.verse_text: Pro 14:18 The simple inherit folly , but the prudent are crowned with knowledge .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as the crown of the prudent — what adorns and marks the person of wisdom.

verses.verse_text: Pro 15:2 The tongue of the wise commends knowledge , but the mouths of fools pour out folly .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as what wise speech honours — the inner capacity expressed through commending words.

verses.verse_text: Pro 15:7 The lips of the wise spread knowledge ; not so the hearts of fools .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as what the wise disseminate — the inner capacity communicated through speech.

verses.verse_text: Pro 15:14 The heart of him who has understanding seeks knowledge , but the mouths of fools feed on folly .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as the object of the understanding heart's seeking — an inner orientation toward further knowing.

verses.verse_text: Pro 17:27 Whoever restrains his words has knowledge , and he who has a cool spirit is a man of understanding .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as what underlies restraint — inner knowing expressed in the discipline of speech.

verses.verse_text: Pro 18:15 An intelligent heart acquires knowledge , and the ear of the wise seeks knowledge .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as what the intelligent heart acquires and the wise ear seeks — active inner pursuit of the capacity.

verses.verse_text: Pro 19:2 Desire without knowledge is not good , and whoever makes haste with his feet misses his way .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as what must accompany desire for it to be good — inner knowing directs desire; without it, haste leads astray.

verses.verse_text: Pro 19:25 Strike a scoffer , and the simple will learn prudence ; reprove a man of understanding , and he will gain knowledge .
verses.meaning: da.at names the knowledge produced by receiving correction — the person of understanding grows in inner capacity through rebuke.

verses.verse_text: Pro 19:27 Cease to hear instruction , my son , and you will stray from the words of knowledge .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as the content of instructive words — ceasing to hear instruction means leaving the sphere of inner knowing.

verses.verse_text: Pro 20:15 There is gold and abundance of costly stones , but the lips of knowledge are a precious jewel .
