verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as a precious inner possession expressed through speech — rare and valuable.

verses.verse_text: Pro 21:11 When a scoffer is punished , the simple becomes wise ; when a wise man is instructed , he gains knowledge .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as the fruit of instruction for the wise — the inner capacity grows with receiving.

verses.verse_text: Pro 22:12 The eyes of the Lord keep watch over knowledge , but he overthrows the words of the traitor .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as what God guards — he watches over the inner capacity and protects it.

verses.verse_text: Pro 22:17 Incline your ear , and hear the words of the wise , and apply your heart to my knowledge ,
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as what the heart must be applied to — inner orientation of the heart toward received wisdom.

verses.verse_text: Pro 22:20 Have I not written for you thirty sayings of counsel and knowledge ,
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as the content of the sayings — the collection is characterised by its inner substance.

verses.verse_text: Pro 23:12 Apply your heart to instruction and your ear to words of knowledge .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as what words of instruction carry — the ear applied to receive their inner content.

verses.verse_text: Pro 24:4 by knowledge the rooms are filled with all precious and pleasant riches .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as what produces abundance — the inner capacity fills what wisdom builds.

verses.verse_text: Pro 24:5 A wise man is full of strength , and a man of knowledge enhances his might ,
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as what augments strength — inner knowing makes the person more effective.

verses.verse_text: Pro 29:7 A righteous man knows the rights of the poor ; a wicked man does not understand such knowledge .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as the inner capacity that moral orientation blocks — the wicked cannot receive it.

verses.verse_text: Pro 30:3 I have not learned wisdom , nor have I knowledge of the Holy One .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge of the Holy One as what Agur disclaims — the inner orientation toward God he does not possess.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 1:16 I said in my heart , “ I have acquired great wisdom , surpassing all who were over Jerusalem before me, and my heart has had great experience of wisdom and knowledge .”
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as an inner experiential possession — the heart's engagement with knowing as part of the quest.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 1:18 For in much wisdom is much vexation , and he who increases knowledge increases sorrow .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as something that brings inner pain — the more one knows, the more the limits and griefs of existence become apparent.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 2:21 because sometimes a person who has toiled with wisdom and knowledge and skill must leave everything to be enjoyed by someone who did not toil for it. This also is vanity and a great evil .
verses.meaning: Names the intellectual effort of the one who toiled — the term identifies knowledge as a valued inner quality that the toiler brought to his labour, only for its fruit to pass to another.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 2:26 For to the one who pleases him God has given wisdom and knowledge and joy , but to the sinner he has given the business of gathering and collecting , only to give to one who pleases God . This also is vanity and a striving after wind .
verses.meaning: Names the capacity God gives to those who please him — the term identifies knowledge as a gift bestowed alongside wisdom and joy, in contrast to the sinner's mere accumulation.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 7:12 For the protection of wisdom is like the protection of money , and the advantage of knowledge is that wisdom preserves the life of him who has it.
verses.meaning: Names the cognitive advantage that wisdom carries — the term identifies knowledge as preserving the life of the one who possesses it, offering an advantage beyond material protection.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 9:10 Whatever your hand finds to do , do it with your might , for there is no work or thought or knowledge or wisdom in Sheol , to which you are going .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as belonging to the living — it ceases at death. Inner knowing is a capacity of the living person.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 12:9 Besides being wise , the Preacher also taught the people knowledge , weighing and studying and arranging many proverbs with great care.
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as what the Preacher transmitted — the inner capacity imparted through the collected proverbs.

verses.verse_text: Isa 5:13 Therefore my people go into exile for lack of knowledge ; their honored men go hungry , and their multitude is parched with thirst .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge of God as what Israel lacks — the absence produces exile. The knowledge of God and national stability are directly linked.

verses.verse_text: Isa 11:2 And the Spirit of the Lord shall rest upon him, the Spirit of wisdom and understanding , the Spirit of counsel and might , the Spirit of knowledge and the fear of the Lord .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as a Spirit-given endowment on the Messiah — one of the sevenfold gifts resting on him.

verses.verse_text: Isa 33:6 and he will be the stability of your times , abundance of salvation , wisdom , and knowledge ; the fear of the Lord is Zion’s treasure .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as part of God's gift of stability — Spirit-given inner knowing as part of the coming abundance.

verses.verse_text: Isa 40:14 Whom did he consult , and who made him understand ? Who taught him the path of justice , and taught him knowledge , and showed him the way of understanding ?
verses.meaning: da.at names the knowledge no one taught God — his knowing is original and uncreated.

verses.verse_text: Isa 44:19 No one considers , nor is there knowledge or discernment to say , “ Half of it I burned in the fire ; I also baked bread on its coals ; I roasted meat and have eaten . And shall I make the rest of it an abomination ? Shall I fall down before a block of wood ?”
verses.meaning: da.at names the knowledge absent in the idol-worshipper — they lack the inner capacity to perceive the absurdity of their practice.

verses.verse_text: Isa 44:25 who frustrates the signs of liars and makes fools of diviners , who turns wise men back and makes their knowledge foolish ,
verses.meaning: da.at names the knowledge God frustrates — the supposed inner knowing of diviners and wise men rendered empty.

verses.verse_text: Isa 47:10 You felt secure in your wickedness ; you said , “No one sees me”; your wisdom and your knowledge led you astray , and you said in your heart , “ I am, and there is no one besides me.”
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge that has become the instrument of self-deception — Babylon's inner knowing has turned against her.

verses.verse_text: Isa 53:11 Out of the anguish of his soul he shall see and be satisfied ; by his knowledge shall the righteous one , my servant , make many to be accounted righteous , and he shall bear their iniquities .
verses.meaning: da.at names the knowledge by which the Servant justifies — his inner knowing is the instrument of the many's righteousness. A remarkable and distinctive use.

verses.verse_text: Isa 58:2 Yet they seek me daily and delight to know my ways , as if they were a nation that did righteousness and did not forsake the judgment of their God ; they ask of me righteous judgments ; they delight to draw near to God .
verses.meaning: da.at names the knowledge of God's ways as what the hypocrites claim to delight in — the inner orientation they perform without genuinely holding.

verses.verse_text: Jer 10:14 Every man is stupid and without knowledge ; every goldsmith is put to shame by his idols , for his images are false , and there is no breath in them .
verses.meaning: da.at names the inner capacity absent in idol-makers — without it, their craft produces only falsehood.

verses.verse_text: Jer 22:16 He judged the cause of the poor and needy ; then it was well . Is not this to know me? declares the Lord .
verses.meaning: da.at names the knowledge of God as expressed in justice for the vulnerable — knowing God and doing justice are equated. A profound definitional statement.

verses.verse_text: Jer 51:17 Every man is stupid and without knowledge ; every goldsmith is put to shame by his idols , for his images are false , and there is no breath in them .
verses.meaning: da.at names the absent inner capacity — same as Jer 10:14.

verses.verse_text: Dan 1:4 youths without blemish , of good appearance and skillful in all wisdom , endowed with knowledge , understanding learning , and competent to stand in the king’s palace , and to teach them the literature and language of the Chaldeans .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as one of the inner qualities God gave the youths — a Spirit-given capacity alongside understanding and learning.

verses.verse_text: Dan 12:4 But you , Daniel , shut up the words and seal the book , until the time of the end . Many shall run to and fro , and knowledge shall increase .”
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as what will increase in the time of the end — an eschatological expansion of inner knowing.

verses.verse_text: Hos 4:1 Hear the word of the Lord , O children of Israel , for the Lord has a controversy with the inhabitants of the land . There is no faithfulness or steadfast love , and no knowledge of God in the land ;
verses.meaning: da.at names the defining absence in Israel — without the knowledge of God, the land has no faithfulness or steadfast love. All three are interlinked.

verses.verse_text: Hos 4:6 My people are destroyed for lack of knowledge ; because you have rejected knowledge , I reject you from being a priest to me. And since you have forgotten the law of your God , I also will forget your children .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge of God as the inner orientation whose rejection produces destruction — the most explicit statement of the consequences of da.at's absence.

verses.verse_text: Hos 6:6 For I desire steadfast love and not sacrifice , the knowledge of God rather than burnt offerings .
verses.meaning: da.at names the knowledge of God as what God desires above sacrifice — relational inner knowing as the substance of true worship. Note: assigned to 1838-001, but this is a positive statement of what God desires, not culpable ignorance. The assignment appears to be a misrouting.

verses.verse_text: Mal 2:7 For the lips of a priest should guard knowledge , and people should seek instruction from his mouth , for he is the messenger of the Lord of hosts .
verses.meaning: da.at names knowledge as the priestly possession that must be guarded and dispensed — the inner capacity held by the priest for the benefit of the community.

verses.verse_text: Pro 15:2 The tongue of the wise commends knowledge , but the mouths of fools pour out folly .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names wisdom as an inner quality whose expression in speech honours and promotes knowledge.

verses.verse_text: Pro 15:7 The lips of the wise spread knowledge ; not so the hearts of fools .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the wise as those whose speech disseminates knowledge — wisdom communicates what it holds inwardly.

verses.verse_text: Pro 22:17 Incline your ear , and hear the words of the wise , and apply your heart to my knowledge ,
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the wise as those whose words deserve to be heard — their inner quality makes their speech authoritative.

verses.verse_text: Gen 3:5 For God knows that when you eat of it your eyes will be opened , and you will be like God , knowing good and evil .”
verses.meaning: ya.da names what the serpent attributes to God — divine self-knowledge of what the fruit does. The temptation is access to what God knows.

verses.verse_text: Gen 3:7 Then the eyes of both were opened , and they knew that they were naked . And they sewed fig leaves together and made themselves loincloths .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the immediate experiential moral knowledge produced by eating — self-awareness of nakedness as the first fruit of the transgression.

verses.verse_text: Gen 3:22 Then the Lord God said , “ Behold , the man has become like one of us in knowing good and evil . Now , lest he reach out his hand and take also of the tree of life and eat , and live forever —”
verses.meaning: ya.da names the moral knowledge now acquired — knowing good and evil as a divine-level capacity. The consequence is permanent inner transformation.

verses.verse_text: Gen 4:1 Now Adam knew Eve his wife , and she conceived and bore Cain , saying , “I have gotten a man with the help of the Lord .”
verses.meaning: Names the sexual act — the term uses knowing as the expression of the intimate union by which Adam and Eve conceived.

verses.verse_text: Gen 4:9 Then the Lord said to Cain , “ Where is Abel your brother ?” He said , “I do not know ; am I my brother’s keeper ?”
verses.meaning: ya.da names Cain's claimed ignorance of Abel's whereabouts — a denial of relational knowledge and responsibility. The self-knowledge here is absent or suppressed.

verses.verse_text: Gen 4:17 Cain knew his wife , and she conceived and bore Enoch . When he built a city , he called the name of the city after the name of his son , Enoch .
verses.meaning: Names the sexual act — the term identifies the intimate union by which Cain knew his wife and she bore a child.

verses.verse_text: Gen 4:25 And Adam knew his wife again , and she bore a son and called his name Seth , for she said, “ God has appointed for me another offspring instead of Abel , for Cain killed him.”
verses.meaning: Names the sexual act — the term identifies Adam's renewed intimate union with his wife, from which Seth was born.

verses.verse_text: Gen 8:11 And the dove came back to him in the evening , and behold , in her mouth was a freshly plucked olive leaf . So Noah knew that the waters had subsided from the earth .
verses.meaning: Names the inference drawn from evidence — the term identifies Noah's inner recognition that the waters had subsided, based on the dove's return with an olive leaf.

verses.verse_text: Gen 9:24 When Noah awoke from his wine and knew what his youngest son had done to him,
verses.meaning: Names the inner realisation upon waking — the term identifies Noah's coming to know what his youngest son had done to him.

verses.verse_text: Gen 12:11 When he was about to enter Egypt , he said to Sarai his wife , “ I know that you are a woman beautiful in appearance ,
verses.meaning: ya.da names Abram's self-aware assessment of his situation — knowledge of a fact that produces anxiety and strategic deception.

verses.verse_text: Gen 15:8 But he said , “O Lord God , how am I to know that I shall possess it ?”
verses.meaning: ya.da names Abram's seeking of covenantal assurance — a request for knowledge of God's promise that goes beyond verbal affirmation.

verses.verse_text: Gen 15:13 Then the Lord said to Abram , “ Know for certain that your offspring will be sojourners in a land that is not theirs and will be servants there, and they will be afflicted for four hundred years .
verses.meaning: ya.da names God's disclosure to Abram of what is coming — covenantal foreknowledge given as inner certainty.

verses.verse_text: Gen 18:19 For I have chosen him, that he may command his children and his household after him to keep the way of the Lord by doing righteousness and justice , so that the Lord may bring to Abraham what he has promised him .”
verses.meaning: ya.da here is rendered "chosen" — God's knowing of Abraham is an election knowing, the covenantal intimacy that results in appointment and purpose.

verses.verse_text: Gen 18:21 I will go down to see whether they have done altogether according to the outcry that has come to me. And if not , I will know .”
verses.meaning: ya.da names God's investigative knowing — not ignorance but the divine process of attending to and fully comprehending the situation.

verses.verse_text: Gen 19:5 And they called to Lot , “ Where are the men who came to you tonight ? Bring them out to us, that we may know them .”
verses.meaning: Names the demand for sexual access — the term identifies the men of Sodom's intention toward the visitors as a form of knowing them.

verses.verse_text: Gen 19:8 Behold , I have two daughters who have not known any man . Let me bring them out to you, and do to them as you please . Only do nothing to these men , for they have come under the shelter of my roof .”
verses.meaning: Names the virgin state — the term identifies Lot's daughters as women who have not known a man, offered in place of the visitors.

verses.verse_text: Gen 19:33 So they made their father drink wine that night . And the firstborn went in and lay with her father . He did not know when she lay down or when she arose .
verses.meaning: Names the absence of conscious awareness — the term identifies Lot's failure to know when his daughter lay down or arose because of his drunkenness.

verses.verse_text: Gen 19:35 So they made their father drink wine that night also . And the younger arose and lay with him, and he did not know when she lay down or when she arose .
verses.meaning: Names the same absence of awareness — the term identifies Lot's failure to know when the younger daughter lay with him.

verses.verse_text: Gen 20:6 Then God said to him in the dream , “ Yes , I know that you have done this in the integrity of your heart , and it was I who kept you from sinning against me. Therefore I did not let you touch her .
verses.meaning: ya.da names God's comprehensive inner knowledge of Abimelech's motives — the integrity of the heart is known to God.

verses.verse_text: Gen 20:7 Now then, return the man’s wife , for he is a prophet , so that he will pray for you , and you shall live . But if you do not return her, know that you shall surely die , you and all who are yours.”
verses.meaning: Names the certain inner knowledge that comes as a warning — the term identifies the knowledge of inevitable death that Abimelech is given if he fails to restore Sarah.

verses.verse_text: Gen 21:26 Abimelech said , “I do not know who has done this thing ; you did not tell me, and I have not heard of it until today .”
verses.meaning: Names the absence of inner awareness — the term identifies Abimelech's genuine ignorance of the well's origin, which he had neither done nor been told about.

verses.verse_text: Gen 22:12 He said , “Do not lay your hand on the boy or do anything to him, for now I know that you fear God , seeing you have not withheld your son , your only son, from me .”
verses.meaning: ya.da names God's knowing confirmed through action — the test has produced knowledge of Abraham's character. Covenantal knowing verified through obedience.

verses.verse_text: Gen 24:14 Let the young woman to whom I shall say , ‘ Please let down your jar that I may drink ,’ and who shall say , ‘ Drink , and I will water your camels ’—let her be the one whom you have appointed for your servant Isaac . By this I shall know that you have shown steadfast love to my master .”
verses.meaning: ya.da names the knowledge of God's covenantal faithfulness that the sign will confirm — recognition of steadfast love through a particular event.

verses.verse_text: Gen 24:16 The young woman was very attractive in appearance , a maiden whom no man had known . She went down to the spring and filled her jar and came up .
verses.meaning: Names the absence of sexual experience — the term identifies Rebekah as a young woman who has not known a man, establishing her virginity.

verses.verse_text: Gen 24:21 The man gazed at her in silence to learn whether the Lord had prospered his journey or not .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the discerning attention directed toward discovering God's will — attentive observation as a form of knowing.

verses.verse_text: Gen 25:27 When the boys grew up, Esau was a skillful hunter , a man of the field , while Jacob was a quiet man , dwelling in tents .
verses.meaning: Names a form of competence — the term identifies Esau as a man who knew or was skilled in hunting, characterising his practical expertise.

verses.verse_text: Gen 27:2 He said , “ Behold , I am old ; I do not know the day of my death .
verses.meaning: ya.da names Isaac's self-awareness of the limit of his knowledge — he knows he is old but not when he will die.

verses.verse_text: Gen 28:16 Then Jacob awoke from his sleep and said , “ Surely the Lord is in this place , and I did not know it.”
verses.meaning: ya.da names the absence and then presence of covenantal awareness — Jacob had not known God's presence in the place until the dream.

verses.verse_text: Gen 29:5 He said to them, “Do you know Laban the son of Nahor ?” They said , “We know him.”
verses.meaning: Names the inner recognition of a person — the term identifies the herdsmen's familiarity with Laban as the basis for Jacob's inquiry.

verses.verse_text: Gen 30:26 Give me my wives and my children for whom I have served you, that I may go , for you know the service that I have given you .”
verses.meaning: Names the acknowledged inner awareness — the term identifies Laban's own knowledge of Jacob's service as a fact Jacob appeals to in requesting release.

verses.verse_text: Gen 30:29 Jacob said to him, “ You yourself know how I have served you, and how your livestock has fared with me .
verses.meaning: Names the self-evident inner awareness — the term identifies what Laban himself knows about how Jacob served him and how the flock fared.

verses.verse_text: Gen 31:6 You know that I have served your father with all my strength ,
verses.meaning: Names the acknowledged knowledge — the term identifies what Rachel and Leah already know about Jacob's whole-hearted service to their father.

verses.verse_text: Gen 31:32 Anyone with whom you find your gods shall not live . In the presence of our kinsmen point out what I have that is yours, and take it.” Now Jacob did not know that Rachel had stolen them .
verses.meaning: Names the absence of inner awareness — the term identifies Jacob's ignorance of Rachel's theft of the household gods.

verses.verse_text: Gen 33:13 But Jacob said to him, “My lord knows that the children are frail , and that the nursing flocks and herds are a care to me. If they are driven hard for one day , all the flocks will die .
verses.meaning: Names the knowledge Jacob appeals to — the term identifies his lord's inner awareness of the frailty of the children and nursing animals as the reason for slow travel.

verses.verse_text: Gen 38:9 But Onan knew that the offspring would not be his. So whenever he went in to his brother’s wife he would waste the semen on the ground , so as not to give offspring to his brother .
verses.meaning: ya.da names Onan's knowledge of the levirate situation — moral knowledge that guides (or in this case, subverts) right action.

verses.verse_text: Gen 38:16 He turned to her at the roadside and said , “ Come , let me come in to you,” for he did not know that she was his daughter-in-law . She said , “ What will you give me, that you may come in to me ?”
verses.meaning: Names the absence of recognition — the term identifies Judah's failure to know that Tamar was his daughter-in-law when he approached her.

verses.verse_text: Gen 38:26 Then Judah identified them and said , “She is more righteous than I, since I did not give her to my son Shelah .” And he did not know her again .
verses.meaning: Names the cessation of sexual relations — the term identifies Judah's act of not knowing Tamar again after the incident was made known.

verses.verse_text: Gen 39:6 So he left all that he had in Joseph’s charge , and because of him he had no concern about anything but the food he ate . Now Joseph was handsome in form and appearance .
verses.meaning: Names the deliberate withholding of oversight — the term identifies Potiphar's choice not to know or concern himself with anything in the house beyond his food.

verses.verse_text: Gen 39:8 But he refused and said to his master’s wife , “ Behold , because of me my master has no concern about anything in the house , and he has put everything that he has in my charge .
verses.meaning: Names Potiphar's absence of oversight — the term identifies the master's inner withdrawal from concern about the household as the context for Joseph's authority.

verses.verse_text: Gen 41:21 but when they had eaten them no one would have known that they had eaten them , for they were still as ugly as at the beginning . Then I awoke .
verses.meaning: Names the absence of visible trace — the term identifies the impossibility of knowing that the gaunt cows had consumed the fat ones because they appeared unchanged.

verses.verse_text: Gen 41:31 and the plenty will be unknown in the land by reason of the famine that will follow , for it will be very severe .
verses.meaning: Names the future invisibility of prosperity — the term identifies how the coming plenty will not be known or recognised in the land because the famine will be so severe.

verses.verse_text: Gen 41:39 Then Pharaoh said to Joseph , “ Since God has shown you all this , there is none so discerning and wise as you are.
verses.meaning: ya.da names divine disclosure — God has shown Joseph, and this showing has produced knowledge beyond human capacity.

verses.verse_text: Gen 42:23 They did not know that Joseph understood them, for there was an interpreter between them .
verses.meaning: Names the brothers' absence of awareness — the term identifies their failure to know that Joseph understood their speech, since an interpreter stood between them.

verses.verse_text: Gen 42:33 Then the man , the lord of the land , said to us, ‘ By this I shall know that you are honest men: leave one of your brothers with me, and take grain for the famine of your households , and go your way.
verses.meaning: Names the inner criterion of discernment — the term identifies Joseph's stated basis for knowing whether the brothers are honest: leaving one behind.

verses.verse_text: Gen 42:34 Bring your youngest brother to me. Then I shall know that you are not spies but honest men, and I will deliver your brother to you, and you shall trade in the land .’”
verses.meaning: Names the inner criterion of verification — the term identifies knowing the brothers are not spies as the condition for releasing the imprisoned one.

verses.verse_text: Gen 43:7 They replied , “The man questioned us carefully about ourselves and our kindred , saying , ‘Is your father still alive ? Do you have another brother ?’ What we told him was in answer to these questions . Could we in any way know that he would say , ‘Bring your brother down ’?”
verses.meaning: Names the impossibility of foreknowledge — the term identifies the brothers' inability to have known in advance that Joseph would demand their youngest brother.

verses.verse_text: Gen 43:22 and we have brought other money down with us to buy food . We do not know who put our money in our sacks .”
verses.meaning: Names the absence of inner awareness — the term identifies the brothers' genuine ignorance about who placed the money in their sacks.

verses.verse_text: Gen 44:15 Joseph said to them, “ What deed is this that you have done ? Do you not know that a man like me can indeed practice divination ?”
verses.meaning: Names the capacity for insight — the term identifies Joseph's ability to practise divination as a known inner capability he appeals to rhetorically.

verses.verse_text: Gen 44:27 Then your servant my father said to us, ‘ You know that my wife bore me two sons.
verses.meaning: Names the acknowledged knowledge — the term identifies what the father himself knows: that his wife bore him two sons.

verses.verse_text: Gen 45:1 Then Joseph could not control himself before all those who stood by him. He cried , “Make everyone go out from me.” So no one stayed with him when Joseph made himself known to his brothers .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the relational self-disclosure — Joseph's revealing of himself as a covenantal act of reconciliation.

verses.verse_text: Gen 47:6 The land of Egypt is before you. Settle your father and your brothers in the best of the land . Let them settle in the land of Goshen , and if you know any able men among them, put them in charge of my livestock .”
verses.meaning: Names the practical inner knowledge — the term identifies the instruction to identify able men among the brothers based on known competence.

verses.verse_text: Gen 48:19 But his father refused and said , “I know , my son , I know . He also shall become a people , and he also shall be great . Nevertheless , his younger brother shall be greater than he, and his offspring shall become a multitude of nations .”
verses.meaning: ya.da names Jacob's prophetic self-knowledge — he knows what he is doing in crossing his hands. The knowing is inner conviction about what God has shown him.

verses.verse_text: Exo 1:8 Now there arose a new king over Egypt , who did not know Joseph .
verses.meaning: Names the absence of historical awareness — the term identifies the new king's failure to know Joseph, which underlies his oppression of Israel.

verses.verse_text: Exo 2:4 And his sister stood at a distance to know what would be done to him .
verses.meaning: Names the inner purpose of watching — the term identifies the sister's reason for positioning herself: to know what would happen to the child.

verses.verse_text: Exo 2:14 He answered , “ Who made you a prince and a judge over us? Do you mean to kill me as you killed the Egyptian ?” Then Moses was afraid , and thought , “ Surely the thing is known .”
verses.meaning: Names the unwilling inner realisation — the term identifies Moses' recognition that the killing had become known, prompting his fear.

verses.verse_text: Exo 2:25 God saw the people of Israel —and God knew .
verses.meaning: ya.da names God's covenantal attending — the knowing is relational recognition of his covenant people in their suffering.

verses.verse_text: Exo 3:7 Then the Lord said , “I have surely seen the affliction of my people who are in Egypt and have heard their cry because of their taskmasters . I know their sufferings ,
verses.meaning: ya.da names God's comprehensive inner knowledge of Israel's affliction — not distant observation but intimate knowing of their condition.

verses.verse_text: Exo 3:19 But I know that the king of Egypt will not let you go unless compelled by a mighty hand .
verses.meaning: ya.da names God's foreknowing of Pharaoh's resistance — inner knowledge of the king's character and what will be required.

verses.verse_text: Exo 4:14 Then the anger of the Lord was kindled against Moses and he said , “Is there not Aaron , your brother , the Levite ? I know that he can speak well . Behold, he is coming out to meet you, and when he sees you, he will be glad in his heart .
verses.meaning: ya.da names God's comprehensive knowledge of Aaron's capacity — inner knowledge of another person's gift.

verses.verse_text: Exo 5:2 But Pharaoh said , “Who is the Lord , that I should obey his voice and let Israel go ? I do not know the Lord , and moreover , I will not let Israel go .”
verses.meaning: ya.da names Pharaoh's refusal of covenantal knowing — his denial of the Lord is both ignorance and defiance.

verses.verse_text: Num 10:31 And he said , “ Please do not leave us, for you know where we should camp in the wilderness , and you will serve as eyes for us.
verses.meaning: Names the practical navigational knowledge — the term identifies Hobab's inner familiarity with the wilderness as the resource Israel needs for camping.

verses.verse_text: Num 11:16 Then the Lord said to Moses , “ Gather for me seventy men of the elders of Israel , whom you know to be the elders of the people and officers over them, and bring them to the tent of meeting , and let them take their stand there with you .
verses.meaning: Names the inner criterion of selection — the term identifies Moses' prior knowledge of the elders as the basis for God's instruction to gather them.

verses.verse_text: Num 12:6 And he said , “ Hear my words : If there is a prophet among you, I the Lord make myself known to him in a vision ; I speak with him in a dream .
verses.meaning: ya.da names God's self-disclosure to prophets — revelatory knowing through vision and dream. The mode of God making himself known.

verses.verse_text: Num 14:31 But your little ones , who you said would become a prey , I will bring in , and they shall know the land that you have rejected .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the experiential covenantal knowing of the land — the children will know it by inhabiting it, which the faithless generation refused.

verses.verse_text: Num 14:34 According to the number of the days in which you spied out the land , forty days , a year for each day , you shall bear your iniquity forty years , and you shall know my displeasure .’
verses.meaning: ya.da names the experiential knowledge of God's judgment — the forty years will produce knowing of what it means to have God against them.

verses.verse_text: Num 16:5 and he said to Korah and all his company , “ In the morning the Lord will show who is his, and who is holy , and will bring him near to him. The one whom he chooses he will bring near to him .
verses.meaning: ya.da names divine self-disclosure through vindicating action — God will make known who belongs to him.

verses.verse_text: Num 16:28 And Moses said , “ Hereby you shall know that the Lord has sent me to do all these works , and that it has not been of my own accord .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the knowledge of divine authority that the sign will produce — covenantal self-authentication through event.

verses.verse_text: Num 16:30 But if the Lord creates something new , and the ground opens its mouth and swallows them up with all that belongs to them, and they go down alive into Sheol , then you shall know that these men have despised the Lord .”
verses.meaning: ya.da names the knowledge that the judgment will produce — the event makes the inner spiritual reality visible and knowable.

verses.verse_text: Num 20:14 Moses sent messengers from Kadesh to the king of Edom : “ Thus says your brother Israel : You know all the hardship that we have met :
verses.meaning: Names the shared historical knowledge — the term identifies Edom's inner awareness of all the hardship Israel has experienced as the basis for the appeal.

verses.verse_text: Num 22:6 Come now , curse this people for me, since they are too mighty for me. Perhaps I shall be able to defeat them and drive them from the land , for I know that he whom you bless is blessed , and he whom you curse is cursed .”
verses.meaning: ya.da names Balak's knowledge of Balaam's covenantal efficacy — recognition of the power God has given him.

verses.verse_text: Num 22:19 So you, too , please stay here tonight , that I may know what more the Lord will say to me .”
verses.meaning: ya.da names Balaam's seeking of divine disclosure — waiting for God to make his will known.

verses.verse_text: Num 22:34 Then Balaam said to the angel of the Lord , “I have sinned , for I did not know that you stood in the road against me. Now therefore, if it is evil in your sight , I will turn back .”
verses.meaning: Names the absence of inner awareness — the term identifies Balaam's genuine ignorance that the angel stood in his way as the ground of his admission of sin.

verses.verse_text: Num 24:16 the oracle of him who hears the words of God , and knows the knowledge of the Most High , who sees the vision of the Almighty , falling down with his eyes uncovered :
verses.meaning: ya.da names the seer's inner knowing of divine knowledge — same verse, same observation about the misassignment.

verses.verse_text: Num 31:17 Now therefore, kill every male among the little ones , and kill every woman who has known man by lying with him .
verses.meaning: Names the criterion of sexual experience — the term identifies women who have known a man sexually as those to be put to death.

verses.verse_text: Num 31:18 But all the young girls who have not known man by lying with him keep alive for yourselves .
verses.meaning: Names the criterion of virginity — the term identifies young girls who have not known a man sexually as those to be kept alive.

verses.verse_text: Num 31:35 and 32,000 persons in all , women who had not known man by lying with him.
verses.meaning: Names the criterion of virginity — the term identifies the 32,000 persons as women who have not known a man sexually.

verses.verse_text: Num 32:23 But if you will not do so , behold , you have sinned against the Lord , and be sure your sin will find you out.
verses.meaning: ya.da names the moral certainty being asserted — this is knowledge of moral consequence.

verses.verse_text: Deu 1:39 And as for your little ones , who you said would become a prey , and your children , who today have no knowledge of good or evil , they shall go in there . And to them I will give it , and they shall possess it .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the moral knowledge that young children lack — an inner developmental state, not culpable ignorance.

verses.verse_text: Deu 2:7 For the Lord your God has blessed you in all the work of your hands . He knows your going through this great wilderness . These forty years the Lord your God has been with you. You have lacked nothing .”’
verses.meaning: ya.da names God's comprehensive covenantal attending — he knows the journey of his people with them.

verses.verse_text: Deu 3:19 Only your wives , your little ones , and your livestock (I know that you have much livestock ) shall remain in the cities that I have given you ,
verses.meaning: Names God's inner awareness — the term identifies God's knowledge of the size of Israel's livestock as the basis for the exemption granted.

verses.verse_text: Deu 4:9 “ Only take care , and keep your soul diligently , lest you forget the things that your eyes have seen , and lest they depart from your heart all the days of your life . Make them known to your children and your children’s children —
verses.meaning: ya.da names the transmission of covenantal memory — what has been known must be made known to the next generation.

verses.verse_text: Deu 4:35 To you it was shown , that you might know that the Lord is God ; there is no other besides him .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the purpose of divine self-disclosure — the events of the Exodus were so that Israel would know God's uniqueness. Covenantal knowing through experience.

verses.verse_text: Deu 4:39 know therefore today , and lay it to your heart , that the Lord is God in heaven above and on the earth beneath ; there is no other .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the inner appropriation of covenantal truth — knowing laid to the heart as the act of receiving divine reality inwardly.

verses.verse_text: Deu 7:9 Know therefore that the Lord your God is God , the faithful God who keeps covenant and steadfast love with those who love him and keep his commandments , to a thousand generations ,
verses.meaning: ya.da names covenantal knowledge of God's character — the command to know is a call to inner recognition of who God is.

verses.verse_text: Deu 7:15 And the Lord will take away from you all sickness , and none of the evil diseases of Egypt , which you knew , will he inflict on you, but he will lay them on all who hate you .
verses.meaning: Names the shared inner experience — the term identifies the evil diseases of Egypt as things Israel has known through direct experience.

verses.verse_text: Deu 8:2 And you shall remember the whole way that the Lord your God has led you these forty years in the wilderness , that he might humble you, testing you to know what was in your heart , whether you would keep his commandments or not .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the knowledge God seeks through the test — not absent knowledge but the process of making the heart's character visible.

verses.verse_text: Deu 8:3 And he humbled you and let you hunger and fed you with manna , which you did not know , nor did your fathers know , that he might make you know that man does not live by bread alone , but man lives by every word that comes from the mouth of the Lord .
verses.meaning: ya.da names God's pedagogical disclosure through the manna — the humbling and feeding produce inner knowledge of dependence on God's word.

verses.verse_text: Deu 8:5 Know then in your heart that, as a man disciplines his son , the Lord your God disciplines you .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the inner appropriation of understanding God's discipline — knowing in the heart as covenantal receiving.

verses.verse_text: Deu 8:16 who fed you in the wilderness with manna that your fathers did not know , that he might humble you and test you, to do you good in the end .
verses.meaning: ya.da names God's purpose in the manna — the testing produces knowing of dependence and produces good.

verses.verse_text: Deu 9:2 a people great and tall , the sons of the Anakim , whom you know , and of whom you have heard it said, ‘ Who can stand before the sons of Anak ?’
verses.meaning: Names prior knowledge through testimony — the term identifies the people's existing awareness of the Anakim through report as the basis of Moses' challenge.

verses.verse_text: Deu 9:3 Know therefore today that he who goes over before you as a consuming fire is the Lord your God . He will destroy them and subdue them before you. So you shall drive them out and make them perish quickly , as the Lord has promised you .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal knowledge being commanded — inner recognition of God as the one fighting for Israel.

verses.verse_text: Deu 9:6 “ Know , therefore, that the Lord your God is not giving you this good land to possess because of your righteousness , for you are a stubborn people .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the corrective knowledge Israel must hold — inner recognition that grace, not merit, is the ground of the gift.

verses.verse_text: Deu 9:24 You have been rebellious against the Lord from the day that I knew you .
verses.meaning: ya.da names Moses's covenantal knowing of Israel — the relational knowing from which he speaks.

verses.verse_text: Deu 11:2 And consider today ( since I am not speaking to your children who have not known or seen it), consider the discipline of the Lord your God , his greatness , his mighty hand and his outstretched arm ,
verses.meaning: ya.da names the remembering and considering that keeps covenantal knowledge alive — the mighty acts known experientially.

verses.verse_text: Deu 11:28 and the curse , if you do not obey the commandments of the Lord your God , but turn aside from the way that I am commanding you today , to go after other gods that you have not known .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the absence of covenantal relationship — gods that Israel has no knowing with, in contrast to the Lord they do know.

verses.verse_text: Deu 13:2 and the sign or wonder that he tells you comes to pass , and if he says , ‘Let us go after other gods ,’ which you have not known , ‘and let us serve them ,’
verses.meaning: ya.da names the relational absence — the false gods have no covenant with Israel, no history of knowing.

verses.verse_text: Deu 13:3 you shall not listen to the words of that prophet or that dreamer of dreams . For the Lord your God is testing you, to know whether you love the Lord your God with all your heart and with all your soul .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the knowledge that the test produces — the inner orientation of love is made visible through the test.

verses.verse_text: Deu 13:6 “ If your brother , the son of your mother , or your son or your daughter or the wife you embrace or your friend who is as your own soul entices you secretly , saying , ‘Let us go and serve other gods ,’ which neither you nor your fathers have known ,
verses.meaning: ya.da names the absence of covenantal relationship with the foreign gods.

verses.verse_text: Deu 13:13 that certain worthless fellows have gone out among you and have drawn away the inhabitants of their city , saying , ‘Let us go and serve other gods ,’ which you have not known ,
verses.meaning: ya.da names the same covenantal absence — these gods have no claim on Israel through knowing.

verses.verse_text: Deu 18:21 And if you say in your heart , ‘ How may we know the word that the Lord has not spoken ?’—
verses.meaning: ya.da names the discernment of authentic divine disclosure — recognising what is genuinely from God.

verses.verse_text: Deu 20:20 Only the trees that you know are not trees for food you may destroy and cut down , that you may build siegeworks against the city that makes war with you, until it falls .
verses.meaning: Names the criterion of identification — the term identifies knowing which trees are non-food trees as the inner basis for lawful destruction in siege.

verses.verse_text: Deu 21:1 “ If in the land that the Lord your God is giving you to possess someone is found slain , lying in the open country , and it is not known who killed him ,
verses.meaning: Names the absence of knowledge as a legal condition — the term identifies the unknown identity of the killer as the situation that triggers the heifer ritual.

verses.verse_text: Deu 22:2 And if he does not live near you and you do not know who he is, you shall bring it home to your house , and it shall stay with you until your brother seeks it. Then you shall restore it to him .
verses.meaning: Names the absence of familiarity — the term identifies not knowing who the owner is as the condition that requires temporary custody of the lost animal.

verses.verse_text: Deu 28:33 A nation that you have not known shall eat up the fruit of your ground and of all your labors , and you shall be only oppressed and crushed continually ,
verses.meaning: Names the absence of prior acquaintance — the term identifies the consuming nation as one Israel has not known, intensifying the alienation of the curse.

verses.verse_text: Deu 28:36 “The Lord will bring you and your king whom you set over you to a nation that neither you nor your fathers have known . And there you shall serve other gods of wood and stone .
verses.meaning: Names the absence of prior acquaintance — the term identifies the nation of exile as one neither Israel nor their fathers have known, deepening the estrangement.

verses.verse_text: Deu 28:64 “And the Lord will scatter you among all peoples , from one end of the earth to the other , and there you shall serve other gods of wood and stone , which neither you nor your fathers have known .
verses.meaning: Names the absence of prior acquaintance — the term identifies the gods of exile as those neither Israel nor their fathers have known.

verses.verse_text: Deu 29:4 But to this day the Lord has not given you a heart to understand or eyes to see or ears to hear .
verses.meaning: ya.da names covenantal knowing as a divine gift withheld — the heart to understand/know must be given; it is not naturally present.

verses.verse_text: Deu 29:6 You have not eaten bread , and you have not drunk wine or strong drink , that you may know that I am the Lord your God .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the goal of the wilderness provision — the events produce inner covenantal recognition of God's identity.

verses.verse_text: Deu 29:16 “ You know how we lived in the land of Egypt , and how we came through the midst of the nations through which you passed .
verses.meaning: Names the shared experiential knowledge — the term identifies Israel's firsthand inner knowledge of life in Egypt as the basis for the covenant appeal.

verses.verse_text: Deu 29:26 and went and served other gods and worshiped them, gods whom they had not known and whom he had not allotted to them .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the relational absence — these gods have no covenant history with Israel; they have not been known or given.

verses.verse_text: Deu 31:13 and that their children , who have not known it, may hear and learn to fear the Lord your God , as long as you live in the land that you are going over the Jordan to possess .”
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal knowing that the new generation lacks and must receive — not yet knowing the Lord as their parents did.

verses.verse_text: Deu 31:21 And when many evils and troubles have come upon them, this song shall confront them as a witness ( for it will live unforgotten in the mouths of their offspring ). For I know what they are inclined to do even today , before I have brought them into the land that I swore to give.”
verses.meaning: ya.da names God's comprehensive foreknowing of Israel's future rebellion — the inner tendency of the people known by God before the event.

verses.verse_text: Deu 31:27 For I know how rebellious and stubborn you are. Behold , even today while I am yet alive with you, you have been rebellious against the Lord . How much more after my death !
verses.meaning: ya.da names God's knowing of Israel's character — comprehensive knowledge of their inner disposition.

verses.verse_text: Deu 31:29 For I know that after my death you will surely act corruptly and turn aside from the way that I have commanded you. And in the days to come evil will befall you, because you will do what is evil in the sight of the Lord , provoking him to anger through the work of your hands .”
verses.meaning: ya.da names Moses's prophetic knowledge of what will happen — inner conviction about future covenant unfaithfulness.

verses.verse_text: Deu 32:17 They sacrificed to demons that were not God , to gods they had never known , to new gods that had come recently , whom your fathers had never dreaded .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the relational absence with the new gods — no history of knowing, no covenant, no claim.

verses.verse_text: Deu 33:9 who said of his father and mother , ‘I regard them not ’; he disowned his brothers and ignored his children . For they observed your word and kept your covenant .
verses.meaning: Names the deliberate setting aside of inner recognition — the term identifies Levi's act of not regarding or knowing his own family as the mark of covenant loyalty.

verses.verse_text: Deu 34:6 and he buried him in the valley in the land of Moab opposite Beth-peor ; but no one knows the place of his burial to this day .
verses.meaning: Names the absence of locatable knowledge — the term identifies the fact that no one knows the place of Moses' burial to the present day.

verses.verse_text: Deu 34:10 And there has not arisen a prophet since in Israel like Moses , whom the Lord knew face to face ,
verses.meaning: ya.da names the intimate covenantal knowing between God and Moses — face to face knowing as the highest form of prophetic relationship.

verses.verse_text: Jos 2:4 But the woman had taken the two men and hidden them. And she said , “ True , the men came to me, but I did not know where they were from.
verses.meaning: Names the claimed absence of knowledge — the term identifies Rahab's stated ignorance of where the men came from as her cover for concealing them.

verses.verse_text: Jos 2:5 And when the gate was about to be closed at dark , the men went out. I do not know where the men went . Pursue them quickly , for you will overtake them .”
verses.meaning: Names the claimed absence of directional knowledge — the term identifies Rahab's stated ignorance of where the men went as part of her misdirection.

verses.verse_text: Jos 2:9 and said to the men , “I know that the Lord has given you the land , and that the fear of you has fallen upon us, and that all the inhabitants of the land melt away before you.
verses.meaning: ya.da names Rahab's inner covenantal recognition — she knows what God has done and what it means for the city. Knowledge producing allegiance.

verses.verse_text: Jos 3:4 Yet there shall be a distance between you and it, about 2,000 cubits in length . Do not come near it, in order that you may know the way you shall go , for you have not passed this way before .”
verses.meaning: ya.da names the knowledge of God's leading that following the ark will produce — a new path made known through divine guidance.

verses.verse_text: Jos 3:7 The Lord said to Joshua , “ Today I will begin to exalt you in the sight of all Israel , that they may know that, as I was with Moses , so I will be with you .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the knowledge of God's presence with Joshua that the exalting sign will produce — covenantal self-disclosure through event.

verses.verse_text: Jos 3:10 And Joshua said , “ Here is how you shall know that the living God is among you and that he will without fail drive out from before you the Canaanites , the Hittites , the Hivites , the Perizzites , the Girgashites , the Amorites , and the Jebusites .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the knowledge of God's presence that the crossing will demonstrate — covenantal knowing through miraculous event.

verses.verse_text: Jos 4:22 then you shall let your children know , ‘ Israel passed over this Jordan on dry ground .’
verses.meaning: ya.da names the transmission of covenantal memory — making known what God did so the next generation knows him through the telling.

verses.verse_text: Jos 4:24 so that all the peoples of the earth may know that the hand of the Lord is mighty , that you may fear the Lord your God forever .”
verses.meaning: ya.da names the universal covenantal recognition that the miracle aims to produce — all peoples knowing God's power.

verses.verse_text: Jos 8:14 And as soon as the king of Ai saw this, he and all his people , the men of the city , hurried and went out early to the appointed place toward the Arabah to meet Israel in battle . But he did not know that there was an ambush against him behind the city .
verses.meaning: Names the absence of tactical awareness — the term identifies the king of Ai's failure to know that an ambush had been set behind the city.

verses.verse_text: Jos 14:6 Then the people of Judah came to Joshua at Gilgal . And Caleb the son of Jephunneh the Kenizzite said to him, “ You know what the Lord said to Moses the man of God in Kadesh-barnea concerning you and me .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the knowledge of a divine word given in the past — Caleb appeals to Joshua's shared covenantal memory.

verses.verse_text: Jos 22:22 “The Mighty One , God , the Lord ! The Mighty One , God , the Lord ! He knows ; and let Israel itself know ! If it was in rebellion or in breach of faith against the Lord , do not spare us today
verses.meaning: ya.da names God's comprehensive covenantal knowing — the appeal to God as the one who knows the inner truth of the situation.

verses.verse_text: Jos 22:31 And Phinehas the son of Eleazar the priest said to the people of Reuben and the people of Gad and the people of Manasseh , “ Today we know that the Lord is in our midst , because you have not committed this breach of faith against the Lord . Now you have delivered the people of Israel from the hand of the Lord .”
verses.meaning: ya.da names the knowledge of God's covenant presence produced by the clarification — relational recognition restored.

verses.verse_text: Jos 23:13 know for certain that the Lord your God will no longer drive out these nations before you, but they shall be a snare and a trap for you, a whip on your sides and thorns in your eyes , until you perish from off this good ground that the Lord your God has given you.
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal knowledge of consequence — what Israel must hold inwardly as certain.

verses.verse_text: Jos 23:14 “And now I am about to go the way of all the earth , and you know in your hearts and souls , all of you, that not one word has failed of all the good things that the Lord your God promised concerning you. All have come to pass for you; not one of them has failed .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal inner knowing — held in the heart and soul, confirmed by experience.

verses.verse_text: Jos 24:31 Israel served the Lord all the days of Joshua , and all the days of the elders who outlived Joshua and had known all the work that the Lord did for Israel .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal experiential knowledge of God's mighty acts — those who had lived through them and knew God through them.

verses.verse_text: Judg 2:10 And all that generation also were gathered to their fathers . And there arose another generation after them who did not know the Lord or the work that he had done for Israel .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal knowing broken between generations — the new generation has no experiential or transmitted knowledge of God.

verses.verse_text: Judg 3:1 Now these are the nations that the Lord left , to test Israel by them, that is, all in Israel who had not experienced all the wars in Canaan .
verses.meaning: Names the absence of experiential knowledge — the term identifies the lack of experience of warfare in Canaan as the condition of the generation left to be tested.

verses.verse_text: Judg 3:2 It was only in order that the generations of the people of Israel might know war, to teach war to those who had not known it before .
verses.meaning: Names the inner acquisition of practical knowledge — the term identifies teaching war to those who had not known it as the purpose of leaving the nations.

verses.verse_text: Judg 3:4 They were for the testing of Israel , to know whether Israel would obey the commandments of the Lord , which he commanded their fathers by the hand of Moses .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the knowledge that the testing will produce — covenantal obedience made visible and knowable.

verses.verse_text: Judg 6:37 behold , I am laying a fleece of wool on the threshing floor . If there is dew on the fleece alone , and it is dry on all the ground , then I shall know that you will save Israel by my hand , as you have said .”
verses.meaning: ya.da names Gideon's seeking of assurance of God's covenantal commitment — the sign will produce inner certainty.

verses.verse_text: Judg 8:16 And he took the elders of the city , and he took thorns of the wilderness and briers and with them taught the men of Succoth a lesson.
verses.meaning: Names the act of teaching through painful discipline — the term identifies Gideon's instruction of the men of Succoth by means of thorns and briers.

verses.verse_text: Judg 11:39 And at the end of two months , she returned to her father , who did with her according to his vow that he had made . She had never known a man , and it became a custom in Israel
verses.meaning: Names the virgin state — the term identifies Jephthah's daughter as a woman who had never known a man, which gives the lament its particular pathos.

verses.verse_text: Judg 13:16 And the angel of the Lord said to Manoah , “If you detain me, I will not eat of your food . But if you prepare a burnt offering , then offer it to the Lord .” (For Manoah did not know that he was the angel of the Lord .)
verses.meaning: Names the absence of recognition — the term identifies Manoah's failure to know that his visitor was the angel of the Lord.

verses.verse_text: Judg 13:21 The angel of the Lord appeared no more to Manoah and to his wife . Then Manoah knew that he was the angel of the Lord .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal recognition produced by the ascending flame — seeing the divine act produces knowing of the divine identity.

verses.verse_text: Judg 14:4 His father and mother did not know that it was from the Lord , for he was seeking an opportunity against the Philistines . At that time the Philistines ruled over Israel .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the absence of covenantal perception — they could not see God's purpose in Samson's choice.

verses.verse_text: Judg 15:11 Then 3,000 men of Judah went down to the cleft of the rock of Etam , and said to Samson , “Do you not know that the Philistines are rulers over us? What then is this that you have done to us?” And he said to them, “ As they did to me, so have I done to them .”
verses.meaning: Names the acknowledged knowledge — the term identifies the Philistines' rule as a fact the men of Judah assume Samson already knows.

verses.verse_text: Judg 16:9 Now she had men lying in ambush in an inner chamber . And she said to him, “The Philistines are upon you, Samson !” But he snapped the bowstrings , as a thread of flax snaps when it touches the fire . So the secret of his strength was not known .
verses.meaning: Names the absence of disclosure — the term identifies the secret of Samson's strength as something not made known, despite the trap set.

verses.verse_text: Judg 16:20 And she said , “The Philistines are upon you, Samson !” And he awoke from his sleep and said , “I will go out as at other times and shake myself free .” But he did not know that the Lord had left him .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal absence that Samson is unaware of — the Lord's departure is an inner spiritual reality he cannot perceive.

verses.verse_text: Judg 17:13 Then Micah said , “ Now I know that the Lord will prosper me, because I have a Levite as priest .”
verses.meaning: ya.da names Micah's inner conviction about God's favour — a covenantal knowing based on having a Levite priest (though misguided).

verses.verse_text: Judg 18:5 And they said to him, “ Inquire of God , please, that we may know whether the journey on which we are setting out will succeed .”
verses.meaning: ya.da names seeking divine disclosure about the future — asking God to make known his will for the journey.

verses.verse_text: Judg 18:14 Then the five men who had gone to scout out the country of Laish said to their brothers , “Do you know that in these houses there are an ephod , household gods , a carved image , and a metal image ? Now therefore consider what you will do .”
verses.meaning: Names the inner awareness of what exists — the term identifies the five scouts' knowledge of the cultic objects in the house as the basis for their counsel.

verses.verse_text: Judg 19:22 As they were making their hearts merry , behold , the men of the city , worthless fellows , surrounded the house , beating on the door . And they said to the old man , the master of the house , “ Bring out the man who came into your house , that we may know him .”
verses.meaning: Names the demand for sexual access — the term identifies the men of Gibeah's intention toward the Levite as a form of knowing him.

verses.verse_text: Judg 19:25 But the men would not listen to him. So the man seized his concubine and made her go out to them. And they knew her and abused her all night until the morning . And as the dawn began to break , they let her go .
verses.meaning: Names the sexual act of violation — the term identifies the men's act of knowing and abusing the concubine through the night.

verses.verse_text: Judg 20:34 And there came against Gibeah 10,000 chosen men out of all Israel , and the battle was hard , but the Benjaminites did not know that disaster was close upon them.
verses.meaning: Names the absence of tactical awareness — the term identifies the Benjaminites' failure to know that disaster was close upon them.

verses.verse_text: Judg 21:11 This is what you shall do : every male and every woman that has lain with a male you shall devote to destruction .”
verses.meaning: Names the criterion of sexual experience — the term identifies women who have lain with a male as those to be devoted to destruction.

verses.verse_text: Judg 21:12 And they found among the inhabitants of Jabesh-gilead 400 young virgins who had not known a man by lying with him , and they brought them to the camp at Shiloh , which is in the land of Canaan .
verses.meaning: Names the criterion of virginity — the term identifies the 400 young women as those who had not known a man by lying with him.

verses.verse_text: Rut 2:11 But Boaz answered her, “ All that you have done for your mother-in-law since the death of your husband has been fully told to me, and how you left your father and mother and your native land and came to a people that you did not know before .
verses.meaning: Names the absence of prior acquaintance — the term identifies the people of Bethlehem as a community Ruth came to without prior knowledge of them.

verses.verse_text: Rut 3:3 Wash therefore and anoint yourself , and put on your cloak and go down to the threshing floor , but do not make yourself known to the man until he has finished eating and drinking .
verses.meaning: Names the deliberate withholding of recognition — the term identifies Ruth's instruction not to make herself known to Boaz until he has finished eating.

verses.verse_text: Rut 3:4 But when he lies down , observe the place where he lies . Then go and uncover his feet and lie down , and he will tell you what to do .”
verses.meaning: Names the act of observation — the term identifies Ruth's instruction to note the place where Boaz lies down, as the basis for the subsequent action.

verses.verse_text: Rut 3:11 And now , my daughter , do not fear . I will do for you all that you ask , for all my fellow townsmen know that you are a worthy woman .
verses.meaning: Names the communal inner knowledge — the term identifies the townspeople's shared awareness that Ruth is a worthy woman.

verses.verse_text: Rut 3:14 So she lay at his feet until the morning , but arose before one could recognize another . And he said , “Let it not be known that the woman came to the threshing floor .”
verses.meaning: Names the desired absence of communal knowledge — the term identifies the wish that Ruth's visit to the threshing floor not become generally known.

verses.verse_text: Rut 3:18 She replied , “ Wait , my daughter , until you learn how the matter turns out , for the man will not rest but will settle the matter today .”
verses.meaning: Names the outcome Ruth is to wait to learn — the term identifies Boaz's settlement of the matter as the information she needs to know before acting.

verses.verse_text: Rut 4:4 So I thought I would tell you of it and say , ‘ Buy it in the presence of those sitting here and in the presence of the elders of my people .’ If you will redeem it, redeem it. But if you will not , tell me, that I may know , for there is no one besides you to redeem it, and I come after you.” And he said , “ I will redeem it.”
verses.meaning: Names the goal of the inquiry — the term identifies the need to know whether the nearer redeemer will act, so that the order of redemption can be determined.

verses.verse_text: 1Sa 1:19 They rose early in the morning and worshiped before the Lord ; then they went back to their house at Ramah . And Elkanah knew Hannah his wife , and the Lord remembered her.
verses.meaning: Names the sexual act — the term identifies Elkanah's intimate union with Hannah, after which the Lord remembered her.

verses.verse_text: 1Sa 2:12 Now the sons of Eli were worthless men . They did not know the Lord .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the absence of covenantal relationship — Eli's sons had no inner knowing of the Lord, which is why they profaned the offering.

verses.verse_text: 1Sa 3:7 Now Samuel did not yet know the Lord , and the word of the Lord had not yet been revealed to him.
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal knowing that had not yet begun — the knowing of God is tied to the receiving of his word.

verses.verse_text: 1Sa 3:13 And I declare to him that I am about to punish his house forever , for the iniquity that he knew , because his sons were blaspheming God, and he did not restrain them .
verses.meaning: ya.da names Eli's knowledge of his sons' sin — the moral knowledge that obligated action and was not acted upon. Knowing without responding.

verses.verse_text: 1Sa 3:20 And all Israel from Dan to Beersheba knew that Samuel was established as a prophet of the Lord .
verses.meaning: Names the communal recognition — the term identifies the acknowledgement by all Israel that Samuel was established as a prophet.

verses.verse_text: 1Sa 4:6 And when the Philistines heard the noise of the shouting , they said , “What does this great shouting in the camp of the Hebrews mean ?” And when they learned that the ark of the Lord had come to the camp ,
verses.meaning: Names the inner recognition of a fact — the term identifies the Philistines' learning that the ark of the Lord had arrived in the camp.

verses.verse_text: 1Sa 6:2 And the Philistines called for the priests and the diviners and said , “What shall we do with the ark of the Lord ? Tell us with what we shall send it to its place .”
verses.meaning: ya.da names the knowledge being sought — practical moral-religious knowledge of how to act rightly with the ark.

verses.verse_text: 1Sa 6:3 They said , “If you send away the ark of the God of Israel , do not send it empty , but by all means return him a guilt offering . Then you will be healed , and it will be known to you why his hand does not turn away from you .”
verses.meaning: Names the desired inner recognition — the term identifies what will become known to the Philistines — the reason God's hand has not turned away — if they return the ark with a guilt offering.

verses.verse_text: 1Sa 6:9 and watch . If it goes up on the way to its own land , to Beth-shemesh , then it is he who has done us this great harm , but if not , then we shall know that it is not his hand that struck us ; it happened to us by coincidence .”
verses.meaning: Names the inner criterion of discernment — the term identifies the test by which the Philistines will know whether their affliction is divine or coincidental.

verses.verse_text: 1Sa 10:8 Then go down before me to Gilgal . And behold , I am coming down to you to offer burnt offerings and to sacrifice peace offerings . Seven days you shall wait , until I come to you and show you what you shall do .”
verses.meaning: Names the communicated inner direction — the term identifies what God will show Samuel to do, framed as knowledge to be imparted.

verses.verse_text: 1Sa 10:11 And when all who knew him previously saw how he prophesied with the prophets , the people said to one another , “ What has come over the son of Kish ? Is Saul also among the prophets ?”
verses.meaning: Names the prior acquaintance — the term identifies all who had previously known Saul as the audience shocked by his prophesying.

verses.verse_text: 1Sa 12:17 Is it not wheat harvest today ? I will call upon the Lord , that he may send thunder and rain . And you shall know and see that your wickedness is great , which you have done in the sight of the Lord , in asking for yourselves a king .”
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal knowledge of sin that the sign will produce — the people will know their own wrongdoing through God's response.

verses.verse_text: 1Sa 14:3 including Ahijah the son of Ahitub , Ichabod’s brother , son of Phinehas , son of Eli , the priest of the Lord in Shiloh , wearing an ephod . And the people did not know that Jonathan had gone .
verses.meaning: Names the absence of inner awareness — the term identifies the people's failure to know that Jonathan had gone out to fight.

verses.verse_text: 1Sa 14:12 And the men of the garrison hailed Jonathan and his armor-bearer and said , “Come up to us, and we will show you a thing .” And Jonathan said to his armor-bearer , “ Come up after me, for the Lord has given them into the hand of Israel .”
verses.meaning: Names the communicated disclosure — the term identifies the garrison's offer to show Jonathan something as the basis for his bold advance.

verses.verse_text: 1Sa 14:38 And Saul said , “ Come here , all you leaders of the people , and know and see how this sin has arisen today .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the moral knowledge of what has happened — discerning the source of the sin through inquiry.

verses.verse_text: 1Sa 16:3 And invite Jesse to the sacrifice , and I will show you what you shall do . And you shall anoint for me him whom I declare to you .”
verses.meaning: ya.da names divine disclosure of the specific act required — God will make known through the occasion what Samuel must do.

verses.verse_text: 1Sa 16:16 Let our lord now command your servants who are before you to seek out a man who is skillful in playing the lyre , and when the harmful spirit from God is upon you, he will play it, and you will be well .”
verses.meaning: Names the practical inner skill — the term identifies being skilled in playing the lyre as the inner competence required for the one to be sought.

verses.verse_text: Neh 2:16 And the officials did not know where I had gone or what I was doing , and I had not yet told the Jews , the priests , the nobles , the officials , and the rest who were to do the work .
verses.meaning: Names the absence of inner awareness — the term identifies the officials' failure to know where Nehemiah had gone or what he was doing.

verses.verse_text: Neh 4:11 And our enemies said , “They will not know or see till we come among them and kill them and stop the work .”
verses.meaning: Names the anticipated inner awareness that the enemies seek to prevent — the term identifies the enemies' plan to strike before the defenders can know or see them coming.

verses.verse_text: Neh 4:15 When our enemies heard that it was known to us and that God had frustrated their plan , we all returned to the wall , each to his work .
verses.meaning: Names the communal inner awareness — the term identifies the enemies' learning that their plan had become known to Nehemiah and had been frustrated by God.

verses.verse_text: Neh 6:16 And when all our enemies heard of it, all the nations around us were afraid and fell greatly in their own esteem , for they perceived that this work had been accomplished with the help of our God .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal recognition of divine action — the enemies perceive God's involvement in the work.

verses.verse_text: Neh 8:12 And all the people went their way to eat and drink and to send portions and to make great rejoicing , because they had understood the words that were declared to them .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the declaration of the words — making known what was being read.

verses.verse_text: Neh 9:10 and performed signs and wonders against Pharaoh and all his servants and all the people of his land , for you knew that they acted arrogantly against our fathers. And you made a name for yourself, as it is to this day .
verses.meaning: ya.da names God's comprehensive knowledge of Pharaoh's arrogance — inner covenantal knowing of the oppressor's character and act.

verses.verse_text: Neh 9:14 and you made known to them your holy Sabbath and commanded them commandments and statutes and a law by Moses your servant .
verses.meaning: ya.da names God's revelatory self-disclosure — making the Sabbath known as a gift of divine self-revelation.

verses.verse_text: Neh 10:28 “The rest of the people , the priests , the Levites , the gatekeepers , the singers , the temple servants , and all who have separated themselves from the peoples of the lands to the Law of God , their wives , their sons , their daughters , all who have knowledge and understanding ,
verses.meaning: ya.da names knowledge as a defining inner quality of those who separate themselves to the Law — the covenant community characterised by knowing.

verses.verse_text: Neh 13:10 I also found out that the portions of the Levites had not been given to them, so that the Levites and the singers , who did the work , had fled each to his field .
verses.meaning: Names the discovered inner awareness — the term identifies Nehemiah's finding out that the Levites had not received their portions.

verses.verse_text: Est 2:11 And every day Mordecai walked in front of the court of the harem to learn how Esther was and what was happening to her .
verses.meaning: Names the practical inner aim of daily attendance — the term identifies Mordecai's purpose in walking by the court: to learn how Esther was faring.

verses.verse_text: Est 2:22 And this came to the knowledge of Mordecai , and he told it to Queen Esther , and Esther told the king in the name of Mordecai .
verses.meaning: Names the channel of knowledge — the term identifies how the conspiracy came to Mordecai's knowledge, and how that knowledge was passed on.

verses.verse_text: Est 4:1 When Mordecai learned all that had been done , Mordecai tore his clothes and put on sackcloth and ashes , and went out into the midst of the city , and he cried out with a loud and bitter cry .
verses.meaning: Names the inner awareness that triggers action — the term identifies Mordecai's learning of all that had been done as the cause of his public mourning.

verses.verse_text: Est 4:5 Then Esther called for Hathach , one of the king’s eunuchs , who had been appointed to attend her, and ordered him to go to Mordecai to learn what this was and why it was.
verses.meaning: Names the sought inner disclosure — the term identifies Esther's purpose in sending Hathach: to learn what was happening with Mordecai and why.

verses.verse_text: Est 4:11 “ All the king’s servants and the people of the king’s provinces know that if any man or woman goes to the king inside the inner court without being called , there is but one law —to be put to death , except the one to whom the king holds out the golden scepter so that he may live . But as for me, I have not been called to come in to the king these thirty days .”
verses.meaning: Names the shared communal knowledge — the term identifies all the king's servants and provincial people as knowing the law about unsummoned access to the king.

verses.verse_text: Est 4:14 For if you keep silent at this time , relief and deliverance will rise for the Jews from another place , but you and your father’s house will perish . And who knows whether you have not come to the kingdom for such a time as this ?”
verses.meaning: ya.da names the open recognition of divine providential knowing — who can know God's purposes? The question assumes God acts with knowledge even when hidden.

verses.verse_text: Job 5:24 You shall know that your tent is at peace , and you shall inspect your fold and miss nothing .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal experiential knowledge of God's blessing — the restored person will know peace and security by experience.

verses.verse_text: Job 5:25 You shall know also that your offspring shall be many , and your descendants as the grass of the earth .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal experiential knowledge of God's fruitfulness — blessing known through lived experience.

verses.verse_text: Job 5:27 Behold , this we have searched out ; it is true . Hear , and know it for your good.”
verses.meaning: ya.da names the knowledge being urged on Job — receiving the wisdom of experience as covenantal knowing.

verses.verse_text: Job 8:9 For we are but of yesterday and know nothing , for our days on earth are a shadow .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the self-knowledge of human transience — Bildad acknowledges the limits of human knowledge.

verses.verse_text: Job 9:2 “Truly I know that it is so: But how can a man be in the right before God ?
verses.meaning: ya.da names Job's inner conviction — self-knowledge of a moral-theological truth about God's justice.

verses.verse_text: Job 9:5 he who removes mountains , and they know it not, when he overturns them in his anger ,
verses.meaning: Names the absence of inner awareness — the term identifies the mountains as objects that do not know when God overturns them in his anger.

verses.verse_text: Job 9:21 I am blameless ; I regard not myself ; I loathe my life .
