verses.meaning: ya.da names Job's self-assessment — he does not regard (know) himself in a way that troubles him about his integrity. A complex use.

verses.verse_text: Job 9:28 I become afraid of all my suffering, for I know you will not hold me innocent .
verses.meaning: ya.da names Job's inner conviction about how God will treat him — self-knowledge of his vulnerability.

verses.verse_text: Job 10:2 I will say to God , Do not condemn me; let me know why you contend against me .
verses.meaning: ya.da names Job's request for knowledge of God's purposes — seeking inner comprehension of his situation.

verses.verse_text: Job 10:13 Yet these things you hid in your heart ; I know that this was your purpose.
verses.meaning: ya.da names Job's inner conviction about God's hidden purposes — a dark knowing of divine intentionality.

verses.verse_text: Job 11:8 It is higher than heaven —what can you do ? Deeper than Sheol —what can you know ?
verses.meaning: ya.da names the limit of human knowledge before divine depth — self-knowledge of its own inadequacy.

verses.verse_text: Job 12:9 Who among all these does not know that the hand of the Lord has done this ?
verses.meaning: ya.da names the knowledge of God's action that creation itself attests — a knowing available to all who observe.

verses.verse_text: Job 13:2 What you know , I also know; I am not inferior to you .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the same knowing — Job's self-assertion that his inner knowledge is equal to theirs.

verses.verse_text: Job 13:18 Behold, I have prepared my case ; I know that I shall be in the right .
verses.meaning: ya.da names Job's inner conviction of his own righteousness — self-knowledge of moral standing.

verses.verse_text: Job 13:23 How many are my iniquities and my sins ? Make me know my transgression and my sin .
verses.meaning: ya.da names Job's request for inner moral knowledge of what he has done wrong — seeking self-disclosure through God's disclosure.

verses.verse_text: Job 15:9 What do you know that we do not know ? What do you understand that is not clear to us ?
verses.meaning: ya.da names the knowledge being disputed — a challenge to Job's claim to inner knowledge.

verses.verse_text: Job 15:23 He wanders abroad for bread , saying, ‘ Where is it?’ He knows that a day of darkness is ready at his hand ;
verses.meaning: ya.da names the wicked man's inner knowledge of his own peril — a dark self-awareness.

verses.verse_text: Job 18:21 Surely such are the dwellings of the unrighteous , such is the place of him who knows not God .”
verses.meaning: ya.da names the absence of covenantal knowing of God as the defining characteristic of the wicked — their ruin is identified by what they do not know.

verses.verse_text: Job 19:6 know then that God has put me in the wrong and closed his net about me.
verses.meaning: ya.da names the inner covenantal conviction Job urges — a recognition of his situation before God.

verses.verse_text: Job 19:13 “He has put my brothers far from me, and those who knew me are wholly estranged from me .
verses.meaning: Names prior personal acquaintance — the term identifies those who had known Job as the people now wholly estranged from him.

verses.verse_text: Job 19:14 My relatives have failed me, my close friends have forgotten me .
verses.meaning: Names the associated absence of those known — the term identifies the close friends whose forgetting parallels the estrangement of those who knew him.

verses.verse_text: Job 19:25 For I know that my Redeemer lives , and at the last he will stand upon the earth .
verses.meaning: ya.da names Job's inner conviction of covenantal hope — a settled inner knowing of the Redeemer's existence that sustains through suffering.

verses.verse_text: Job 19:29 be afraid of the sword , for wrath brings the punishment of the sword , that you may know there is a judgment .”
verses.meaning: ya.da names the knowledge of divine judgment that the warning aims to produce — inner recognition of moral accountability.

verses.verse_text: Job 20:4 Do you not know this from of old , since man was placed on earth ,
verses.meaning: Names the rhetorical appeal to ancient inner knowledge — the term identifies what Zophar's argument assumes his audience already knows from of old.

verses.verse_text: Job 20:20 “ Because he knew no contentment in his belly , he will not let anything in which he delights escape him.
verses.meaning: Names the inner state of restless desire — the term identifies not knowing contentment in his belly as the inner condition that drives the wicked man's acquisitiveness.

verses.verse_text: Job 21:19 You say, ‘ God stores up their iniquity for their children .’ Let him pay it out to them, that they may know it.
verses.meaning: ya.da names the experiential knowing of God's judgment — recompense producing inner recognition of justice.

verses.verse_text: Job 21:27 “ Behold , I know your thoughts and your schemes to wrong me.
verses.meaning: ya.da names Job's inner knowledge of his friends' hidden motives — seeing through their arguments to their purpose.

verses.verse_text: Job 22:13 But you say , ‘ What does God know ? Can he judge through the deep darkness ?
verses.meaning: ya.da names the knowledge being denied to God — the challenge to divine omniscience by those who think he cannot see.

verses.verse_text: Job 23:3 Oh , that I knew where I might find him, that I might come even to his seat !
verses.meaning: ya.da names the longed-for covenantal knowing of God's location — Job's agonised desire to encounter the God who seems hidden.

verses.verse_text: Job 23:5 I would know what he would answer me and understand what he would say to me .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal knowledge Job seeks — hearing God's answer as the goal of his seeking.

verses.verse_text: Job 23:10 But he knows the way that I take ; when he has tried me, I shall come out as gold .
verses.meaning: ya.da names God's comprehensive covenantal knowledge of Job's path — the comfort is that God knows even when Job cannot find him.

verses.verse_text: Job 24:1 “ Why are not times of judgment kept by the Almighty , and why do those who know him never see his days ?
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal relationship of those who know God — those in relationship with him who cannot understand his timing.

verses.verse_text: Job 24:16 In the dark they dig through houses ; by day they shut themselves up ; they do not know the light .
verses.meaning: ya.da names moral knowledge absent in the wicked — they do not know the light; their moral orientation is darkness.

verses.verse_text: Job 28:7 “That path no bird of prey knows , and the falcon’s eye has not seen it.
verses.meaning: Names the absence of awareness — the term identifies the path to wisdom as unknown even to the sharpest-eyed birds of prey.

verses.verse_text: Job 28:13 Man does not know its worth , and it is not found in the land of the living .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the limit of human knowledge — wisdom's worth is beyond what humans can know or locate.

verses.verse_text: Job 29:16 I was a father to the needy , and I searched out the cause of him whom I did not know .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the moral knowledge guiding just action — Job sought justice even for strangers he did not personally know.

verses.verse_text: Job 30:23 For I know that you will bring me to death and to the house appointed for all living .
verses.meaning: ya.da names Job's inner certainty about his approaching death — self-knowledge of his end.

verses.verse_text: Job 31:6 (Let me be weighed in a just balance , and let God know my integrity !)
verses.meaning: ya.da names Job's appeal to God's knowing of his inner moral state — calling for divine knowledge of his integrity as grounds for vindication.

verses.verse_text: Job 32:7 I said , ‘Let days speak , and many years teach wisdom .’
verses.meaning: Names the inner acquisition of wisdom — the term identifies the teaching that many years impart as a form of knowing that Elihu had deferred to.

verses.verse_text: Job 32:22 For I do not know how to flatter , else my Maker would soon take me away .
verses.meaning: ya.da names self-knowledge of one's own speech — Elihu's inner honesty about his incapacity for flattery.

verses.verse_text: Job 34:4 Let us choose what is right ; let us know among ourselves what is good .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the moral knowledge to be discerned — knowing the good as the goal of their discussion.

verses.verse_text: Job 34:33 Will he then make repayment to suit you, because you reject it? For you must choose , and not I; therefore declare what you know .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the knowledge Job is challenged to express — inner moral knowing called to account.

verses.verse_text: Job 35:15 And now , because his anger does not punish , and he does not take much note of transgression ,
verses.meaning: Names the absence of close attention — the term identifies God's not taking much note of transgression as the condition Elihu challenges.

verses.verse_text: Job 36:26 Behold , God is great , and we know him not ; the number of his years is unsearchable .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal knowing that human limitation cannot reach — God's greatness exceeds human knowing.

verses.verse_text: Job 37:5 God thunders wondrously with his voice ; he does great things that we cannot comprehend .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the limit of human self-knowing before divine acts — we cannot comprehend what God does.

verses.verse_text: Job 37:7 He seals up the hand of every man , that all men whom he made may know it.
verses.meaning: ya.da names the divine purpose in sealing human hands in winter — all created persons are to know God's sovereign power through it.

verses.verse_text: Job 37:15 Do you know how God lays his command upon them and causes the lightning of his cloud to shine ?
verses.meaning: ya.da names God's governing command — knowledge of how God directs natural phenomena is beyond human reach.

verses.verse_text: Job 37:16 Do you know the balancings of the clouds , the wondrous works of him who is perfect in knowledge ,
verses.meaning: Names the inner capacity that the divine challenge exposes as absent — the term identifies knowing the balancings of the clouds as the knowledge required to answer God.

verses.verse_text: Job 37:19 Teach us what we shall say to him; we cannot draw up our case because of darkness .
verses.meaning: ya.da names Elihu's admission of human ignorance — he asks Job to teach what to say to God, knowing that they cannot approach unaided.

verses.verse_text: Job 38:3 Dress for action like a man ; I will question you, and you make it known to me .
verses.meaning: Names the disclosure expected of Job — the term identifies what God demands Job make known as the condition of answering the divine challenge.

verses.verse_text: Job 38:5 Who determined its measurements — surely you know ! Or who stretched the line upon it?
verses.meaning: Names the ironic inner knowledge the challenge assumes — the term identifies knowing who measured the earth as knowledge Job surely possesses, spoken with divine irony.

verses.verse_text: Job 38:12 “Have you commanded the morning since your days began, and caused the dawn to know its place ,
verses.meaning: Names the awareness commanded into creation — the term identifies the dawn knowing its place as the inner recognition instilled in it by God's command.

verses.verse_text: Pro 1:23 If you turn at my reproof , behold , I will pour out my spirit to you; I will make my words known to you .
verses.meaning: ya.da names Wisdom's self-disclosure — she will reveal her words to those who turn at her reproof. Covenantal disclosure.

verses.verse_text: Pro 3:6 In all your ways acknowledge him, and he will make straight your paths .
verses.meaning: ya.da names acknowledgment of God as the relational covenantal orienting — the knowing that shapes the whole direction of life.

verses.verse_text: Pro 4:19 The way of the wicked is like deep darkness; they do not know over what they stumble .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the absence of moral self-knowledge in the wicked — they cannot perceive their own stumbling.

verses.verse_text: Pro 5:6 she does not ponder the path of life ; her ways wander , and she does not know it.
verses.meaning: ya.da names the absence of moral self-knowledge in folly personified — she wanders without perceiving it.

verses.verse_text: Pro 7:23 till an arrow pierces its liver ; as a bird rushes into a snare ; he does not know that it will cost him his life .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the absence of moral foresight — the young man lacks the inner knowledge of consequence.

verses.verse_text: Pro 9:13 The woman Folly is loud ; she is seductive and knows nothing .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the total inner ignorance of folly — she has no knowledge; her noise is empty.

verses.verse_text: Pro 9:18 But he does not know that the dead are there , that her guests are in the depths of Sheol .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the fatal absence of moral knowledge — the person entering folly cannot perceive where it leads.

verses.verse_text: Pro 10:9 Whoever walks in integrity walks securely , but he who makes his ways crooked will be found out .
verses.meaning: Names the outcome of crooked conduct — the term identifies being found out or made known as the consequence of dishonest ways.

verses.verse_text: Pro 10:32 The lips of the righteous know what is acceptable , but the mouth of the wicked , what is perverse .
verses.meaning: ya.da names moral knowledge that guides speech — the righteous person's inner knowing of what is right shapes expression.

verses.verse_text: Pro 12:10 Whoever is righteous has regard for the life of his beast , but the mercy of the wicked is cruel .
verses.meaning: ya.da names moral knowing expressed in care — the righteous person's inner knowledge extends to regard for the animal.

verses.verse_text: Pro 14:7 Leave the presence of a fool , for there you do not meet words of knowledge .
verses.meaning: Names the absence of what can be encountered — the term identifies words of knowledge as the inner resource not found in the presence of a fool.

verses.verse_text: Pro 14:10 The heart knows its own bitterness , and no stranger shares its joy .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the heart's self-knowledge of its own inner states — bitterness and joy are known inwardly and cannot be fully shared.

verses.verse_text: Pro 17:27 Whoever restrains his words has knowledge , and he who has a cool spirit is a man of understanding .
verses.meaning: Names the inner quality that restraint of speech signals — the term identifies knowledge as the inner endowment characterised by measured words.

verses.verse_text: Pro 22:19 That your trust may be in the Lord , I have made them known to you today , even to you .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the teacher's disclosure — making known the thirty sayings as a covenantal transmission of wisdom.

verses.verse_text: Pro 22:21 to make you know what is right and true , that you may give a true answer to those who sent you ?
verses.meaning: ya.da names moral knowing as the goal — the sayings aim to produce inner knowledge of what is right.

verses.verse_text: Pro 23:35 “They struck me,” you will say, “but I was not hurt ; they beat me, but I did not feel it. When shall I awake ? I must have another drink.”
verses.meaning: Names the absence of physical perception — the term identifies the drunkard's failure to know or feel being struck as evidence of the deadening effect of wine.

verses.verse_text: Pro 24:14 Know that wisdom is such to your soul ; if you find it, there will be a future , and your hope will not be cut off .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal inner knowing of wisdom's value — held in the soul as certain hope.

verses.verse_text: Pro 24:22 for disaster will arise suddenly from them, and who knows the ruin that will come from them both ?
verses.meaning: Names the acknowledged uncertainty — the term identifies who knows the ruin that will come from both God and king as an open question that functions as a warning.

verses.verse_text: Pro 27:1 Do not boast about tomorrow , for you do not know what a day may bring .
verses.meaning: Names the recognised limit of inner knowledge — the term identifies not knowing what a day may bring as the ground for the prohibition against boasting about tomorrow.

verses.verse_text: Pro 27:23 Know well the condition of your flocks , and give attention to your herds ,
verses.meaning: Names the directed inner attention — the term identifies knowing well the condition of one's flocks as the practical wisdom required of a shepherd.

verses.verse_text: Pro 28:22 A stingy man hastens after wealth and does not know that poverty will come upon him .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the absence of moral foresight in the stingy man — he cannot perceive the consequence of his greed.

verses.verse_text: Pro 29:7 A righteous man knows the rights of the poor ; a wicked man does not understand such knowledge .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the moral knowledge of justice — the righteous person's inner knowing extends to the claims of the vulnerable.

verses.verse_text: Pro 30:3 I have not learned wisdom , nor have I knowledge of the Holy One .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal knowing of the Holy One that Agur lacks — relational inner knowing of God.

verses.verse_text: Pro 30:4 Who has ascended to heaven and come down ? Who has gathered the wind in his fists ? Who has wrapped up the waters in a garment ? Who has established all the ends of the earth ? What is his name , and what is his son’s name ? Surely you know !
verses.meaning: Names the ironic inner competence the challenge demands — the term identifies knowing the name of the one who ordered creation as the knowledge required to answer.

verses.verse_text: Pro 30:18 Three things are too wonderful for me; four I do not understand :
verses.meaning: Names the limit of inner comprehension — the term identifies the speaker's failure to understand four things as the frame for what follows.

verses.verse_text: Pro 31:23 Her husband is known in the gates when he sits among the elders of the land .
verses.meaning: Names communal recognition — the term identifies a husband's being known in the gates as a mark of public standing and social honour.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 1:17 And I applied my heart to know wisdom and to know madness and folly . I perceived that this also is but a striving after wind .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the deliberate inner pursuit of knowing — turning the heart toward wisdom and its opposites as a quest for self-knowledge.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 2:14 The wise person has his eyes in his head , but the fool walks in darkness . And yet I perceived that the same event happens to all of them.
verses.meaning: ya.da names the inner recognition of a pattern — Qoheleth's self-knowing of a bitter truth about mortality.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 3:12 I perceived that there is nothing better for them than to be joyful and to do good as long as they live ;
verses.meaning: ya.da names the inner recognition of a practical wisdom — what Qoheleth has come to know about human flourishing.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 3:14 I perceived that whatever God does endures forever ; nothing can be added to it, nor anything taken from it. God has done it, so that people fear before him.
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal recognition of God's permanence — Qoheleth's inner knowing of divine sovereignty.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 3:21 Who knows whether the spirit of man goes upward and the spirit of the beast goes down into the earth ?
verses.meaning: ya.da names the limit of self-knowledge before death — no one can know what happens to the spirit.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 4:13 Better was a poor and wise youth than an old and foolish king who no longer knew how to take advice .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the moral knowledge of one's own limitation — the king could no longer know how to receive counsel.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 5:1 Guard your steps when you go to the house of God . To draw near to listen is better than to offer the sacrifice of fools , for they do not know that they are doing evil .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the absent inner moral knowledge of the thoughtless worshipper — they cannot perceive their own wrongdoing.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 6:5 Moreover , it has not seen the sun or known anything , yet it finds rest rather than he.
verses.meaning: Names the absence of any experience — the term identifies the stillborn child as one that has not seen the sun or known anything, yet rests more peacefully than the toiler.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 6:10 Whatever has come to be has already been named , and it is known what man is, and that he is not able to dispute with one stronger than he .
verses.meaning: Names the established inner recognition — the term identifies what man is as something already known, forming the basis of the assertion that he cannot contend with one stronger.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 6:12 For who knows what is good for man while he lives the few days of his vain life , which he passes like a shadow ? For who can tell man what will be after him under the sun ?
verses.meaning: Names the acknowledged inner uncertainty — the term identifies who knows what is good for man as an open question that undercuts confident claims about life's value.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 7:22 Your heart knows that many times you yourself have cursed others .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the heart's inner self-knowledge of its own failures — the honest inner recognition that one has done what one is condemning.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 8:7 For he does not know what is to be, for who can tell him how it will be?
verses.meaning: Names the inner absence of foreknowledge — the term identifies not knowing what is to be as the condition of the one who cannot be told how it will be.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 8:12 Though a sinner does evil a hundred times and prolongs his life, yet I know that it will be well with those who fear God , because they fear before him.
verses.meaning: ya.da names Qoheleth's inner conviction of God's justice — a settled inner knowing that transcends the apparent contradictions.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 8:16 When I applied my heart to know wisdom , and to see the business that is done on earth , how neither day nor night do one’s eyes see sleep ,
verses.meaning: ya.da names the deliberate inner turning toward knowing — the heart applied as a means of pursuit.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 8:17 then I saw all the work of God , that man cannot find out the work that is done under the sun . However much man may toil in seeking , he will not find it out. Even though a wise man claims to know , he cannot find it out.
verses.meaning: ya.da names the claimed knowledge that cannot reach God's work — the limit of human knowing before divine mystery.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 9:1 But all this I laid to heart , examining it all , how the righteous and the wise and their deeds are in the hand of God . Whether it is love or hate , man does not know ; both are before him.
verses.meaning: ya.da names the impenetrable limit of self-knowledge before God's disposition — the inner uncertainty before divine hiddenness.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 9:5 For the living know that they will die , but the dead know nothing , and they have no more reward , for the memory of them is forgotten .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the inner knowledge of mortality in the living — knowing one's end is a distinguishing mark of being alive.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 9:11 Again I saw that under the sun the race is not to the swift , nor the battle to the strong , nor bread to the wise , nor riches to the intelligent , nor favor to those with knowledge , but time and chance happen to them all .
verses.meaning: Names the absence of reliable correlation — the term identifies favour as not belonging to those with knowledge, since time and chance happen to all.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 9:12 For man does not know his time . Like fish that are taken in an evil net , and like birds that are caught in a snare , so the children of man are snared at an evil time , when it suddenly falls upon them.
verses.meaning: ya.da names the inner ignorance of one's own end — a universal human condition.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 10:14 A fool multiplies words , though no man knows what is to be, and who can tell him what will be after him ?
verses.meaning: Names the acknowledged limit of inner awareness — the term identifies no man knowing what is to be as the counterpoint to the fool's multiplication of words.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 10:15 The toil of a fool wearies him, for he does not know the way to the city .
verses.meaning: Names the absence of practical inner knowledge — the term identifies not knowing the way to the city as the mark of the fool whose toil wearies him.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 11:2 Give a portion to seven , or even to eight , for you know not what disaster may happen on earth .
verses.meaning: Names the acknowledged absence of foreknowledge — the term identifies not knowing what disaster may happen on earth as the ground for diversified giving.

verses.verse_text: Isa 1:3 The ox knows its owner , and the donkey its master’s crib , but Israel does not know , my people do not understand .”
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal knowing Israel lacks — even animals recognise their master; Israel does not recognise God.

verses.verse_text: Isa 5:5 And now I will tell you what I will do to my vineyard . I will remove its hedge , and it shall be devoured ; I will break down its wall , and it shall be trampled down .
verses.meaning: Names the declared inner intent — the term identifies God's disclosure of what he will do as an act of making known his purpose to those addressed.

verses.verse_text: Isa 5:19 who say : “Let him be quick , let him speed his work that we may see it; let the counsel of the Holy One of Israel draw near , and let it come , that we may know it!”
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal knowledge of God's counsel being sought ironically — the mockers demand what they refuse to receive.

verses.verse_text: Isa 7:15 He shall eat curds and honey when he knows how to refuse the evil and choose the good .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the moral knowing of good and evil — a developmental inner capacity.

verses.verse_text: Isa 7:16 For before the boy knows how to refuse the evil and choose the good , the land whose two kings you dread will be deserted .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the same moral knowing as a developmental marker — before it is reached, the sign will be fulfilled.

verses.verse_text: Isa 8:4 for before the boy knows how to cry ‘My father ’ or ‘ My mother ,’ the wealth of Damascus and the spoil of Samaria will be carried away before the king of Assyria .”
verses.meaning: Names the developmental threshold of inner awareness — the term identifies the moment before a child knows how to cry out for parents as the timeframe for the predicted conquest.

verses.verse_text: Isa 9:9 and all the people will know , Ephraim and the inhabitants of Samaria , who say in pride and in arrogance of heart :
verses.meaning: ya.da names the knowledge that judgment will produce — the people will know their situation, though they are currently boasting in arrogance.

verses.verse_text: Isa 12:4 And you will say in that day : “Give thanks to the Lord , call upon his name , make known his deeds among the peoples , proclaim that his name is exalted .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the transmission of covenantal knowledge — proclaiming among the nations what God has done.

verses.verse_text: Isa 12:5 “Sing praises to the Lord , for he has done gloriously ; let this be made known in all the earth .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the universal disclosure of God's glorious acts — covenantal self-disclosure through proclamation.

verses.verse_text: Isa 19:21 And the Lord will make himself known to the Egyptians , and the Egyptians will know the Lord in that day and worship with sacrifice and offering , and they will make vows to the Lord and perform them.
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal self-disclosure of God to a foreign nation and their resulting knowing — a remarkable eschatological extension of covenant knowing.

verses.verse_text: Isa 29:11 And the vision of all this has become to you like the words of a book that is sealed . When men give it to one who can read , saying , “ Read this,” he says , “I cannot , for it is sealed .”
verses.meaning: Names the inability to read — the term identifies the literate person as one who cannot access the sealed vision, revealing that the barrier is not literacy but concealment.

verses.verse_text: Isa 29:12 And when they give the book to one who cannot read , saying , “ Read this ,” he says , “I cannot read .”
verses.meaning: Names the disability of illiteracy — the term identifies the non-reader's inability to read as the reason they cannot access the vision.

verses.verse_text: Isa 29:15 Ah , you who hide deep from the Lord your counsel , whose deeds are in the dark , and who say , “ Who sees us? Who knows us ?”
verses.meaning: ya.da names the divine knowing they deny — those who hide from God claim he cannot know their deeds, which is precisely what God knows.

verses.verse_text: Isa 29:24 And those who go astray in spirit will come to understanding , and those who murmur will accept instruction .”
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal knowledge of God that restoration produces — the erring will come to know.

verses.verse_text: Isa 32:4 The heart of the hasty will understand and know , and the tongue of the stammerers will hasten to speak distinctly .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the moral knowing that will come in the renewal — the hasty heart will finally perceive rightly.

verses.verse_text: Isa 33:13 Hear , you who are far off , what I have done ; and you who are near , acknowledge my might .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal recognition of God's power — those near are called to inner acknowledgment of his acts.

verses.verse_text: Isa 37:20 So now , O Lord our God , save us from his hand , that all the kingdoms of the earth may know that you alone are the Lord .”
verses.meaning: ya.da names the universal covenantal recognition that Hezekiah's prayer aims for — all nations knowing God's uniqueness through his deliverance.

verses.verse_text: Isa 37:28 “‘I know your sitting down and your going out and coming in , and your raging against me .
verses.meaning: ya.da names God's comprehensive knowledge of the Assyrian king — every movement and attitude is known.

verses.verse_text: Isa 38:19 The living , the living , he thanks you, as I do this day ; the father makes known to the children your faithfulness .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the intergenerational transmission of covenantal knowing — parents passing on knowledge of God's faithfulness.

verses.verse_text: Isa 40:13 Who has measured the Spirit of the Lord , or what man shows him his counsel ?
verses.meaning: Names the scope of divine self-sufficiency — the term identifies making known his counsel as the divine prerogative that no one has exercised on God's behalf.

verses.verse_text: Isa 40:14 Whom did he consult , and who made him understand ? Who taught him the path of justice , and taught him knowledge , and showed him the way of understanding ?
verses.meaning: Names the modes of inner instruction — the term identifies understanding and knowledge as what God was taught by no one, establishing his unrivalled inner competence.

verses.verse_text: Isa 40:21 Do you not know ? Do you not hear ? Has it not been told you from the beginning ? Have you not understood from the foundations of the earth ?
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal knowledge that should have been received from the beginning — Israel has had access to this knowing through tradition.

verses.verse_text: Isa 40:28 Have you not known ? Have you not heard ? The Lord is the everlasting God , the Creator of the ends of the earth . He does not faint or grow weary ; his understanding is unsearchable .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal knowing of God's eternal nature — what should be known by his people.

verses.verse_text: Isa 41:22 Let them bring them, and tell us what is to happen . Tell us the former things , what they are, that we may consider them, that we may know their outcome ; or declare to us the things to come .
verses.meaning: Names the inner goal of prophetic disclosure — the term identifies knowing the outcome of former things as the purpose for which Israel challenges the idols to speak.

verses.verse_text: Isa 41:23 Tell us what is to come hereafter , that we may know that you are gods ; do good , or do harm , that we may be dismayed and terrified .
verses.meaning: Names the inner criterion of divine identity — the term identifies knowing the future as the test by which the idols' deity would be established.

verses.verse_text: Isa 41:26 Who declared it from the beginning , that we might know , and beforehand , that we might say , “He is right ”? There was none who declared it, none who proclaimed , none who heard your words .
verses.meaning: Names the inner recognition that accurate prophecy would produce — the term identifies knowing that the speaker is right as the effect that prior declaration would have achieved.

verses.verse_text: Isa 42:16 And I will lead the blind in a way that they do not know , in paths that they have not known I will guide them. I will turn the darkness before them into light , the rough places into level ground . These are the things I do , and I do not forsake them .
verses.meaning: Names the prior absence of familiarity — the term identifies paths the blind have not known as the unfamiliar ways through which God will guide them.

verses.verse_text: Isa 42:25 So he poured on him the heat of his anger and the might of battle ; it set him on fire all around , but he did not understand ; it burned him up, but he did not take it to heart .
verses.meaning: Names the absence of inner comprehension — the term identifies the failure to understand or take to heart the consuming fire of divine wrath as a form of inner blindness.

verses.verse_text: Isa 43:10 “ You are my witnesses ,” declares the Lord , “and my servant whom I have chosen , that you may know and believe me and understand that I am he. Before me no god was formed , nor shall there be any after me.
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal knowing of God's identity as the goal of Israel's witness — knowing that God is who he is, uniquely.

verses.verse_text: Isa 43:19 Behold , I am doing a new thing ; now it springs forth , do you not perceive it? I will make a way in the wilderness and rivers in the desert .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal perception of what God is doing — the new thing requires inner perception to be received.

verses.verse_text: Isa 44:8 Fear not , nor be afraid ; have I not told you from of old and declared it? And you are my witnesses ! Is there a God besides me? There is no Rock ; I know not any.”
verses.meaning: ya.da names God's self-knowledge — his inner knowing finds no other God. The divine self-knowledge is the ground of monotheism.

verses.verse_text: Isa 44:9 All who fashion idols are nothing , and the things they delight in do not profit . Their witnesses neither see nor know , that they may be put to shame .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the moral blindness of idol-witnesses — they cannot perceive or know the truth.

verses.verse_text: Isa 45:3 I will give you the treasures of darkness and the hoards in secret places , that you may know that it is I , the Lord , the God of Israel , who call you by your name .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal knowing of God's identity that the treasures will produce in Cyrus — an extraordinary extension of covenantal self-disclosure.

verses.verse_text: Isa 45:4 For the sake of my servant Jacob , and Israel my chosen , I call you by your name , I name you, though you do not know me .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal knowing Cyrus lacks — God calls him by name without the relationship of knowing.

verses.verse_text: Isa 45:5 I am the Lord , and there is no other , besides me there is no God ; I equip you, though you do not know me ,
verses.meaning: ya.da names the same absence of covenantal knowing — Cyrus is equipped without the relationship.

verses.verse_text: Isa 45:6 that people may know , from the rising of the sun and from the west , that there is none besides me; I am the Lord , and there is no other .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the universal covenantal recognition of God's uniqueness as the goal of history — all peoples knowing.

verses.verse_text: Isa 45:20 “Assemble yourselves and come ; draw near together , you survivors of the nations ! They have no knowledge who carry about their wooden idols , and keep on praying to a god that cannot save .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the absent moral knowing of idol-carriers — the one who prays to a god that cannot save has no inner knowledge.

verses.verse_text: Isa 47:8 Now therefore hear this , you lover of pleasures , who sit securely , who say in your heart , “ I am, and there is no one besides me; I shall not sit as a widow or know the loss of children ”:
verses.meaning: ya.da names Babylon's self-deceiving inner certainty — she claims she will not experience what she in fact will experience.

verses.verse_text: Isa 47:11 But evil shall come upon you, which you will not know how to charm away ; disaster shall fall upon you, for which you will not be able to atone ; and ruin shall come upon you suddenly , of which you know nothing .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the absent inner knowledge of how to avert disaster — Babylon will be helpless.

verses.verse_text: Isa 47:13 You are wearied with your many counsels ; let them stand forth and save you, those who divide the heavens , who gaze at the stars , who at the new moons make known what shall come upon you .
verses.meaning: Names the service of astrological counsel — the term identifies those who make known what is coming upon their clients as the inner function of Babylon's stargazers.

verses.verse_text: Isa 48:4 Because I know that you are obstinate , and your neck is an iron sinew and your forehead brass ,
verses.meaning: ya.da names God's comprehensive knowledge of Israel's character — inner knowing of their stubbornness as the reason for the way he addressed them.

verses.verse_text: Isa 48:6 “You have heard ; now see all this; and will you not declare it? From this time forth I announce to you new things , hidden things that you have not known .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the new divine disclosures — things God is now revealing that were previously unknown.

verses.verse_text: Isa 48:7 They are created now , not long ago ; before today you have never heard of them, lest you should say , ‘ Behold , I knew them .’
verses.meaning: Names the anticipated false claim of prior inner knowledge — the term identifies the danger that Israel might claim to have already known the new things, which God preempts.

verses.verse_text: Isa 48:8 You have never heard , you have never known , from of old your ear has not been opened . For I knew that you would surely deal treacherously , and that from before birth you were called a rebel .
verses.meaning: ya.da names God's foreknowing of Israel's rebellion — comprehensive inner knowledge of their character before they acted.

verses.verse_text: Isa 49:23 Kings shall be your foster fathers , and their queens your nursing mothers . With their faces to the ground they shall bow down to you, and lick the dust of your feet . Then you will know that I am the Lord ; those who wait for me shall not be put to shame .”
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal recognition of God's identity produced by the restoration — those who waited will know.

verses.verse_text: Isa 49:26 I will make your oppressors eat their own flesh , and they shall be drunk with their own blood as with wine . Then all flesh shall know that I am the Lord your Savior , and your Redeemer , the Mighty One of Jacob .”
verses.meaning: ya.da names the universal covenantal knowing of God's identity produced by judgment on oppressors.

verses.verse_text: Isa 50:4 The Lord God has given me the tongue of those who are taught , that I may know how to sustain with a word him who is weary . Morning by morning he awakens ; he awakens my ear to hear as those who are taught .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal knowing the Servant receives morning by morning — the knowing of how to speak life into the weary.

verses.verse_text: Isa 50:7 But the Lord God helps me; therefore I have not been disgraced ; therefore I have set my face like a flint , and I know that I shall not be put to shame .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the Servant's inner conviction of vindication — settled inner knowledge of God's support.

verses.verse_text: Isa 51:7 “ Listen to me, you who know righteousness , the people in whose heart is my law ; fear not the reproach of man , nor be dismayed at their revilings .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal knowing of righteousness — God's law in the heart is its content.

verses.verse_text: Isa 52:6 Therefore my people shall know my name . Therefore in that day they shall know that it is I who speak ; here I am.”
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal knowledge of God's name as a future promise — Israel will know who he is by name.

verses.verse_text: Isa 55:5 Behold , you shall call a nation that you do not know , and a nation that did not know you shall run to you, because of the Lord your God , and of the Holy One of Israel , for he has glorified you .
verses.meaning: Names the absence of prior acquaintance — the term identifies the nation that does not know Israel and the nation that did not know Israel as those who will now run to them.

verses.verse_text: Isa 56:10 His watchmen are blind ; they are all without knowledge ; they are all silent dogs ; they cannot bark , dreaming , lying down , loving to slumber .
verses.meaning: Names the absence of inner awareness — the term identifies Israel's watchmen as those who are all without knowledge, completely unable to perceive.

verses.verse_text: Isa 58:3 ‘ Why have we fasted , and you see it not ? Why have we humbled ourselves , and you take no knowledge of it?’ Behold , in the day of your fast you seek your own pleasure , and oppress all your workers .
verses.meaning: ya.da names God's attending — the people claim God does not perceive their fast. The accusation is about divine knowing.

verses.verse_text: Isa 59:8 The way of peace they do not know , and there is no justice in their paths ; they have made their roads crooked ; no one who treads on them knows peace .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the absent moral knowledge of the unjust — they cannot perceive the path of peace.

verses.verse_text: Hos 2:8 And she did not know that it was I who gave her the grain , the wine , and the oil , and who lavished on her silver and gold , which they used for Baal .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the absent covenantal recognition of God as provider — Israel received but did not know the source.

verses.verse_text: Hos 2:20 I will betroth you to me in faithfulness . And you shall know the Lord .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the restored covenantal knowing as the goal of betrothal — the covenant renewed produces the knowing of the Lord.

verses.verse_text: Hos 5:3 I know Ephraim , and Israel is not hidden from me; for now, O Ephraim , you have played the whore ; Israel is defiled .
verses.meaning: ya.da names God's comprehensive covenantal knowing of the nation's condition — their sin is fully visible to him.

verses.verse_text: Hos 5:4 Their deeds do not permit them to return to their God . For the spirit of whoredom is within them, and they know not the Lord .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the absent covenantal knowing — the spirit of whoredom has displaced knowing of the Lord.

verses.verse_text: Hos 5:9 Ephraim shall become a desolation in the day of punishment ; among the tribes of Israel I make known what is sure .
verses.meaning: Names the act of disclosure — the term identifies God making known what is sure among the tribes as a form of judicial announcement within Israel.

verses.verse_text: Hos 6:3 Let us know ; let us press on to know the Lord ; his going out is sure as the dawn ; he will come to us as the showers , as the spring rains that water the earth .”
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal knowing of the Lord as the goal of urgent pursuit — pressing on to know God as a relational aspiration.

verses.verse_text: Hos 7:9 Strangers devour his strength , and he knows it not; gray hairs are sprinkled upon him, and he knows it not.
verses.meaning: ya.da names the absent self-knowledge of Ephraim — he cannot perceive his own decline.

verses.verse_text: Hos 8:2 To me they cry , “My God , we— Israel — know you.”
verses.meaning: ya.da names Israel's claimed covenantal knowing — a claim made while pursuing other gods. The knowing they claim is hollow.

verses.verse_text: Hos 8:4 They made kings , but not through me. They set up princes , but I knew it not. With their silver and gold they made idols for their own destruction .
verses.meaning: ya.da names God's non-acknowledgment — he did not know it, meaning he did not sanction or recognise it.

verses.verse_text: Hos 9:7 The days of punishment have come ; the days of recompense have come ; Israel shall know it. The prophet is a fool ; the man of the spirit is mad , because of your great iniquity and great hatred .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal knowledge of judgment that Israel will receive — they will know the days of punishment by experiencing them.

verses.verse_text: Hos 11:3 Yet it was I who taught Ephraim to walk; I took them up by their arms , but they did not know that I healed them .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the absent covenantal recognition of God's care — the father taught and healed, but was not known as the one doing it.

verses.verse_text: Hos 13:4 But I am the Lord your God from the land of Egypt ; you know no God but me, and besides me there is no savior .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the exclusive covenantal knowing — Israel's only knowing of God is in relation to the one who delivered them from Egypt.

verses.verse_text: Hos 13:5 It was I who knew you in the wilderness , in the land of drought ;
verses.meaning: ya.da names God's covenantal election knowing of Israel in their most vulnerable place — intimate relational knowing in the desert.

verses.verse_text: Amo 3:2 “You only have I known of all the families of the earth ; therefore I will punish you for all your iniquities .
verses.meaning: ya.da names God's elective covenantal knowing of Israel — uniquely known, uniquely accountable. One of the defining statements of election as knowing.

verses.verse_text: Amo 3:10 “They do not know how to do right ,” declares the Lord , “those who store up violence and robbery in their strongholds .”
verses.meaning: ya.da names the absent moral knowledge of the unjust — they lack the inner knowing of what right conduct is.

verses.verse_text: Amo 5:12 For I know how many are your transgressions and how great are your sins — you who afflict the righteous , who take a bribe , and turn aside the needy in the gate .
verses.meaning: ya.da names God's comprehensive moral knowing of Israel's sin — specific and complete.

verses.verse_text: Amo 5:16 Therefore thus says the Lord , the God of hosts , the Lord : “ In all the squares there shall be wailing , and in all the streets they shall say , ‘ Alas ! Alas !’ They shall call the farmers to mourning and to wailing those who are skilled in lamentation ,
verses.meaning: Names the inner competence of skilled lamentation — the term identifies those skilled in mourning as those who know how to perform the communal rite of grief.

verses.verse_text: Jon 1:7 And they said to one another , “ Come , let us cast lots , that we may know on whose account this evil has come upon us.” So they cast lots , and the lot fell on Jonah .
verses.meaning: Names the goal of the lot — the term identifies knowing on whose account the evil has come as the information the lot is meant to disclose.

verses.verse_text: Jon 1:10 Then the men were exceedingly afraid and said to him, “ What is this that you have done !” For the men knew that he was fleeing from the presence of the Lord , because he had told them .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the pagan sailors' recognition of Jonah's situation — they come to covenantal knowledge of the Lord through the events.

verses.verse_text: Jon 1:12 He said to them , “ Pick me up and hurl me into the sea ; then the sea will quiet down for you, for I know it is because of me that this great tempest has come upon you .”
verses.meaning: ya.da names Jonah's self-knowledge of his own culpability — settled inner recognition of his responsibility.

verses.verse_text: Jon 3:9 Who knows ? God may turn and relent and turn from his fierce anger , so that we may not perish .”
verses.meaning: ya.da names the open covenantal possibility — no one can know for certain what God will do; the appeal to him is worth making.

verses.verse_text: Jon 4:2 And he prayed to the Lord and said , “ O Lord , is not this what I said when I was yet in my country ? That is why I made haste to flee to Tarshish ; for I knew that you are a gracious God and merciful , slow to anger and abounding in steadfast love , and relenting from disaster .
verses.meaning: ya.da names Jonah's covenantal knowledge of God's character — the knowing that motivated his flight.

verses.verse_text: Jon 4:11 And should not I pity Nineveh , that great city , in which there are more than 120,000 persons who do not know their right hand from their left , and also much cattle ?”
verses.meaning: ya.da names moral immaturity — the Ninevites who cannot distinguish right from left lack the inner moral knowing that would make them accountable.

verses.verse_text: Mic 3:1 And I said : Hear , you heads of Jacob and rulers of the house of Israel ! Is it not for you to know justice ?—
verses.meaning: ya.da names the moral knowledge of justice as the obligation of leaders — they should know it; their failure to practise it is not ignorance but refusal.

verses.verse_text: Mic 6:5 O my people , remember what Balak king of Moab devised , and what Balaam the son of Beor answered him, and what happened from Shittim to Gilgal , that you may know the righteous acts of the Lord .”
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal knowing of God's saving history that remembrance produces — recalling Balak and Balaam produces recognition of God's faithfulness.

verses.verse_text: Nah 1:7 The Lord is good , a stronghold in the day of trouble ; he knows those who take refuge in him .
verses.meaning: ya.da names God's covenantal knowing of his own — those who come to him are personally known to him.

verses.verse_text: Nah 3:17 Your princes are like grasshoppers , your scribes like clouds of locusts settling on the fences in a day of cold — when the sun rises , they fly away ; no one knows where they are .
verses.meaning: Names the absence of locatable knowledge — the term identifies the disappeared Assyrian officials as those whose whereabouts no one knows.

verses.verse_text: Hab 2:14 For the earth will be filled with the knowledge of the glory of the Lord as the waters cover the sea .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the eschatological universal covenantal knowing — the whole earth filled with knowledge of God.

verses.verse_text: Hab 3:2 O Lord , I have heard the report of you, and your work, O Lord , do I fear . In the midst of the years revive it ; in the midst of the years make it known ; in wrath remember mercy .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the appeal for divine self-disclosure — Habakkuk asks God to make his work known again in the current time.

verses.verse_text: Zep 3:5 The Lord within her is righteous ; he does no injustice ; every morning he shows forth his justice ; each dawn he does not fail ; but the unjust knows no shame .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the absent moral self-knowledge of the unjust — they cannot perceive their own wrongdoing or feel shame for it.

verses.verse_text: Zec 2:9 “ Behold , I will shake my hand over them, and they shall become plunder for those who served them. Then you will know that the Lord of hosts has sent me .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal recognition of divine mission that the act will produce.

verses.verse_text: Zec 2:11 And many nations shall join themselves to the Lord in that day , and shall be my people . And I will dwell in your midst , and you shall know that the Lord of hosts has sent me to you .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the same covenantal recognition — repeated in the Zechariah oracles.

verses.verse_text: Zec 4:5 Then the angel who talked with me answered and said to me, “Do you not know what these are?” I said , “ No , my lord .”
verses.meaning: Names the absence of inner understanding — the term identifies the prophet's failure to know what the lampstand and olive trees signify as the occasion for the angel's explanation.

verses.verse_text: Zec 4:9 “The hands of Zerubbabel have laid the foundation of this house ; his hands shall also complete it. Then you will know that the Lord of hosts has sent me to you .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal recognition that the completion of the temple will produce.

verses.verse_text: Zec 4:13 He said to me, “Do you not know what these are?” I said , “ No , my lord .”
verses.meaning: Names the same absence of inner understanding — the term identifies the same failure to know in a repeated exchange, again prompting explanation.

verses.verse_text: Zec 6:15 “And those who are far off shall come and help to build the temple of the Lord . And you shall know that the Lord of hosts has sent me to you. And this shall come to pass, if you will diligently obey the voice of the Lord your God .”
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal recognition produced by the distant builders' coming to help — the pattern is repeated throughout Zechariah.

verses.verse_text: Zec 7:14 “and I scattered them with a whirlwind among all the nations that they had not known . Thus the land they left was desolate , so that no one went to and fro , and the pleasant land was made desolate .”
verses.meaning: Names the absence of prior acquaintance — the term identifies the nations of exile as those Israel had not known, underlining the foreignness of the scattering.

verses.verse_text: Zec 11:11 So it was annulled on that day , and the sheep traders , who were watching me, knew that it was the word of the Lord .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal recognition of divine speech — observers perceive that the act is the word of the Lord.

verses.verse_text: Zec 14:7 And there shall be a unique day , which is known to the Lord , neither day nor night , but at evening time there shall be light .
verses.meaning: Names the exclusive divine awareness — the term identifies the unique coming day as one that is known only to the Lord, beyond human foreknowledge.

verses.verse_text: Mal 2:4 So shall you know that I have sent this command to you, that my covenant with Levi may stand, says the Lord of hosts .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal knowledge of divine authority that the fulfilment will produce.

verses.verse_text: Joe 2:14 Who knows whether he will not turn and relent , and leave a blessing behind him, a grain offering and a drink offering for the Lord your God ?
verses.meaning: ya.da names the divine freedom before human action — no one can claim to know what God will do; he is not compelled.

verses.verse_text: Joe 2:27 You shall know that I am in the midst of Israel , and that I am the Lord your God and there is none else . And my people shall never again be put to shame .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal knowledge of God's presence produced by restoration.

verses.verse_text: Joe 3:17 “So you shall know that I am the Lord your God , who dwells in Zion , my holy mountain . And Jerusalem shall be holy , and strangers shall never again pass through it.
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal recognition of God's dwelling — knowing him in his holy mountain.

verses.verse_text: Dan 2:21 He changes times and seasons ; he removes kings and sets up kings ; he gives wisdom to the wise and knowledge to those who have understanding ;
verses.meaning: man.da names knowledge as God's gift to those who have understanding — the inner capacity flows from God to receptive persons.

verses.verse_text: Dan 4:34 At the end of the days I , Nebuchadnezzar , lifted my eyes to heaven , and my reason returned to me, and I blessed the Most High , and praised and honored him who lives forever , for his dominion is an everlasting dominion , and his kingdom endures from generation to generation ;
verses.meaning: man.da names reason as the inner defining capacity of humanness — its loss is the loss of personhood; its return restores dignity. A powerful use: reason as the inner seat of the human person.

verses.verse_text: Dan 4:36 At the same time my reason returned to me, and for the glory of my kingdom , my majesty and splendor returned to me. My counselors and my lords sought me , and I was established in my kingdom , and still more greatness was added to me .
verses.meaning: man.da names the same restored capacity — the same verse pattern. Reason's return is immediately linked to restored honour and greatness.

verses.verse_text: Dan 5:12 because an excellent spirit , knowledge , and understanding to interpret dreams , explain riddles , and solve problems were found in this Daniel , whom the king named Belteshazzar . Now let Daniel be called , and he will show the interpretation .”
verses.meaning: man.da names knowledge as part of Daniel's Spirit-given capacity — alongside understanding, enabling interpretation of mysteries.

verses.verse_text: Isa 44:19 No one considers , nor is there knowledge or discernment to say , “ Half of it I burned in the fire ; I also baked bread on its coals ; I roasted meat and have eaten . And shall I make the rest of it an abomination ? Shall I fall down before a block of wood ?”
verses.meaning: te.vu.nah names discernment as the inner capacity that idol-worshippers lack — unable to perceive the absurdity of their practice.

subgroups.label: Discernment and practical judgment
subgroups.description: Terms that name the applied perceptive act — judging situations wisely, making right moral-practical distinctions, and the conscience-adjacent evaluation of thoughts. Includes directionally neutral terms (same inner quality serves righteousness or cunning depending on object).

verses.verse_text: Heb 5:14 But solid food is for the mature , for those who have their powers of discernment trained by constant practice to distinguish good from evil .
verses.meaning: aisthētērion names the inner faculty of moral discernment — specifically as a trained capacity, developed through practice. The faculty is not automatic but cultivated by repeated exercise.

verses.verse_text: Mat 16:3 And in the morning , ‘It will be stormy today , for the sky is red and threatening .’ You know how to interpret the appearance of the sky , but you cannot interpret the signs of the times .
verses.meaning: diakrinō names the inner discerning act of reading and judging signs — a capacity the Pharisees have for natural phenomena but not for spiritual reality.

verses.verse_text: Mat 21:21 And Jesus answered them , “ Truly , I say to you , if you have faith and do not doubt , you will not only do what has been done to the fig tree , but even if you say to this mountain , ‘Be taken up and thrown into the sea ,’ it will happen .
verses.meaning: diakrinō names the inner division of doubt — the heart split between faith and hesitation rather than unified in trust.

verses.verse_text: Mar 11:23 Truly , I say to you , whoever says to this mountain , ‘Be taken up and thrown into the sea ,’ and does not doubt in his heart , but believes that what he says will come to pass , it will be done for him .
verses.meaning: diakrinō names the inner self-division of doubting — the heart at war with itself rather than aligned in faith.

verses.verse_text: Act 10:20 Rise and go down and accompany them without hesitation , for I have sent them .”
verses.meaning: diakrinō names the inner division that produces hesitation — the Spirit commands Peter to go without the inner wavering that discrimination between clean/unclean would produce.

verses.verse_text: Act 11:2 So when Peter went up to Jerusalem , the circumcision party criticized him , saying ,
verses.meaning: diakrinō names the inner judging and discriminating of the circumcision party — they made distinctions and brought criticism based on those distinctions.

verses.verse_text: Act 11:12 And the Spirit told me to go with them , making no distinction . These six brothers also accompanied me , and we entered the man’s house .
verses.meaning: diakrinō names the inner discriminating act the Spirit told Peter not to make — no evaluative distinction between Jew and Gentile in this context.

verses.verse_text: Act 15:9 and he made no distinction between us and them , having cleansed their hearts by faith .
verses.meaning: diakrinō names the divine non-discrimination — God's inner act of cleansing by faith makes no evaluative distinction between persons.

verses.verse_text: Rom 4:20 No unbelief made him waver concerning the promise of God , but he grew strong in his faith as he gave glory to God ,
verses.meaning: diakrinō names the inner wavering of divided faith — Abraham did not waver, i.e., did not experience the inner self-division between trust and doubt.

verses.verse_text: Rom 14:23 But whoever has doubts is condemned if he eats , because the eating is not from faith . For whatever does not proceed from faith is sin .
verses.meaning: diakrinō names the inner division of conscience about food — the person who eats while inwardly divided has not acted from faith, which is sin.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 4:7 For who sees anything different in you ? What do you have that you did not receive ? If then you received it, why do you boast as if you did not receive it?
verses.meaning: diakrinō names the inner discriminating judgment that would see one person as superior — Paul challenges the Corinthians' self-distinguishing.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 6:5 I say this to your shame . Can it be that there is no one among you wise enough to settle a dispute between the brothers ,
verses.meaning: diakrinō names the act of judging and discriminating in a dispute — arbitrating between parties by making evaluative distinctions.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 11:29 For anyone who eats and drinks without discerning the body eats and drinks judgment on himself .
verses.meaning: diakrinō names the inner discerning act of recognising what the body of Christ is — failure to make this distinction in the Lord's Supper produces self-judgment.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 11:31 But if we judged ourselves truly, we would not be judged .
verses.meaning: diakrinō names self-judgment — the inner act of discriminating evaluation applied to oneself. True self-discernment would prevent divine judgment.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 14:29 Let two or three prophets speak , and let the others weigh what is said.
verses.meaning: diakrinō names the communal act of evaluating prophecy — the inner discerning judgment of others that tests what is said against truth.

verses.verse_text: Jam 1:6 But let him ask in faith , with no doubting , for the one who doubts is like a wave of the sea that is driven and tossed by the wind .
verses.meaning: diakrinō names the inner self-division of doubting — the doubting person is inwardly unstable, driven without direction.

verses.verse_text: Jam 2:4 have you not then made distinctions among yourselves and become judges with evil thoughts ?
verses.meaning: diakrinō names the discriminating judgment that shows partiality — inner evaluative distinctions between persons based on wealth that produce unjust treatment.

verses.verse_text: Jude 9 But when the archangel Michael , contending with the devil , was disputing about the body of Moses , he did not presume to pronounce a blasphemous judgment , but said , “The Lord rebuke you .”
verses.meaning: diakrinō names the act of contending/disputing — Michael engaged in a discriminating contest with the devil.

verses.verse_text: Jude 22 And have mercy on those who doubt ;
verses.meaning: diakrinō names the inner self-division of those wavering in faith — they are to receive mercy rather than condemnation.

verses.verse_text: Rom 2:15 They show that the work of the law is written on their hearts , while their conscience also bears witness , and their conflicting thoughts accuse or even excuse them
verses.meaning: logismos names the inner cognitive conflict of conscience — the thoughts of the heart as the site of moral self-evaluation that produces accusation or defence.

verses.verse_text: 2Cor 10:5 We destroy arguments and every lofty opinion raised against the knowledge of God , and take every thought captive to obey Christ ,
verses.meaning: logismos names the inner arguments and reasoning structures that are opposed to God — they are the target of apostolic warfare. Every thought is to be brought under Christ's authority.

verses.verse_text: Deu 32:29 If they were wise , they would understand this ; they would discern their latter end !
verses.meaning: bin names the perceptive faculty needed to grasp the consequences of one's choices — discerning the end from the present.

verses.verse_text: Psa 94:8 Understand , O dullest of the people ! Fools , when will you be wise ?
verses.meaning: bin names the inner perceptive faculty being commanded — the dull are called to exercise the understanding they refuse.

verses.verse_text: Pro 14:8 The wisdom of the prudent is to discern his way , but the folly of fools is deceiving .
verses.meaning: bin names discernment as the inner perceptive act of the prudent — understanding one's own situation and path.

verses.verse_text: Pro 14:15 The simple believes everything , but the prudent gives thought to his steps .
verses.meaning: bin names the reflective perceptive attending to one's own conduct — the inner faculty directed toward self-awareness and deliberate action.

verses.verse_text: Isa 29:16 You turn things upside down ! Shall the potter be regarded as the clay , that the thing made should say of its maker , “He did not make me”; or the thing formed say of him who formed it, “He has no understanding ”?
verses.meaning: bin names understanding attributed or denied to the Creator — the absurdity of the created thing judging its maker's inner comprehension.

verses.verse_text: Isa 44:18 They know not , nor do they discern , for he has shut their eyes , so that they cannot see , and their hearts , so that they cannot understand .
verses.meaning: bin names the failed inner perceptive faculty of idol-worshippers — God has shut their eyes and hearts.

verses.verse_text: Dan 11:33 And the wise among the people shall make many understand , though for some days they shall stumble by sword and flame , by captivity and plunder .
verses.meaning: bin names the inner perceptive comprehension that the wise impart to many — wisdom as enabling understanding in others during crisis.

verses.verse_text: Job 5:13 He catches the wise in their own craftiness , and the schemes of the wily are brought to a quick end .
verses.meaning: cha.kham names the wise person caught by their own craftiness — wisdom that serves cunning becomes the trap. The inner quality turns against its possessor.

verses.verse_text: Gen 15:6 And he believed the Lord , and he counted it to him as righteousness .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the divine act of moral crediting — God reckons Abram's faith as righteousness. The inner act of counting/imputing is here divine, not human.

verses.verse_text: Gen 31:15 Are we not regarded by him as foreigners ? For he has sold us, and he has indeed devoured our money .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the inner evaluation of social status — Laban has regarded his daughters as foreigners to be sold. Relational regard with negative valuation.

verses.verse_text: Lev 7:18 If any of the flesh of the sacrifice of his peace offering is eaten on the third day , he who offers it shall not be accepted , neither shall it be credited to him. It is tainted , and he who eats of it shall bear his iniquity .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the moral crediting/reckoning — the offering eaten late is not credited; the person bears iniquity instead.

verses.verse_text: Lev 17:4 and does not bring it to the entrance of the tent of meeting to offer it as a gift to the Lord in front of the tabernacle of the Lord , bloodguilt shall be imputed to that man . He has shed blood , and that man shall be cut off from among his people .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the imputation of moral guilt — the inner act of reckoning wrongdoing to the offender.

verses.verse_text: Lev 25:27 let him calculate the years since he sold it and pay back the balance to the man to whom he sold it, and then return to his property .
verses.meaning: Names the act of calculating the years since sale — the term identifies the mental reckoning required to determine the redemption price.

verses.verse_text: Lev 25:31 But the houses of the villages that have no wall around them shall be classified with the fields of the land . They may be redeemed , and they shall be released in the jubilee .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names a legal categorisation — assigning something to a class for the purpose of law. The inner act of reckoning determines the category.

verses.verse_text: Lev 25:50 He shall calculate with his buyer from the year when he sold himself to him until the year of jubilee , and the price of his sale shall vary with the number of years . The time he was with his owner shall be rated as the time of a hired worker .
verses.meaning: Names the act of calculating the time served — the term identifies the inner reckoning used to determine the proportional price of redemption.

verses.verse_text: Lev 25:52 If there remain but a few years until the year of jubilee , he shall calculate and pay for his redemption in proportion to his years of service.
verses.meaning: Names the act of proportional reckoning — the term identifies the priest's mental calculation of remaining years as the basis for the redemption payment.

verses.verse_text: Lev 27:18 but if he dedicates his field after the jubilee , then the priest shall calculate the price according to the years that remain until the year of jubilee , and a deduction shall be made from the valuation .
verses.meaning: Names the act of calculating the remaining years — the term identifies the valuation process as dependent on a careful mental reckoning of time.

verses.verse_text: Lev 27:23 then the priest shall calculate the amount of the valuation for it up to the year of jubilee , and the man shall give the valuation on that day as a holy gift to the Lord .
verses.meaning: Names the priestly act of calculating the valuation — the term identifies the mental reckoning assigned to the priest to determine the amount owed.

verses.verse_text: Num 18:27 And your contribution shall be counted to you as though it were the grain of the threshing floor , and as the fullness of the winepress .
verses.meaning: Names the act of counting or crediting — the term identifies the process by which the Levites' tithe offering is reckoned as equivalent to the threshing-floor produce.

verses.verse_text: Num 18:30 Therefore you shall say to them, ‘ When you have offered from it the best of it, then the rest shall be counted to the Levites as produce of the threshing floor , and as produce of the winepress .
verses.meaning: Names the act of reckoning the remainder — the term identifies the crediting of the best-offered portion so that what remains is counted as ordinary produce.

verses.verse_text: Num 23:9 For from the top of the crags I see him, from the hills I behold him; behold , a people dwelling alone , and not counting itself among the nations !
verses.meaning: cha.shav names Israel's own self-regard — they do not count themselves as one of the nations. An inner assessment of identity and distinctiveness.

verses.verse_text: Jos 13:3 ( from the Shihor , which is east of Egypt , northward to the boundary of Ekron , it is counted as Canaanite ; there are five rulers of the Philistines , those of Gaza , Ashdod , Ashkelon , Gath , and Ekron ), and those of the Avvim ,
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the territorial classification — how the land is reckoned for the purpose of allocation.

verses.verse_text: 2Sa 4:2 Now Saul’s son had two men who were captains of raiding bands ; the name of the one was Baanah , and the name of the other Rechab , sons of Rimmon a man of Benjamin from Beeroth ( for Beeroth also is counted part of Benjamin ;
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the territorial reckoning — how a place is classified within tribal assignments.

verses.verse_text: 2Sa 19:19 and said to the king , “Let not my lord hold me guilty or remember how your servant did wrong on the day my lord the king left Jerusalem . Do not let the king take it to heart .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the inner act of moral reckoning — Shimei asks not to be regarded as guilty.

verses.verse_text: 1Ki 10:21 All King Solomon’s drinking vessels were of gold , and all the vessels of the House of the Forest of Lebanon were of pure gold . None were of silver ; silver was not considered as anything in the days of Solomon .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the evaluative regard — silver was not reckoned as having value given the abundance of gold. Relative valuation through inner assessment.

verses.verse_text: 2Ki 12:15 And they did not ask for an accounting from the men into whose hand they delivered the money to pay out to the workmen , for they dealt honestly .
verses.meaning: Names the absence of a required accounting — the term identifies the non-imposition of a formal reckoning from those entrusted with the repair funds.

verses.verse_text: 2Ki 22:7 But no accounting shall be asked from them for the money that is delivered into their hand , for they deal honestly .”
verses.meaning: Names the absence of required accounting — the term identifies the deliberate non-demand for a reckoning from those who deal honestly with the funds.

verses.verse_text: 2Ch 9:20 All King Solomon’s drinking vessels were of gold , and all the vessels of the House of the Forest of Lebanon were of pure gold . Silver was not considered as anything in the days of Solomon .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the same evaluative reckoning — parallel passage to 1Ki 10:21.

verses.verse_text: Neh 13:13 And I appointed as treasurers over the storehouses Shelemiah the priest , Zadok the scribe , and Pedaiah of the Levites , and as their assistant Hanan the son of Zaccur , son of Mattaniah , for they were considered reliable , and their duty was to distribute to their brothers .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the evaluative regard of persons — they have been assessed as reliable and appointed on that basis.

verses.verse_text: Psa 32:2 Blessed is the man against whom the Lord counts no iniquity , and in whose spirit there is no deceit .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the divine non-imputation of iniquity — the person whose sin is not reckoned against them is blessed. The inner divine act of not counting guilt.

verses.verse_text: Psa 44:22 Yet for your sake we are killed all the day long; we are regarded as sheep to be slaughtered .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the social evaluative regard — the covenant people are counted as disposable by their enemies.

verses.verse_text: Psa 88:4 I am counted among those who go down to the pit ; I am a man who has no strength ,
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the evaluative regard — the psalmist has been reckoned by God (as experienced) among those who are as good as dead.

verses.verse_text: Psa 106:31 And that was counted to him as righteousness from generation to generation forever .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the imputation of righteousness to Phinehas — his zealous act is reckoned by God as righteousness. The same pattern as Gen 15:6.

verses.verse_text: Pro 27:14 Whoever blesses his neighbor with a loud voice , rising early in the morning , will be counted as cursing .
verses.meaning: Names the act of social reckoning or imputation — the term identifies how an exuberant public blessing, by its very excess, is counted against the one giving it.

verses.verse_text: Isa 13:17 Behold , I am stirring up the Medes against them, who have no regard for silver and do not delight in gold .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the evaluative non-regard — the Medes do not reckon silver as worth caring about. Their inner assessment removes the usual motivation.

verses.verse_text: Isa 33:8 The highways lie waste ; the traveler ceases . Covenants are broken ; cities are despised ; there is no regard for man .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the absence of regard for persons — in the time of desolation, the inner evaluation of human worth has collapsed.

verses.verse_text: Isa 40:15 Behold , the nations are like a drop from a bucket , and are accounted as the dust on the scales ; behold , he takes up the coastlands like fine dust .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names God's evaluative regard of the nations — they are reckoned as negligible before him. The divine inner evaluation is the measure.

verses.verse_text: Isa 40:17 All the nations are as nothing before him, they are accounted by him as less than nothing and emptiness .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the divine reckoning of the nations as emptiness — God's inner evaluation sets the ultimate measure of worth.

verses.verse_text: Isa 53:3 He was despised and rejected by men , a man of sorrows , and acquainted with grief ; and as one from whom men hide their faces he was despised , and we esteemed him not .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the failure of evaluative regard — the community did not reckon the Servant as worthy of esteem. Note: assigned to 5099-002 (abstaining from taste/mourning), which appears to be a misassignment. The span is about evaluative regard, not tasting.

verses.verse_text: Lam 4:2 The precious sons of Zion , worth their weight in fine gold , how they are regarded as earthen pots , the work of a potter’s hands !
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the devastating devaluation of those who were precious — their worth has been reckoned at the lowest possible level.

verses.verse_text: Hos 8:12 Were I to write for him my laws by the ten thousands, they would be regarded as a strange thing.
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the evaluative regard of God's laws — Israel reckons them as foreign rather than their own. A devastating inner assessment of God's instruction.

verses.verse_text: Mal 3:16 Then those who feared the Lord spoke with one another . The Lord paid attention and heard them, and a book of remembrance was written before him of those who feared the Lord and esteemed his name .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the evaluative regard of God's name — these are persons whose inner reckoning holds the name of the Lord as precious. The credit or imputation here moves toward God: they esteem him.

verses.verse_text: Gen 38:15 When Judah saw her , he thought she was a prostitute , for she had covered her face .
