verses.meaning: cha.shav names the inner act of forming a judgment from observation — Judah sees and forms an assessment, which turns out to be wrong.

verses.verse_text: Deu 2:11 Like the Anakim they are also counted as Rephaim , but the Moabites call them Emim .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the classificatory regard — how others count or categorise this people.

verses.verse_text: Deu 2:20 (It is also counted as a land of Rephaim . Rephaim formerly lived there—but the Ammonites call them Zamzummim —
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the same classificatory reckoning — how the land is regarded and named.

verses.verse_text: 1Sa 1:13 Hannah was speaking in her heart ; only her lips moved , and her voice was not heard . Therefore Eli took her to be a drunken woman .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names Eli's inner formation of a mistaken judgment — observing her lips moving silently, he assessed her as drunk. The inner perceptive act that misreads.

verses.verse_text: Job 6:26 Do you think that you can reprove words , when the speech of a despairing man is wind ?
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the inner formation of a judgment — Job challenges his friends' assumption that his words can be evaluated as if they were ordinary speech.

verses.verse_text: Job 13:24 Why do you hide your face and count me as your enemy ?
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the evaluative regard — Job protests God's reckoning of him as an adversary when he believes himself to be innocent.

verses.verse_text: Job 18:3 Why are we counted as cattle ? Why are we stupid in your sight ?
verses.meaning: cha.shav names Bildad's protest at how Job has regarded them — they feel dismissed as stupid animals in Job's inner evaluation.

verses.verse_text: Job 19:11 He has kindled his wrath against me and counts me as his adversary .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names God's reckoning of Job as adversary — the inner evaluation that has produced the experience of divine hostility.

verses.verse_text: Job 19:15 The guests in my house and my maidservants count me as a stranger ; I have become a foreigner in their eyes .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the social evaluative regard — those of his own household now reckon him as an outsider.

verses.verse_text: Job 33:10 Behold , he finds occasions against me, he counts me as his enemy ,
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the same evaluative regard as Job 19:11 — Elihu reports Job's complaint that God reckons him as adversary.

verses.verse_text: Job 35:2 “Do you think this to be just ? Do you say , ‘It is my right before God ,’
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the inner formation of a moral judgment — Elihu challenges Job's inner conviction of his own rightness before God.

verses.verse_text: Job 41:27 He counts iron as straw , and bronze as rotten wood .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the Leviathan's evaluative regard of weapons — iron and bronze are reckoned as nothing by the creature. A figure of invincible contempt.

verses.verse_text: Job 41:29 Clubs are counted as stubble ; he laughs at the rattle of javelins .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the same contemptuous reckoning — weapons are counted as worthless.

verses.verse_text: Job 41:32 Behind him he leaves a shining wake ; one would think the deep to be white-haired .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the inner forming of an impression from observation — the wake left by Leviathan produces the thought that the sea has become white.

verses.verse_text: Psa 73:16 But when I thought how to understand this , it seemed to me a wearisome task,
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the inner deliberative effort to form a judgment — the psalmist struggling to think through the problem of the prosperity of the wicked.

verses.verse_text: Psa 77:5 I consider the days of old , the years long ago .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the inner deliberative attending to past experience — pondering the memory of God's former acts as a means of finding orientation.

verses.verse_text: Psa 119:59 When I think on my ways , I turn my feet to your testimonies ;
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the deliberate inner attending to one's own conduct — the reflective self-examination that produces reorientation toward God's testimonies.

verses.verse_text: Psa 144:3 O Lord , what is man that you regard him, or the son of man that you think of him ?
verses.meaning: cha.shav names God's inner thinking of the human person — the marvel is that God attends to human beings at all. Divine cognitive attending as the ground of dignity.

verses.verse_text: Pro 17:28 Even a fool who keeps silent is considered wise ; when he closes his lips , he is deemed intelligent .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the social evaluative regard — others reckon the silent fool as wise. The inner quality of wisdom is being attributed based on external behaviour that conceals the absence of the inner reality.

verses.verse_text: Isa 2:22 Stop regarding man in whose nostrils is breath , for of what account is he ?
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the evaluative regard of the human person — the question challenges the assessment of human worth, arguing that mortal breath-holders deserve no ultimate regard.

verses.verse_text: Isa 5:28 their arrows are sharp , all their bows bent , their horses ’ hoofs seem like flint , and their wheels like the whirlwind .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the inner forming of a vivid impression — the army's power creates the perception of flint-hard hooves.

verses.verse_text: Isa 29:16 You turn things upside down ! Shall the potter be regarded as the clay , that the thing made should say of its maker , “He did not make me”; or the thing formed say of him who formed it, “He has no understanding ”?
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the absurd evaluative inversion — regarding the maker as the thing made is the confusion of categories.

verses.verse_text: Isa 29:17 Is it not yet a very little while until Lebanon shall be turned into a fruitful field , and the fruitful field shall be regarded as a forest ?
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the eschatological reclassification — the transformation of what things are causes a revaluation of how they are regarded.

verses.verse_text: Isa 32:15 until the Spirit is poured upon us from on high , and the wilderness becomes a fruitful field , and the fruitful field is deemed a forest .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the same evaluative reclassification after the Spirit is poured out — abundance so great that the fruitful field is reckoned as a forest.

verses.verse_text: Isa 53:4 Surely he has borne our griefs and carried our sorrows ; yet we esteemed him stricken , smitten by God , and afflicted .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the community's false evaluative judgment of the Servant — they assessed his suffering as divine punishment of his own sin. A decisive misreading of what they observed.

verses.verse_text: Jer 23:27 who think to make my people forget my name by their dreams that they tell one another , even as their fathers forgot my name for Baal ?
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the deliberate inner purposing of the false prophets — they think and plan to achieve the displacement of God's name.

verses.verse_text: 1Sa 14:24 And the men of Israel had been hard pressed that day , so Saul had laid an oath on the people , saying , “ Cursed be the man who eats food until it is evening and I am avenged on my enemies .” So none of the people had tasted food .
verses.meaning: Names the act of tasting as a form of perception — the term identifies the physical experience that the oath forbids, with perception of food functioning as the marker of transgression.

verses.verse_text: 1Sa 14:29 Then Jonathan said , “My father has troubled the land . See how my eyes have become bright because I tasted a little of this honey .
verses.meaning: Names the perceptual experience of tasting — the term identifies Jonathan's act of tasting honey as giving him a discernible inner brightening of perception.

verses.verse_text: 1Sa 14:43 Then Saul said to Jonathan , “ Tell me what you have done .” And Jonathan told him, “I tasted a little honey with the tip of the staff that was in my hand . Here I am; I will die .”
verses.meaning: Names the perceptual act Jonathan admits to — the term identifies the tasting of honey as the transgression under examination.

verses.verse_text: 2Sa 3:35 Then all the people came to persuade David to eat bread while it was yet day . But David swore , saying , “ God do so to me and more also , if I taste bread or anything else till the sun goes down !”
verses.meaning: ta.am names the deliberate abstaining from tasting as the outward form of inner mourning for Abner. The refusal to taste marks the inner state of grief expressed through the body.

verses.verse_text: 2Sa 19:35 I am this day eighty years old . Can I discern what is pleasant and what is not ? Can your servant taste what he eats or what he drinks ? Can I still listen to the voice of singing men and singing women ? Why then should your servant be an added burden to my lord the king ?
verses.meaning: ta.am names the inner faculty of discrimination applied to food — tasting as the capacity to discern what is pleasant. The aged Barzillai uses taste as the figure for diminished inner discernment.

verses.verse_text: Job 12:11 Does not the ear test words as the palate tastes food ?
verses.meaning: ta.am names taste as the figure for inner discernment — the ear's testing of words is like the palate's tasting of food. The inner faculty of discrimination applied to speech.

verses.verse_text: Job 34:3 for the ear tests words as the palate tastes food .
verses.meaning: ta.am names the same figure as Job 12:11 — inner discrimination by ear is like the palate's tasting. The faculty of discernment is the connecting image.

verses.verse_text: Psa 34:8 Oh, taste and see that the Lord is good ! Blessed is the man who takes refuge in him !
verses.meaning: ta.am names inner discernment as the figure for experiencing God — tasting God's goodness is the inner act of personally testing and finding him good. The figurative use is central to the VCG.

verses.verse_text: Pro 31:18 She perceives that her merchandise is profitable . Her lamp does not go out at night .
verses.meaning: ta.am names the inner discernment applied to commercial life — the capable wife's inner faculty for testing and evaluating applied to the profitability of her work.

verses.verse_text: Jon 3:7 And he issued a proclamation and published through Nineveh , “ By the decree of the king and his nobles : Let neither man nor beast , herd nor flock , taste anything . Let them not feed or drink water ,
verses.meaning: Names the act of tasting as a form of physical perception — the term identifies the prohibition laid upon all living creatures in Nineveh's fast.

verses.verse_text: Pro 12:16 The vexation of a fool is known at once , but the prudent ignores an insult .
verses.meaning: Names the immediate visibility of inner state — the term identifies a fool's vexation as something known at once, in contrast to the prudent man who conceals an insult.

verses.verse_text: Isa 41:20 that they may see and know , may consider and understand together , that the hand of the Lord has done this , the Holy One of Israel has created it .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the covenantal recognition of God's creative acts — seeing, knowing, considering, understanding together as the response to divine action.

verses.verse_text: Isa 44:18 They know not , nor do they discern , for he has shut their eyes , so that they cannot see , and their hearts , so that they cannot understand .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the absent moral knowing of idol-worshippers — God has shut their hearts.

verses.verse_text: Isa 53:3 He was despised and rejected by men , a man of sorrows , and acquainted with grief ; and as one from whom men hide their faces he was despised , and we esteemed him not .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the Servant's intimate self-knowledge of suffering — not merely experiencing grief but deeply knowing it from within.

verses.verse_text: Gen 3:1 Now the serpent was more crafty than any other beast of the field that the Lord God had made . He said to the woman , “Did God actually say , ‘You shall not eat of any tree in the garden ’?”
verses.meaning: a.rum names craftiness as the serpent's defining inner disposition — perceptive cunning directed entirely toward deception. The same inner quality that is valued as prudence is here the instrument of the fall.

verses.verse_text: Job 15:5 For your iniquity teaches your mouth , and you choose the tongue of the crafty .
verses.meaning: a.rum names the inner disposition of cunning that shapes speech — Eliphaz accuses Job's words of reflecting craftiness rather than innocence.

verses.verse_text: Pro 12:16 The vexation of a fool is known at once , but the prudent ignores an insult .
verses.meaning: a.rum names prudence as the inner disposition that governs response to provocation — the prudent person sees the situation clearly and chooses not to react.

verses.verse_text: Pro 12:23 A prudent man conceals knowledge , but the heart of fools proclaims folly .
verses.meaning: a.rum names the inner disposition of prudent discretion — knowing when not to speak is the mark of the a.rum person.

verses.verse_text: Pro 13:16 Every prudent man acts with knowledge , but a fool flaunts his folly .
verses.meaning: a.rum names the inner capacity that governs action — prudent persons act from inner knowledge; fools display their emptiness.

verses.verse_text: Pro 14:8 The wisdom of the prudent is to discern his way , but the folly of fools is deceiving .
verses.meaning: a.rum names the discerning inner capacity — the prudent person's wisdom is specifically the ability to see their own path clearly.

verses.verse_text: Pro 14:15 The simple believes everything , but the prudent gives thought to his steps .
verses.meaning: a.rum names the inner deliberative quality of the prudent person — not credulous but thoughtful and careful.

verses.verse_text: Pro 14:18 The simple inherit folly , but the prudent are crowned with knowledge .
verses.meaning: a.rum names the prudent as those whose inner quality is crowned with knowledge — prudence and knowledge are the upward path against the downward drift of the simple.

verses.verse_text: Pro 22:3 The prudent sees danger and hides himself, but the simple go on and suffer for it .
verses.meaning: a.rum names the inner perceptive quality of the prudent that enables them to see ahead and act protectively — practical foresight as the expression of the prudent disposition.

verses.verse_text: Pro 27:12 The prudent sees danger and hides himself , but the simple go on and suffer for it .
verses.meaning: a.rum names the same prudent foresight — identical proverb to Pro 22:3.

verses.verse_text: 1Sa 23:22 Go , make yet more sure . Know and see the place where his foot is, and who has seen him there , for it is told me that he is very cunning .
verses.meaning: a.rom names David's craftiness as perceived by Saul's agents — the inner disposition of shrewd, evasive intelligence. Here used to describe David navigating persecution.

verses.verse_text: Job 5:13 He catches the wise in their own craftiness , and the schemes of the wily are brought to a quick end .
verses.meaning: a.rom names the inner disposition of cunning that becomes the trap — God uses the crafty person's own strategy against them.

verses.verse_text: Pro 15:5 A fool despises his father’s instruction , but whoever heeds reproof is prudent .
verses.meaning: a.rom names prudence as a learnable inner orientation — the simple who heed correction acquire the prudent inner disposition. Responsiveness to instruction is what makes one prudent.

verses.verse_text: Pro 19:25 Strike a scoffer , and the simple will learn prudence ; reprove a man of understanding , and he will gain knowledge .
verses.meaning: a.rom names prudence as what the simple can acquire by observing the scoffer's discipline — the inner orientation of prudence is learnable even by the naive.

verses.verse_text: Gen 3:6 So when the woman saw that the tree was good for food , and that it was a delight to the eyes , and that the tree was to be desired to make one wise , she took of its fruit and ate , and she also gave some to her husband who was with her, and he ate .
verses.meaning: sa.khal names the desirability of wisdom as what the tree offered — the woman's perception of what eating would produce. The span locates inner desire for wisdom as the ground of the transgression. Note: assigned to 5099-002 (abstaining from taste as grief/penitence) which does not fit. This verse is about desire to make wise, not abstaining from taste.

verses.verse_text: Deu 29:9 Therefore keep the words of this covenant and do them, that you may prosper in all that you do .
verses.meaning: sa.khal names prudent wisdom expressed in covenant keeping that produces prosperity — the inner capacity oriented toward God's ways produces outward success.

verses.verse_text: Deu 32:29 If they were wise , they would understand this ; they would discern their latter end !
verses.meaning: sa.khal names the prudent wisdom that would enable self-perception and awareness of consequences — wisdom as the capacity to see clearly what one's end will be.

verses.verse_text: Jos 1:7 Only be strong and very courageous , being careful to do according to all the law that Moses my servant commanded you. Do not turn from it to the right hand or to the left , that you may have good success wherever you go .
verses.meaning: sa.khal names the wise conduct that produces success — wisdom aligned with Torah produces prospered action.

verses.verse_text: Jos 1:8 This Book of the Law shall not depart from your mouth , but you shall meditate on it day and night , so that you may be careful to do according to all that is written in it. For then you will make your way prosperous , and then you will have good success .
verses.meaning: sa.khal names the fruit of meditative engagement with the Law — wisdom and success as the outcome of sustained inner engagement with God's word.

verses.verse_text: 1Sa 18:5 And David went out and was successful wherever Saul sent him, so that Saul set him over the men of war . And this was good in the sight of all the people and also in the sight of Saul’s servants .
verses.meaning: sa.khal names the wisdom-in-action that produces consistent success — an inner quality expressed in effective conduct.

verses.verse_text: 1Sa 18:14 And David had success in all his undertakings , for the Lord was with him .
verses.meaning: sa.khal names success as the fruit of God's presence — the Lord's being with David is the ground of his wise conduct and outcome.

verses.verse_text: 1Sa 18:15 And when Saul saw that he had great success , he stood in fearful awe of him.
verses.meaning: sa.khal names the visible success that produces awe in the observer — wisdom's fruit inspires fear.

verses.verse_text: 1Sa 18:30 Then the commanders of the Philistines came out to battle, and as often as they came out David had more success than all the servants of Saul , so that his name was highly esteemed .
verses.meaning: sa.khal names the repeated pattern of successful wise conduct — consistent wisdom expressed in action producing lasting reputation.

verses.verse_text: 1Ki 2:3 and keep the charge of the Lord your God , walking in his ways and keeping his statutes , his commandments , his rules , and his testimonies , as it is written in the Law of Moses , that you may prosper in all that you do and wherever you turn ,
verses.meaning: sa.khal names the wisdom-in-action that keeping God's ways produces — covenantal faithfulness expressed in wise conduct leading to prosperity.

verses.verse_text: 2Ki 18:7 And the Lord was with him; wherever he went out , he prospered . He rebelled against the king of Assyria and would not serve him .
verses.meaning: Names the condition of prospering through prudent conduct — the term characterises Hezekiah's inner wise management as the ground of his success.

verses.verse_text: 1Ch 28:19 “ All this he made clear to me in writing from the hand of the Lord , all the work to be done according to the plan .”
verses.meaning: [Note: sa.khal is a M15-D term appearing in this M15-E verse table, likely due to proximity in spans. The span is about divine disclosure of the building plan.]

verses.verse_text: 2Ch 30:22 And Hezekiah spoke encouragingly to all the Levites who showed good skill in the service of the Lord . So they ate the food of the festival for seven days , sacrificing peace offerings and giving thanks to the Lord , the God of their fathers .
verses.meaning: sa.khal names the inner capacity for prudent, skilled service — wisdom expressed in the competent performance of temple ministry.

verses.verse_text: Neh 8:13 On the second day the heads of fathers’ houses of all the people , with the priests and the Levites , came together to Ezra the scribe in order to study the words of the Law .
verses.meaning: sa.khal names the study that produces prudent understanding — engaging the Law with the inner orientation of wise discernment.

verses.verse_text: Neh 9:20 You gave your good Spirit to instruct them and did not withhold your manna from their mouth and gave them water for their thirst .
verses.meaning: sa.khal names the Spirit's instructing work — the Spirit giving Israel prudent wisdom through guidance in the wilderness.

verses.verse_text: Job 22:2 “Can a man be profitable to God ? Surely he who is wise is profitable to himself.
verses.meaning: sa.khal names wisdom as what benefits its possessor — Eliphaz argues that wisdom is for the person's own gain, not God's.

verses.verse_text: Job 34:27 because they turned aside from following him and had no regard for any of his ways ,
verses.meaning: Names the inner failing of those who turned away from God — the term identifies the absence of regard for God's ways as the opposite of prudent attentiveness.

verses.verse_text: Job 34:35 ‘ Job speaks without knowledge ; his words are without insight .’
verses.meaning: sa.khal names insight as what Job's words lack — prudent wisdom that would give substance to speech.

verses.verse_text: Psa 2:10 Now therefore, O kings , be wise ; be warned , O rulers of the earth .
verses.meaning: sa.khal names the prudent wisdom being commanded of rulers — the inner capacity for right judgment directed toward recognition of God's sovereignty.

verses.verse_text: Psa 14:2 The Lord looks down from heaven on the children of man , to see if there are any who understand , who seek after God .
verses.meaning: sa.khal names the understanding that seeks God — prudent wisdom oriented toward God is what the divine search finds absent.

verses.verse_text: Psa 32:8 I will instruct you and teach you in the way you should go ; I will counsel you with my eye upon you.
verses.meaning: sa.khal names the prudent instruction that imparts wisdom for living — God's instructing produces inner discernment for the path.

verses.verse_text: Psa 36:3 The words of his mouth are trouble and deceit ; he has ceased to act wisely and do good .
verses.meaning: sa.khal names wise action as what the wicked person has abandoned — prudent wisdom and moral goodness are linked; their absence marks the slide into transgression.

verses.verse_text: To the choirmaster . A Psalm of David . Psa 41:1 Blessed is the one who considers the poor ! In the day of trouble the Lord delivers him;
verses.meaning: sa.khal names the perceptive attention to the poor as a form of wisdom — prudent inner attending directed toward the vulnerable.

verses.verse_text: Psa 53:2 God looks down from heaven on the children of man to see if there are any who understand , who seek after God .
verses.meaning: sa.khal names understanding oriented toward God as what the divine search seeks — the same as Psa 14:2, parallel passage.

verses.verse_text: Psa 64:9 Then all mankind fears ; they tell what God has brought about and ponder what he has done .
verses.meaning: sa.khal names the reflective perceptive attending to God's acts — pondering what God has done as an expression of prudent wisdom.

verses.verse_text: Psa 94:8 Understand , O dullest of the people ! Fools , when will you be wise ?
verses.meaning: sa.khal names wisdom as what the dull people are called to — prudent understanding of who God is and what he perceives.

verses.verse_text: Psa 101:2 I will ponder the way that is blameless . Oh when will you come to me? I will walk with integrity of heart within my house ;
verses.meaning: sa.khal names the reflective deliberative attending to the blameless path — wisdom expressed in sustained inner pondering of integrity.

verses.verse_text: Psa 106:7 Our fathers , when they were in Egypt , did not consider your wondrous works ; they did not remember the abundance of your steadfast love , but rebelled by the sea , at the Red Sea .
verses.meaning: sa.khal names the wise perceptive attending that was absent in the fathers — failure to consider God's works is the root of their rebellion.

verses.verse_text: Pro 1:3 to receive instruction in wise dealing , in righteousness , justice , and equity ;
verses.meaning: sa.khal names wise dealing as the practical expression of the inner wisdom Proverbs aims to produce — wisdom applied to moral and social life.

verses.verse_text: Pro 10:5 He who gathers in summer is a prudent son , but he who sleeps in harvest is a son who brings shame .
verses.meaning: sa.khal names prudent inner wisdom expressed in foresighted practical action — gathering in season is the form wise judgment takes in agricultural life.

verses.verse_text: Pro 10:19 When words are many , transgression is not lacking , but whoever restrains his lips is prudent .
verses.meaning: sa.khal names prudent wisdom expressed in restraint of speech — the inner capacity for self-governance shown in controlled expression.

verses.verse_text: Pro 14:35 A servant who deals wisely has the king’s favor , but his wrath falls on one who acts shamefully .
verses.meaning: sa.khal names wise dealing as what earns royal recognition — the inner quality expressed in effective conduct.

verses.verse_text: Pro 15:24 The path of life leads upward for the prudent , that he may turn away from Sheol beneath .
verses.meaning: sa.khal names the prudent person whose inner quality directs them upward — wisdom as the orientation away from death and toward life.

verses.verse_text: Pro 16:20 Whoever gives thought to the word will discover good , and blessed is he who trusts in the Lord .
verses.meaning: sa.khal names the perceptive attending to the word — deliberate inner engagement that produces good.

verses.verse_text: Pro 16:23 The heart of the wise makes his speech judicious and adds persuasiveness to his lips .
verses.meaning: sa.khal names judiciousness as the quality the wise heart produces in speech — inner wisdom expressed in carefully calibrated words.

verses.verse_text: Pro 17:2 A servant who deals wisely will rule over a son who acts shamefully and will share the inheritance as one of the brothers .
verses.meaning: sa.khal names wise dealing as what enables a servant to surpass a shameful son — inner wisdom producing social elevation.

verses.verse_text: Pro 17:8 A bribe is like a magic stone in the eyes of the one who gives it; wherever he turns he prospers .
verses.meaning: Names the experience of effectiveness that accompanies the gift — the term characterises the bribe as producing a prosperous outcome in the eyes of its giver.

verses.verse_text: Pro 19:14 House and wealth are inherited from fathers , but a prudent wife is from the Lord .
verses.meaning: sa.khal names prudent wisdom as a divine gift in a spouse — the inner quality of discernment in a wife is attributed to God's provision.

verses.verse_text: Pro 21:11 When a scoffer is punished , the simple becomes wise ; when a wise man is instructed , he gains knowledge .
verses.meaning: sa.khal names the prudent wisdom that grows with instruction — the wise person's inner capacity is enriched by receiving teaching.

verses.verse_text: Pro 21:12 The Righteous One observes the house of the wicked ; he throws the wicked down to ruin .
verses.meaning: sa.khal names the perceptive observing of God — attending wisely to the situation and acting accordingly. The divine prudent attending to wickedness produces judgment.

verses.verse_text: Pro 21:16 One who wanders from the way of good sense will rest in the assembly of the dead .
verses.meaning: sa.khal names good sense as the path that must not be abandoned — departure from the inner orientation of wisdom leads to death.

verses.verse_text: Isa 41:20 that they may see and know , may consider and understand together , that the hand of the Lord has done this , the Holy One of Israel has created it .
verses.meaning: sa.khal names the understanding that perceives God's creative acts — prudent discernment applied to recognising what God has done.

verses.verse_text: Isa 44:18 They know not , nor do they discern , for he has shut their eyes , so that they cannot see , and their hearts , so that they cannot understand .
verses.meaning: sa.khal names the understanding that is absent in idol-worshippers — God has closed their inner capacity for prudent discernment.

verses.verse_text: Isa 52:13 Behold , my servant shall act wisely ; he shall be high and lifted up , and shall be exalted .
verses.meaning: sa.khal names the wise action of the Servant — his conduct is both wise and productive of exaltation. The inner capacity expressed in effective, God-aligned action produces the highest outcome.

verses.verse_text: Jer 3:15 “‘And I will give you shepherds after my own heart , who will feed you with knowledge and understanding .
verses.meaning: sa.khal names the understanding these shepherds will give — prudent wisdom imparted through their shepherding.

verses.verse_text: Jer 9:24 but let him who boasts boast in this , that he understands and knows me, that I am the Lord who practices steadfast love , justice , and righteousness in the earth . For in these things I delight , declares the Lord .”
verses.meaning: sa.khal names understanding of God as the only legitimate boast — inner knowing of God that produces relational orientation toward his character.

verses.verse_text: Jer 10:21 For the shepherds are stupid and do not inquire of the Lord ; therefore they have not prospered , and all their flock is scattered .
verses.meaning: sa.khal names the prosperity that comes from seeking God — its absence is directly caused by the shepherds' failure to inquire. Prudent wisdom and seeking God are linked.

verses.verse_text: Jer 20:11 But the Lord is with me as a dread warrior ; therefore my persecutors will stumble ; they will not overcome me. They will be greatly shamed , for they will not succeed . Their eternal dishonor will never be forgotten .
verses.meaning: Names the incapacity of the persecutors to succeed — the term identifies their failure to act with effective prudence as the ground of their inevitable shame.

verses.verse_text: Jer 23:5 “ Behold , the days are coming , declares the Lord , when I will raise up for David a righteous Branch , and he shall reign as king and deal wisely , and shall execute justice and righteousness in the land .
verses.meaning: sa.khal names the wise ruling of the messianic Branch — prudent conduct producing justice and righteousness. Wisdom in governance as the eschatological ideal.

verses.verse_text: Jer 50:9 For behold , I am stirring up and bringing against Babylon a gathering of great nations , from the north country . And they shall array themselves against her. From there she shall be taken . Their arrows are like a skilled warrior who does not return empty-handed .
verses.meaning: sa.khal names skill as the inner quality of the warrior — the arrows are like a skilled warrior in their effectiveness.

verses.verse_text: Dan 1:4 youths without blemish , of good appearance and skillful in all wisdom , endowed with knowledge , understanding learning , and competent to stand in the king’s palace , and to teach them the literature and language of the Chaldeans .
verses.meaning: sa.khal names skillfulness as one of the inner qualities of the chosen youths — competence in wisdom as an inner capacity.

verses.verse_text: Dan 1:17 As for these four youths , God gave them learning and skill in all literature and wisdom , and Daniel had understanding in all visions and dreams .
verses.meaning: sa.khal names skill as a God-given inner endowment — divine gift of competence in literature and wisdom for the youths.

verses.verse_text: Dan 9:13 As it is written in the Law of Moses , all this calamity has come upon us; yet we have not entreated the favor of the Lord our God , turning from our iniquities and gaining insight by your truth .
verses.meaning: sa.khal names the insight that comes through turning to God's truth — prudent discernment acquired through repentance and engagement with divine truth.

verses.verse_text: Dan 9:22 He made me understand , speaking with me and saying , “O Daniel , I have now come out to give you insight and understanding .
verses.meaning: sa.khal names the insight Gabriel brings to Daniel — divinely imparted prudent discernment of the vision.

verses.verse_text: Dan 9:25 Know therefore and understand that from the going out of the word to restore and build Jerusalem to the coming of an anointed one , a prince , there shall be seven weeks . And for sixty-two weeks it shall be built again with squares and moat , but in a troubled time .
verses.meaning: sa.khal names the understanding required to grasp the prophetic timeline — prudent discernment applied to interpreting divine disclosure.

verses.verse_text: Dan 11:33 And the wise among the people shall make many understand , though for some days they shall stumble by sword and flame , by captivity and plunder .
verses.meaning: sa.khal names wisdom as a teaching capacity in crisis — the wise use their inner discernment to help others comprehend what is happening.

verses.verse_text: Dan 11:35 and some of the wise shall stumble , so that they may be refined , purified , and made white , until the time of the end , for it still awaits the appointed time .
verses.meaning: sa.khal names the wise as those who suffer for their wisdom — the inner quality does not exempt from difficulty but is refined through it.

verses.verse_text: Dan 12:3 And those who are wise shall shine like the brightness of the sky above; and those who turn many to righteousness , like the stars forever and ever .
verses.meaning: sa.khal names eschatological wisdom as what produces radiance — the wise who turn many to righteousness share in the glory of the resurrection.

verses.verse_text: Dan 12:10 Many shall purify themselves and make themselves white and be refined , but the wicked shall act wickedly . And none of the wicked shall understand , but those who are wise shall understand .
verses.meaning: sa.khal names wisdom as the moral condition that enables understanding of the end — wisdom and wickedness are incompatible with each other's outcomes.

verses.verse_text: Amo 5:13 Therefore he who is prudent will keep silent in such a time , for it is an evil time .
verses.meaning: Names the disposition of inner discernment that leads to silence in an evil time — the term identifies the prudent person's choice to withhold speech as a form of wisdom.

subgroups.label: Deliberative planning, counsel, and purposive intent
subgroups.description: Terms that name the inner capacity of purposive forward-orientation — forming intent, devising plans, giving and receiving counsel, making deliberate decisions. Includes divine and human purposive activity.

verses.verse_text: Rom 1:13 I do not want you to be unaware , brothers , that I have often intended to come to you ( but thus far have been prevented ), in order that I may reap some harvest among you as well as among the rest of the Gentiles .
verses.meaning: Names the reader's potential state of unawareness about Paul's intended visit — the term frames the disclosure as correcting an inner gap in knowledge.

verses.verse_text: Luk 14:31 Or what king , going out to encounter another king in war , will not sit down first and deliberate whether he is able with ten thousand to meet him who comes against him with twenty thousand ?
verses.meaning: bouleuō names the inner deliberative act of weighing options — the parable uses military planning as the figure for the inner consideration that discipleship requires.

verses.verse_text: Joh 11:53 So from that day on they made plans to put him to death .
verses.meaning: bouleuō names the deliberate inner resolve formed against Jesus — the council's purposive decision to kill him, made and held from that moment.

verses.verse_text: Joh 12:10 So the chief priests made plans to put Lazarus to death as well ,
verses.meaning: bouleuō names the extended deliberate resolve — the same purposive intent extended to include Lazarus because his existence was evidence for Jesus.

verses.verse_text: Act 5:33 When they heard this, they were enraged and wanted to kill them .
verses.meaning: bouleuō names the inner resolve formed in anger — the council's purposive intention to kill the apostles.

verses.verse_text: Act 15:37 Now Barnabas wanted to take with them John called Mark .
verses.meaning: bouleuō names Barnabas's inner deliberate intent — his resolved purpose about the mission companion.

verses.verse_text: Act 27:39 Now when it was day , they did not recognize the land , but they noticed a bay with a beach , on which they planned if possible to run the ship ashore.
verses.meaning: bouleuō names the deliberate inner resolve formed in a crisis — navigating by deliberate decision toward the best available option.

verses.verse_text: 2Cor 1:17 Was I vacillating when I wanted to do this ? Do I make my plans according to the flesh , ready to say “ Yes , yes ” and “ No , no ” at the same time?
verses.meaning: bouleuō names Paul's purposive intent about travel — the charge of vacillation prompts the defence that his deliberate plans are not fleshly.

verses.verse_text: Mat 1:19 And her husband Joseph , being a just man and unwilling to put her to shame , resolved to divorce her quietly .
verses.meaning: boulomai names Joseph's inner volitional resolution — he forms a deliberate purpose to act in a way that protects Mary while complying with his understanding of the law.

verses.verse_text: Mat 11:27 All things have been handed over to me by my Father , and no one knows the Son except the Father , and no one knows the Father except the Son and anyone to whom the Son chooses to reveal him.
verses.meaning: boulomai names Christ's sovereign inner will in disclosure — the choosing is an act of divine purposive will.

verses.verse_text: Mar 15:15 So Pilate , wishing to satisfy the crowd , released for them Barabbas , and having scourged Jesus , he delivered him to be crucified .
verses.meaning: boulomai names Pilate's inner volitional intent — he wills to satisfy rather than to do justice, and acts accordingly.

verses.verse_text: Luk 10:22 All things have been handed over to me by my Father , and no one knows who the Son is except the Father , or who the Father is except the Son and anyone to whom the Son chooses to reveal him.”
verses.meaning: boulomai names Christ's sovereign willing of disclosure — same as Mat 11:27 parallel.

verses.verse_text: Luk 22:42 saying , “ Father , if you are willing , remove this cup from me . Nevertheless , not my will , but yours , be done .”
verses.meaning: boulomai names the human willing of Christ in Gethsemane — the prayer expresses both his inner desire (remove the cup) and his submission to the Father's sovereign will. The tension between human and divine willing is at the centre.

verses.verse_text: Joh 18:39 But you have a custom that I should release one man for you at the Passover . So do you want me to release to you the King of the Jews ?”
verses.meaning: boulomai names the inner willing of the crowd as Pilate presents the choice — he appeals to their want/desire to shape the outcome.

verses.verse_text: Act 5:28 saying , “We strictly charged you not to teach in this name , yet here you have filled Jerusalem with your teaching , and you intend to bring this man’s blood upon us .”
verses.meaning: boulomai names the imputed intent of the apostles as perceived by the authorities — their teaching is read as purposive intention.

verses.verse_text: Act 12:4 And when he had seized him , he put him in prison , delivering him over to four squads of soldiers to guard him , intending after the Passover to bring him out to the people .
verses.meaning: boulomai names Herod's purposive intent — the plan formed within him that shapes the imprisonment.

verses.verse_text: Act 17:20 For you bring some strange things to our ears . We wish to know therefore what these things mean .”
verses.meaning: boulomai names the Athenians' inner desire and intent — purposive wanting directed toward understanding the new teaching.

verses.verse_text: Act 18:15 But since it is a matter of questions about words and names and your own law , see to it yourselves . I refuse to be a judge of these things .”
verses.meaning: boulomai names Gallio's inner resolved refusal — a deliberate act of will declining jurisdiction.

verses.verse_text: Act 18:27 And when he wished to cross to Achaia , the brothers encouraged him and wrote to the disciples to welcome him . When he arrived , he greatly helped those who through grace had believed ,
verses.meaning: boulomai names Apollos's inner intent formed about travel — a purposive desire oriented toward the mission.

verses.verse_text: Act 19:30 But when Paul wished to go in among the crowd , the disciples would not let him .
verses.meaning: boulomai names Paul's inner purposive intent — the desire to enter and the disciples' preventive action.

verses.verse_text: Act 22:30 But on the next day , desiring to know the real reason why he was being accused by the Jews , he unbound him and commanded the chief priests and all the council to meet , and he brought Paul down and set him before them .
verses.meaning: boulomai names the tribune's purposive inner desire — wanting to know motivates the inquiry.

verses.verse_text: Act 23:28 And desiring to know the charge for which they were accusing him , I brought him down to their council .
verses.meaning: boulomai names the same purposive inner desire directed toward legal clarity.

verses.verse_text: Act 25:20 Being at a loss how to investigate these questions , I asked whether he wanted to go to Jerusalem and be tried there regarding them .
verses.meaning: boulomai names Paul's inner desire/will as the object of Festus's inquiry — what Paul wanted shapes the options available.

verses.verse_text: Act 25:22 Then Agrippa said to Festus , “ I would like to hear the man myself .” “ Tomorrow ,” said he, “you will hear him .”
verses.meaning: boulomai names Agrippa's inner desire — purposive wanting that sets the agenda.

verses.verse_text: Act 27:43 But the centurion , wishing to save Paul , kept them from carrying out their plan . He ordered those who could swim to jump overboard first and make for the land ,
verses.meaning: boulomai names the centurion's inner purposive will — a desire that overrides the soldiers' plan.

verses.verse_text: Act 28:18 When they had examined me , they wished to set me at liberty , because there was no reason for the death penalty in my case.
verses.meaning: boulomai names the examiners' inner volitional conclusion — their purposive intent was release.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 12:11 All these are empowered by one and the same Spirit , who apportions to each one individually as he wills .
verses.meaning: boulomai names the Spirit's sovereign inner will — the decisive factor in the distribution of gifts. Divine purposive willing that is absolute.

verses.verse_text: 2Cor 1:15 Because I was sure of this , I wanted to come to you first , so that you might have a second experience of grace .
verses.meaning: boulomai names Paul's inner purposive desire — a formed intention that circumstances prevented.

verses.verse_text: Phili 1:12 I want you to know , brothers , that what has happened to me has really served to advance the gospel ,
verses.meaning: boulomai names Paul's purposive communicative desire — his inner will to share this knowledge with the community.

verses.verse_text: 1Ti 2:8 I desire then that in every place the men should pray , lifting holy hands without anger or quarreling ;
verses.meaning: boulomai names Paul's apostolic inner purposive willing — what he desires and wills for the community's practice.

verses.verse_text: 1Ti 5:14 So I would have younger widows marry , bear children , manage their households, and give the adversary no occasion for slander .
verses.meaning: boulomai names Paul's apostolic inner purpose for younger widows — a deliberate recommendation arising from his formed judgment.

verses.verse_text: 1Ti 6:9 But those who desire to be rich fall into temptation , into a snare , into many senseless and harmful desires that plunge people into ruin and destruction .
verses.meaning: boulomai names the purposive inner desire for wealth — the wanting that leads into the trap.

verses.verse_text: Tit 3:8 The saying is trustworthy , and I want you to insist on these things , so that those who have believed in God may be careful to devote themselves to good works . These things are excellent and profitable for people .
verses.meaning: boulomai names Paul's apostolic purposive will — what he deliberately intends for Titus's ministry.

verses.verse_text: Heb 6:17 So when God desired to show more convincingly to the heirs of the promise the unchangeable character of his purpose , he guaranteed it with an oath ,
verses.meaning: boulomai names God's inner purposive will directed toward assurance for his people — divine wanting that produces the oath.

verses.verse_text: Jam 1:18 Of his own will he brought us forth by the word of truth , that we should be a kind of firstfruits of his creatures .
verses.meaning: boulomai names God's sovereign purposive will in regeneration — the new birth is an act of divine wanting, not human initiative.

verses.verse_text: Jam 3:4 Look at the ships also : though they are so large and are driven by strong winds , they are guided by a very small rudder wherever the will of the pilot directs .
verses.meaning: boulomai names the pilot's inner purposive will — a small act of directing will that governs the entire vessel. The figure illustrates the tongue's similar power.

verses.verse_text: Jam 4:4 You adulterous people ! Do you not know that friendship with the world is enmity with God ? Therefore whoever wishes to be a friend of the world makes himself an enemy of God .
verses.meaning: boulomai names the inner purposive desire directed toward worldly friendship — a deliberate wanting that constitutes enmity with God.

verses.verse_text: 2Pe 3:9 The Lord is not slow to fulfill his promise as some count slowness , but is patient toward you , not wishing that any should perish , but that all should reach repentance .
verses.meaning: boulomai names God's inner purposive will regarding human destiny — the divine want directed universally toward repentance and life. One of the defining NT statements of divine willing.

verses.verse_text: Phile 13 I would have been glad to keep him with me , in order that he might serve me on your behalf during my imprisonment for the gospel ,
verses.meaning: boulomai names Paul's inner purposive desire — what he would have wanted if he had acted unilaterally.

verses.verse_text: 2Jo 12 Though I have much to write to you , I would rather not use paper and ink . Instead I hope to come to you and talk face to face , so that our joy may be complete .
verses.meaning: boulomai names the elder's inner preference and purposive intent — a deliberately formed choice about the mode of communication.

verses.verse_text: 3Jo 10 So if I come , I will bring up what he is doing , talking wicked nonsense against us . And not content with that , he refuses to welcome the brothers , and also stops those who want to and puts them out of the church .
verses.meaning: boulomai names John's purposive intent — a deliberate resolution about what he will do on arrival.

verses.verse_text: Jude 5 Now I want to remind you , although you once fully knew it , that Jesus , who saved a people out of the land of Egypt , afterward destroyed those who did not believe .
verses.meaning: boulomai names Jude's inner purposive communicative intent — his desire to bring the community to remember.

verses.verse_text: Act 20:3 There he spent three months , and when a plot was made against him by the Jews as he was about to set sail for Syria , he decided to return through Macedonia .
verses.meaning: gnōmē names the considered inner resolution — Paul's deliberate judgment about the route, formed in response to the threat.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 1:10 I appeal to you , brothers , by the name of our Lord Jesus Christ , that all of you agree , and that there be no divisions among you , but that you be united in the same mind and the same judgment .
verses.meaning: gnōmē names the considered communal resolution — the shared inner judgment that produces unity rather than division.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 7:25 Now concerning the betrothed , I have no command from the Lord , but I give my judgment as one who by the Lord’s mercy is trustworthy .
verses.meaning: gnōmē names Paul's authoritative inner assessment offered without a dominical command — a considered personal judgment given with apostolic weight.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 7:40 Yet in my judgment she is happier if she remains as she is . And I think that I too have the Spirit of God .
verses.meaning: gnōmē names the same apostolic inner judgment — Paul's considered resolution on the question of the widow.

verses.verse_text: 2Cor 8:10 And in this matter I give my judgment : this benefits you , who a year ago started not only to do this work but also to desire to do it.
verses.meaning: gnōmē names Paul's considered pastoral judgment — an inner resolution about what will serve the community's best interest.

verses.verse_text: Rev 17:13 These are of one mind , and they hand over their power and authority to the beast .
verses.meaning: gnōmē names the shared purposive resolution of the kings — a collective inner judgment placing all authority behind the beast.

verses.verse_text: Rev 17:17 for God has put it into their hearts to carry out his purpose by being of one mind and handing over their royal power to the beast , until the words of God are fulfilled .
verses.meaning: gnōmē names the shared inner purpose placed by God into the kings' hearts — the collective resolution that serves divine ends even through human opposition. God's purposing working through the unified purposing of the hostile kings.

verses.verse_text: Phile 14 but I preferred to do nothing without your consent in order that your goodness might not be by compulsion but of your own accord .
verses.meaning: gnōmē names Philemon's considered inner resolution that Paul will not bypass — the gnōmē of the other is respected as the basis of free action.

verses.verse_text: Act 24:2 And when he had been summoned , Tertullus began to accuse him, saying : “Since through you we enjoy much peace , and since by your foresight , most excellent Felix , reforms are being made for this nation ,
verses.meaning: pronoia names the capacity for purposeful governance foresight — the inner capacity to anticipate and plan for the good of those under one's care. Tertullus applies it flattery-wise to Felix.

verses.verse_text: Rom 13:14 But put on the Lord Jesus Christ , and make no provision for the flesh , to gratify its desires .
verses.meaning: pronoia names deliberate inner provision — the act of planning ahead to satisfy desire. The command is not to make purposive plans for the flesh's gratification.

verses.verse_text: Rom 1:13 I do not want you to be unaware , brothers , that I have often intended to come to you ( but thus far have been prevented ), in order that I may reap some harvest among you as well as among the rest of the Gentiles .
verses.meaning: protithēmi names Paul's purposive inner intention — a goal set before the will that has been repeatedly frustrated.

verses.verse_text: Rom 3:25 whom God put forward as a propitiation by his blood , to be received by faith . This was to show God’s righteousness , because in his divine forbearance he had passed over former sins .
verses.meaning: protithēmi names God's purposive inner intention publicly displayed — Christ as propitiation is what God set forth as his intended means of atonement.

verses.verse_text: Eph 1:9 making known to us the mystery of his will , according to his purpose , which he set forth in Christ
verses.meaning: protithēmi names God's eternal purposive intention set before his will and now disclosed — the mystery of his will is the purpose he has always intended in Christ.

verses.verse_text: Isa 3:3 the captain of fifty and the man of rank , the counselor and the skillful magician and the expert in charms .
verses.meaning: bin names expertise — the specialised inner knowledge and perceptive skill qualifying the person for a particular role.

verses.verse_text: Gen 11:6 And the Lord said , “Behold, they are one people , and they have all one language , and this is only the beginning of what they will do . And nothing that they propose to do will now be impossible for them.
verses.meaning: za.mam names the human capacity for purposeful resolve — what they form as intention and set out to accomplish. The divine concern is about the power of unified human purposing when unchecked.

verses.verse_text: Jer 4:28 “ For this the earth shall mourn , and the heavens above be dark ; for I have spoken ; I have purposed ; I have not relented , nor will I turn back .”
verses.meaning: za.mam names God's resolute divine purposing — what he has formed as intention will not be withdrawn. The triple affirmation (spoken, purposed, not relented) frames divine counsel as irrevocable.

verses.verse_text: Jer 51:12 “Set up a standard against the walls of Babylon ; make the watch strong ; set up watchmen ; prepare the ambushes ; for the Lord has both planned and done what he spoke concerning the inhabitants of Babylon .
verses.meaning: za.mam names God's resolute purposing carried through — intent formed and executed, not relented from.

verses.verse_text: Lam 2:17 The Lord has done what he purposed ; he has carried out his word , which he commanded long ago ; he has thrown down without pity ; he has made the enemy rejoice over you and exalted the might of your foes .
verses.meaning: za.mam names God's resolute ancient purposing fulfilled — what was formed as intention long ago has now been enacted.

verses.verse_text: Isa 3:3 the captain of fifty and the man of rank , the counselor and the skillful magician and the expert in charms .
verses.meaning: Names a type of practitioner — the term identifies the skillful or wise person as one of several figures of social authority whose removal signals national collapse.

verses.verse_text: Isa 19:12 Where then are your wise men? Let them tell you that they might know what the Lord of hosts has purposed against Egypt .
verses.meaning: Names the class of advisors whose counsel has failed — the term identifies Egypt's wise men as those whose inner wisdom ought to be sufficient to read the Lord's purposes.

verses.verse_text: Gen 50:20 As for you , you meant evil against me, but God meant it for good , to bring it about that many people should be kept alive , as they are today .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the deliberate purposive intent of both the brothers (evil) and God (good) — the same word for both, showing divine and human purposing working through the same events toward opposite ends.

verses.verse_text: 1Sa 18:25 Then Saul said , “ Thus shall you say to David , ‘The king desires no bride-price except a hundred foreskins of the Philistines , that he may be avenged of the king’s enemies .’” Now Saul thought to make David fall by the hand of the Philistines .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names Saul's deliberate inner scheming — a purposive plan formed in the mind to use the bride-price as a trap.

verses.verse_text: 2Sa 14:13 And the woman said , “ Why then have you planned such a thing against the people of God ? For in giving this decision the king convicts himself, inasmuch as the king does not bring his banished one home again.
verses.meaning: cha.shav names David's royal purposing that has had an unintended consequence — the wise woman uses it to press the case for Absalom's return.

verses.verse_text: Neh 6:2 Sanballat and Geshem sent to me, saying , “ Come and let us meet together at Hakkephirim in the plain of Ono .” But they intended to do me harm .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the deliberate evil intent of Sanballat and Geshem — inner purposeful devising of harm concealed beneath the invitation.

verses.verse_text: Neh 6:6 In it was written , “It is reported among the nations , and Geshem also says it, that you and the Jews intend to rebel ; that is why you are building the wall . And according to these reports you wish to become their king .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the imputed intent in Sanballat's false accusation — the letter claims Nehemiah has formed a rebellious purpose.

verses.verse_text: Est 9:24 For Haman the Agagite , the son of Hammedatha , the enemy of all the Jews , had plotted against the Jews to destroy them, and had cast Pur (that is, cast lots ), to crush and to destroy them .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the deliberate inner scheming of Haman — purposeful devising of destruction against a people.

verses.verse_text: Psa 10:2 In arrogance the wicked hotly pursue the poor ; let them be caught in the schemes that they have devised .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the wicked person's deliberate inner scheming against the poor — purposeful harm-devising that the psalmist asks will turn back on them.

verses.verse_text: Psa 21:11 Though they plan evil against you, though they devise mischief , they will not succeed .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names deliberate inner scheming against God — purposeful devising of evil that cannot succeed.

verses.verse_text: Psa 35:4 Let them be put to shame and dishonor who seek after my life ! Let them be turned back and disappointed who devise evil against me!
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the deliberate inner scheming of enemies — purposeful evil-devising directed against the psalmist's life.

verses.verse_text: Psa 36:4 He plots trouble while on his bed ; he sets himself in a way that is not good ; he does not reject evil .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the deliberate inner scheming of the wicked person — the bed is the place where purposeful harm is formed in the mind.

verses.verse_text: Psa 40:17 As for me , I am poor and needy , but the Lord takes thought for me. You are my help and my deliverer ; do not delay , O my God !
verses.meaning: cha.shav names God's purposive attending and planning on behalf of the psalmist — divine inner purposing directed toward the needy person as care.

verses.verse_text: Psa 40:17 As for me , I am poor and needy , but the Lord takes thought for me. You are my help and my deliverer ; do not delay , O my God !
verses.meaning: cha.shav names God's purposive attending and planning on behalf of the psalmist — divine inner purposing directed toward the needy person as care.

verses.verse_text: Psa 41:7 All who hate me whisper together about me; they imagine the worst for me.
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the deliberate inner purposing of enemies — forming and speaking hostile plans against the suffering person.

verses.verse_text: Psa 52:2 Your tongue plots destruction , like a sharp razor , you worker of deceit .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the deliberate inner devising of destruction expressed through speech — purposeful harm-planning given voice.

verses.verse_text: Psa 140:2 who plan evil things in their heart and stir up wars continually .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the deliberate inner forming of evil plans — the heart as the site of purposive harm-devising that produces violence.

verses.verse_text: Psa 140:4 Guard me, O Lord , from the hands of the wicked ; preserve me from violent men , who have planned to trip up my feet .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the deliberate inner planning of harm — purposeful intent to cause the psalmist to stumble.

verses.verse_text: Pro 16:9 The heart of man plans his way , but the Lord establishes his steps .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the human capacity for inner purposive planning — the heart forms intent, but God determines the outcome.

verses.verse_text: Pro 16:30 Whoever winks his eyes plans dishonest things ; he who purses his lips brings evil to pass .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the deliberate inner devising of dishonest schemes — the physical gesture signals the purposive intent within.

verses.verse_text: Pro 24:8 Whoever plans to do evil will be called a schemer .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the inner purposive forming of evil plans — the person whose inner life is occupied with scheming is named by their character.

verses.verse_text: Isa 10:7 But he does not so intend , and his heart does not so think ; but it is in his heart to destroy , and to cut off nations not a few ;
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the Assyrian king's actual inner purposing — not God's instrument consciously, but his own destructive intent.

verses.verse_text: Jer 11:19 But I was like a gentle lamb led to the slaughter . I did not know it was against me they devised schemes , saying, “Let us destroy the tree with its fruit , let us cut him off from the land of the living , that his name be remembered no more .”
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the deliberate inner scheming of Jeremiah's opponents — purposeful devising of destruction against the prophet.

verses.verse_text: Jer 18:8 and if that nation , concerning which I have spoken , turns from its evil , I will relent of the disaster that I intended to do to it .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names divine purposive intent that is conditionally held — God has formed the intention of disaster but will relent if the nation turns.

verses.verse_text: Jer 18:11 Now , therefore, say to the men of Judah and the inhabitants of Jerusalem : ‘ Thus says the Lord , Behold , I am shaping disaster against you and devising a plan against you. Return , every one from his evil way , and amend your ways and your deeds .’
verses.meaning: cha.shav names God's deliberate purposive planning of judgment — not reactive but formed and intentional, like a potter with clay.

verses.verse_text: Jer 18:18 Then they said , “ Come , let us make plots against Jeremiah , for the law shall not perish from the priest , nor counsel from the wise , nor the word from the prophet . Come , let us strike him with the tongue , and let us not pay attention to any of his words .”
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the deliberate inner scheming of the conspirators — purposeful devising of harm against the prophet.

verses.verse_text: Jer 26:3 It may be they will listen , and every one turn from his evil way , that I may relent of the disaster that I intend to do to them because of their evil deeds .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names God's conditional purposive intent — disaster has been formed as intention but is held open to relenting.

verses.verse_text: Jer 29:11 For I know the plans I have for you, declares the Lord , plans for welfare and not for evil , to give you a future and a hope .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names God's purposive planning for his people — deliberate divine intent formed for their welfare. One of the most significant divine-purposing verses in the OT.

verses.verse_text: Jer 36:3 It may be that the house of Judah will hear all the disaster that I intend to do to them, so that every one may turn from his evil way , and that I may forgive their iniquity and their sin .”
verses.meaning: cha.shav names God's purposive intent of judgment held open to response — the same conditional structure as Jer 26:3.

verses.verse_text: Jer 48:2 the renown of Moab is no more . In Heshbon they planned disaster against her: ‘ Come , let us cut her off from being a nation !’ You also , O Madmen , shall be brought to silence ; the sword shall pursue you.
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the deliberate inner devising of destruction against Moab — purposeful planning of national ruin.

verses.verse_text: Jer 49:20 Therefore hear the plan that the Lord has made against Edom and the purposes that he has formed against the inhabitants of Teman : Even the little ones of the flock shall be dragged away . Surely their fold shall be appalled at their fate.
verses.meaning: cha.shav names God's purposive intent against Edom — divine planning that cannot be reversed. Both ya.ats and cha.shav appear in this verse.

verses.verse_text: Jer 49:30 Flee , wander far away , dwell in the depths , O inhabitants of Hazor ! declares the Lord . For Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon has made a plan against you and formed a purpose against you.
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the purposive planning of a human king — Nebuchadnezzar's deliberate inner intent directed against Hazor.

verses.verse_text: Jer 50:45 Therefore hear the plan that the Lord has made against Babylon , and the purposes that he has formed against the land of the Chaldeans : Surely the little ones of their flock shall be dragged away ; surely their fold shall be appalled at their fate .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names God's purposive planning of Babylon's judgment — deliberate divine intent formed against the oppressor.

verses.verse_text: Lam 2:8 The Lord determined to lay in ruins the wall of the daughter of Zion ; he stretched out the measuring line ; he did not restrain his hand from destroying ; he caused rampart and wall to lament ; they languished together .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names God's purposive determination of judgment — a deliberate, formed intention of destruction. The verb is divine and intentional, not reluctant.

verses.verse_text: Eze 11:2 And he said to me, “ Son of man , these are the men who devise iniquity and who give wicked counsel in this city ;
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the deliberate inner devising of wickedness — purposeful evil-planning in Jerusalem that combines with wicked counsel. Both cha.shav and ya.ats appear in this verse.

verses.verse_text: Eze 38:10 “ Thus says the Lord God : On that day , thoughts will come into your mind , and you will devise an evil scheme
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the deliberate inner forming of an evil scheme — the eschatological enemy's purposive intent against God's people.

verses.verse_text: Dan 11:24 Without warning he shall come into the richest parts of the province , and he shall do what neither his fathers nor his fathers ’ fathers have done , scattering among them plunder , spoil , and goods . He shall devise plans against strongholds , but only for a time .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the deliberate inner devising of military strategy — purposeful scheming against fortified places.

verses.verse_text: Dan 11:25 And he shall stir up his power and his heart against the king of the south with a great army . And the king of the south shall wage war with an exceedingly great and mighty army , but he shall not stand , for plots shall be devised against him.
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the purposive scheming of enemies against the southern king — inner deliberate plotting.

verses.verse_text: Hos 7:15 Although I trained and strengthened their arms , yet they devise evil against me.
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the deliberate inner devising of evil against God himself — the people's purposive rebellion against the one who strengthened them.

verses.verse_text: Jon 1:4 But the Lord hurled a great wind upon the sea , and there was a mighty tempest on the sea , so that the ship threatened to break up.
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the purposive force of the storm — the ship is described as if it deliberates to break apart. The term applies purposive language to the inanimate danger. An unusual use.

verses.verse_text: Mic 2:1 Woe to those who devise wickedness and work evil on their beds ! When the morning dawns , they perform it, because it is in the power of their hand .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the deliberate inner devising of wickedness — the night-time formation of plans enacted at dawn.

verses.verse_text: Mic 2:3 Therefore thus says the Lord : behold , against this family I am devising disaster , from which you cannot remove your necks , and you shall not walk haughtily , for it will be a time of disaster .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names God's purposive devising of judgment against those who devised wickedness — a deliberate divine response matching the structure of their own devising.

verses.verse_text: Nah 1:9 What do you plot against the Lord ? He will make a complete end; trouble will not rise up a second time .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the purposive scheming against God — deliberate devising of opposition that will be completely ended.

verses.verse_text: Nah 1:11 From you came one who plotted evil against the Lord , a worthless counselor .
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the deliberate inner devising of evil against God — both cha.shav and ya.ats appear in this verse.

verses.verse_text: Zec 7:10 do not oppress the widow , the fatherless , the sojourner , or the poor , and let none of you devise evil against another in your heart .”
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the deliberate inner devising of evil — the heart as the site where purposive harm is formed against neighbour.

verses.verse_text: Zec 8:17 do not devise evil in your hearts against one another , and love no false oath , for all these things I hate , declares the Lord .”
verses.meaning: cha.shav names the same deliberate inner devising — the prohibition of purposive harm-planning against fellow community members.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 7:25 I turned my heart to know and to search out and to seek wisdom and the scheme of things, and to know the wickedness of folly and the foolishness that is madness .
verses.meaning: chesh.bon names the rational faculty seeking to grasp the underlying structure of reality — Qoheleth's quest for the scheme of things as an inner explanatory capacity.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 7:27 Behold , this is what I found , says the Preacher , while adding one thing to another to find the scheme of things—
verses.meaning: chesh.bon names the same explanatory quest — the accumulation of observations in search of an underlying pattern or explanation.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 9:10 Whatever your hand finds to do , do it with your might , for there is no work or thought or knowledge or wisdom in Sheol , to which you are going .
verses.meaning: chesh.bon names thought/calculation as one of the inner capacities that ceases at death — the rational faculty that reasons and calculates belongs to the living.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 7:25 I turned my heart to know and to search out and to seek wisdom and the scheme of things, and to know the wickedness of folly and the foolishness that is madness .
verses.meaning: ya.da names the deliberate inner quest — turning the heart toward knowing as a comprehensive life pursuit.

verses.verse_text: Isa 19:12 Where then are your wise men? Let them tell you that they might know what the Lord of hosts has purposed against Egypt .
verses.meaning: Names the inner capacity of wise counsel — the term identifies Egypt's wise men as those who ought to be able to know and declare the Lord's purposes against Egypt.

verses.verse_text: Exo 18:19 Now obey my voice ; I will give you advice , and God be with you ! You shall represent the people before God and bring their cases to God ,
verses.meaning: ya.ats names counsel given between persons — Jethro offers wisdom to Moses for structuring governance. The counsel is grounded in the advisor's inner orientation and shapes the recipient's.

verses.verse_text: Num 24:14 And now , behold , I am going to my people . Come , I will let you know what this people will do to your people in the latter days .”
verses.meaning: ya.ats names Balaam's counsel to Balak — the giving of knowledge about future events as a form of counsel that shapes intent.

verses.verse_text: 2Sa 15:12 And while Absalom was offering the sacrifices , he sent for Ahithophel the Gilonite , David’s counselor , from his city Giloh . And the conspiracy grew strong , and the people with Absalom kept increasing .
verses.meaning: ya.ats names Ahithophel as David's counselor — the person whose inner wisdom shapes the plans of those who consult him. The counselor role is defined by this inner quality.

verses.verse_text: 2Sa 16:23 Now in those days the counsel that Ahithophel gave was as if one consulted the word of God ; so was all the counsel of Ahithophel esteemed, both by David and by Absalom .
verses.meaning: ya.ats names counsel of extraordinary quality — Ahithophel's inner wisdom is so accurate that receiving it is like consulting divine speech.

verses.verse_text: 2Sa 17:7 Then Hushai said to Absalom , “ This time the counsel that Ahithophel has given is not good .”
verses.meaning: ya.ats names Hushai's counter-assessment of the counsel given — he judges the advice and declares it inadequate.

verses.verse_text: 2Sa 17:11 But my counsel is that all Israel be gathered to you, from Dan to Beersheba , as the sand by the sea for multitude , and that you go to battle in person .
verses.meaning: ya.ats names Hushai's own counsel offered as alternative — inner purposive recommendation shaping the king's will.

verses.verse_text: 2Sa 17:15 Then Hushai said to Zadok and Abiathar the priests , “ Thus and so did Ahithophel counsel Absalom and the elders of Israel , and thus and so have I counseled .
verses.meaning: ya.ats names the competing counsel given — both counselors named by the same word, each shaping deliberation.

verses.verse_text: 2Sa 17:21 After they had gone , the men came up out of the well , and went and told King David . They said to David , “ Arise , and go quickly over the water , for thus and so has Ahithophel counseled against you.”
verses.meaning: ya.ats names hostile counsel — advice purposively directed against the king's interests.

verses.verse_text: 1Ki 1:12 Now therefore come , let me give you advice , that you may save your own life and the life of your son Solomon .
verses.meaning: ya.ats names life-preserving counsel — wisdom given between persons whose inner orientation seeks the recipient's welfare.

verses.verse_text: 1Ki 12:6 Then King Rehoboam took counsel with the old men , who had stood before Solomon his father while he was yet alive , saying , “ How do you advise me to answer this people ?”
verses.meaning: ya.ats names the seeking of wisdom from experienced advisors — deliberate consultation to shape a consequential decision.

verses.verse_text: 1Ki 12:8 But he abandoned the counsel that the old men gave him and took counsel with the young men who had grown up with him and stood before him.
verses.meaning: ya.ats names the rejected counsel and the alternative counsel sought — two sets of advisors offering different inner purposive recommendations.

verses.verse_text: 1Ki 12:9 And he said to them, “ What do you advise that we answer this people who have said to me, ‘ Lighten the yoke that your father put on us ’?”
verses.meaning: ya.ats names the seeking of purposive wisdom — Rehoboam asking his young counselors what to recommend.

verses.verse_text: 1Ki 12:13 And the king answered the people harshly , and forsaking the counsel that the old men had given him ,
verses.meaning: ya.ats names the abandoned wisdom — the elders' counsel was sound but rejected. The consequence follows directly.

verses.verse_text: 1Ki 12:28 So the king took counsel and made two calves of gold . And he said to the people, “You have gone up to Jerusalem long enough . Behold your gods , O Israel , who brought you up out of the land of Egypt .”
verses.meaning: ya.ats names political counsel directed toward idolatry — the inner purposing that produces the golden calves is framed as counsel taken.

verses.verse_text: 2Ki 6:8 Once when the king of Syria was warring against Israel , he took counsel with his servants , saying , “At such and such a place shall be my camp .”
verses.meaning: ya.ats names military deliberation — the king consulting with advisors to form plans.

verses.verse_text: 1Ch 13:1 David consulted with the commanders of thousands and of hundreds , with every leader .
verses.meaning: ya.ats names David's broad consultation before a major decision — purposive wisdom gathered from many sources.

verses.verse_text: 1Ch 26:14 The lot for the east fell to Shelemiah . They cast lots also for his son Zechariah , a shrewd counselor , and his lot came out for the north .
verses.meaning: ya.ats names Zechariah by his counseling function — the person whose inner wisdom makes him the one consulted.

verses.verse_text: 1Ch 27:32 Jonathan , David’s uncle , was a counselor , being a man of understanding and a scribe . He and Jehiel the son of Hachmoni attended the king’s sons .
verses.meaning: ya.ats names the counselor role defined by understanding — inner wisdom expressed in the giving of guidance to the king.

verses.verse_text: 1Ch 27:33 Ahithophel was the king’s counselor , and Hushai the Archite was the king’s friend .
verses.meaning: ya.ats names Ahithophel's role — two kinds of relationship with the king: counselor (purposive wisdom) and friend.

verses.verse_text: 2Ch 10:6 Then King Rehoboam took counsel with the old men , who had stood before Solomon his father while he was yet alive , saying , “ How do you advise me to answer this people ?”
verses.meaning: ya.ats names the consultation — same as 1Ki 12:6, parallel account.

verses.verse_text: 2Ch 10:8 But he abandoned the counsel that the old men gave him, and took counsel with the young men who had grown up with him and stood before him.
verses.meaning: ya.ats names the rejected wisdom — same as 1Ki 12:8, parallel account.

verses.verse_text: 2Ch 10:9 And he said to them, “ What do you advise that we answer this people who have said to me, ‘ Lighten the yoke that your father put on us ’?”
verses.meaning: ya.ats names the seeking of counsel — same as 1Ki 12:9.

verses.verse_text: 2Ch 20:21 And when he had taken counsel with the people , he appointed those who were to sing to the Lord and praise him in holy attire , as they went before the army , and say , “Give thanks to the Lord , for his steadfast love endures forever .”
verses.meaning: ya.ats names the deliberate communal counsel that shapes a military-liturgical decision — wisdom given to the king and acted upon.

verses.verse_text: 2Ch 22:3 He also walked in the ways of the house of Ahab , for his mother was his counselor in doing wickedly .
verses.meaning: ya.ats names counsel that shapes inner moral character toward evil — the counselor's inner moral orientation is wicked, and it forms the king's.

verses.verse_text: 2Ch 22:4 He did what was evil in the sight of the Lord , as the house of Ahab had done. For after the death of his father they were his counselors , to his undoing .
verses.meaning: ya.ats names destructive counsel — advisors whose inner purposes directed the king toward his ruin.
