verses.verse_text: 2Ch 25:16 But as he was speaking , the king said to him, “Have we made you a royal counselor ? Stop ! Why should you be struck down ?” So the prophet stopped , but said , “I know that God has determined to destroy you, because you have done this and have not listened to my counsel .”
verses.meaning: ya.ats names the prophet's role as counselor — the king refuses the prophetic counsel and the prophet withdraws it.

verses.verse_text: 2Ch 25:17 Then Amaziah king of Judah took counsel and sent to Joash the son of Jehoahaz , son of Jehu , king of Israel , saying , “ Come , let us look one another in the face .”
verses.meaning: ya.ats names the deliberate consultation before a military confrontation — inner purposing formed through seeking wisdom.

verses.verse_text: 2Ch 30:2 For the king and his princes and all the assembly in Jerusalem had taken counsel to keep the Passover in the second month —
verses.meaning: ya.ats names the deliberate communal decision formed through consultation — a major liturgical choice arrived at by seeking wisdom together.

verses.verse_text: 2Ch 30:23 Then the whole assembly agreed together to keep the feast for another seven days . So they kept it for another seven days with gladness .
verses.meaning: ya.ats names the collective inner resolution — the assembly forms a shared purpose through communal agreement.

verses.verse_text: 2Ch 32:3 he planned with his officers and his mighty men to stop the water of the springs that were outside the city ; and they helped him .
verses.meaning: ya.ats names strategic planning — Hezekiah deliberating with advisors to form a defensive plan.

verses.verse_text: Ezr 4:5 and bribed counselors against them to frustrate their purpose , all the days of Cyrus king of Persia , even until the reign of Darius king of Persia .
verses.meaning: ya.ats names hostile counsel — advisors whose inner purposing is directed against the building of the temple. Counsel as adversarial tool.

verses.verse_text: Ezr 7:28 and who extended to me his steadfast love before the king and his counselors , and before all the king’s mighty officers . I took courage , for the hand of the Lord my God was on me, and I gathered leading men from Israel to go up with me .
verses.meaning: ya.ats names the counselors as those around the king whose support shapes royal decisions.

verses.verse_text: Ezr 8:25 And I weighed out to them the silver and the gold and the vessels , the offering for the house of our God that the king and his counselors and his lords and all Israel there present had offered .
verses.meaning: ya.ats names the counselors as part of the royal establishment whose participation validates the offering.

verses.verse_text: Neh 6:7 And you have also set up prophets to proclaim concerning you in Jerusalem , ‘There is a king in Judah .’ And now the king will hear of these reports . So now come and let us take counsel together .”
verses.meaning: ya.ats names the false invitation to deliberate together — Sanballat frames the hostile invitation as mutual counsel.

verses.verse_text: Job 3:14 with kings and counselors of the earth who rebuilt ruins for themselves ,
verses.meaning: ya.ats names counselors as those who give guidance to kings — in this context, even they rest in Sheol.

verses.verse_text: Job 12:17 He leads counselors away stripped, and judges he makes fools .
verses.meaning: ya.ats names God's sovereignty over counselors — even the wisest advisors are rendered helpless by divine action.

verses.verse_text: Job 26:3 How you have counseled him who has no wisdom , and plentifully declared sound knowledge !
verses.meaning: ya.ats names the hollow counsel Bildad has given — the irony is that his advice is empty despite his pretension.

verses.verse_text: Psa 16:7 I bless the Lord who gives me counsel ; in the night also my heart instructs me.
verses.meaning: ya.ats names divine counsel given directly to the person — God's inner intentionality directed toward the psalmist through night-time heart-instruction.

verses.verse_text: Psa 16:7 I bless the Lord who gives me counsel ; in the night also my heart instructs me.
verses.meaning: ya.ats names divine counsel given directly to the person — God's inner intentionality directed toward the psalmist through night-time heart-instruction.

verses.verse_text: Psa 32:8 I will instruct you and teach you in the way you should go ; I will counsel you with my eye upon you.
verses.meaning: ya.ats names divine counsel given to the person — God's purposive inner intention expressed as watching-over guidance.

verses.verse_text: Psa 62:4 They only plan to thrust him down from his high position . They take pleasure in falsehood . They bless with their mouths , but inwardly they curse . Selah
verses.meaning: ya.ats names hostile counsel — inwardly they devise hostility while outwardly they bless. Counsel as the form of inner moral duplicity.

verses.verse_text: Psa 71:10 For my enemies speak concerning me; those who watch for my life consult together
verses.meaning: ya.ats names hostile conspiratorial counsel — enemies forming shared purpose against the psalmist.

verses.verse_text: Psa 83:3 They lay crafty plans against your people ; they consult together against your treasured ones .
verses.meaning: ya.ats names hostile counsel directed against God's people — deliberate purposing of harm through shared consultation.

verses.verse_text: Psa 83:5 For they conspire with one accord ; against you they make a covenant —
verses.meaning: ya.ats names the conspiratorial counsel unified against God — hostile purposing directed at the Lord himself.

verses.verse_text: Pro 11:14 Where there is no guidance , a people falls , but in an abundance of counselors there is safety .
verses.meaning: ya.ats names multiple counselors as the source of communal safety — diverse inner wisdom consulted together produces better outcomes.

verses.verse_text: Pro 12:20 Deceit is in the heart of those who devise evil , but those who plan peace have joy .
verses.meaning: ya.ats names purposive counsel directed toward peace — inner purposing oriented toward shalom as the source of joy.

verses.verse_text: Pro 13:10 By insolence comes nothing but strife , but with those who take advice is wisdom .
verses.meaning: ya.ats names the receptive stance toward counsel — the person who takes advice receives wisdom.

verses.verse_text: Pro 15:22 Without counsel plans fail , but with many advisers they succeed .
verses.meaning: ya.ats names the advisers whose collective inner wisdom enables plans to succeed — multiplied counsel produces better purposing.

verses.verse_text: Pro 24:6 for by wise guidance you can wage your war , and in abundance of counselors there is victory .
verses.meaning: ya.ats names the collective wisdom of counselors as the means of effective warfare — purposive inner wisdom multiplied produces victory.

verses.verse_text: Isa 1:26 And I will restore your judges as at the first , and your counselors as at the beginning . Afterward you shall be called the city of righteousness , the faithful city .”
verses.meaning: ya.ats names counselors as part of the eschatological restoration — the return of wise advisors is part of what God will give back to Jerusalem.

verses.verse_text: Isa 3:3 the captain of fifty and the man of rank , the counselor and the skillful magician and the expert in charms .
verses.meaning: ya.ats names the counselor as part of the institutional wisdom class whose removal signals judgment — counsel as a social-institutional form of inner wisdom.

verses.verse_text: Isa 7:5 Because Syria , with Ephraim and the son of Remaliah , has devised evil against you, saying ,
verses.meaning: ya.ats names hostile counsel directed against Judah — the inner purposing of enemies expressed as devised evil.

verses.verse_text: Isa 9:6 For to us a child is born , to us a son is given ; and the government shall be upon his shoulder , and his name shall be called Wonderful Counselor , Mighty God , Everlasting Father , Prince of Peace .
verses.meaning: ya.ats names the Messiah's defining role as Counselor — his inner wisdom directed toward those he governs is perfect and wonderful. One of the defining divine titles.

verses.verse_text: Isa 14:24 The Lord of hosts has sworn : “ As I have planned , so shall it be, and as I have purposed , so shall it stand ,
verses.meaning: ya.ats names God's resolute divine purposing — what he has formed as intention cannot be altered. Divine counsel as the ground of prophetic certainty.

verses.verse_text: Isa 14:26 This is the purpose that is purposed concerning the whole earth , and this is the hand that is stretched out over all the nations .
verses.meaning: ya.ats names God's comprehensive sovereign purposing over all nations — divine inner intentionality directed globally.

verses.verse_text: Isa 14:27 For the Lord of hosts has purposed , and who will annul it? His hand is stretched out , and who will turn it back ?
verses.meaning: ya.ats names divine purposing as irresistible — what God has formed as intent cannot be undone. The rhetorical question frames God's will as sovereign.

verses.verse_text: Isa 19:12 Where then are your wise men? Let them tell you that they might know what the Lord of hosts has purposed against Egypt .
verses.meaning: ya.ats names God's purposive intention directed at a nation — sovereign planning that the Egyptian wise men cannot know or prevent.

verses.verse_text: Isa 19:17 And the land of Judah will become a terror to the Egyptians . Everyone to whom it is mentioned will fear because of the purpose that the Lord of hosts has purposed against them .
verses.meaning: ya.ats names the same divine purposing — fear spreading because God's intent against Egypt is known.

verses.verse_text: Isa 23:8 Who has purposed this against Tyre , the bestower of crowns , whose merchants were princes , whose traders were the honored of the earth ?
verses.meaning: ya.ats names the divine purposing behind Tyre's fall — the question reveals that what looks like historical contingency is God's formed intention.

verses.verse_text: Isa 23:9 The Lord of hosts has purposed it, to defile the pompous pride of all glory , to dishonor all the honored of the earth .
verses.meaning: ya.ats names the moral content of divine purposing — God's inner intention has a specific aim: to humble pride.

verses.verse_text: Isa 32:7 As for the scoundrel — his devices are evil ; he plans wicked schemes to ruin the poor with lying words , even when the plea of the needy is right .
verses.meaning: ya.ats names the scoundrel's purposive inner planning — counsel as wicked purposing directed against the vulnerable.

verses.verse_text: Isa 32:8 But he who is noble plans noble things , and on noble things he stands .
verses.meaning: ya.ats names the noble person's inner purposing — counsel as the expression of moral character; noble inner intentions produce noble plans.

verses.verse_text: Isa 40:14 Whom did he consult , and who made him understand ? Who taught him the path of justice , and taught him knowledge , and showed him the way of understanding ?
verses.meaning: ya.ats names the consultation no one gave God — his wisdom is self-originating. The rhetorical question asserts divine independence from any counselor.

verses.verse_text: Isa 41:28 But when I look , there is no one ; among these there is no counselor who, when I ask , gives an answer .
verses.meaning: ya.ats names the absent counsel of the idols — no counselor among them can answer the divine challenge.

verses.verse_text: Isa 45:21 Declare and present your case; let them take counsel together ! Who told this long ago ? Who declared it of old ? Was it not I , the Lord ? And there is no other god besides me, a righteous God and a Savior ; there is none besides me .
verses.meaning: ya.ats names the divine challenge to the idols — take counsel together and then explain who prophesied this. The idols' counsel cannot produce this knowledge.

verses.verse_text: Jer 38:15 Jeremiah said to Zedekiah , “ If I tell you, will you not surely put me to death ? And if I give you counsel , you will not listen to me .”
verses.meaning: ya.ats names the hopeless counsel — Jeremiah has wisdom to give but no confidence that the king will receive it.

verses.verse_text: Jer 49:20 Therefore hear the plan that the Lord has made against Edom and the purposes that he has formed against the inhabitants of Teman : Even the little ones of the flock shall be dragged away . Surely their fold shall be appalled at their fate.
verses.meaning: ya.ats names divine counsel directed as sovereign plan against a nation — same verse, both terms naming the divine purposing.

verses.verse_text: Jer 49:30 Flee , wander far away , dwell in the depths , O inhabitants of Hazor ! declares the Lord . For Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon has made a plan against you and formed a purpose against you.
verses.meaning: ya.ats names the same human purposing — Nebuchadnezzar as counselor and planner against the nation. Same verse, both terms.

verses.verse_text: Jer 50:45 Therefore hear the plan that the Lord has made against Babylon , and the purposes that he has formed against the land of the Chaldeans : Surely the little ones of their flock shall be dragged away ; surely their fold shall be appalled at their fate .
verses.meaning: ya.ats names the divine counsel that produced the plan — same verse, both terms.

verses.verse_text: Eze 11:2 And he said to me, “ Son of man , these are the men who devise iniquity and who give wicked counsel in this city ;
verses.meaning: ya.ats names the wicked counsel that expresses their inner moral character — counsel as the outward form of their inner purposing.

verses.verse_text: Mic 4:9 Now why do you cry aloud ? Is there no king in you? Has your counselor perished , that pain seized you like a woman in labor ?
verses.meaning: ya.ats names the counselor whose absence is the mark of crisis — without wise purposive counsel, the nation is like a woman in labor without help.

verses.verse_text: Mic 6:5 O my people , remember what Balak king of Moab devised , and what Balaam the son of Beor answered him, and what happened from Shittim to Gilgal , that you may know the righteous acts of the Lord .”
verses.meaning: ya.ats names the hostile counsel Balak formed — purposive intent to curse Israel that was redirected by God.

verses.verse_text: Nah 1:11 From you came one who plotted evil against the Lord , a worthless counselor .
verses.meaning: ya.ats names the counselor as one whose inner moral character is worthless — his counsel is the expression of inner wickedness.

verses.verse_text: Hab 2:10 You have devised shame for your house by cutting off many peoples ; you have forfeited your life .
verses.meaning: ya.ats names the hostile counsel turned against its own author — the purposing of destruction against others has produced shame for oneself.

verses.verse_text: Judg 19:30 And all who saw it said , “ Such a thing has never happened or been seen from the day that the people of Israel came up out of the land of Egypt until this day ; consider it, take counsel , and speak .”
verses.meaning: uts names the deliberate communal inner purposing — the people are called to gather their collective will in response to what has happened.

verses.verse_text: Isa 8:10 Take counsel together, but it will come to nothing ; speak a word , but it will not stand , for God is with us.
verses.meaning: uts names the deliberate communal purposing of the nations — their counsel-taking and word-speaking against God's people will not prevail.

verses.verse_text: Psa 83:3 They lay crafty plans against your people ; they consult together against your treasured ones .
verses.meaning: Names the calculating, craftily strategic inner activity of those devising plans against God's people — the term identifies hostile cunning directed toward a community.

verses.verse_text: Jon 1:6 So the captain came and said to him, “ What do you mean, you sleeper ? Arise , call out to your god ! Perhaps the god will give a thought to us, that we may not perish .”
verses.meaning: a.shat names the hoped-for divine inner attending — the captain appeals for the god to direct thought toward the crew in their danger. The appeal is for purposive divine attention to their need.

verses.verse_text: Dan 6:3 Then this Daniel became distinguished above all the other high officials and satraps , because an excellent spirit was in him. And the king planned to set him over the whole kingdom .
verses.meaning: a.shit names the king's inner decisional planning — the royal inner act of purposing an appointment, disclosed as the result of Daniel's excellent spirit.

verses.verse_text: Exo 7:23 Pharaoh turned and went into his house , and he did not take even this to heart .
verses.meaning: shit names the failure of inner attending — Pharaoh's deliberate refusal to set his heart to the signs. The inner-attending faculty is present but deliberately not engaged.

verses.verse_text: 1Sa 4:20 And about the time of her death the women attending her said to her, “Do not be afraid , for you have borne a son .” But she did not answer or pay attention .
verses.meaning: shit names the collapse of inner attending — the dying woman could not direct her attention to what was being said. Not refusal but the failure of the faculty itself under the weight of grief.

verses.verse_text: 2Sa 13:20 And her brother Absalom said to her, “Has Amnon your brother been with you? Now hold your peace , my sister . He is your brother ; do not take this to heart .” So Tamar lived , a desolate woman , in her brother Absalom’s house .
verses.meaning: shit names the inner-attending faculty being addressed — Absalom counsels Tamar not to direct her heart's attention to what Amnon has done. The counsel is to suppress attending, not to not care.

verses.verse_text: Psa 13:2 How long must I take counsel in my soul and have sorrow in my heart all the day ? How long shall my enemy be exalted over me ?
verses.meaning: shit names the deliberate inner attending — the psalmist taking counsel within his own soul, turning the matter over in sustained inner deliberation.

verses.verse_text: Psa 48:13 consider well her ramparts , go through her citadels , that you may tell the next generation
verses.meaning: shit names the deliberate inner attending to what is being observed — the worshipper is called to set their attention carefully on Zion's defences so they can report to the next generation.

verses.verse_text: Pro 24:32 Then I saw and considered it; I looked and received instruction .
verses.meaning: shit names the deliberate inner attending to what was observed — the observer sets attention upon the desolate field and receives wisdom from sustained looking.

verses.verse_text: Jer 31:21 “Set up road markers for yourself; make yourself guideposts ; consider well the highway , the road by which you went . Return , O virgin Israel , return to these your cities .
verses.meaning: shit names the deliberate inner attending to the path of return — Israel is called to set their heart's attention upon the road that leads back.

subgroups.label: Meditative and reflective inner activity
subgroups.description: Terms that name the inner turning of the mind on received material — musing, pondering, the inner dialogue of self-reasoning, and the contemplative considering of a person before God. Activity-oriented (distinct from static thought-content).

verses.verse_text: Mat 16:7 And they began discussing it among themselves , saying , “We brought no bread .”
verses.meaning: dialogizomai names the communal inner reasoning about a practical matter — discussion among themselves as a form of shared deliberation.

verses.verse_text: Mat 16:8 But Jesus , aware of this, said , “O you of little faith , why are you discussing among yourselves the fact that you have no bread ?
verses.meaning: dialogizomai names the disciples' anxious shared reasoning — Jesus perceives their collective deliberation and challenges it.

verses.verse_text: Mat 21:25 The baptism of John , from where did it come ? From heaven or from man ?” And they discussed it among themselves , saying , “ If we say , ‘ From heaven ,’ he will say to us , ‘ Why then did you not believe him ?’
verses.meaning: dialogizomai names the calculating shared inner reasoning of the leaders — they reason through the political consequences of each answer.

verses.verse_text: Mar 2:6 Now some of the scribes were sitting there , questioning in their hearts ,
verses.meaning: dialogizomai names the scribes' inner silent reasoning — questioning in the heart as an inner deliberative act not yet spoken.

verses.verse_text: Mar 2:8 And immediately Jesus , perceiving in his spirit that they thus questioned within themselves , said to them , “ Why do you question these things in your hearts ?
verses.meaning: dialogizomai names the inner questioning that Jesus perceives — the deliberative reasoning within the heart that challenges the healing-authority claim.

verses.verse_text: Mar 8:16 And they began discussing with one another the fact that they had no bread .
verses.meaning: dialogizomai names the disciples' shared anxious reasoning — again about bread, the same failure as Mat 16:7.

verses.verse_text: Mar 9:33 And they came to Capernaum . And when he was in the house he asked them , “ What were you discussing on the way ?”
verses.meaning: dialogizomai names the shared deliberation the disciples had on the road — they had been reasoning about who was greatest.

verses.verse_text: Luk 1:29 But she was greatly troubled at the saying , and tried to discern what sort of greeting this might be .
verses.meaning: dialogizomai names Mary's inner deliberative effort to understand the angel's greeting — the heart working to make sense of what has been said.

verses.verse_text: Luk 3:15 As the people were in expectation , and all were questioning in their hearts concerning John , whether he might be the Christ ,
verses.meaning: dialogizomai names the inner deliberation of the expectant crowd — reasoning within themselves about John's identity as they awaited the coming one.

verses.verse_text: Luk 5:21 And the scribes and the Pharisees began to question , saying , “ Who is this who speaks blasphemies ? Who can forgive sins but God alone ?”
verses.meaning: dialogizomai names the inner questioning of the religious leaders — the beginning of their deliberative challenge to Jesus's authority.

verses.verse_text: Luk 5:22 When Jesus perceived their thoughts , he answered them , “ Why do you question in your hearts ?
verses.meaning: dialogizomai names the questioning in the heart that Jesus perceives — the inner deliberative reasoning that has not yet been spoken. Both dialogizomai and dialogismos appear in this verse.

verses.verse_text: Luk 12:17 and he thought to himself , ‘ What shall I do , for I have nowhere to store my crops ?’
verses.meaning: dialogizomai names the rich man's inner deliberation with himself — reasoning within, presented as interior monologue.

verses.verse_text: Luk 20:14 But when the tenants saw him , they said to themselves , ‘ This is the heir . Let us kill him , so that the inheritance may be ours .’
verses.meaning: dialogizomai names the tenants' inner deliberation — reasoning among themselves that produces a murderous decision.

verses.verse_text: Mat 15:19 For out of the heart come evil thoughts , murder , adultery , sexual immorality , theft , false witness , slander .
verses.meaning: dialogismos names the evil inner reasoning/thoughts that originate in the heart — these are not idle thoughts but the corrupt deliberative movements of the inner person that produce sinful action.

verses.verse_text: Mar 7:21 For from within , out of the heart of man , come evil thoughts , sexual immorality , theft , murder , adultery ,
verses.meaning: dialogismos names the evil inner reasoning/deliberating from the heart — the source is within; the thoughts precede and generate the actions. Same theology as Mat 15:19, Markan parallel.

verses.verse_text: Luk 2:35 ( and a sword will pierce through your own soul also ), so that thoughts from many hearts may be revealed .”
verses.meaning: dialogismos names the inner reasonings/deliberations of many hearts that will be exposed by the child — the hidden inner opinions and doubts that the light of Christ will surface.

verses.verse_text: Luk 5:22 When Jesus perceived their thoughts , he answered them , “ Why do you question in your hearts ?
verses.meaning: dialogismos names the inner thought-content that Jesus perceives — the formed inner deliberations that his knowing grasps.

verses.verse_text: Luk 6:8 But he knew their thoughts , and he said to the man with the withered hand , “ Come and stand here .” And he rose and stood there.
verses.meaning: dialogismos names the inner reasonings of the Pharisees about the Sabbath healing — their deliberative inner questioning is known to Jesus before they speak.

verses.verse_text: Luk 9:46 An argument arose among them as to which of them was the greatest .
verses.meaning: dialogismos names the inner disputed reasoning that becomes outward argument — the disciples' deliberating about status surfaces as communal controversy.

verses.verse_text: Luk 9:47 But Jesus , knowing the reasoning of their hearts , took a child and put him by his side
verses.meaning: dialogismos names the inner deliberative reasoning of the disciples — Jesus knows it and answers it with an enacted parable.

verses.verse_text: Luk 24:38 And he said to them , “ Why are you troubled , and why do doubts arise in your hearts ?
verses.meaning: dialogismos names the inner questioning/doubting of the disciples before the risen Jesus — the deliberative inner uncertainty that the resurrection appearance confronts.

verses.verse_text: Rom 1:21 For although they knew God , they did not honor him as God or give thanks to him, but they became futile in their thinking , and their foolish hearts were darkened .
verses.meaning: dialogismos names the corrupt inner reasoning that produces futility — the deliberative thought-life of those who knew God but did not honour him becomes empty.

verses.verse_text: Rom 14:1 As for the one who is weak in faith , welcome him , but not to quarrel over opinions .
verses.meaning: dialogismos names the disputed inner reasonings about food and days — the deliberative inner doubts and differing views that should not become the grounds of conflict.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 3:20 and again , “The Lord knows the thoughts of the wise , that they are futile .”
verses.meaning: dialogismos names the inner reasonings of the wise that God sees as futile — human deliberative cleverness under divine scrutiny is empty.

verses.verse_text: Phili 2:14 Do all things without grumbling or disputing ,
verses.meaning: dialogismos names the inner disputed reasoning/questioning that produces grumbling — what begins as an inner deliberative challenge surfaces as contention.

verses.verse_text: 1Ti 2:8 I desire then that in every place the men should pray , lifting holy hands without anger or quarreling ;
verses.meaning: dialogismos names the inner disputing/quarreling from which prayer must be free — what the hands do in prayer must not be accompanied by inner deliberative controversy.

verses.verse_text: Jam 2:4 have you not then made distinctions among yourselves and become judges with evil thoughts ?
verses.meaning: dialogismos names the evil inner reasoning that produces unjust discrimination — the deliberative inner evaluation of persons by appearance is named as evil.

verses.verse_text: Mat 1:20 But as he considered these things , behold , an angel of the Lord appeared to him in a dream , saying , “ Joseph , son of David , do not fear to take Mary as your wife , for that which is conceived in her is from the Holy Spirit .
verses.meaning: enthumeomai names Joseph's sustained inner reflection on the situation with Mary — the deliberate inner attending to a perplexing moral situation that is the context into which the angel speaks.

verses.verse_text: Mat 9:4 But Jesus , knowing their thoughts , said , “ Why do you think evil in your hearts ?
verses.meaning: enthumeomai names the inner reflective-deliberative activity of the scribes — thinking/reasoning within themselves, which Jesus perceives and names.

verses.verse_text: Act 10:19 And while Peter was pondering the vision , the Spirit said to him , “ Behold , three men are looking for you .
verses.meaning: enthumeomai names Peter's sustained inner reflection on the vision — the deliberate attending to what he has seen that is the context into which the Spirit speaks.

verses.verse_text: Mat 9:4 But Jesus , knowing their thoughts , said , “ Why do you think evil in your hearts ?
verses.meaning: enthumēsis names the inner thought-content as what Jesus perceives — the conceived thoughts that his knowing grasps directly. Both enthumeomai (activity) and enthumēsis (content) appear in the same verse.

verses.verse_text: Mat 12:25 Knowing their thoughts , he said to them , “ Every kingdom divided against itself is laid waste , and no city or house divided against itself will stand .
verses.meaning: enthumēsis names the inner conceptions of the Pharisees that Jesus knows — the formed thoughts that arose in response to the healing.

verses.verse_text: Act 17:29 Being then God’s offspring , we ought not to think that the divine being is like gold or silver or stone , an image formed by the art and imagination of man .
verses.meaning: enthumēsis names the inner conception of God that human art embodies — the imaginative act of forming a conception of the divine is the target of Paul's critique. God is not the product of human inner conceiving.

verses.verse_text: Heb 4:12 For the word of God is living and active , sharper than any two-edged sword , piercing to the division of soul and of spirit , of joints and of marrow , and discerning the thoughts and intentions of the heart .
verses.meaning: enthumēsis names the inner conceived thoughts that the word of God discerns — the cognitive acts of the heart are among the inner realities the living word penetrates and exposes.

verses.verse_text: Psa 119:27 Make me understand the way of your precepts , and I will meditate on your wondrous works .
verses.meaning: bin names the inner faculty the psalmist asks God to open — petitionary seeking of understanding for the precepts.

verses.verse_text: Psa 49:3 My mouth shall speak wisdom ; the meditation of my heart shall be understanding .
verses.meaning: ha.gut names heart-meditation as the activity that produces understanding — the reflective inner engagement of the mind before God is what understanding is made of.

verses.verse_text: To the choirmaster : for the flutes . A Psalm of David . Psa 5:1 Give ear to my words , O Lord ; consider my groaning .
verses.meaning: ha.gig names inner musing that has become groaning — deep reflective engagement with distress that the psalmist presents to God as the substance of prayer.

verses.verse_text: Psa 39:3 My heart became hot within me. As I mused , the fire burned ; then I spoke with my tongue :
verses.meaning: ha.gig names the deep inner reflective engagement that generates heat within — musing that intensifies to burning and finally breaks out as speech.

verses.verse_text: Psa 19:14 Let the words of my mouth and the meditation of my heart be acceptable in your sight , O Lord , my rock and my redeemer .
verses.meaning: hig.ga.von names the heart's meditation as what is offered to God alongside the words of the mouth — the inner reflective activity is the inward counterpart to outward speech. Both are offered as worship.

verses.verse_text: Psa 92:3 to the music of the lute and the harp , to the melody of the lyre .
verses.meaning: hig.ga.von set aside as no inner-being content — musical instrument sound, not meditative activity.

verses.verse_text: Lam 3:62 The lips and thoughts of my assailants are against me all the day long.
verses.meaning: hig.ga.von names the hostile meditations/thoughts of the assailants — what they dwell on inwardly and utter with their lips is turned against the suffering person.

verses.verse_text: Amo 4:13 For behold , he who forms the mountains and creates the wind , and declares to man what is his thought , who makes the morning darkness , and treads on the heights of the earth — the Lord , the God of hosts , is his name !
verses.meaning: se.ach names the divine knowledge of human thought — God knowing and declaring what is in the person's inner meditative life. The creative God who forms mountains also knows the inner thought of the creature.

verses.verse_text: Judg 5:10 “Tell of it , you who ride on white donkeys , you who sit on rich carpets and you who walk by the way .
verses.meaning: Names the activity of those who sit and reflect — the term characterises travellers pausing to meditate or speak of what they have witnessed.

verses.verse_text: 1Ch 16:9 Sing to him, sing praises to him; tell of all his wondrous works !
verses.meaning: si.ach names the expressive telling of God's wondrous acts — the inner musing poured outward in proclamation. The meditative attending to God's works produces the speech of praise.

verses.verse_text: Job 7:11 “ Therefore I will not restrain my mouth ; I will speak in the anguish of my spirit ; I will complain in the bitterness of my soul .
verses.meaning: si.ach names complaint as the outward expression of inner anguish — what the soul has been musingly dwelling on in bitterness is now spoken.

verses.verse_text: Job 12:8 or the bushes of the earth , and they will teach you; and the fish of the sea will declare to you.
verses.meaning: Names the act of inner reflection and communication — the term identifies the capacity of the earth's creatures to teach through their own musing or declaring.

verses.verse_text: Psa 55:17 Evening and morning and at noon I utter my complaint and moan , and he hears my voice .
verses.meaning: si.ach names the complaint that is the sustained inner expression of distress — three times daily the psalmist voices the musing of the burdened inner life.

verses.verse_text: Psa 69:12 I am the talk of those who sit in the gate , and the drunkards make songs about me.
verses.meaning: si.ach names the derisive talk of others — the psalmist's situation has become the subject of public musing and mockery. Here si.ach applies to others' inner dwelling turned hostile.

verses.verse_text: Psa 77:3 When I remember God , I moan ; when I meditate , my spirit faints . Selah
verses.meaning: si.ach names meditative reflection that produces anguish rather than comfort — the inner dwelling on God's apparent absence intensifies distress.

verses.verse_text: Psa 77:6 I said, “Let me remember my song in the night ; let me meditate in my heart .” Then my spirit made a diligent search :
verses.meaning: si.ach names deliberate inner meditative attending in the night — the psalmist turns the mind inward to search for what God has done.

verses.verse_text: Psa 77:12 I will ponder all your work , and meditate on your mighty deeds .
verses.meaning: si.ach names sustained meditative attention to God's acts — the decision to dwell inwardly on what God has done as the path through distress.

verses.verse_text: Psa 105:2 Sing to him, sing praises to him; tell of all his wondrous works !
verses.meaning: si.ach names the telling of God's wondrous works — meditative inner attending turned to outward proclamation in praise. Same as 1Ch 16:9.

verses.verse_text: Psa 119:15 I will meditate on your precepts and fix my eyes on your ways .
verses.meaning: si.ach names sustained meditative attention to God's precepts — the inner dwelling on the law that orients the eyes and the will toward God's ways.

verses.verse_text: Psa 119:23 Even though princes sit plotting against me, your servant will meditate on your statutes .
verses.meaning: si.ach names meditative devotion as the counter to external hostility — the inner dwelling on God's statutes is maintained even under threat.

verses.verse_text: Psa 119:27 Make me understand the way of your precepts , and I will meditate on your wondrous works .
verses.meaning: si.ach names meditative engagement promised in response to understanding received — the psalmist will dwell inwardly on God's wonders when given the capacity to understand.

verses.verse_text: Psa 119:48 I will lift up my hands toward your commandments , which I love , and I will meditate on your statutes .
verses.meaning: si.ach names meditative inner activity alongside physical gesture — both the lifted hands and the inner musing express devotion to the commandments.

verses.verse_text: Psa 119:78 Let the insolent be put to shame, because they have wronged me with falsehood ; as for me , I will meditate on your precepts .
verses.meaning: si.ach names meditative devotion as the psalmist's response to the insolent — while they act in falsehood, the inner life is sustained by dwelling on God's precepts.

verses.verse_text: Psa 119:148 My eyes are awake before the watches of the night , that I may meditate on your promise .
verses.meaning: si.ach names meditative attention to God's promise as the reason for wakefulness — the inner dwelling on what God has said sustains the night-vigil.

verses.verse_text: Psa 143:5 I remember the days of old ; I meditate on all that you have done ; I ponder the work of your hands .
verses.meaning: si.ach names the pondering of God's past acts — sustained inner attending to divine works as the means of finding orientation in present distress.

verses.verse_text: Psa 145:5 On the glorious splendor of your majesty , and on your wondrous works , I will meditate .
verses.meaning: si.ach names meditative dwelling on divine majesty and works — inner attending as an act of sustained worship and wonder.

verses.verse_text: Pro 6:22 When you walk , they will lead you; when you lie down , they will watch over you; and when you awake , they will talk with you .
verses.meaning: si.ach names the wisdom's inner conversation with the person who has received it — parental wisdom becomes a speaking inner companion, musing with the person upon waking.

verses.verse_text: Isa 53:8 By oppression and judgment he was taken away ; and as for his generation , who considered that he was cut off out of the land of the living , stricken for the transgression of my people ?
verses.meaning: si.ach names the inner meditative attending that no one directed toward the Servant's death — his generation did not dwell on or consider the meaning of what was happening to him.

verses.verse_text: Job 15:4 But you are doing away with the fear of God and hindering meditation before God .
verses.meaning: si.chah names sustained inner meditative attending before God as what Job's words are accused of undermining — meditation before God is a defining form of the inner life that Eliphaz considers Job to be threatening.

verses.verse_text: Mem Psa 119:97 Oh how I love your law ! It is my meditation all the day .
verses.meaning: si.chah names all-day sustained meditative engagement with the law — the love for the law is expressed in and sustained by continuous inner dwelling upon it.

verses.verse_text: Psa 119:99 I have more understanding than all my teachers , for your testimonies are my meditation .
verses.meaning: si.chah names testimonies-meditation as what produces understanding surpassing human teachers — the sustained inner engagement with God's word produces more than external instruction.

verses.verse_text: Psa 119:99 I have more understanding than all my teachers , for your testimonies are my meditation .
verses.meaning: Names the inner attainment the speaker claims through meditation on God's testimonies — the term identifies the surplus of understanding that comes from attending to divine instruction.

verses.verse_text: Dan 7:8 I considered the horns , and behold , there came up among them another horn , a little one, before which three of the first horns were plucked up by the roots . And behold , in this horn were eyes like the eyes of a man , and a mouth speaking great things .
verses.meaning: se.khal names Daniel's focused contemplative attention directed at the vision — a deliberate inner act of attending closely to what he sees in the revelatory vision, producing discernment of its elements.

verses.verse_text: Psa 49:3 My mouth shall speak wisdom ; the meditation of my heart shall be understanding .
verses.meaning: te.vu.nah names understanding as what the heart's meditation produces — the inner reflective activity generates understanding as its fruit.

subgroups.label: Inner thought-content — the mind's formed thoughts
subgroups.description: Terms that name the formed thoughts held within the mind — inner content as objects held, which can be guarded, captured, led astray, or disclosed. Static (distinct from reflective activity).

verses.verse_text: Luk 11:17 But he , knowing their thoughts , said to them , “ Every kingdom divided against itself is laid waste , and a divided household falls .
verses.meaning: dianoēma names the inner thoughts of the crowd that Jesus knows — the formed inner thought-content of persons that divine perception grasps directly.

verses.verse_text: Heb 4:12 For the word of God is living and active , sharper than any two-edged sword , piercing to the division of soul and of spirit , of joints and of marrow , and discerning the thoughts and intentions of the heart .
verses.meaning: ennoia names the inner intentions or purposes of the heart as what the word of God discerns — the purposeful inner thought-dispositions alongside the enthumēsis cognitive thoughts.

verses.verse_text: 1Pe 4:1 Since therefore Christ suffered in the flesh , arm yourselves with the same way of thinking , for whoever has suffered in the flesh has ceased from sin ,
verses.meaning: ennoia names the way of thinking or inner thought-orientation to be taken on — arming oneself with the same inner disposition that Christ had in suffering.

verses.verse_text: Act 8:22 Repent , therefore , of this wickedness of yours , and pray to the Lord that, if possible , the intent of your heart may be forgiven you .
verses.meaning: epinoia names the purposeful inner intent of Simon's heart — the formed inner thought-disposition that animated his offer of money for the Spirit. This intent requires repentance and forgiveness.

verses.verse_text: 2Cor 2:11 so that we would not be outwitted by Satan ; for we are not ignorant of his designs .
verses.meaning: noēma names Satan's designs or thought-schemes — the formed inner strategy of the adversary that the apostolic community knows and guards against.

verses.verse_text: 2Cor 3:14 But their minds were hardened . For to this day , when they read the old covenant , that same veil remains unlifted , because only through Christ is it taken away .
verses.meaning: noēma names the minds of those who read the old covenant — the inner cognitive faculty in its state of hardening, unable to receive what the covenant communicates.

verses.verse_text: 2Cor 4:4 In their case the god of this world has blinded the minds of the unbelievers , to keep them from seeing the light of the gospel of the glory of Christ , who is the image of God .
verses.meaning: noēma names the inner cognitive faculty of unbelievers that the god of this world has blinded — the thought-mind rendered unable to see the light of the gospel.

verses.verse_text: 2Cor 10:5 We destroy arguments and every lofty opinion raised against the knowledge of God , and take every thought captive to obey Christ ,
verses.meaning: noēma names the individual formed thought as what must be captured — the inner cognitive content brought under the authority of Christ.

verses.verse_text: 2Cor 11:3 But I am afraid that as the serpent deceived Eve by his cunning , your thoughts will be led astray from a sincere and pure devotion to Christ .
verses.meaning: noēma names the inner thoughts that can be corrupted — the formed cognitive content that the serpent's cunning may redirect away from Christ.

verses.verse_text: Phili 4:7 And the peace of God , which surpasses all understanding , will guard your hearts and your minds in Christ Jesus .
verses.meaning: noēma names the minds that God's peace will guard — the inner cognitive faculty held secure by divine peace in Christ.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 14:20 Brothers , do not be children in your thinking . Be infants in evil , but in your thinking be mature .
verses.meaning: frēn names the faculty of mature thinking — the capacity for rational and moral discernment that marks adult inner life. To be children in thinking is to be undeveloped in the faculty; to be mature is to have it trained and functioning at full capacity.

verses.verse_text: 2Ch 1:10 Give me now wisdom and knowledge to go out and come in before this people , for who can govern this people of yours, which is so great ?”
verses.meaning: mad.da names knowledge as the inner capacity Solomon requests for wise governance — he asks for it alongside wisdom as the inner equipment required for the task.

verses.verse_text: 2Ch 1:11 God answered Solomon , “ Because this was in your heart , and you have not asked for possessions , wealth , honor , or the life of those who hate you, and have not even asked for long life , but have asked for wisdom and knowledge for yourself that you may govern my people over whom I have made you king ,
verses.meaning: mad.da names the knowledge that was the content of Solomon's heart-request — God identifies what was in Solomon's heart as the basis for granting it. The knowledge-content of the request is exactly what was in the inner person.

verses.verse_text: 2Ch 1:12 wisdom and knowledge are granted to you. I will also give you riches , possessions , and honor , such as none of the kings had who were before you, and none after you shall have the like .”
verses.meaning: mad.da names knowledge as what God grants in response — the inner capacity Solomon requested is now given as divine gift.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 10:20 Even in your thoughts , do not curse the king , nor in your bedroom curse the rich , for a bird of the air will carry your voice , or some winged creature tell the matter .
verses.meaning: mad.da names the inner thought-life or consciousness as the moral seat — even private thoughts carry moral accountability. The word serves here as a reference to inner conscious life.

verses.verse_text: Dan 1:4 youths without blemish , of good appearance and skillful in all wisdom , endowed with knowledge , understanding learning , and competent to stand in the king’s palace , and to teach them the literature and language of the Chaldeans .
verses.meaning: mad.da names learning as one of the inner qualities of the chosen youths — the formed knowledge held within that enables them to serve in the royal court.

verses.verse_text: Dan 1:17 As for these four youths , God gave them learning and skill in all literature and wisdom , and Daniel had understanding in all visions and dreams .
verses.meaning: mad.da names learning as a God-given inner endowment — the formed knowledge-content that God placed within the four youths.

verses.verse_text: Job 12:5 In the thought of one who is at ease there is contempt for misfortune ; it is ready for those whose feet slip .
verses.meaning: ash.tut names the inner thought or attitude of the comfortable person — the formed inner disposition of those at ease, from which contempt for the suffering arises. Ease-induced inner contempt.

verses.verse_text: Psa 146:4 When his breath departs , he returns to the earth ; on that very day his plans perish .
verses.meaning: esh.to.nah names the plans that cease at death — the formed inner thoughts and purposes that belong to the living person and vanish the moment life ends.

verses.verse_text: Psa 139:2 You know when I sit down and when I rise up ; you discern my thoughts from afar .
verses.meaning: re.a names the psalmist's inner thoughts as what God discerns from afar — the formed inner content of the person's mind is fully transparent to divine knowing.

verses.verse_text: Psa 139:17 How precious to me are your thoughts , O God ! How vast is the sum of them!
verses.meaning: re.a names God's thoughts as the object of the psalmist's wondering — divine inner thought-content is precious and vast beyond counting. The same word that named the psalmist's thoughts known by God is now applied to God's own thoughts that are precious to the psalmist.

verses.verse_text: Dan 2:29 To you , O king , as you lay in bed came thoughts of what would be after this , and he who reveals mysteries made known to you what is to be .
verses.meaning: ra.yon names Nebuchadnezzar's inner thoughts arising in the night — the formed thought-content produced by the king's mind in sleep, which produced the dream.

verses.verse_text: Dan 2:30 But as for me , this mystery has been revealed to me, not because of any wisdom that I have more than all the living , but in order that the interpretation may be made known to the king , and that you may know the thoughts of your mind .
verses.meaning: ra.yon names the king's mind-thoughts as what the revelation will make known — the formed inner content of the royal mind is the object of the interpretation.

verses.verse_text: Dan 4:19 Then Daniel , whose name was Belteshazzar , was dismayed for a while , and his thoughts alarmed him. The king answered and said , “ Belteshazzar , let not the dream or the interpretation alarm you.” Belteshazzar answered and said , “My lord , may the dream be for those who hate you and its interpretation for your enemies !
verses.meaning: ra.yon names Daniel's formed inner thoughts in response to the dream — the thought-content arising in his mind at the revelation alarms him.

verses.verse_text: Dan 5:6 Then the king’s color changed , and his thoughts alarmed him; his limbs gave way , and his knees knocked together .
verses.meaning: ra.yon names Belshazzar's inner thoughts produced by seeing the writing — the formed inner content of fear that immediately produces physical collapse.

verses.verse_text: Dan 5:10 The queen , because of the words of the king and his lords , came into the banqueting hall , and the queen declared , “O king , live forever ! Let not your thoughts alarm you or your color change .
verses.meaning: ra.yon names the alarming thoughts the queen counsels the king not to let disturb him — the inner formed thought-content of fear that she urges should not master him.

verses.verse_text: Dan 7:28 “ Here is the end of the matter . As for me , Daniel , my thoughts greatly alarmed me, and my color changed , but I kept the matter in my heart .”
verses.meaning: ra.yon names Daniel's inner thoughts produced by the vision — the formed thought-content of the revelation that disturbs and must be held inwardly.

subgroups.label: Logos — the word as inner-being engagement
subgroups.description: Verses where the term logos (G3056) names the word as it enters, dwells in, addresses, or shapes the inner being — the inner engagement of the spoken or written word with conscience, mind, heart, and orientation toward God. Held as a distinct sub-group in M15 because logos sits across the wisdom/understanding/knowledge cluster without fitting any single existing sub-group and warrants its own analytical frame.
subgroups.db_state: not-yet-applied — sub-group does not exist in cluster_subgroup

verses.verse_text: Rom 3:4 By no means ! Let God be true though every one were a liar , as it is written , “ That you may be justified in your words , and prevail when you are judged .”
verses.meaning: logos P-status — quotation of Psalm, no distinctive inner-being content in span.

verses.verse_text: Rom 9:6 But it is not as though the word of God has failed . For not all who are descended from Israel belong to Israel ,
verses.meaning: Names the divine word whose integrity is at stake — the term identifies the word of God as the active inner principle whose reliability Paul defends against the implication of failure.

verses.verse_text: Rom 9:9 For this is what the promise said : “ About this time next year I will return , and Sarah shall have a son .”
verses.meaning: Names the content of the promise — the term identifies the word as the vehicle of divine commitment, carrying the specific content of God's pledge to Abraham.

verses.verse_text: Rom 9:28 for the Lord will carry out his sentence upon the earth fully and without delay .”
verses.meaning: Names the judicial sentence the Lord will execute — the term identifies the word as a decisive inner determination that will be carried out fully and swiftly on the earth.

verses.verse_text: Rom 13:9 For the commandments, “You shall not commit adultery , You shall not murder , You shall not steal , You shall not covet ,” and any other commandment , are summed up in this word : “You shall love your neighbor as yourself .”
verses.meaning: logos names the summarising word that holds all commandments — the word as inner-dwelling moral principle. All the law's moral content is gathered into the single neighbour-love word, which becomes the internal moral standard.

verses.verse_text: Rom 14:12 So then each of us will give an account of himself to God .
verses.meaning: Names what each person must render — the term identifies the account or word as the personal disclosure that each will give before God.

verses.verse_text: Rom 15:18 For I will not venture to speak of anything except what Christ has accomplished through me to bring the Gentiles to obedience —by word and deed ,
verses.meaning: Names the mode of Christ's work through Paul — the term identifies word as one of two means (alongside deed) through which the Gentiles are brought to obedience.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 1:5 that in every way you were enriched in him in all speech and all knowledge —
verses.meaning: Names the capacity in which the community has been enriched — the term identifies word or speech as one of two inner gifts (alongside knowledge) bestowed through Christ.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 1:17 For Christ did not send me to baptize but to preach the gospel , and not with words of eloquent wisdom , lest the cross of Christ be emptied of its power.
verses.meaning: Names the rhetorical resource Paul was not sent to deploy — the term identifies the eloquence of wise speech as a human capacity whose use would empty the cross of its power.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 1:18 For the word of the cross is folly to those who are perishing , but to us who are being saved it is the power of God .
verses.meaning: logos names the word of the cross as an inner-differentiating reality — the same word produces opposite inner responses: folly to the perishing, power to the saved. The word addresses the inner life and reveals its condition.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 2:1 And I , when I came to you , brothers , did not come proclaiming to you the testimony of God with lofty speech or wisdom .
verses.meaning: Names the mode Paul deliberately avoided — the term identifies lofty speech as a human rhetorical inner quality he set aside when he came to the Corinthians.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 2:4 and my speech and my message were not in plausible words of wisdom , but in demonstration of the Spirit and of power ,
verses.meaning: Names the quality Paul's speech and message lacked — the term identifies plausible words of wisdom as a rhetorical resource he consciously withheld.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 2:13 And we impart this in words not taught by human wisdom but taught by the Spirit , interpreting spiritual truths to those who are spiritual .
verses.meaning: logos names the Spirit-taught words that carry spiritual truth — inner wisdom expressed in word-form produced by the Spirit, not human inner wisdom.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 4:19 But I will come to you soon , if the Lord wills , and I will find out not the talk of these arrogant people but their power .
verses.meaning: logos names the talk that reveals the inner person's nature — the arrogant logos does not match the power that would evidence genuine inner life. The word is evaluated by what inner power it carries.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 4:20 For the kingdom of God does not consist in talk but in power .
verses.meaning: Names what the kingdom does not consist in — the term identifies talk or speech as a human inner activity that is insufficient to constitute God's kingdom.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 12:8 For to one is given through the Spirit the utterance of wisdom , and to another the utterance of knowledge according to the same Spirit ,
verses.meaning: Names one of the Spirit's gifts — the term identifies the utterance of wisdom as an inner capacity given to one member of the body for the community's benefit.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 14:9 So with yourselves , if with your tongue you utter speech that is not intelligible , how will anyone know what is said ? For you will be speaking into the air .
verses.meaning: Names intelligible speech — the term identifies the utterance that communicates inner content as the prerequisite for understanding between speaker and hearer.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 14:19 Nevertheless , in church I would rather speak five words with my mind in order to instruct others , than ten thousand words in a tongue .
verses.meaning: logos names the words spoken with the mind (nous) — the inner-directed, intelligible word that reaches the inner person of the hearer and instructs. Quantity is irrelevant; the inner engagement of the mind is what counts.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 14:36 Or was it from you that the word of God came ? Or are you the only ones it has reached ?
verses.meaning: Names the divine word whose origin Paul questions — the term identifies the word of God as the active inner disclosure whose source the Corinthians are challenged to consider.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 15:2 and by which you are being saved , if you hold fast to the word I preached to you — unless you believed in vain .
verses.meaning: Names the proclaimed content by which the community is being saved — the term identifies the word as the inner message Paul preached, to which holding fast is necessary.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 15:54 When the perishable puts on the imperishable , and the mortal puts on immortality , then shall come to pass the saying that is written : “ Death is swallowed up in victory .”
verses.meaning: Names the saying written in Scripture — the term identifies the word as the scriptural utterance whose fulfilment will coincide with the final defeat of death.

verses.verse_text: 2Cor 1:18 As surely as God is faithful , our word to you has not been Yes and No .
verses.meaning: Names the apostolic word whose integrity Paul defends — the term identifies the word as the personal commitment Paul and his co-workers have given, which has not wavered.

verses.verse_text: 2Cor 2:17 For we are not , like so many , peddlers of God’s word , but as men of sincerity , as commissioned by God , in the sight of God we speak in Christ .
verses.meaning: Names the divine word whose peddling Paul refuses — the term identifies the word of God as the sacred inner disclosure that is not to be traded as merchandise but spoken with sincerity.

verses.verse_text: 2Cor 4:2 But we have renounced disgraceful , underhanded ways. We refuse to practice cunning or to tamper with God’s word , but by the open statement of the truth we would commend ourselves to everyone’s conscience in the sight of God .
verses.meaning: logos names the word of truth as the means of reaching the conscience — the open truthful word is directed at the inner conscience of every hearer. The logos functions as conscience-addressing word.

verses.verse_text: 2Cor 5:19 that is , in Christ God was reconciling the world to himself , not counting their trespasses against them , and entrusting to us the message of reconciliation .
verses.meaning: Names the message entrusted to the apostles — the term identifies the word of reconciliation as an inner disclosure placed in their hands for proclamation to the world.

verses.verse_text: 2Cor 6:7 by truthful speech , and the power of God ; with the weapons of righteousness for the right hand and for the left ;
verses.meaning: Names the means of truthful communication — the term identifies word alongside deed as the vehicle of authentic inner expression in the apostolic ministry.

verses.verse_text: 2Cor 8:7 But as you excel in everything —in faith , in speech , in knowledge , in all earnestness , and in our love for you — see that you excel in this act of grace also .
verses.meaning: Names one of the capacities in which the community excels — the term identifies speech or word as an inner gift that Paul holds up alongside faith and knowledge.

verses.verse_text: 2Cor 10:10 For they say , “His letters are weighty and strong , but his bodily presence is weak , and his speech of no account .”
verses.meaning: Names the quality others attribute to Paul's letters — the term identifies the weight and force of his written word, which his critics contrast with his personal presence.

verses.verse_text: 2Cor 10:11 Let such a person understand that what we say by letter when absent , we do when present .
verses.meaning: Names what Paul says by letter — the term identifies his spoken or written word as consistent with his actions, countering the charge of inconsistency.

verses.verse_text: 2Cor 11:6 Even if I am unskilled in speaking , I am not so in knowledge ; indeed , in every way we have made this plain to you in all things .
verses.meaning: Names the capacity Paul claims despite his critics — the term identifies speech as the communicative inner ability in which he acknowledges deficiency, while asserting fullness in knowledge.

verses.verse_text: Gal 5:14 For the whole law is fulfilled in one word : “You shall love your neighbor as yourself .”
verses.meaning: logos names the single word that fulfils the entire law — the moral totality of the law compressed into neighbour-love. As in Rom 13:9, logos here is the inner moral principle.

verses.verse_text: Gal 6:6 Let the one who is taught the word share all good things with the one who teaches .
verses.meaning: Names the content being taught — the term identifies the word as the inner disclosure communicated in instruction, whose reception creates a sharing obligation.

verses.verse_text: Eph 1:13 In him you also , when you heard the word of truth , the gospel of your salvation , and believed in him , were sealed with the promised Holy Spirit ,
verses.meaning: Names the message by which salvation came — the term identifies the word of truth as the inner content of the gospel, upon hearing which the readers believed and were sealed.

verses.verse_text: Eph 4:29 Let no corrupting talk come out of your mouths , but only such as is good for building up , as fits the occasion , that it may give grace to those who hear .
verses.meaning: logos names speech evaluated by its inner moral quality and its effect on the hearer — the logos must carry grace, fit the occasion, and build up. The word is assessed by what it produces in the inner life of those who hear.

verses.verse_text: Eph 5:6 Let no one deceive you with empty words , for because of these things the wrath of God comes upon the sons of disobedience .
verses.meaning: Names the instrument of deception to be resisted — the term identifies empty words as a hollow inner communication used to mislead, whose consequence is divine wrath.

verses.verse_text: Eph 6:19 and also for me , that words may be given to me in opening my mouth boldly to proclaim the mystery of the gospel ,
verses.meaning: Names what Paul requests for himself — the term identifies words as the given capacity of inner expression he needs to proclaim the gospel boldly.

verses.verse_text: Col 1:5 because of the hope laid up for you in heaven . Of this you have heard before in the word of the truth , the gospel ,
verses.meaning: Names the message heard in advance — the term identifies the word of truth as the inner content of the gospel that the recipients had already received.

verses.verse_text: Col 1:25 of which I became a minister according to the stewardship from God that was given to me for you , to make the word of God fully known ,
verses.meaning: Names the content Paul was commissioned to make fully known — the term identifies the word of God as the inner disclosure entrusted to his stewardship.

verses.verse_text: Col 2:23 These have indeed an appearance of wisdom in promoting self-made religion and asceticism and severity to the body , but they are of no value in stopping the indulgence of the flesh .
verses.meaning: Names the appearance of wisdom that certain practices carry — the term identifies word or discourse as the rhetorical form that makes self-imposed religion seem plausible.

verses.verse_text: Col 3:16 Let the word of Christ dwell in you richly , teaching and admonishing one another in all wisdom , singing psalms and hymns and spiritual songs , with thankfulness in your hearts to God .
verses.meaning: logos names the word of Christ as the inner-dwelling presence — it takes up residence within the person richly, shaping inner wisdom and producing community formation. The word is a dwelling inner content.

verses.verse_text: Col 3:17 And whatever you do , in word or deed , do everything in the name of the Lord Jesus , giving thanks to God the Father through him .
verses.meaning: Names one mode of action to be done in the Lord's name — the term identifies word as the communicative dimension of human activity to be conducted under Christ's authority.

verses.verse_text: Col 4:3 At the same time , pray also for us , that God may open to us a door for the word , to declare the mystery of Christ , on account of which I am in prison —
verses.meaning: Names the content for which an open door is sought — the term identifies the word as the inner disclosure concerning the mystery of Christ that Paul seeks opportunity to declare.

verses.verse_text: Col 4:6 Let your speech always be gracious , seasoned with salt , so that you may know how you ought to answer each person .
verses.meaning: Names the quality of speech Paul urges — the term identifies gracious and seasoned speech as an inner communicative quality that enables fitting answers.

subgroups.label: Functional, supporting, and cluster-reassignment candidates
subgroups.description: Terms that carry inner-being relevance in their confirmed verses but do not carry an inner characteristic as their primary semantic load: functional roles (translation, arbitration, teaching), formative activities (training), speech acts (insisting), comparators (resembling), or terms flagged for cluster reassignment (see DIR-20260508-002).

verses.verse_text: Act 23:15 Now therefore you , along with the council , give notice to the tribune to bring him down to you , as though you were going to determine his case more exactly . And we are ready to kill him before he comes near .”
verses.meaning: diaginōskō NR status — not recorded in programme as inner-being content. Legal determination function.

verses.verse_text: Act 24:22 But Felix , having a rather accurate knowledge of the Way , put them off , saying , “ When Lysias the tribune comes down , I will decide your case .”
verses.meaning: diaginōskō NR status — legal determination, not inner-being content.

verses.verse_text: Act 20:9 And a young man named Eutychus , sitting at the window , sank into a deep sleep as Paul talked still longer . And being overcome by sleep , he fell down from the third story and was taken up dead .
verses.meaning: dialegō names the extended talking that caused Eutychus to fall — the length of Paul's reasoning discourse.

verses.verse_text: Jude 9 But when the archangel Michael , contending with the devil , was disputing about the body of Moses , he did not presume to pronounce a blasphemous judgment , but said , “The Lord rebuke you .”
verses.meaning: dialegō names the dispute/disputation as a form of inner-faculty engagement — even angelic conflict over a contested matter takes the form of reasoned disputation.

verses.verse_text: Act 13:8 But Elymas the magician ( for that is the meaning of his name ) opposed them , seeking to turn the proconsul away from the faith .
verses.meaning: methermēneuō set aside as P-status — factual name translation without inner-being content in the span.

verses.verse_text: Luk 12:14 But he said to him , “ Man , who made me a judge or arbitrator over you ?”
verses.meaning: Names the role Jesus declines — the term identifies the capacity of one appointed to adjudicate between parties, which Jesus refuses to assume over the man's dispute.
